heuristics.rule: If you are an F-1 student who needs to drop below full-time enrollment, get DSO approval BEFORE dropping the course — not after.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is considering dropping below a full course load for any reason
heuristics.action: Obtain written authorization from the Designated School Official prior to dropping any course, and only if the reason falls within the four permitted exceptions (academic difficulty, medical condition, final semester, or annual vacation).
heuristics.because: A student who drops below full-time without prior DSO approval is automatically considered out of status under 8 CFR 214.2(f)(6)(iii), regardless of how legitimate the reason may seem. Out-of-status requires a full USCIS reinstatement process.

heuristics.rule: Apply for PGWP as soon as you receive your letter of completion — do not wait for your diploma, certificate, or convocation ceremony.
heuristics.condition: When you have just finished your final coursework or placement and are waiting for official graduation documents
heuristics.action: Submit your PGWP application online within 180 days of receiving your letter of completion, using that letter plus unofficial transcript as proof
heuristics.because: The 180-day clock starts from the letter of completion date, not convocation. Waiting for a diploma wastes your window and risks missing the deadline entirely.

heuristics.rule: Track your hours from day one of PGWP employment — do not assume full-time work automatically meets the 1,560-hour threshold without verification.
heuristics.condition: When you start working in Canada on a PGWP in a TEER 0/1/2/3 occupation
heuristics.action: Log weekly hours from the first day and calculate your 1,560-hour milestone date proactively so you know exactly when CEC eligibility is reached
heuristics.because: The 1-year threshold is hours-based (1,560), not calendar-based; part-time or variable schedules can delay eligibility by months without the student realizing it

heuristics.rule: If you are an international student on a PGWP, prioritize accumulating 1 year of TEER 0–3 skilled work experience in Canada before any other Express Entry strategy — it unlocks CEC eligibility, which is the single most reliable pathway to an ITA.
heuristics.condition: When an international student has just graduated and received a PGWP
heuristics.action: Accept any qualifying skilled job offer immediately and track hours to reach 1,560 hours (1 year full-time) within 36 months, while simultaneously building language scores
heuristics.because: CEC eligibility from Canadian experience adds direct CRS points AND unlocks a faster, more competitive pool compared to FSWP, where candidates have no Canadian experience advantage

heuristics.rule: If you are applying to a Canadian college (non-degree) program, verify the program's CIP code against IRCC's eligible fields list BEFORE submitting your study permit application — not after admission.
heuristics.condition: When a prospective international student is choosing between college programs in Canada and intends to use PGWP as a bridge to PR
heuristics.action: Look up the exact CIP code of your target program on the IRCC eligible program list, confirm eligibility, then apply
heuristics.because: PGWP eligibility for non-degree programs is determined by CIP code, not program name — a plausible-sounding program title may map to an ineligible CIP code, leaving you without work authorization after graduation

heuristics.rule: Run your CRS score before you choose a program, not after you graduate
heuristics.condition: When a prospective international student is deciding which Canadian program to enroll in
heuristics.action: Use the federal CRS calculator to project your post-graduation score based on age, language, education, and planned work experience before committing to a program
heuristics.because: Most CRS-relevant variables (program type, age at graduation, education level) are locked by enrollment decisions made years before you enter the immigration funnel — late awareness cannot reverse them

heuristics.rule: If your CRS score is below the typical Express Entry cutoff (~490+), pursue a provincial nomination immediately — don't wait to accumulate more CRS points organically.
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder has 1+ year of Canadian work experience but a CRS score too low to receive an ITA through federal draws alone
heuristics.action: Simultaneously apply to eligible PNP streams (e.g., Saskatchewan Tech Talent Pathway for lower CRS, or Ontario HCP if no job offer) rather than waiting for CRS to improve passively
heuristics.because: A provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points, virtually guaranteeing an ITA — no amount of language or education points can replicate this lever as efficiently

heuristics.rule: Obtain DSO approval for Reduced Course Load BEFORE dropping any course — never after
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student faces any situation (medical, academic difficulty, final semester) that may require dropping below full-time enrollment
heuristics.action: Contact your DSO immediately, provide required documentation, and wait for written DSO authorization in SEVIS before submitting any course drop request
heuristics.because: Retroactive RCL approval does not exist in federal regulations — dropping first and asking later results in immediate out-of-status, which requires a USCIS reinstatement application to fix

heuristics.rule: Never start working on OPT until the physical EAD card is in hand — not when the application is approved, not when the receipt notice arrives.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student has applied for OPT and received an I-797C receipt or approval notice but the EAD card has not yet arrived physically
heuristics.action: Wait for the physical EAD card before beginning any work, even one day
heuristics.because: Even one day of unauthorized employment terminates F-1 status, bars reinstatement, and permanently closes pathways to H-1B and green card. The approval notice is not work authorization.

heuristics.rule: Apply for OPT 60–90 days before your program completion date, not after graduation
heuristics.condition: When you are an F-1 student approaching the end of your degree program
heuristics.action: File I-765 with USCIS after DSO authorizes in SEVIS, targeting submission 60–90 days before academic completion
heuristics.because: USCIS processing takes 3–5 months; applying at or after graduation creates a work authorization gap during which you cannot legally work

heuristics.rule: Report address changes to your DSO within 15 days (F-1) or 10 days (J-1) — no exceptions, no delays
heuristics.condition: When you move to a new apartment, dorm, or residence at any point during your program
heuristics.action: Send the address update email to your DSO the same day you move or within the first week at the latest; do not wait until it feels convenient
heuristics.because: Failure to report is a recorded SEVIS violation that surfaces during OPT, H-1B, and green card processing — causing RFEs, delays, and potential denial even years after the move

heuristics.rule: Plan 3–5 years ahead, not 6–12 months ahead
heuristics.condition: When making any decision about program, employer, or family that intersects with immigration
heuristics.action: Map out the immigration dependencies of your decision today — what visa becomes available, when, under what conditions — before committing
heuristics.because: Immigration systems have critical path dependencies; a wrong choice made years earlier (wrong program type, wrong employer) cannot always be corrected when the deadline arrives

heuristics.rule: If you are India-born or China-born and pursuing EB-2, treat the priority date backlog — not your qualifications — as the primary planning variable.
heuristics.condition: When an Indian or Chinese national begins planning a US green card pathway
heuristics.action: Check the Visa Bulletin immediately and calculate your realistic wait time under per-country quotas before committing to a US long-term career strategy
heuristics.because: Per-country quotas cap each nation at 7% of annual EB visas regardless of demand — India and China face 50-100+ year backlogs under current law, making qualifications nearly irrelevant to timing

heuristics.rule: Negotiate your starting salary as high as possible on OPT — it directly multiplies your H-1B lottery entries
heuristics.condition: When you are an international student on OPT negotiating a job offer with a company that may sponsor H-1B
heuristics.action: Push salary to reach DOL wage Level III or IV (OEWS-based), because the registration wage level determines lottery entries (3x or 4x vs. 1x), not just your take-home pay
heuristics.because: Under the FY2027 wage-based system, a Level IV salary gets 4 entries vs. 1 entry for Level I — this can shift selection probability from near-zero to near-certain if total registrations drop as projected

heuristics.rule: File OPT application within the strict window: no earlier than 90 days before graduation and no later than 60 days after the graduation end date.
heuristics.condition: When you are an F-1 student approaching graduation and planning to work in the US...
heuristics.action: Calculate and calendar both boundary dates immediately upon knowing your graduation date, and submit the OPT application inside that window.
heuristics.because: Missing either boundary permanently forfeits OPT authorization — there is no appeal or extension mechanism for this deadline.

heuristics.rule: Apply for OPT exactly 90 days before your program end date — not a day later.
heuristics.condition: When you are an F-1 student approaching graduation and planning to work in the US
heuristics.action: Submit your OPT application (Form I-765) to USCIS as close to 90 days before your program end date as possible
heuristics.because: USCIS processing takes 2–5 months; applying late risks a gap in work authorization that can cost you a job offer or trigger unemployment day accumulation

heuristics.rule: If you are an F-1 student who has been out of status, file for reinstatement within 5 months — waiting longer makes you ineligible for reinstatement through the school.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student has fallen out of status for any reason
heuristics.action: Contact the DSO immediately and initiate the reinstatement process through USCIS before the 5-month threshold passes.
heuristics.because: Students out of status for more than 5 months cannot request reinstatement unless they can prove exceptional circumstances prevented earlier filing. Missing this window may require departure from the US.

heuristics.rule: Submit your PGWP application before your study permit expires, not after — even a single day of expired status can disqualify you.
heuristics.condition: When your study permit expiry date is approaching and you have not yet graduated or applied for PGWP
heuristics.action: Confirm your study permit is valid at the time of PGWP submission; if it has expired, consult an RCIC immediately about restoration options
heuristics.because: IRCC requires your study permit to have been valid at some point during the 180 days after graduation AND at the time of application — both conditions must be met.

heuristics.rule: If your CRS score is below the current CEC cutoff, pivot immediately to PNP rather than waiting for a draw that may never reach your score.
heuristics.condition: When you are CEC-eligible but your CRS score is too low to receive an ITA in recent draw history
heuristics.action: Research and apply to a Provincial Nominee Program aligned with your occupation and province — a nomination adds 600 CRS points, making an ITA near-certain
heuristics.because: Waiting passively in the Express Entry pool burns PGWP time; PNP is the structural fix for low CRS, not luck or waiting

heuristics.rule: Do not count co-op work terms or self-employment toward CEC eligibility — they are explicitly excluded.
heuristics.condition: When an international student is calculating whether they meet the 1-year Canadian skilled work requirement for CEC
heuristics.action: Count only post-graduation paid employment in TEER 0–3 roles; exclude all co-op, internship, and self-employed hours
heuristics.because: Incorrectly including these hours leads to filing a CEC application without meeting the eligibility threshold, resulting in refusal and wasted application fees and time

heuristics.rule: If your Canadian study permit was submitted before November 1, 2024, apply for PGWP under the old rules — do not let advisors or IRCC officers redirect you to the new field-of-study requirements.
heuristics.condition: When a student graduated from a non-degree program and is now applying for PGWP after the November 2024 rule change
heuristics.action: Document your study permit submission date and explicitly assert grandfathering status in your PGWP application
heuristics.because: Students who submitted study permits before the cutoff date are exempt from the new field-of-study and language requirements regardless of when they graduated or apply for PGWP

heuristics.rule: If your program is a diploma or certificate and your study permit application was filed on or after June 25, 2025, verify PGWP eligibility against the current eligible fields list before enrolling
heuristics.condition: When a diploma or certificate student is selecting a Canadian institution and program
heuristics.action: Cross-check the specific program field against IRCC's post-June 25, 2025 eligible fields list — do not assume eligibility based on older advice or peer experience
heuristics.because: 178 fields were removed from PGWP eligibility; students in ineligible programs will not receive a PGWP regardless of academic performance, leaving them with no legal work authorization after graduation

heuristics.rule: If you have a tech job offer in BC, apply to BC PNP Tech Stream before exploring other immigration pathways — it processes in 2–3 months with no LMIA required.
heuristics.condition: When an international graduate on PGWP has a full-time, indeterminate or 1-year+ job offer from a BC employer in one of 29 eligible tech occupations
heuristics.action: File a BC PNP Tech Stream application immediately and in parallel maintain an active Express Entry profile to receive the +600 CRS boost upon nomination
heuristics.because: BC PNP processes faster than most federal pathways, requires no LMIA (which is costly and slow), and draws are run weekly — early application maximizes chances before employer circumstances change

heuristics.rule: If you have been out of F-1 status, file for USCIS reinstatement within 5 months — not after
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student has violated their status (e.g., dropped below full-time without DSO approval)
heuristics.action: Initiate USCIS reinstatement application immediately and no later than the 5-month mark from the date of the violation
heuristics.because: After 5 months out of status, reinstatement requires proving exceptional circumstances — a significantly higher and harder-to-meet standard that USCIS rarely grants

heuristics.rule: Keep full-time CPT under 12 cumulative months to preserve OPT eligibility — stop at 11 months if necessary.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is considering or currently on full-time CPT for an extended internship or co-op
heuristics.action: Track cumulative full-time CPT months carefully and ensure total does not reach 12 months
heuristics.because: Full-time CPT of 12 or more cumulative months permanently and irreversibly eliminates OPT eligibility, closing the primary bridge to H-1B sponsorship after graduation.

heuristics.rule: Set your SEVIS transfer release date AFTER your OPT end date if you plan to continue OPT
heuristics.condition: When you are on post-completion OPT and considering transferring to a new school
heuristics.action: Delay setting the transfer release date in SEVIS until after your OPT authorization period ends
heuristics.because: OPT authorization terminates automatically the moment a transfer release date is set; there are zero exceptions and OPT cannot be transferred to the new school

heuristics.rule: Never work a single day before your EAD card is physically in your hands
heuristics.condition: When you are waiting for an Employment Authorization Document and your start date is approaching
heuristics.action: Do not begin any compensated work — not one hour — until the physical EAD card is received, regardless of what your employer says about approval likelihood
heuristics.because: One unauthorized workday creates a permanent SEVIS record that can block H-1B approval and green card processing; the rationalization 'the EAD is almost here' does not constitute authorization

heuristics.rule: Always have a Plan A, B, and C for each immigration stage transition
heuristics.condition: When approaching any status expiration or visa transition point
heuristics.action: Identify at least three viable pathways before the current status expires and know the trigger conditions for each
heuristics.because: Single-path planning means a single failure (e.g., lottery loss, employer layoff) leaves you with no legal recourse and no time to act

heuristics.rule: If you qualify for NIW, file it as a self-petition rather than waiting for employer PERM sponsorship — it saves approximately 1 year of process time.
heuristics.condition: When a STEM PhD, healthcare professional, or entrepreneur can demonstrate substantial merit and national importance to the US
heuristics.action: Pursue the NIW track to bypass PERM labor certification entirely
heuristics.because: PERM alone takes approximately 1 year (6 months recruitment + ~158 days DOL processing), and NIW removes both the employer dependency and that entire timeline segment

heuristics.rule: If your employer misses the 14-day March registration window, your H-1B opportunity for that fiscal year is gone — there is no late filing exception
heuristics.condition: When you are on OPT approaching the March H-1B registration period
heuristics.action: Confirm with your employer's HR or immigration counsel no later than February that they have your registration prepared and the $215 fee ready
heuristics.because: The registration window is only 14 days (March 4–19) and USCIS grants zero exceptions — missing it means waiting a full additional year

heuristics.rule: Verify STEM OPT degree eligibility before graduation, not after.
heuristics.condition: When you are pursuing a degree in any STEM-adjacent field and plan to use STEM OPT...
heuristics.action: Cross-reference your exact degree program against the DHS STEM designated degree program list before your final semester.
heuristics.because: Many degrees that sound STEM do not appear on the official list; discovering ineligibility after graduation eliminates 24 months of authorized work and multiple H-1B lottery attempts.

heuristics.rule: After your DSO recommends OPT in SEVIS, you have exactly 30 days to file Form I-765 — treat this as a hard deadline with zero flexibility.
heuristics.condition: When your Designated School Official has recommended OPT in the SEVIS system
heuristics.action: Submit Form I-765 to USCIS within 30 days of the DSO SEVIS recommendation
heuristics.because: Missing this 30-day window results in automatic denial — there is no grace period or appeal process for this specific deadline

heuristics.rule: If you are an F-1 student planning to use full-time CPT for 12 or more months during your program, you will forfeit all post-completion OPT eligibility.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is considering extended full-time CPT engagement
heuristics.action: Use CPT part-time when possible, or limit full-time CPT to under 12 cumulative months if post-graduation OPT is a career priority.
heuristics.because: 12 or more months of full-time CPT eliminates OPT eligibility entirely under 8 CFR 214.2(f)(10)(i), cutting off the primary post-graduation work authorization pathway.

heuristics.rule: If you submitted your study permit application on or after November 1, 2024, verify your program's CIP code against IRCC's eligible fields list before applying for PGWP.
heuristics.condition: When you are a post-November 2024 study permit holder preparing to apply for PGWP
heuristics.action: Cross-check your NSCC program's CIP code against IRCC's current eligible fields list (agriculture, healthcare, STEM, trades, transport) before submitting
heuristics.because: Field-of-study eligibility is now a hard requirement for this cohort — applying without confirming eligibility wastes the $255 fee and risks a denial that ends your legal status.

heuristics.rule: Do not count co-op or work-integrated learning hours toward CEC work experience — verify your employment type before calculating eligibility.
heuristics.condition: When an international student or recent graduate is calculating how close they are to CEC eligibility
heuristics.action: Exclude any hours worked while enrolled as a full-time student; only post-graduation PGWP work or other authorized full-time work counts
heuristics.because: Co-op terms are explicitly excluded from CEC-eligible experience; miscounting this delays PR applications or leads to refusals

heuristics.rule: Target category-based Express Entry draws if your occupation falls in healthcare, STEM, trades, or French-language roles — do not wait for a general draw if your CRS score is below current general cut-offs.
heuristics.condition: When a candidate's CRS score is below the recent general round cut-off but they work in a targeted sector
heuristics.action: Monitor IRCC draw announcements specifically for category-based rounds; align job search and profile to targeted NOC codes
heuristics.because: Category-based draws consistently have lower CRS cut-offs, meaning lower-scoring candidates in targeted fields receive ITAs that they would never receive in a general draw

heuristics.rule: If you are in a transport or agriculture program in Canada and submitted your study permit after November 1, 2024, treat PGWP eligibility as lost — start planning alternative pathways now.
heuristics.condition: When a student is enrolled in a transport or agriculture program with a study permit dated after November 1, 2024
heuristics.action: Consult a Regulated Canadian Immigration Consultant (RCIC) immediately to evaluate closed work permit sponsorship, Provincial Nominee Programs, or transferring to an eligible program
heuristics.because: The entire transport category and nearly all agriculture programs were removed from PGWP eligibility in June 2025 — students in these fields will not qualify for the open work permit needed for CEC-pathway PR

heuristics.rule: Prioritize French language training if your CRS score is stuck below the general draw cut-off (~520+)
heuristics.condition: When a non-French-speaking Express Entry candidate cannot reach competitive CRS scores through other means
heuristics.action: Invest 6–12 months in French language training to reach CLB 7, unlocking both significant CRS point additions and access to French-language draws with cut-offs as low as 379–428
heuristics.because: French proficiency is the single highest-ROI CRS lever available — it adds points and opens entirely separate draw categories with dramatically lower thresholds

heuristics.rule: If you lack a Canadian job offer but have Express Entry eligibility in a tech occupation, prioritize building an Ontario HCP-eligible Express Entry profile over waiting for a job offer to unlock other PNP streams.
heuristics.condition: When a tech worker is in the Express Entry pool without a provincial job offer
heuristics.action: Ensure your Express Entry profile is complete and active with a tech NOC code so Ontario can identify you for a Notification of Interest
heuristics.because: Ontario HCP is the primary PNP stream that does not require a job offer — missing this path by not having an active profile is a preventable error

heuristics.rule: Count only ONE online course (up to 3 credits) toward your full-time enrollment minimum per semester
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is registering for courses that include online or distance-learning sections
heuristics.action: Ensure remaining credits needed to hit the full-time minimum (12 for undergrad, 9–12 for master's, 7+ for doctoral) come from in-person courses
heuristics.because: Federal regulations cap the online course credit that counts toward F-1 full-time load at one 3-credit course per semester — additional online courses do not satisfy the enrollment requirement

heuristics.rule: Before accepting a job offer for STEM OPT extension, verify the employer is enrolled in E-Verify — not just willing to enroll, but actively enrolled.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 STEM degree holder is negotiating or accepting a job offer intended to support a STEM OPT extension
heuristics.action: Ask the employer's HR team to confirm active E-Verify enrollment before signing the offer letter
heuristics.because: E-Verify enrollment is required at the time of STEM OPT extension filing; a non-enrolled employer disqualifies the extension regardless of willingness to sponsor.

heuristics.rule: Get your I-20 travel-endorsed by your DSO before every international departure during OPT
heuristics.condition: When you are on OPT and planning to travel outside the US
heuristics.action: Visit your DSO to obtain a travel signature on your I-20 (valid for 6 months) before leaving the country
heuristics.because: CBP will deny re-entry if the I-20 travel signature is missing or expired, regardless of your valid visa or EAD

heuristics.rule: File for F-1 reinstatement within 5 months of the status violation — every day past that window dramatically reduces your chances
heuristics.condition: When you discover you have fallen out of F-1 status for any reason
heuristics.action: Immediately consult your DSO and an immigration attorney; begin assembling reinstatement paperwork without waiting to see if the situation resolves itself
heuristics.because: The 5-month window is a hard legal deadline; past it, USCIS reinstatement is unavailable, and unlawful presence continues accumulating toward the 180-day and 365-day re-entry bars

heuristics.rule: Verify an employer's actual sponsorship execution capacity, not just their stated willingness
heuristics.condition: When an employer offers verbal or written assurance they will sponsor your visa
heuristics.action: Ask specifically: how many employees have they sponsored, what immigration firm do they use, what is their approval rate, and who internally tracks your status deadlines
heuristics.because: Employers who have never executed a sponsorship frequently fail at the operational level — wrong filings, missed deadlines, wrong attorneys — even with good intentions

heuristics.rule: Use I-140 premium processing when employer timeline pressure or personal status uncertainty exists — the $2,805 fee buys a decision in 15 business days vs. 8+ months.
heuristics.condition: When an H-1B holder's status is nearing expiration or an employer needs certainty before extending a job offer
heuristics.action: File Form I-907 alongside I-140 to elect premium processing
heuristics.because: Standard I-140 processing averages 8.1 months as of 2025; delays in I-140 approval delay everything downstream including AC21 protections and 3-year H-1B extensions

heuristics.rule: If not selected in the H-1B lottery, immediately evaluate cap-exempt employer options before giving up on US work authorization
heuristics.condition: When an H-1B registration comes back as 'Not Selected'
heuristics.action: Target universities, nonprofit research organizations, and government research organizations that are cap-exempt and can hire H-1B workers year-round without lottery
heuristics.because: Cap-exempt employers bypass the lottery entirely, allowing indefinite US employment authorization without competing in annual selection rounds

heuristics.rule: If H-1B was not selected in the lottery and OPT is still active, file for cap-gap extension immediately — do not wait for employer guidance.
heuristics.condition: When your OPT expires while your H-1B petition is pending after lottery selection...
heuristics.action: Confirm cap-gap protection applies and ensure the H-1B petition was filed before OPT expiration; consult an attorney at once if timing is uncertain.
heuristics.because: Cap-gap protection only applies if the H-1B was filed before OPT expired; any gap in filing creates unlawful presence.

heuristics.rule: Before accepting any job offer for STEM OPT, verify E-Verify enrollment — not after signing.
heuristics.condition: When you hold a STEM degree and are negotiating a job offer during or leading into your STEM OPT extension period
heuristics.action: Confirm the employer is enrolled in E-Verify before accepting the offer, not after
heuristics.because: A non-E-Verify employer legally cannot employ you on STEM OPT; accepting the offer first creates pressure to join anyway, which violates your immigration status

heuristics.rule: Apply for OPT 60 to 90 days before your program completion date — do not wait until graduation day.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is approaching program completion
heuristics.action: Submit OPT application to DSO and USCIS within the 60–90 day pre-completion window; confirm employment begins within 90 days of OPT approval start date.
heuristics.because: OPT processing takes time, and employment cannot legally begin until the EAD is approved and the authorized start date arrives. Missing the filing window can delay or forfeit work authorization.

heuristics.rule: Do not travel outside Canada after submitting your PGWP application unless you have a valid TRV or eTA in your passport.
heuristics.condition: When your PGWP application is pending and you are considering international travel
heuristics.action: Check your TRV/eTA validity before booking travel; if expired, wait until your PGWP is approved before renewing, since TRV renewal requires new status
heuristics.because: You cannot renew a TRV while in PGWP-pending limbo; without a valid TRV or eTA you will be unable to re-enter Canada, stranding you outside the country.

heuristics.rule: Verify your NOC/TEER classification before accepting a job offer — not after you've worked a year.
heuristics.condition: When evaluating a job offer while on a PGWP and planning to use CEC for PR
heuristics.action: Look up the NOC code for the role and confirm it falls in TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3 before committing to the position
heuristics.because: A year of work in a TEER 4 or 5 role generates zero CEC-eligible experience, wasting critical PGWP time with no PR progress

heuristics.rule: If you have or can achieve CLB 7+ in French and CLB 5+ in English, invest in French language training immediately — it is worth up to 50 additional CRS points plus access to French-stream draws.
heuristics.condition: When a candidate is within 50 points of the recent general draw cut-off and has any French language background
heuristics.action: Register for French language testing (TEF Canada or TCF Canada), target CLB 7 in all four skills, and resubmit Express Entry profile with updated scores
heuristics.because: 50 CRS points is often the margin between receiving an ITA and waiting indefinitely in the pool; French skills also open draws that are entirely separate from general competition

heuristics.rule: If you want the maximum PGWP duration, ensure your Canadian program is at least 2 years long — anything shorter gives you a shorter work permit, compressing your window to accumulate CEC-eligible work experience.
heuristics.condition: When an international student is deciding between a 1-year graduate certificate and a 2-year college diploma or master's degree in Canada
heuristics.action: Choose the 2+ year program to lock in a 3-year PGWP
heuristics.because: A 3-year PGWP gives you significantly more time to accumulate the work experience required for Canadian Experience Class Express Entry, whereas a 1-year PGWP may expire before you reach eligibility thresholds

heuristics.rule: Ensure your Canadian work experience is in NOC TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3 — not TEER 4 or 5 — before counting it toward CEC eligibility
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder is planning work experience to qualify for Canadian Experience Class
heuristics.action: Look up the NOC TEER classification of any job offer before accepting it, and prioritize roles in TEER 0–3 even if TEER 4–5 roles pay comparably
heuristics.because: CEC requires 1 year of skilled work in TEER 0–3; retail, food service, and manual labor roles (TEER 4–5) do not count, and students who work 1–2 years in ineligible roles must restart the clock

heuristics.rule: Pursue Express Entry CEC and provincial PNP simultaneously after 1 year of Canadian work experience — do not treat them as sequential alternatives.
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder reaches the 1-year mark of TEER 0/1/2/3 Canadian work experience
heuristics.action: File CEC Express Entry profile AND apply to all eligible PNP streams at the same time
heuristics.because: Whichever pathway resolves first wins; running both in parallel eliminates the opportunity cost of sequential applications and protects against any single stream's delays or rejections

heuristics.rule: Do not treat summer non-enrollment logic as transferable to fall or spring semesters
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student considers skipping enrollment in a fall or spring semester without authorization
heuristics.action: Enroll full-time in all fall and spring semesters unless one of the 4 RCL exceptions applies and has been approved by the DSO
heuristics.because: The annual vacation exception only applies to summer; applying it to fall or spring results in immediate out-of-status

heuristics.rule: J-1 students must obtain written RO authorization before any on-campus work — do not assume it is automatic like F-1.
heuristics.condition: When a J-1 student wants to work on campus and assumes the rules are the same as for F-1 students
heuristics.action: Contact the Responsible Officer and obtain written authorization before starting any on-campus employment
heuristics.because: Unlike F-1 status where on-campus work is incident to status and requires no special authorization, J-1 students performing on-campus work without written RO authorization are in unauthorized employment status.

heuristics.rule: Track your OPT unemployment days as a hard-count depleting balance starting from OPT start date
heuristics.condition: When you are on post-completion OPT and between jobs or waiting for USCIS processing
heuristics.action: Count every calendar day without authorized employment against your 90-day limit and alert your DSO when approaching 60 days
heuristics.because: Exceeding 90 cumulative unemployment days terminates OPT status automatically — USCIS does not send a warning before termination

heuristics.rule: If you are on J-1, maintain health insurance continuously — treat a lapse as a five-alarm emergency, not an administrative inconvenience
heuristics.condition: When your J-1 health insurance coverage expires, lapses, or is about to lapse for any reason
heuristics.action: Reinstate or purchase qualifying coverage immediately and notify your DSO; document the lapse duration and cause
heuristics.because: Willful failure to maintain J-1 health insurance is uniquely catastrophic: it terminates J-1 status AND bars reinstatement permanently — there is no recovery path

heuristics.rule: Spend $200–500 on an immigration attorney consultation before any major status decision
heuristics.condition: When facing a decision with real immigration stakes (program choice, job offer acceptance, status transition)
heuristics.action: Book a paid consultation with a reputable immigration attorney rather than relying solely on forums or peer advice
heuristics.because: The cost of a wrong status decision is typically measured in years lost and tens of thousands of dollars; a single consultation is a near-zero-cost risk mitigation

heuristics.rule: If you are an India-born EB-2 holder and EB-3 priority dates are advancing faster than EB-2, consult an attorney immediately about a strategic EB-2 to EB-3 downgrade.
heuristics.condition: When the monthly Visa Bulletin shows EB-3 India dates moving faster than EB-2 India dates
heuristics.action: Request an attorney assessment of filing a concurrent or downgrade I-140 under EB-3 to capture the earlier priority date movement
heuristics.because: The EB-2 to EB-3 downgrade is a legal strategy that can accelerate green card issuance by years if EB-3 retrogression is temporarily less severe than EB-2

heuristics.rule: Do not let an employer register you at the salary's actual OEWS level if a higher level is technically eligible — insist on the highest level the salary qualifies for
heuristics.condition: When an employer is preparing your H-1B registration and your offered salary is at or near a level threshold
heuristics.action: Ask your employer's immigration attorney to register at the highest OEWS wage level the offered salary meets, regardless of job title classification
heuristics.because: OEWS registration level is determined by salary amount, not job title — registering one level higher can mean 1–2 additional lottery entries, multiplying selection probability

heuristics.rule: Negotiate salary to reach OEWS Wage Level III or IV before H-1B filing under the post-2025 wage-weighted lottery system.
heuristics.condition: When you are preparing an H-1B petition under the new wage-weighted selection process...
heuristics.action: Research OEWS wage levels for your occupation and location, then negotiate your offer to at least Wage Level III before your employer files.
heuristics.because: Higher wage levels now carry materially better lottery selection probability; failing to negotiate simultaneously sacrifices income and immigration odds.

heuristics.rule: If your H-1B petition is filed and pending when OPT expires, do not file any separate cap-gap paperwork — it is automatic.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student's OPT expiration date falls before October 1 and an H-1B petition has been filed on their behalf
heuristics.action: Verify with employer that the H-1B petition has been filed, then continue working — no separate USCIS filing is needed for cap-gap
heuristics.because: Cap-gap extension of F-1 status and work authorization is automatic by regulation; filing unnecessary documents wastes money and can confuse the record

heuristics.rule: If you are traveling outside the US on OPT, secure all four required documents before departure — not just your passport and visa.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student on OPT plans to travel internationally
heuristics.action: Carry: (1) unexpired passport with valid F-1 visa, (2) SEVIS I-20 endorsed for OPT travel, (3) EAD card, and (4) employer confirmation letter including salary if visa renewal is needed.
heuristics.because: CBP officers at the port of entry require all four documents for re-admission on OPT. Missing any one of them can result in denial of re-entry.

heuristics.rule: Select 'No' for the job offer question during the PGWP application questionnaire even if you already have a job lined up.
heuristics.condition: When completing the IRCC online questionnaire for an open work permit / PGWP
heuristics.action: Answer 'No' to 'Do you have a written job offer?' to correctly route your application to an open work permit rather than an employer-specific permit
heuristics.because: Answering 'Yes' routes you to a closed, employer-specific work permit — losing the open-work-permit flexibility that is the entire value of the PGWP.

heuristics.rule: As a CEC applicant, do not budget for or worry about settlement funds — but do confirm your language scores meet the TEER-specific CLB threshold before submitting your Express Entry profile.
heuristics.condition: When preparing a CEC Express Entry profile
heuristics.action: Confirm CLB 7 for TEER 0/1 roles or CLB 5 for TEER 2/3 roles across all four skills; skip settlement fund preparation (not required for CEC)
heuristics.because: Language scores below threshold = automatic ineligibility; settlement funds are a FSW requirement that does not apply to CEC, so conflating the two wastes time and causes unnecessary stress

heuristics.rule: After March 25, 2025, do not factor a Canadian job offer into your Express Entry CRS score calculation — it no longer adds any points.
heuristics.condition: When advising a PGWP holder who has received a full-time job offer and is calculating their CRS score
heuristics.action: Remove arranged employment points from CRS estimate; evaluate the job offer purely on its career and experience accumulation merits
heuristics.because: The arranged employment bonus of 50–200 CRS points was removed effective March 25, 2025; including it in calculations produces an inflated score and false confidence in ITA likelihood

heuristics.rule: If you are a university degree graduate (bachelor's, master's, PhD) in Canada, do not waste time verifying field-of-study eligibility — focus entirely on meeting the language requirement (CLB 7).
heuristics.condition: When a university-level graduate is preparing their PGWP application post-November 2024
heuristics.action: Prioritize scheduling IELTS, CELPIP, or TEF early to avoid delays; field of study is irrelevant for degree holders
heuristics.because: University degree holders are fully exempt from the field-of-study restriction — their only new requirement is the language benchmark, which is the actual bottleneck

heuristics.rule: Have your spouse take IELTS and document their education credentials as soon as you begin your PGWP period
heuristics.condition: When a married or common-law PGWP holder is building toward an Express Entry application
heuristics.action: Get your spouse's language tested and education assessed (ECA if foreign credentials) — do not wait until you are ready to file
heuristics.because: Spousal language and education scores can add 10–40 CRS points, a meaningful difference at current cut-offs, and assessments take months to complete

heuristics.rule: If your CRS score is low and you are open to smaller cities, target Saskatchewan Tech Talent Pathway specifically — it is designed for lower-CRS candidates with a job offer.
heuristics.condition: When a tech worker has a Saskatchewan employer job offer but a CRS score that would not be competitive in BC or Alberta PNP streams
heuristics.action: Apply to Saskatchewan Tech Talent Pathway and accept that geographic flexibility is a direct immigration advantage
heuristics.because: Saskatchewan explicitly accepts lower CRS scores than other provinces, making it one of the most accessible PNP routes for candidates who would otherwise be stuck in the Express Entry pool

heuristics.rule: Use the 60-day grace period deliberately — do not let it expire passively
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student completes their academic program
heuristics.action: Within 60 days of program completion, take a concrete action: apply for OPT, transfer to another school, change immigration status, or depart the US
heuristics.because: The grace period is not an extension of status — it is a window to take action; failing to act within it results in unlawful presence accrual

heuristics.rule: Begin OPT-authorized work within 90 days of the OPT start date on the EAD — do not treat the start date as flexible.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student has received their EAD but has not yet secured a job or is delaying start for any reason
heuristics.action: Track unemployment days from the OPT start date and ensure work begins within the 90-day window
heuristics.because: The 90-day unemployment limit begins on the OPT start date printed on the EAD, not on the date work actually begins; exceeding it violates status.

heuristics.rule: Apply for STEM OPT extension before your current OPT EAD expires
heuristics.condition: When you hold a STEM degree, are employed by an E-Verify employer, and are in the final months of standard 12-month OPT
heuristics.action: Submit the STEM extension application (with I-983 Training Plan signed by employer) while current OPT is still valid
heuristics.because: Filing after OPT expiration means your work authorization has already lapsed; there is no grace period for late STEM extension filings

heuristics.rule: Treat pre-graduation status violations as active threats even after you receive your degree
heuristics.condition: When you are applying for OPT, H-1B, or any post-completion benefit after graduating
heuristics.action: Before filing any post-graduation immigration benefit, review your full SEVIS compliance history with a DSO or attorney and resolve any outstanding issues proactively
heuristics.because: USCIS cross-references SEVIS records against immigration applications; violations that occurred before graduation surface during OPT and H-1B processing and cause denials or RFEs

heuristics.rule: Reframe the stay-vs-return decision as a 10-year compounding question, not a current-conditions comparison
heuristics.condition: When evaluating whether to stay in the destination country or return home after graduation
heuristics.action: Compare career, financial, relational, and personal compounding trajectories over 10 years across both paths — including the full cost of immigration uncertainty
heuristics.because: Comparing current living conditions is emotionally loaded and systematically ignores the immigration stability tax and long-run trajectory differences between markets

heuristics.rule: Once your I-140 is approved, ensure your employer files for H-1B extensions beyond the 6-year cap under AC21 Section 104(c) — do not assume HR knows to do this automatically.
heuristics.condition: When an H-1B holder reaches or approaches the standard 6-year cap with an approved I-140 but no current priority date
heuristics.action: Proactively alert your immigration attorney and HR to file for 3-year H-1B extensions under AC21
heuristics.because: Without this extension, status lapses at the 6-year mark — but AC21 explicitly permits indefinite 3-year extensions once I-140 is approved, and many employers miss this trigger

heuristics.rule: If you hold an advanced degree from a US institution, confirm your employer is filing under the master's cap exemption, not just the regular cap
heuristics.condition: When you have a US master's degree or higher and your employer is registering you for H-1B
heuristics.action: Verify with your employer's counsel that you are being entered in the 20,000 advanced degree exemption pool in addition to (or instead of) the regular 65,000 cap pool
heuristics.because: The master's cap provides an additional 20,000 visas annually — failure to use it wastes a significant statistical advantage you are entitled to

heuristics.rule: Register for the H-1B lottery during the March 1–18 window every eligible year — missing it costs a full additional year of waiting.
heuristics.condition: When you are on OPT or STEM OPT and H-1B cap-subject filing is your target status...
heuristics.action: Set multi-channel reminders for the registration window and confirm with your employer's immigration counsel at least 60 days in advance.
heuristics.because: There is no late registration option; a missed window means at minimum one additional year on OPT or alternative status, consuming a lottery attempt.

heuristics.rule: Start tracking unemployment days from Day 1 of your OPT period — not from when you first notice you are approaching the limit.
heuristics.condition: When post-completion OPT begins
heuristics.action: Create a personal log of all unemployed days starting from the OPT start date on your EAD card
heuristics.because: The 90-day limit (regular OPT) and cumulative 150-day limit (STEM OPT) are strictly enforced; USCIS does not send warnings, and exceeding the limit terminates F-1 status automatically

heuristics.rule: If you are a J-1 student who lets health insurance lapse, your status can be terminated and you cannot reinstate it.
heuristics.condition: When a J-1 student considers canceling or allowing health insurance to expire
heuristics.action: Maintain continuous J-1 compliant health insurance coverage throughout the entire duration of status, including during Academic Training periods.
heuristics.because: Under 22 CFR 62.14(h)(i), willful failure to maintain health insurance requires the RO to terminate J-1 status in SEVIS, and terminated J-1 students cannot reinstate under 22 CFR 62.45(f)(1). This is a non-recoverable status loss.

heuristics.rule: Upload your language test results under 'Client Information,' not under 'Proof of Language Proficiency,' because no such dedicated field exists in the IRCC portal.
heuristics.condition: When uploading documents for a PGWP application in the IRCC online system
heuristics.action: Place CELPIP/IELTS/PTE Core results in the 'Client Information' document upload section
heuristics.because: Students who look for a dedicated language proficiency field and find none may skip uploading their scores entirely, causing application refusal for missing evidence.

heuristics.rule: If you are applying under FSWP and have not studied or worked in Canada, obtain an Educational Credential Assessment (ECA) early — before submitting your Express Entry profile — to avoid delays that can expire your profile.
heuristics.condition: When an international student intends to apply under FSWP from outside Canada or without Canadian study credentials
heuristics.action: Initiate ECA through a designated organization (e.g., WES) at least 3–4 months before planned profile submission
heuristics.because: Without a valid ECA, the education points (up to 150) cannot be claimed and the profile may not meet the 67-point FSWP minimum, making the candidate ineligible entirely

heuristics.rule: If your Canadian study permit was submitted before June 25, 2025, and your program was removed in the June 2025 overhaul, apply for PGWP immediately and assert the pre-June-2025 grandfathering rule.
heuristics.condition: When a student's program was on the eligible list at the time of their study permit application but was subsequently removed in the June 2025 overhaul
heuristics.action: File the PGWP application with clear documentation of your study permit submission date and a record of the eligible fields list as it existed at that date
heuristics.because: IRCC's grandfathering policy locks in the rules effective at the time of the study permit application — students in removed programs are still eligible if their permit predates the overhaul

heuristics.rule: Monitor PNP draw streams for your NOC code actively — do not wait passively for a federal Express Entry draw
heuristics.condition: When a candidate has a base CRS score below the current federal draw cut-off but has Canadian work experience in a specific occupation
heuristics.action: Identify which provinces have active PNP streams aligned to your NOC code and apply when your score meets provincial thresholds
heuristics.because: A provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points, effectively guaranteeing an ITA regardless of base score — it is often faster than waiting for a general federal draw

heuristics.rule: Use part-time CPT instead of full-time CPT when possible, as it does not affect OPT eligibility regardless of duration.
heuristics.condition: When a student needs CPT authorization and the role can be structured as part-time
heuristics.action: Request part-time CPT authorization from DSO rather than full-time CPT
heuristics.because: Part-time CPT has no cumulative limit that affects OPT; only full-time CPT counted at 12+ months triggers permanent OPT ineligibility.

heuristics.rule: Identify your program completion date from academic records, not from the graduation ceremony calendar
heuristics.condition: When calculating the start of your F-1 60-day grace period
heuristics.action: Confirm with your registrar the exact date all academic requirements are fulfilled — this is the legal completion date
heuristics.because: Graduation ceremonies often occur months after actual academic completion; relying on ceremony date causes students to miscalculate the grace period and accrue unlawful presence

heuristics.rule: If on J-1, check whether you are subject to the two-year home residency requirement before making any H-1B or immigrant visa plans
heuristics.condition: When a J-1 student begins planning their post-graduation immigration pathway
heuristics.action: Request a two-year rule determination from your DSO or review your DS-2019 and funding source; if subject, pursue waiver options early
heuristics.because: The two-year home residency requirement blocks H-1B and immigrant visa applications; students who ignore it build career plans on a foundation that collapses at visa application time

heuristics.rule: Build an O-1 or EB-1 eligible track record continuously, not when you need it
heuristics.condition: When you are early in your career and have any possibility of needing an extraordinary ability visa later
heuristics.action: Document publications, awards, speaking invitations, and peer recognition consistently from early career stages
heuristics.because: O-1 and EB-1 eligibility requires a documented multi-year track record; you cannot retroactively manufacture the evidence when you urgently need the visa

heuristics.rule: Consult an immigration attorney at least 6 months before OPT expiration, not after receiving a lottery rejection.
heuristics.condition: When any F-1 student is within 6 months of OPT or STEM OPT expiration...
heuristics.action: Schedule a paid attorney consultation to map cap-gap, alternative visa, or departure options before the deadline pressure eliminates options.
heuristics.because: Several alternatives (O-1 preparation, L-1 arrangement, cap-exempt employer search) require months of lead time that does not exist after a late-March lottery result.

heuristics.rule: When changing employers during STEM OPT, report the change to your DSO within 10 days and submit a new Form I-983 — do not wait until the next check-in.
heuristics.condition: When a student on STEM OPT accepts a new job or leaves a previous employer
heuristics.action: Notify DSO within 10 days and prepare a new I-983 Training Plan with the new employer before starting work
heuristics.because: Failure to report within 10 days is a SEVIS compliance violation; working without a valid I-983 on file exposes the student to status termination

heuristics.rule: Get your DSO to sign and date your I-20 for travel at least 3 to 4 weeks before you leave the US.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 or J-1 student plans to travel outside the United States
heuristics.action: Request a travel signature on the Certificate of Eligibility from your DSO or RO at least 3–4 weeks before departure to allow time to gather financial documents for possible visa renewal.
heuristics.because: Re-entry requires a valid travel-endorsed I-20 or DS-2019. Without adequate lead time, students may be unable to gather required financial documentation for visa reissuance at a consulate abroad.

heuristics.rule: Once you submit your PGWP application with a valid study permit and qualifying program history, begin working full-time immediately — you do not need to wait for the permit to arrive.
heuristics.condition: When your study permit was valid at submission, your program was full-time and longer than eight months, and you did not violate your work-hour limits as a student
heuristics.action: Start full-time work as soon as the PGWP application is submitted and confirmed received by IRCC
heuristics.because: IRCC allows 'maintained status' work authorization during processing, but only if all three qualifying criteria are met — missing any one of them means you are not authorized to work and could face removal.

heuristics.rule: Pursue a Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) stream aggressively if your CRS score is below the general draw cut-off — a provincial nomination adds 600 points and virtually guarantees an ITA.
heuristics.condition: When a candidate has been in the Express Entry pool for 6+ months without receiving an ITA and their CRS score is consistently below recent cut-offs
heuristics.action: Research PNP streams aligned to your occupation and province of residence or intended residence; apply to the provincial stream in parallel with maintaining Express Entry profile
heuristics.because: 600 additional CRS points from an enhanced provincial nomination essentially removes competition — no other single factor comes close to this impact on ITA likelihood

heuristics.rule: Apply for PGWP immediately after graduation — do not wait for employer confirmation or job offers
heuristics.condition: When a student completes their eligible Canadian study program
heuristics.action: Submit the PGWP application as soon as your final transcript or graduation confirmation is available, including the now-required language test results
heuristics.because: PGWP applications must be filed while your study permit is still valid or within the implied status period; delays can result in loss of eligibility and gaps in work authorization

heuristics.rule: Account for the part-time pre-completion OPT deduction when planning post-graduation work authorization — every 2 months part-time pre-OPT costs 1 month of post-OPT.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is considering using pre-completion OPT during their final semesters
heuristics.action: Calculate how many months of post-completion OPT will remain after the pre-OPT deduction and assess whether that leaves enough time to bridge to H-1B
heuristics.because: Students who use 6 months of part-time pre-completion OPT lose 3 months from their 12-month post-OPT authorization, potentially disrupting H-1B cap-gap timing.

heuristics.rule: If planning to pursue a second degree after the first, preserve each degree level's independent OPT eligibility
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is strategically considering enrollment in a new degree program to reset OPT eligibility
heuristics.action: Enroll in a degree at a different level (e.g., bachelor's → master's → PhD) to access a fresh 12-month OPT authorization
heuristics.because: Each degree level independently qualifies for its own 12-month OPT; this is a legal mechanism to extend total authorized work time in the US

heuristics.rule: Use immigration forums only to generate questions, not to make decisions
heuristics.condition: When researching an immigration question online via Reddit, Blind, or peer groups
heuristics.action: Treat forum answers as hypotheses to verify with an attorney, not as reliable guidance for your specific case
heuristics.because: Forum advice is subject to survivorship bias, outdated rules, and cannot account for the individual circumstances that determine actual immigration outcomes

heuristics.rule: If you are a Canadian or Mexican national in a qualifying profession, evaluate TN visa before pursuing H-1B.
heuristics.condition: When a Canadian or Mexican citizen is planning US work authorization strategy...
heuristics.action: Confirm whether your profession appears on the TN designated occupation list; if yes, treat TN as the primary pathway and H-1B as a backup.
heuristics.because: TN has no annual cap, no lottery, Canadians can apply at the port of entry same-day, and it is renewable indefinitely — structurally superior to H-1B for eligible nationals.

heuristics.rule: Negotiate the highest salary possible during STEM OPT — it directly affects H-1B lottery selection odds starting FY2027.
heuristics.condition: When a student on OPT or STEM OPT is evaluating a job offer and H-1B sponsorship is a future goal
heuristics.action: Prioritize compensation negotiation as a strategic immigration tool, not just a financial one
heuristics.because: Under the wage-based H-1B lottery system (FY2027+), higher wage level registrations are selected at higher rates, making salary a direct determinant of visa lottery success

heuristics.rule: If your OPT ends and you transfer to another school, set the transfer release date after OPT completion — not before.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student on OPT wants to transfer to another SEVP-certified school
heuristics.action: Coordinate with your DSO to set the SEVIS transfer release date for a date after your OPT end date, not before or during active OPT.
heuristics.because: OPT authorization automatically terminates on the transfer release date. If release happens before OPT ends, the remaining OPT is forfeited and cannot be transferred to the new school.

heuristics.rule: Ensure your language test results are no older than 2 years at the time of PGWP application.
heuristics.condition: When you took an approved language test (CELPIP, IELTS General, PTE Core) more than 18 months ago and are approaching graduation
heuristics.action: Retake the test before submitting your PGWP application if results will be over 2 years old at submission date
heuristics.because: Expired language test scores are treated as if no test was submitted — the application will be refused regardless of your actual English proficiency.

heuristics.rule: Do not rely on job offer points as a CRS strategy — they were removed as of March 25, 2025
heuristics.condition: When a candidate is evaluating whether to pursue a specific employer in hopes of boosting their Express Entry score
heuristics.action: Redirect effort previously spent on employer-based CRS boosts toward language scores, French proficiency, and PNP streams
heuristics.because: Job offer points no longer exist in CRS scoring; acting on outdated advice wastes strategic energy that could be spent on still-valid levers

heuristics.rule: If you have publications, awards, or documented high salary evidence, assess O-1 eligibility before assuming H-1B is the only path.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student with strong academic or professional credentials does not get selected in the H-1B lottery...
heuristics.action: Compile a list of publications, citations, awards, media coverage, and salary comparisons and present them to an immigration attorney for O-1 assessment.
heuristics.because: O-1 is filed year-round with no cap or lottery, and strong academic records often satisfy more criteria than students realize; dismissing it without assessment wastes a viable path.

heuristics.rule: If you are a J-1 student who used 12 months of Academic Training at a prior institution, your post-doctoral AT is capped at 24 months, not 36.
heuristics.condition: When a J-1 PhD student plans post-doctoral Academic Training
heuristics.action: Track all prior AT usage across all institutions and programs before assuming maximum eligibility, and verify the cumulative total with your RO.
heuristics.because: AT is counted cumulatively across all institutions. Students who assume fresh 36-month eligibility after each program level will miscalculate their remaining authorized training period.

heuristics.rule: Do not begin on-campus employment as an F-1 student more than 30 days before classes actually start, even if you arrived earlier.
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student arrives in the US before the program start date and is offered on-campus work
heuristics.action: Delay start of any on-campus employment until within the 30-day pre-class window.
heuristics.because: Commencing on-campus work more than 30 days before the actual start of classes violates F-1 status conditions under 8 CFR 214.2(f)(9)(i).

heuristics.rule: French language testing (TEF Canada to CLB 7) is the highest ROI single action for CRS score improvement
heuristics.condition: When your CRS score is in the 440–490 range and you lack a job offer or provincial nomination
heuristics.action: Invest 3 months in French study and take TEF Canada to reach CLB 7, targeting the +50 bilingualism bonus
heuristics.because: The original poster documented gaining ~50 CRS points from French alone in 3 months using accessible resources (Duolingo + italki), requiring no employer or provincial government cooperation — purely self-driven
heuristics._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

heuristics.rule: Apply for PGWP within 180 days of graduation, not the day your study permit expires
heuristics.condition: When you have graduated from an eligible Canadian institution and plan to stay and work in Canada...
heuristics.action: Submit PGWP application as early as possible after receiving official proof of graduation, but no later than 180 days post-graduation
heuristics.because: The source explicitly states the 180-day window as a hard deadline after which eligibility is lost; the poster applied on day 3 post-graduation
heuristics._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

heuristics.rule: Before making any program change, consult your international student advisor about PGWP impact first
heuristics.condition: When a student is considering switching programs, transferring schools, or modifying their academic path in Canada
heuristics.action: Book a meeting with the international student office specifically to assess PGWP duration consequences before any academic decision is finalized
heuristics.because: The post author explicitly states this as their core recommendation, validated by 234-like and 189-like replies confirming others hadn't thought of this either — academic advisors and immigration advisors optimize for different outcomes
heuristics._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

heuristics.rule: If you are Chinese or Indian background, seriously consider the Canada route before committing to US green card
heuristics.condition: When a Chinese or Indian international student or worker is deciding between US and Canada immigration paths
heuristics.action: Evaluate EB-2/EB-3 priority date backlog for your country of birth before assuming you can get a US green card within your working career
heuristics.because: EB-2 India priority dates were cited as being from 2012 as of the poster's graduation year, meaning decades-long waits; multiple commenters corroborated this with personal experience
heuristics._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

heuristics.rule: Add a one-line work authorization statement at the top of your resume to preempt HR confusion about PGWP
heuristics.condition: When applying to Canadian jobs on a PGWP, especially at small-to-mid-size companies where HR may not know immigration status types
heuristics.action: Include a line such as 'Authorized to work in Canada (Open Work Permit – no sponsorship required)' visibly near the top of your resume
heuristics.because: Multiple commenters confirmed this proactively removes the friction of HR misreading 'work permit' as requiring employer sponsorship, with the top reply receiving 189 likes endorsing this specific tactic
heuristics._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

heuristics.rule: Use L1-B as an H1B lottery bypass via overseas assignment, but plan the 5-year clock from day one
heuristics.condition: When an employer offers an L1-B transfer path requiring 1 year abroad and you have failed the H1B lottery multiple times...
heuristics.action: Accept the L1-B path but immediately start planning EB1B or EB2 green card filing within the 5-year L1-B window, not after it expires
heuristics.because: L1-B has a hard 5-year cap in the US; failure to convert within that window forces a return overseas for 1 year before a new L1 can be filed, resetting the clock

heuristics.rule: Anchor your NIW petition to U.S. national policy priorities and government reports
heuristics.condition: When writing the Personal Statement for EB-2 NIW Dhanasar prong 1 (substantial merit and national importance)...
heuristics.action: Explicitly cite U.S. government reports, federal agency priorities, and national policy documents to connect your research to stated national interests
heuristics.because: The petitioner reported this strategy as key to passing prong 1, and it aligns with the Dhanasar framework requiring benefit to the U.S. broadly, not just a single employer
heuristics._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

heuristics.rule: Actively research Plan B until it becomes a genuinely acceptable option, not just a fallback
heuristics.condition: When facing repeated H-1B lottery failures and anxiety about future status...
heuristics.action: Spend real time researching alternative paths (Canada PGWP/Express Entry, return home) in enough depth that they feel like real choices rather than admissions of defeat
heuristics.because: Multiple high-voted comments confirm that treating Plan B as real — not hypothetical — reduces chronic anxiety and paralysis; the poster explicitly states that researching Canada made it 'genuinely acceptable, not just a backup'
heuristics._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

heuristics.rule: If your goal is long-term US residency, avoid Day 1 CPT — the short-term work authorization is not worth the downstream immigration risk
heuristics.condition: When a post-undergrad international student is considering a Day 1 CPT program to extend work authorization
heuristics.action: Use STEM OPT as the standard path and avoid degree mills offering immediate CPT
heuristics.because: USCIS can retroactively invalidate CPT authorization, turning prior work into unlawful presence and triggering 3- or 10-year entry bans — a risk that survives even if H-1B is eventually approved
heuristics._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

heuristics.rule: If H-1B lottery fails repeatedly and OPT is expiring, pursue cap-exempt employer sponsorship through a university or nonprofit research institution as a concurrent employment arrangement
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 holder has exhausted OPT/STEM OPT and their primary employer cannot or will not sponsor H-1B further
heuristics.action: Find a cap-exempt employer (university, nonprofit research org, national lab) willing to sponsor H-1B; work part-time (10-15 hrs/week) there while continuing full-time at original employer under concurrent employment
heuristics.because: Cap-exempt employers (universities, nonprofit research institutions, DOE national labs) can file H-1B petitions outside the annual lottery cap at any time of year, providing an alternative path when cap-subject sponsorship fails
heuristics._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

heuristics.rule: Secure the offer letter and confirm start date before resigning
heuristics.condition: When changing jobs during OPT and trying to avoid unemployment day accumulation
heuristics.action: Get a signed offer letter with a confirmed start date first, then submit resignation, then notify DSO the same day
heuristics.because: The poster's second job change succeeded using this sequence; the first failed because they resigned first and accumulated 95 days of unemployment, exceeding the 90-day cumulative limit
heuristics._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

heuristics.rule: Pursue French bilingual points before retaking English tests past CLB 10
heuristics.condition: When your English IELTS is already at CLB 10 and you are below the current ITA cutoff
heuristics.action: Study for TEF Canada to achieve B1 level instead of grinding for CLB 11 on English
heuristics.because: French bilingual advantage yields ~50 CRS points; improving English from CLB 10 to CLB 11 yields only ~6 marginal points, confirmed by both OP and top comment (445 upvotes)

heuristics.rule: If you are an Indian national in CS, calculate the actual EB-2 priority date wait before assuming the US is the better option
heuristics.condition: When an Indian national with a CS/tech background is deciding between the US and Canada immigration paths
heuristics.action: Look up the current EB-2 India priority date and extrapolate realistically — if it is 40+ years, treat the US green card as functionally unavailable and plan accordingly
heuristics.because: The post author and top comment (478 upvotes) both independently emphasize that the EB-2 India backlog is multigenerational and widely misunderstood — people compare salary without factoring in the immigration timeline reality

heuristics.rule: Set a fixed schedule for checking IRCC portal status rather than checking daily or multiple times per day
heuristics.condition: When you are in an active immigration wait period (PGWP, PR application, Express Entry pool) and find yourself compulsively checking for updates
heuristics.action: Limit portal checks to once per week on a set day
heuristics.because: Multiple commenters (upvoted 234) confirmed that constant checking amplifies anxiety without adding information — the system does not update frequently enough to justify daily checks

heuristics.rule: Apply for PGWP within 180 days of receiving final marks/transcript — do not wait for the physical degree parchment
heuristics.condition: When you have received your official transcript or final marks confirming program completion...
heuristics.action: Submit PGWP application immediately using the registrar's official completion/convocation letter, not a professor email or informal confirmation
heuristics.because: IRCC has denied PGWP applications from applicants who waited beyond the 180-day window, and the physical degree parchment is not required — the registrar's official document suffices (corroborated by OP and comment rank=9, upvotes=198)

heuristics.rule: Submit the I-765 as early as possible within the 90-day window
heuristics.condition: When applying for OPT EAD and your program end date is approaching...
heuristics.action: File up to 90 days before your program end date to get earlier placement in the processing queue
heuristics.because: Earlier submission means earlier queue position; processing is batch-based and not strictly first-in-first-out, but submitting late compresses your buffer against delays

heuristics.rule: Before switching programs, calculate the PGWP length impact first
heuristics.condition: When a student is considering switching to a shorter program mid-degree for academic or personal reasons
heuristics.action: Consult your international student advisor to quantify the PGWP length difference before making the switch
heuristics.because: PGWP length is directly tied to program length; switching from a 2-year Master's to a 1-year Graduate Certificate costs 2 years of work permit and potentially 2 years of Canadian work experience toward PR

heuristics.rule: Explicitly state PGWP open work permit status in bold at the top of resume and in interview intro
heuristics.condition: When job searching in Canada on a PGWP
heuristics.action: Write 'Open Work Permit — no sponsorship needed' prominently on resume and repeat in first contact with recruiters
heuristics.because: Canadian employers frequently confuse 'work permit' with 'requires sponsorship,' causing silent rejection; proactive clarification eliminates this filter before it operates

heuristics.rule: Don't disclose visa status in phone screens unless directly asked
heuristics.condition: When you are an international student on OPT going through early-stage interview rounds
heuristics.action: Stay silent on visa status during phone screens; raise it before accepting an offer or in final rounds
heuristics.because: Early disclosure adds friction before the company has invested in you; later disclosure comes after they've decided they want you, shifting negotiating leverage

heuristics.rule: Get H-1B sponsorship commitment in writing before accepting an offer
heuristics.condition: When accepting a job offer as an F-1/OPT holder who will need H-1B sponsorship...
heuristics.action: Request explicit written language in the offer letter — e.g., 'Company will file H-1B petition on employee's behalf for the next available cap season' — and do not accept based on verbal assurances alone
heuristics.because: Verbal promises are not legally binding for H-1B commitments; companies can change policy at any time and the employee has no enforceable recourse without written documentation (corroborated by top comment at 287 upvotes and OP's own lesson)

heuristics.rule: The 60-day grace period does not authorize work — it is a departure/transition window only
heuristics.condition: When your F-1 program end date has passed and you are in the 60-day grace period
heuristics.action: Do not work; use the period only to find new status, transfer schools, or prepare to depart
heuristics.because: 8 CFR 214.2(f)(5)(iv) explicitly limits the grace period to transitioning out of status, not continuing or initiating employment

heuristics.rule: Track your own CPT hours — do not rely on your DSO or co-op office to flag the 12-month limit
heuristics.condition: When you are an F-1 student doing full-time CPT (20+ hours/week) across multiple semesters or co-ops
heuristics.action: Maintain a personal log of all full-time CPT start and end dates, sum total days yourself, and alert your DSO proactively before any new authorization
heuristics.because: Multiple commenters report that DSOs approved CPT without ever mentioning the OPT-elimination rule; one commenter lost OPT eligibility by 3 weeks because the internship ran long and no one flagged it

heuristics.rule: If your goal is long-term US immigration, avoid Day 1 CPT schools regardless of short-term work authorization needs
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student is considering a Day 1 CPT school primarily to gain immediate work authorization
heuristics.action: Weigh the permanent immigration bar risk against the temporary work benefit — in most cases, decline
heuristics.because: USCIS can retroactively determine CPT was improper, triggering unlawful presence findings that result in 3-year or 10-year bars; this risk persists years later into H-1B petitions

heuristics.rule: Register for the master's cap if you have a US master's degree — it is a separate pool with better odds
heuristics.condition: When you hold a US master's degree and are registering for the H-1B lottery
heuristics.action: Register specifically for the master's cap exemption (20k pool), not only the general 65k cap
heuristics.because: Multiple commenters confirmed this is a separate, smaller pool that provides an additional selection chance at no extra cost — yet many eligible candidates miss it

heuristics.rule: After two consecutive H-1B lottery losses with STEM OPT running out, explore cap-exempt employer arrangements as a legitimate alternative
heuristics.condition: When an F-1 student has lost the H-1B lottery twice and STEM OPT expiration is approaching...
heuristics.action: Consult an immigration attorney about cap-exempt employer strategies before OPT runs out — do not wait for a third lottery attempt
heuristics.because: Cap-exempt petitions bypass the lottery entirely and can provide a legal path to H-1B status without competing in the annual cap; post author and at least one corroborating commenter (upvotes: 134) used this path successfully

heuristics.rule: Build a Plan B you genuinely want, not just a fallback you'd tolerate
heuristics.condition: When facing H-1B lottery uncertainty and needing a contingency plan...
heuristics.action: Actively research, visit, and talk to people in the backup option (e.g., Canada) until it feels like a real choice, not a consolation prize
heuristics.because: Multiple commenters report that the desperation and anxiety of H-1B waiting diminished significantly once their Plan B felt genuinely acceptable — the catastrophizing comes from having only one viable path
