heuristics.rule: Match your country of birth to your immigration pathway — India/China nationals should weight Canada more heavily
heuristics.condition: When an international student from India or China is choosing between US and Canada as a long-term destination...
heuristics.action: Seriously consider Canada's Express Entry pathway rather than defaulting to the US H-1B/GC route
heuristics.because: India EB-2 GC applicants from 2012 are still waiting as of 2026; Canada Express Entry can deliver PR in 6-12 months regardless of nationality

heuristics.rule: Apply for STEM OPT extension 90 days before OPT expiry
heuristics.condition: When your OPT is approaching expiration and you intend to use the STEM extension
heuristics.action: Submit the I-765 application at least 90 days before your OPT EAD expires to create buffer for RFEs and processing delays
heuristics.because: The post author explicitly identified the 90-day window as 'correct' and noted that the grace period after OPT expiry is thin — the buffer absorbs RFE response time without causing a gap in work authorization

heuristics.rule: PGWP eligibility is determined by enrollment date, not program-change date
heuristics.condition: When a student changed programs after their initial enrollment but before a new PGWP policy took effect
heuristics.action: Check the original enrollment date against the grandfathering cutoff date (2024-11-01), not the date of the program change
heuristics.because: IRCC's 2024 November policy explicitly uses 'enrollment date' as the key eligibility trigger; the policy text states students enrolled before November 1, 2024 are exempt from new program requirements

heuristics.rule: Apply for OPT 60-90 days before graduation; USCIS processing takes 3-5 months
heuristics.condition: When you are an F-1 student approaching graduation and planning to use OPT work authorization
heuristics.action: Submit the OPT application to your DSO and then USCIS 60-90 days before your program end date to ensure EAD arrives before you need to start working
heuristics.because: USCIS processing time for EAD is 3-5 months; late applications risk a gap between graduation and work authorization

heuristics.rule: Start planning Plan B after the first H-1B miss, not after the third
heuristics.condition: When you are on STEM OPT and have not yet won the H-1B lottery
heuristics.action: Begin researching and preparing alternative visa/immigration paths (Canada PR, O-1, NIW, cap-exempt employers) in parallel with the next lottery attempt

heuristics.rule: Learn French (TEF Canada to CLB 7) as the highest ROI score boost if your CRS is borderline

heuristics.action: Treat CRS score optimization as a controllable variable unlike H-1B lottery; build a plan around hitting a target CRS score

heuristics.condition: When you have failed H1B lottery multiple times and are open to a temporary salary reduction...

heuristics.condition: When building evidence for Dhanasar prong 2 (national in scope)...

heuristics.rule: Have an explicit conversation with your employer about contingency plans before the third lottery cycle
heuristics.condition: When you have already missed the H-1B lottery twice and are approaching a third attempt...

heuristics.rule: Always consult a licensed immigration attorney before enrolling in any program offering Day 1 CPT — do not rely on school representatives or peer referrals
heuristics.condition: When evaluating any school that advertises immediate CPT eligibility
heuristics.action: Get an independent attorney opinion before enrolling

heuristics.rule: The research or work at the cap-exempt employer must be genuine, not nominal
heuristics.condition: When pursuing cap-exempt H-1B through a university or research institution
heuristics.action: Ensure the research role is a real, active collaboration with deliverables — not a name-only arrangement

heuristics.rule: Notify DSO within 10 days of starting a new job to update SEVIS
heuristics.condition: When any job change occurs during OPT (standard or STEM)
heuristics.action: Contact DSO on or before day 10 after the employment change to ensure SEVIS record is updated

heuristics.rule: Optimize spouse language score as a secondary lever before other tactics
heuristics.condition: When you have a spouse or common-law partner and they have not taken a language test
heuristics.action: Have spouse take IELTS and aim for at least CLB 7
heuristics.because: CLB 7 from a spouse added ~20 points in OP's case; corroborated by comment [3] (267 upvotes) calling it a 'sleeper' that commenters had not accounted for

heuristics.rule: Use PNPs as a fallback if your federal CRS score is not clearing Express Entry draws
heuristics.condition: When a candidate's CRS score is not high enough to receive an ITA through federal Express Entry draws
heuristics.action: Actively research and pursue Provincial Nominee Programs — particularly Alberta and Saskatchewan, which have been described as aggressive in nominations
heuristics.because: The original poster identifies PNPs as a 'genuine backdoor' for candidates whose federal score isn't clearing, corroborated by the upvoted comment asking about Saskatchewan and Alberta PNP requirements

heuristics.rule: Research and concretize your Plan B rather than leaving it as a vague worst-case scenario
heuristics.condition: When the possibility of returning home is functioning as a catastrophic narrative driving desperation in your immigration decisions
heuristics.action: Actively research what returning home would realistically look like — job market, salary, quality of life — and write it down concretely
heuristics.because: OP and a top commenter (upvotes: 176) both reported that converting a vague catastrophe into a concrete realistic alternative significantly reduced desperation and improved decision-making clarity

heuristics.rule: Use the official registrar convocation or completion letter — not a program completion email from a professor
heuristics.condition: When gathering documents to prove program completion for PGWP...
heuristics.action: Request the formal letter from the registrar's office before applying
heuristics.because: IRCC explicitly requires the official registrar document; OP almost made this mistake themselves and flagged it as a near-failure point (rank=6)

heuristics.rule: If EAD hasn't arrived 30+ days before your needed start date, escalate immediately
heuristics.condition: When your EAD is pending and you are within 30 days of needing to begin work...
heuristics.action: Contact the USCIS contact center AND have your DSO submit a formal inquiry simultaneously
heuristics.because: Multiple commenters confirm that USCIS contact center inquiries alone yield boilerplate responses; dual escalation through DSO channels is more effective

heuristics.rule: Explore within-program changes before switching programs
heuristics.condition: When the academic issue (e.g., research focus, supervisor) can potentially be resolved without a formal program change
heuristics.action: Work with graduate advisors to change focus, supervisor, or track within the existing program rather than officially switching programs
heuristics.because: The OP achieved the same academic outcome (changed research focus) without losing PGWP eligibility by staying in the same program

heuristics.rule: Shift from cold applications to active networking after 4 weeks of poor response
heuristics.condition: When cold application response rate is below 5% after 4+ weeks
heuristics.action: Pivot to local meetups, tech events, and direct relationship-building to generate referrals
heuristics.because: Referral response rate (~30%) is roughly 15x cold application rate (~2%), as corroborated by multiple commenters; Canadian hiring networks are locally weighted

heuristics.rule: Clarify what a company means by 'no sponsorship' before accepting a rejection
heuristics.condition: When a recruiter says 'we don't sponsor visas' and you are currently on OPT or STEM OPT
heuristics.action: Ask whether they mean no H-1B specifically, or whether they also cannot support OPT or STEM OPT extension forms
heuristics.because: Multiple corroborated comments show recruiters conflate all visa-related processes; some companies that say 'no sponsorship' will still complete STEM OPT I-983 forms since it is a lighter lift than H-1B

heuristics.rule: Be upfront about visa status and sponsorship timeline during job interviews
heuristics.condition: When job searching on STEM OPT with a hard deadline for H-1B filing...
heuristics.action: Disclose OPT status and H-1B sponsorship need early in the interview process, including the specific cap-season deadline
heuristics.because: Honest screening saves time for both parties; a commenter with 176 upvotes confirmed finding a new sponsor within 8 months using this approach

heuristics.rule: Track aggregate unemployment days on post-completion OPT — the limit is 90 days total, not per job gap
heuristics.condition: When you are on post-completion OPT and between jobs
heuristics.action: Count all cumulative unemployment days across the entire OPT period; do not assume each gap resets the counter
heuristics.because: A top commenter (234 upvotes) reported their OPT became technically invalid after 95 days between jobs, believing they had buffer — corroborated by the original post's rule summary

heuristics.rule: Verify E-Verify enrollment before accepting an offer if you are relying on STEM OPT extension
heuristics.condition: When you are a STEM-degree holder approaching the end of your 12-month OPT and planning to apply for the 24-month STEM OPT extension
heuristics.action: Confirm employer E-Verify enrollment status before signing the offer letter — treat it as a non-negotiable due-diligence step
heuristics.because: A commenter lost 24 months of authorized work because their startup would not enroll in E-Verify; STEM OPT extension is legally unavailable from non-E-Verify employers regardless of employer intent

heuristics.rule: Treat ICE SEVP school shutdowns as a separate and immediate risk from USCIS adjudication risk
heuristics.condition: When evaluating a Day 1 CPT school
heuristics.action: Check whether the school has received SEVP audits or warnings, not just whether USCIS has taken action
heuristics.because: SEVP has terminated Day 1 CPT school programs mid-semester, stranding students without status — a harm independent of the longer-term immigration bar risk

heuristics.rule: Use a specific, accurate SOC code that matches the actual job duties rather than a generic catch-all code
heuristics.condition: When filing an H-1B petition for a role that could be classified multiple ways
heuristics.action: Work with an immigration-specialized attorney to identify the most precise SOC code with documented specialty occupation evidence

heuristics.rule: Cap-exempt dual-employment arrangements must be substantively real — not nominal — to withstand USCIS scrutiny
heuristics.condition: When structuring a cap-exempt H-1B through a university or nonprofit research partnership...
heuristics.action: Ensure the employee genuinely performs work for the cap-exempt employer (e.g., actual research contribution), not just a paper arrangement
heuristics.because: Post explicitly warns that USCIS audits for genuine employment, and fraudulent arrangements carry serious legal consequences; immigration attorneys are required to structure this correctly

heuristics.rule: Have contingency plans finalized before the March–May lottery window opens
heuristics.condition: When preparing for H-1B lottery registration season...
heuristics.action: Finalize and emotionally accept backup plans before the registration period, not after results come out
heuristics.because: The waiting period between registration and results is identified by multiple commenters as the peak anxiety window — having plans ready before it starts reduces the psychological toll

heuristics.rule: Choose Canada program length strategically to maximize PGWP duration
heuristics.condition: When selecting a Canadian graduate program as an international student...
heuristics.action: Choose a program of at least 2 years to qualify for a 3-year PGWP (the maximum), rather than a 1-year diploma that yields only a 1-year PGWP
heuristics.because: PGWP length equals program length, capped at 3 years; a 1-year program gives only 1 year of open work authorization, severely compressing the timeline to PR

heuristics.rule: Involve your DSO early, not after an RFE arrives
heuristics.condition: When preparing STEM OPT paperwork, especially if the employer is filling out I-983 for the first time
heuristics.action: Loop in the school DSO before submission to review the I-983 and STEM connection narrative

heuristics.rule: Notify DSO and update SEVIS within 10 days of changing employers on OPT

heuristics.rule: Pursue cap-exempt employer structures if you cannot win the H-1B lottery
heuristics.condition: When a candidate has failed H-1B lottery multiple times but wants to remain in the US
heuristics.action: Seek employment at universities, nonprofit research institutions, or government research agencies, which can sponsor H-1B at any time without lottery
heuristics.because: These employer categories are explicitly exempt from the H-1B annual cap, removing the lottery dependency entirely
heuristics._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

heuristics.rule: If your CRS is below 450 federally, pivot strategy to Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) rather than waiting for a federal draw
heuristics.condition: When a candidate's CRS score is below 450 and short-term score improvement is limited
heuristics.action: Prioritize learning French for +50 points, research target-province PNP streams, consult a provincial immigration advisor, and continue accumulating Canadian work experience
heuristics.because: Top-ranked answer (1654 upvotes) explicitly states that getting an ITA below 450 is very difficult unless a federal category-based draw occurs, and recommends this three-step pivot
heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

heuristics.rule: 法语CLB 7是CRS加分ROI最高的单一动作（约+50分）
heuristics.condition: 当你的CRS分数不足以进入联邦综合类抽签，且英语已达标时
heuristics.action: 备考TEF Canada至CLB 7（约B1水平），以获得法语加分
heuristics.because: 多位高赞评论指出TEF Canada CLB 7可加50分，且难度相对可控，被描述为'ROI非常高'
heuristics._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

heuristics.rule: Use Canada's Super Visa as a near-term parental reunion bridge while pursuing long-term PR sponsorship
heuristics.condition: When you are in Canada and want parents present within 1–2 years rather than waiting for PR lottery...
heuristics.action: Apply for Super Visa (processing ~3–8 months), allowing parents up to 2 years per entry, renewable
heuristics.because: A commenter with 456 upvotes confirms parents have been living in Canada nearly 3 years under Super Visa through repeated entries, calling it more flexible than most countries' policies
heuristics._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

heuristics.rule: PGWP holders must proactively explain their open work permit status to Canadian employers
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder is job-searching in Canada and an employer's HR sees 'work permit' on paperwork...
heuristics.action: Explicitly tell HR: 'I can work for you without any sponsorship or application on your part' — do not assume they understand PGWP is employer-unrestricted
heuristics.because: HR commenter (634 upvotes) states many Canadian employers conflate 'work permit' with employer-specific permits requiring sponsorship, and will self-eliminate as potential employers based on that misunderstanding
heuristics._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

heuristics.rule: For India-born applicants, EB-2 India wait time now exceeds most reasonable career planning horizons — treat Canada PR as a parallel or alternative path, not a fallback
heuristics.condition: When an India-born professional is early in their US career and evaluating green card strategy...
heuristics.action: Model the Canada Express Entry path concurrently, not as a plan B after EB-2 fails
heuristics.because: Multiple high-upvote comments confirm EB-2 India current priority date (~2012 as of 2024) implies 50+ year queue for new entrants; one commenter corroborates Canada PR via Express Entry as a realized alternative (14 months to PR)
heuristics._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

heuristics.rule: Monitor category-based draws (tech, healthcare, etc.) separately from all-program draws if you are from a high-competition background
heuristics.condition: When your CRS is below the all-programs cutoff (often 530+ for Indian/Chinese applicants) but above category draw cutoffs
heuristics.action: Track IRCC's category-specific invitation rounds rather than waiting for an all-program draw
heuristics.because: The poster explicitly noted that category draws (e.g., tech worker) have significantly lower CRS cutoffs than all-program draws, and this is what ultimately got them an ITA at ~490
heuristics._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

heuristics.rule: If you have previously submitted biometrics, expect a faster PGWP processing timeline due to biometric exemption
heuristics.condition: When you have already provided biometrics during a prior Canadian visa/permit process...
heuristics.action: Note your biometric exemption status in your application; do not plan extra time for biometric appointment delays
heuristics.because: The poster's 5-week timeline was partly enabled by biometric exemption at day 15 rather than requiring a fresh appointment
heuristics._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

heuristics.rule: Explore intra-program flexibility before switching programs
heuristics.condition: When a student wants to change research direction or specialization but is enrolled in a multi-year program
heuristics.action: Negotiate with supervisor and departmental advisor to change direction within the existing program rather than formally switching to a new program

heuristics._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

heuristics.rule: PGWP open work permit status removes job-change anxiety that H-1B status creates
heuristics.condition: When a worker values career mobility and the ability to switch employers without immigration risk
heuristics.action: Factor open work permit freedom into the Canada vs. US comparison, not just salary
heuristics.because: Poster described H-1B transfer 60-day window as high psychological pressure; PGWP's open nature was cited as a key structural advantage appearing across multiple comments

heuristics.rule: Pursue co-op or Canadian internship experience during school if available
heuristics.condition: When enrolled in a Canadian program that offers co-op opportunities
heuristics.action: Prioritize completing at least one co-op placement before graduating
heuristics.because: Original poster and multiple commenters noted that Canadian employers place significant weight on 'Canadian experience', making co-op a material differentiator in the post-graduation job search

heuristics.rule: For EB1A self-petition, assess your credentials against all 10 USCIS criteria — meeting 3 is sufficient
heuristics.condition: When you are a non-Indian national with publications, open-source contributions, or documented professional influence...
heuristics.action: Evaluate EB1A self-petition seriously before defaulting to PERM-based EB2, since EB1A skips PERM (saving 2-4 years) and has minimal wait for non-Indian nationals
heuristics.because: Community commenter clarified EB1A criteria are broader than commonly assumed, and the PERM bypass is a substantial time advantage

heuristics.rule: Treat the Personal Statement as a narrative, not a list
heuristics.condition: When drafting the NIW Personal Statement...
heuristics.action: Write a story that explains why your work matters to the U.S., rather than enumerating credentials
heuristics.because: The petitioner explicitly identified this as the most important document in the package and distinguished story-telling from list-making as the differentiating approach
heuristics._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

heuristics.rule: For STEM OPT job changes, ensure E-Verify registration and I-983 are completed before your first day
heuristics.condition: When switching employers on STEM OPT extension
heuristics.action: Confirm new employer is E-Verify registered and submit I-983 Training Plan prior to start date, not after
heuristics.because: The original poster explicitly flags this as a common error; STEM OPT authorization depends on employer compliance being in place before work begins
heuristics._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

heuristics.rule: Target a qualifying job offer as a major CRS lever if below cutoff
heuristics.condition: When stuck below 470 and no current job offer exists
heuristics.action: Actively seek a job offer from an employer willing to submit LMIA or qualifying offer, particularly in TEER 0/1 NOC categories
heuristics.because: A qualifying job offer added 50 points in OP's case (TEER 0/1 federal draw level); OP moved from 460 to 491 using this in combination with French

heuristics.rule: Treat Canadian PR as a timing-sensitive milestone, not just a score milestone
heuristics.condition: When planning your Express Entry application timeline
heuristics.action: Monitor draw cutoffs over time rather than assuming a static CRS score is sufficient — a 480 score may clear one draw and not another
heuristics.because: The original poster explicitly notes that draw cutoffs fluctuate, meaning score alone does not guarantee invitation — timing of draws matters independently

heuristics.rule: Learn the immigration system mechanics deeply enough to identify which factors you can actually control
heuristics.condition: When CRS score fluctuations and draw uncertainty are causing generalized anxiety
heuristics.action: Study National Draw Profiles and identify the specific CRS components within your control (language scores, additional education, etc.) and act on those
heuristics.because: OP reported that understanding controllable factors restored a sense of agency, directly reducing anxiety

heuristics.rule: Check biometrics validity before applying — valid biometrics from a study permit do not need to be redone
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP applicant already has unexpired biometrics on file from a prior study permit application...
heuristics.action: Confirm biometrics are still valid and note this in application; do not assume a new biometrics appointment is needed
heuristics.because: Corroborated by OP's timeline (no new biometrics needed) and comment rank=11 (upvotes=134) confirming the same experience

heuristics.rule: For severely delayed EAD cases, have your DSO submit a congressional inquiry
heuristics.condition: When your I-765 has been pending far beyond published processing times with no movement...
heuristics.action: Ask your DSO to submit an inquiry to your congressional representative's or senator's office on your behalf
heuristics.because: Multiple corroborated accounts confirm congressional inquiries often produce movement within weeks when standard USCIS contact center inquiries fail

heuristics.rule: Consecutive programs at DLIs can be combined for PGWP length, but only with no meaningful gap
heuristics.condition: When a student has completed or will complete multiple programs sequentially
heuristics.action: Ensure programs are consecutive (typical academic gaps like April graduation to September start may be acceptable, but verify with IRCC guidance), both at DLIs, and combined length is accurate
heuristics.because: IRCC allows combining consecutive program lengths for PGWP calculation, but a gap between programs risks disqualifying the combination

heuristics.rule: Target mid-size companies (50–200 employees) in Canadian tech corridors over large brand-name employers
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder is searching for first Canadian tech job
heuristics.action: Focus applications on mid-size companies in Toronto/Waterloo rather than Amazon, Google, or Shopify
heuristics.because: Large Canadian tech companies (Shopify, etc.) are flooded with applications; mid-size companies have lower applicant volumes and are more likely to engage — confirmed by multiple commenters

heuristics.rule: When asked 'are you authorized to work in the US?', answer yes confidently if on F-1 OPT
heuristics.condition: When you hold valid F-1 OPT status and a recruiter asks about work authorization
heuristics.action: Answer yes without hedging; do not volunteer H-1B dependency unprompted at this stage
heuristics.because: OPT is the student's own work authorization — employer is not sponsoring anything at that stage; conflating OPT with sponsorship confuses recruiters and weakens your candidacy unnecessarily

heuristics.rule: Identify your H-1B filing deadline and reverse-engineer your job search timeline
heuristics.condition: When on STEM OPT and needing to secure H-1B sponsorship...
heuristics.action: Calculate backward from the April cap registration window (early March deadline) to determine the latest acceptable start date for a new job search, and treat that date as a hard constraint
heuristics.because: H-1B cap season has fixed annual windows; missing one means waiting another full year, which may exceed remaining OPT authorization (OP's own timeline analysis, no contradicting comments)

heuristics.rule: On STEM OPT, the unemployment limit increases to 150 days aggregate, not 90
heuristics.condition: When you are on STEM OPT extension and lose your job
heuristics.action: Apply the 150-day aggregate unemployment limit, not the standard 90-day rule
heuristics.because: Explicitly stated in the correction post's OPT authorization section, distinguishing standard post-completion OPT from STEM OPT

heuristics.rule: File your H-1B petition before your OPT EAD expiration date to preserve cap-gap protection
heuristics.condition: When you are on OPT and applying for H-1B in the April lottery, and your OPT EAD expires before or near September 30
heuristics.action: Monitor your EAD expiration date and confirm the H-1B petition is filed before that date — cap-gap only applies if the petition was filed while OPT was still valid
heuristics.because: Commenters flag that students assume automatic cap-gap coverage but miss that the protection requires a timely-filed petition before OPT expiry; those whose OPT expires before April 1 have no protection at all

heuristics.rule: Use STEM OPT extension as a multi-year runway to survive multiple H-1B lottery cycles
heuristics.condition: When you are on F-1 OPT and have a STEM-eligible degree
heuristics.action: Apply for STEM OPT extension immediately to secure up to 3 years of total work authorization, covering up to 2 additional lottery rounds
heuristics.because: The original poster explicitly stated STEM OPT bought the extra 2 years needed to reach their third and successful lottery registration

heuristics.rule: If you've lost the H-1B lottery 2+ times, model a Canada pivot seriously
heuristics.condition: When an F-1/STEM OPT holder has lost H-1B multiple consecutive times...
heuristics.action: Evaluate a move to Canada via PGWP + Express Entry rather than continuing to lottery-loop
heuristics.because: Multiple community members corroborate switching after repeated H-1B losses and obtaining Canadian PR in under a year; the compounding probability of failure grows with each additional lottery cycle

heuristics.rule: Know your timely-filed protection before your EAD expires
heuristics.condition: When an I-765 receipt notice is obtained before the current OPT EAD expires
heuristics.action: Continue working legally even if the new EAD card has not physically arrived — do not stop work based on employer panic
heuristics.because: Multiple high-voted comments (223 and 167 upvotes) confirmed that timely-filed notice allows continued employment; employers frequently do not know this and wrongly ask employees to stop

heuristics.rule: Preserve all enrollment and program-change documentation
heuristics.condition: When a student has changed programs during their studies and plans to apply for PGWP
heuristics.action: Retain original enrollment letter (showing initial enrollment date), program-change approval documents, and full-time status records
heuristics.because: Commenters with direct experience advise this as a practical safeguard for demonstrating grandfathering eligibility to IRCC

heuristics.rule: Start H-1B sponsorship conversations with your employer from OPT year one, not the final year of STEM OPT
heuristics.condition: When you are an F-1 student on OPT or STEM OPT who needs H-1B sponsorship
heuristics.action: Raise H-1B sponsorship intentions with your employer during your first OPT year to allow time for internal approval processes
heuristics.because: Most employers require time to navigate internal legal and budget approvals; waiting until the last year of STEM OPT leaves insufficient runway
heuristics._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

heuristics.rule: Use EB-2 NIW as a self-petition path for STEM graduates who cannot get employer sponsorship
heuristics.condition: When a STEM-background applicant lacks a sponsoring employer or has lost H-1B lottery multiple times
heuristics.action: Evaluate eligibility under the Matter of Dhanasar three-prong test and file EB-2 NIW self-petition
heuristics.because: NIW does not require employer sponsorship, and the community notes the threshold is lower than many assume for STEM candidates

heuristics.rule: Have your spouse take a language test (CLB 7) to gain ~20 additional CRS points for the principal applicant
heuristics.condition: When the principal applicant has a spouse and the couple's combined CRS is borderline
heuristics.action: Have the accompanying spouse prepare for and pass a recognized language test to CLB 7

heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

heuristics.rule: CRS分数不足时，优先考虑Alberta或Saskatchewan PNP而非继续等待联邦抽签

heuristics.rule: For Chinese nationals, EB-2 wait time is 5–10 years — plan around it, not past it
heuristics.condition: When a Chinese national is evaluating the US green card path under EB-2...
heuristics.action: Model the 5–10 year wait as a real constraint in career and life planning, and assess whether you can tolerate that uncertainty window before committing to the US path

heuristics._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

heuristics.rule: EB-1A (self-petition, no employer sponsor, no PERM) is a structurally different pathway that should be evaluated independently from EB-1B and EB-2
heuristics.condition: When a China-born or India-born applicant wants to reduce dependency on employer continuity...
heuristics.action: Assess EB-1A eligibility separately — it requires no employer sponsorship and bypasses PERM entirely
heuristics.because: A 678-upvote comment explicitly flags this as widely unknown, noting EB-1A has shorter backlog than EB-2 for China-born applicants and removes employer dependency risk
heuristics._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

heuristics.rule: Accumulating a second year of Canadian work experience adds more CRS points than the first year
heuristics.condition: When you are on PGWP and approaching or completing your first year of Canadian work experience
heuristics.action: Factor time as an asset — continuing to work toward year 2 yields additional CRS points incrementally
heuristics.because: The poster explicitly identified this as a scoring lever ('the second year gives more points than the first'), confirmed by CRS table structure

heuristics.rule: Include at least a majority of independent (non-collaborator) recommenders
heuristics.condition: When assembling recommendation letters for EB-2 NIW...
heuristics.action: Aim for more than half of your recommenders to be independent — people who know your work but have no co-authorship or direct collaboration

heuristics.rule: Always consult your DSO before changing jobs on OPT
heuristics.condition: When considering any job change during OPT period
heuristics.action: Contact DSO proactively before making any employment decisions

heuristics.rule: For applicants from high-cutoff countries (India, China), treat French bilingual and Canadian job offer as the two primary levers
heuristics.condition: When you are from India or China and CRS is stuck in the 460–480 range
heuristics.action: Prioritize French language study and securing a qualifying job offer above all other score optimization tactics
heuristics.because: OP explicitly states these are the two biggest real levers for high-ITA-score country nationals; corroborated by community agreement in top comments

heuristics.rule: Use the precise framing: 'I can work for any Canadian employer without employer-specific authorization. You do not need to sponsor me or file any applications. I am pursuing PR independently.'
heuristics.condition: When communicating work authorization status to Canadian employers unfamiliar with PGWP
heuristics.action: Deploy this specific language in cover letters, recruiter calls, and interview intros
heuristics.because: Generic references to 'work permit' trigger sponsorship assumptions; spelling out exactly what the employer does NOT need to do removes the barrier

heuristics.rule: Use a neutral, factual script when disclosing future H-1B need
heuristics.condition: When you need to proactively disclose that you will require H-1B sponsorship in the future
heuristics.action: Say: 'I'm currently on OPT and authorized to work. I'd need H-1B sponsorship in the future. Is that something [company] does?'
heuristics.because: This framing separates current authorization from future need, signals self-awareness, and converts a potentially awkward topic into a practical logistics question

heuristics.rule: Use premium processing if your OPT is expiring soon and you need a timely decision
heuristics.condition: When H-1B is selected in the lottery and OPT expiration is imminent
heuristics.action: Pay for premium processing (~$2,805) to get a 15 business day decision
heuristics.because: A commenter with 98 upvotes recommended this for status certainty — avoiding a gap between OPT expiration and H-1B approval

heuristics.rule: Confirm employer will pay for premium processing before H-1B is filed
heuristics.condition: When your employer is filing an H-1B petition on your behalf
heuristics.action: Confirm upfront that the employer will cover premium processing ($2,805) to receive a decision within 15 business days
heuristics.because: Without premium processing, the approval timeline creates a status limbo period where you cannot be certain of your authorization continuity
heuristics._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

heuristics.rule: Plan CRS improvement on a multi-year timeline rather than panicking in year one of PGWP
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder's CRS is insufficient in their first year of Canadian work experience
heuristics.action: Map out the timeline: one more year of Canadian work experience plus a French test can close a significant gap; do not make rash decisions under pressure
heuristics.because: Comment with 423 upvotes explicitly describes candidates who were short in year one but sufficient by year two due to +work experience and French score, concluding 'don't panic, plan on the timeline'
heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

heuristics.rule: Consider PNP streams if federal Express Entry score is not clearing
heuristics.condition: When CRS is insufficient for federal all-program draws
heuristics.action: Investigate provincial nominee programs with lower effective CRS requirements, especially those tied to provincial job offers or sector-specific streams
heuristics.because: Comment [7] (145 upvotes) notes PNPs often have lower CRS requirements than federal draws and are worth pursuing if federal score is not competitive

heuristics.rule: Hire an immigration-specialized attorney, not a general employment attorney, for H-1B filings
heuristics.condition: When preparing to file or re-file an H-1B petition
heuristics.action: Switch to a law firm with dedicated immigration specialization
heuristics.because: The original poster identified switching law firms as one of three concrete changes made in year 3 that contributed to approval

heuristics.rule: Write a genuine, role-specific I-983 Training Plan — do not use a generic template
heuristics.condition: When applying for STEM OPT extension and submitting the I-983 Training Plan
heuristics.action: Customize the I-983 to specifically describe how the job tasks connect to your STEM degree field
heuristics.because: Multiple community members confirmed that copy-paste template I-983 forms triggered NOIDs (Notices of Intent to Deny)
heuristics._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

heuristics.rule: Research your NOC code's PNP stream eligibility before you need the nomination, not after
heuristics.condition: When a candidate is building their immigration strategy and has a specific job/NOC code
heuristics.action: Look up which provincial PNP streams (e.g., BC Tech Pilot, Ontario Human Capital Priority) target your specific NOC code early in the process
heuristics.because: Comment with 356 upvotes warns against waiting until nomination stage to check stream requirements, as different provinces have different occupational category requirements
heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: Cumulative full-time CPT usage
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Using 12 or more months of full-time CPT eliminates OPT eligibility entirely, removing the primary post-graduation work authorization pathway for F-1 students.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask DSO for a CPT authorization history. Count all full-time CPT months. If approaching 12 months, switch to part-time CPT or stop CPT entirely to preserve OPT.

decision_factors.factor: Study permit application date relative to November 1, 2024
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Students who applied for their study permit on or after November 1, 2024 face an additional field-of-study eligibility requirement that earlier cohorts do not; this can render a completed degree worthless for PGWP purposes
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the original study permit application submission date (not approval date) against November 1, 2024; then verify the program's CIP code against IRCC's current eligible fields list

decision_factors.factor: TEER classification of your Canadian job
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether your work experience counts toward CEC at all; also sets the language benchmark you must meet
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Search the NOC database at canada.ca using your job title and duties; confirm TEER level matches 0, 1, 2, or 3

decision_factors.factor: CEC eligibility vs. FSWP eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC candidates compete in a smaller, often lower-cut-off pool and earn additional CRS points for Canadian experience; FSWP candidates compete globally without that advantage
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm whether candidate has 1,560 hours of TEER 0–3 skilled work in Canada within the past 36 months, excluding co-op and self-employment

decision_factors.factor: Degree level (university degree vs. non-degree college/diploma)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: University degree holders are exempt from the field-of-study requirement entirely; college/diploma graduates must meet it — this creates a binary eligibility difference, not a spectrum
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether the credential is a bachelor's, master's, or PhD awarded by a university — graduate certificates and graduate diplomas do NOT qualify as university degrees for PGWP purposes even if offered at a university

decision_factors.factor: Program type: university degree vs. diploma/certificate
decision_factors.why_it_matters: University degree students (bachelor's and above) retain PGWP eligibility regardless of field; diploma/certificate students are subject to the post-June 2025 eligible fields list, making program selection a direct immigration variable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm the credential type with the institution (degree vs. diploma/certificate) and check the current IRCC eligible fields list for diploma programs

decision_factors.factor: Current CRS Score
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines how urgently a PNP nomination is needed and which streams are accessible — lower scores make PNP the primary (not backup) strategy
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate your current CRS score using the IRCC tool; compare against historical ITA cutoffs for CEC and Canadian Experience Class draws; if 50+ points below recent cutoffs, treat PNP as essential

decision_factors.factor: Whether DSO authorization was obtained before or after a course drop
decision_factors.why_it_matters: This single sequencing fact determines whether the student is in status or out of status — the action itself (dropping below full-time) is not the violation; doing it without prior DSO approval is
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether the DSO has entered the RCL authorization in SEVIS and the student has received written confirmation before any course drop is submitted to the registrar

decision_factors.factor: Cumulative full-time CPT months used
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Crossing 12 cumulative months of full-time CPT permanently eliminates OPT eligibility — the single most irreversible decision in F-1 work authorization
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask DSO for total cumulative full-time CPT months recorded on all I-20s; do not rely on employer or personal records

decision_factors.factor: Program completion date vs. graduation ceremony date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 60-day grace period and OPT application window are triggered by academic completion, not the ceremony — conflating them causes students to miscalculate all downstream deadlines
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Request the official program completion date from the registrar; compare it to the graduation ceremony date on the academic calendar

decision_factors.factor: Willfulness of the violation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Willful violations permanently disqualify a student from reinstatement and are treated far more severely in future immigration proceedings than technical or unknowing violations
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Determine whether the student had knowledge of the rule at the time of violation; documented DSO communications, enrollment records, and prior warnings are evidence of knowledge

decision_factors.factor: Career compounding trajectory by market
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The ceiling, meritocracy, and compensation structure differ significantly between home country and destination country for specific roles — choosing the wrong market means slower career velocity for years
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research senior-level roles in your field in both markets: compensation, equity availability, decision-making autonomy, and whether your nationality is an asset or a disadvantage at the top

decision_factors.factor: Country of birth
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Per-country quotas (7% cap) create radically different wait times — a qualified Indian EB-2 applicant may wait 50-100+ years while an equally or less qualified applicant from an undersubscribed country may have no wait at all
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the USCIS Visa Bulletin Final Action Dates table for your country of birth (not citizenship) in the EB-2 row

decision_factors.factor: Offered salary relative to DOL OEWS prevailing wage levels
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Under the wage-based lottery, salary determines the number of entries (1–4), which directly and dramatically changes selection probability
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the DOL OEWS wage levels for your specific occupation and geographic area; determine which level (I–IV) your offered salary meets or exceeds

decision_factors.factor: STEM degree list eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether the student gets 24 additional months of OPT, which means up to 2 additional H-1B lottery attempts — a decisive difference in total runway.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the exact degree program name on the current DHS STEM Designated Degree Program List; do not rely on the department or field name alone.

decision_factors.factor: STEM degree CIP code eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Only degrees whose CIP code appears on the DHS STEM Designated Degree Program list qualify for the 24-month STEM OPT extension; a non-qualifying degree means only 12 months of OPT total
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the DHS STEM Designated Degree Program list against your specific degree's CIP code via your university registrar or DSO before selecting a program or major

decision_factors.factor: Whether reason for reduced course load falls within the four permitted exceptions
decision_factors.why_it_matters: No discretion exists outside the four regulatory exceptions. A reason that seems valid but doesn't qualify results in automatic out-of-status.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify the reason against the four categories: (1) academic difficulty with English/methods/placement, (2) documented medical condition, (3) final semester completion, (4) annual vacation. If none apply, do not drop below full-time.

decision_factors.factor: Study permit validity at time of PGWP application
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A lapsed study permit at the moment of submission is a hard disqualifier — there is no grace period
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare study permit expiry date against your intended application submission date; flag any gap greater than zero days as a critical risk requiring legal advice

decision_factors.factor: Current CRS cutoff scores in Express Entry CEC draws
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether you will receive an ITA passively or need to pursue PNP — the single biggest strategic fork in the road
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor IRCC draw results on the official site; compare your projected CRS score against the last 6–12 CEC-specific draw cutoffs

decision_factors.factor: Current CRS score relative to recent draw cut-offs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A CRS score below recent cut-offs means indefinite waiting unless the candidate improves a scoreable factor or targets category-based draws
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use IRCC's CRS tool; compare current score against the last 6 months of general and category-based draw cut-offs from the IRCC website

decision_factors.factor: Program's CIP code vs. program name
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP eligibility is determined by CIP code, not the descriptive name of a program — two programs with similar names can have different CIP codes and thus different eligibility statuses
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Request the CIP code directly from the institution's registrar and cross-reference with IRCC's published eligible programs list; do not rely on the institution's marketing materials

decision_factors.factor: Study permit application date relative to June 25, 2025
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines which PGWP eligibility rules apply — students who applied before this date are protected under previous rules; those who applied after must meet the new program field requirements
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the date on your study permit application confirmation from IRCC; this is a binary threshold, not a spectrum

decision_factors.factor: Existence and Location of Canadian Job Offer
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Most PNP streams (BC, Alberta, Saskatchewan) require a provincial job offer; Ontario HCP does not — this single factor determines which streams you can access
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm whether your job offer is full-time, indeterminate or 1-year+, and from an employer in a province with an active tech PNP stream; verify the NOC code maps to eligible occupations

decision_factors.factor: Which of the 4 RCL exceptions applies to the student's situation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: DSOs have zero discretion to create exceptions outside the 4 federally defined categories — if the student's situation does not fit one of the 4, RCL authorization is legally unavailable regardless of how sympathetic the circumstances are
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Match the student's situation to: (1) academic difficulty, (2) medical condition with licensed provider documentation, (3) final semester with fewer credits needed, or (4) improper course level placement

decision_factors.factor: Employer E-Verify enrollment status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether a STEM OPT extension is even possible with that employer; an unenrolled employer disqualifies the extension at filing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR directly and verify on the E-Verify employer search tool (everify.uscis.gov) before accepting the offer

decision_factors.factor: STEM degree eligibility for OPT extension
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM-eligible students can extend OPT by 24 months beyond the standard 12, dramatically increasing time to secure H-1B sponsorship or alternative status
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether your CIP code appears on the DHS STEM designated degree program list; confirm employer is enrolled in E-Verify

decision_factors.factor: Duration of unlawful presence accumulated
decision_factors.why_it_matters: At 180 days, a 3-year re-entry bar triggers; at 365 days, a 10-year bar triggers — these bars apply even if the student was unaware of the violation
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate from the date the status violation occurred (not the date of discovery); cross-reference I-94 records, SEVIS history, and enrollment records

decision_factors.factor: Purchasing power vs. absolute salary
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A high nominal salary in a destination country can generate less actual savings and wealth than a lower nominal salary in a lower cost-of-living home market
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate post-tax take-home in each market, subtract realistic cost of living, and compare savings rate and investment opportunity — not gross salary

decision_factors.factor: NIW eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: NIW allows self-petition without an employer sponsor, removing PERM dependency and approximately 1 year of processing time — fundamentally changes the strategic pathway
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess against three Dhanasar prongs: substantial merit and national importance of work, well-positioned to advance it, and US benefit from waiving labor certification — STEM PhDs with citations/publications are the strongest candidates

decision_factors.factor: Whether employer is cap-exempt
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Cap-exempt employers can file H-1B petitions at any time without lottery — this eliminates annual selection risk entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check if the employer is a university, nonprofit research organization affiliated with a university, or government research organization

decision_factors.factor: OEWS wage level of the offered position
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Under the post-2025 wage-weighted H-1B lottery, wage level directly determines selection probability — a Level I or II position has materially lower odds than Level III or IV.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the Department of Labor's Foreign Labor Certification Data Center to look up the prevailing wage for your specific occupation and geographic area, then ask the employer which level they intend to file at.

decision_factors.factor: Employer E-Verify enrollment status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Without E-Verify enrollment, an employer cannot legally hire a STEM OPT student; accepting an offer from a non-enrolled employer forces the student to choose between violating status or losing the job
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR directly before the offer stage; alternatively search the E-Verify employer search tool at uscis.gov

decision_factors.factor: J-1 student's primary funding source
decision_factors.why_it_matters: J-1 students must have government, university, or international organization as primary/major sponsor. Individual or personal funds are treated as secondary. This affects visa issuance and program eligibility.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review the DS-2019 application funding breakdown. If personal funds constitute the majority of support, the application may not qualify for J-1 status.

decision_factors.factor: Full-time enrollment status throughout the program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A single semester (other than the final term) where you were enrolled part-time can disqualify you from PGWP entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review official enrollment history with the registrar for every semester; flag any semester listed as part-time and consult an RCIC before applying

decision_factors.factor: PGWP duration remaining at point of CEC eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: You must maintain valid status throughout the PR process; if PGWP expires before PR is approved, you face a status gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate PGWP expiry date; subtract expected PR processing time (~6 months post-ITA); confirm you have sufficient buffer

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency level
decision_factors.why_it_matters: French skills at CLB 7+ add up to 50 CRS points and access to French-stream draws with consistently lower cut-offs — one of the highest-yield improvable factors
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check current French test scores (TEF Canada / TCF Canada); estimate cost and time investment to reach CLB 7 vs. CRS score gap to recent cut-off

decision_factors.factor: Study permit submission date relative to November 1, 2024 and June 25, 2025 cutoffs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Grandfathering rules mean students who submitted before each cutoff are assessed under the rules in effect at that time — the same program can be PGWP-eligible or ineligible depending solely on when the permit was submitted
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Pull the IRCC application submission confirmation (not approval date, not start date — submission date) and map it against both cutoffs

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency (current and achievable level)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: French at CLB 7+ unlocks both additional CRS points and access to French-language draws with cut-offs 90–150 points lower than general draws — this single factor can be the difference between qualifying and not qualifying
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take a French placement test and estimate realistic time to CLB 7 given current level; compare the CRS gain against other available levers

decision_factors.factor: Province of Employment or Intended Settlement
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Provincial nominations carry an expectation of settlement in that province — applying to a province you have no intention of staying in creates compliance risk and ethical exposure
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess where your employer, social network, and housing situation are anchored; avoid nominating provinces purely for immigration convenience if you plan to immediately relocate

decision_factors.factor: Time elapsed since the student fell out of status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 5-month threshold is a legal dividing line — under 5 months allows standard reinstatement; over 5 months requires proving exceptional circumstances, which USCIS rarely approves
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate the exact date the violation began (date course was dropped without authorization or date enrollment fell below minimum) and count calendar days from that date

decision_factors.factor: OPT start date vs. expected job start date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the OPT start date is set too early relative to when work will actually begin, unemployment days accumulate and the 90-day limit may be exceeded before a job starts
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Coordinate with DSO to set the OPT start date as close as possible to the anticipated job start date without going earlier than needed

decision_factors.factor: Cumulative unemployment days consumed
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Crossing 90 days (standard OPT) or 150 days (STEM extension) triggers automatic termination with no reinstatement option
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Maintain a personal log tracking every day without an active employer on record since OPT start date; count USCIS processing gaps if EAD was delayed

decision_factors.factor: Type of visa status at time of violation (F-1 vs. J-1)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: J-1 violations are categorically more severe: J-1 termination requires immediate departure with no grace period, and certain J-1 violations (health insurance) have no reinstatement path
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify the student's exact visa classification; do not assume F-1 and J-1 rules are interchangeable

decision_factors.factor: Immigration stability tax
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The mental energy, financial cost, and opportunity cost of maintaining and transitioning visa status is a real drag on career performance and life quality that is systematically underweighted in stay-vs-return decisions
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Estimate hours per year spent on immigration administration, attorney fees, and enumerate specific opportunities foregone due to visa restrictions (entrepreneurship, job switching, travel)

decision_factors.factor: Employer willingness to sponsor PERM
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PERM requires employer commitment to a multi-step, ~1-year DOL process including recruitment, prevailing wage filings, and significant legal costs — many small or mid-size employers refuse
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask explicitly during offer negotiation whether the company has sponsored PERM before, how many times, and whether they will commit to sponsorship in writing in your offer letter

decision_factors.factor: Whether applicant holds a US master's degree or higher
decision_factors.why_it_matters: US advanced degree holders qualify for the additional 20,000 master's cap exemption pool, improving statistical odds
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm degree conferral date and institution; degree must be from a US institution to qualify

decision_factors.factor: Employer's cap-exempt status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Cap-exempt employers (universities, university-affiliated nonprofits, government research organizations) can file H-1B year-round without lottery, eliminating the primary risk of the US immigration funnel.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR or immigration counsel directly whether the organization has successfully filed cap-exempt H-1B petitions before; verify it meets the statutory definition, not just a nonprofit label.

decision_factors.factor: OPT application timing relative to graduation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Late applications cause gaps in work authorization that accumulate unemployment days and can jeopardize job offers; early applications maximize the buffer for USCIS processing delays
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count back 90 days from your official program end date and mark that as the earliest filing date; build your graduation and job search timeline around this window

decision_factors.factor: Whether the student has maintained continuous full-time enrollment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Status maintenance is contingent on full-time enrollment each semester. A single unauthorized under-enrollment creates out-of-status, which can block OPT, AT, transfer, and reinstatement options.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review SEVIS record and enrollment history each semester. Any gap or under-enrollment without documented DSO authorization is a red flag.

decision_factors.factor: Language test score and recency
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Minimum CLB 5 is required for college graduates; scores older than 2 years are rejected regardless of score level
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm test score meets CLB 5 equivalency for the specific test taken (CELPIP, IELTS General, or PTE Core) and that the test date is within 2 years of the intended PGWP submission date

decision_factors.factor: Language test scores (CLB level achieved)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Sets your CEC eligibility floor and directly adds CRS points — higher scores above the minimum significantly boost your CRS
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take IELTS General or CELPIP and convert to CLB; determine whether you meet the minimum for your TEER level and whether retaking could raise CRS materially

decision_factors.factor: Age trajectory
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CRS awards maximum age points (110 without spouse) between ages 20–29; points decline after 29 — waiting to improve other factors may cost age points that cannot be recovered
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate current age points and projected age points at the earliest realistic ITA date; if declining, factor the net CRS impact into strategy timing

decision_factors.factor: Program duration relative to the 2-year threshold
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Programs of 2+ years yield the maximum 3-year PGWP; programs under 2 years yield a shorter permit, directly reducing the window for accumulating CEC-eligible work experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate total program length in months; if choosing between programs, assess whether extending by a semester crosses the 2-year threshold

decision_factors.factor: Age at time of Express Entry application
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CRS age points peak at 20–29 and decrease progressively after 30, accelerating after 35 — a 2-year delay in filing can cost 15–30+ CRS points that cannot be recovered
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the CRS calculator to model your score at current age vs. age at graduation vs. 1 year post-graduation; identify whether urgency is warranted

decision_factors.factor: NOC TEER Category of Current Job
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Only TEER 0/1/2/3 experience counts toward CEC eligibility and most PNP tech streams; TEER 4/5 work does not qualify
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up your job title on the IRCC NOC database and verify the TEER level; confirm with your employer's HR that the job duties match the NOC description

decision_factors.factor: History of repeated or willful F-1 violations
decision_factors.why_it_matters: USCIS denies reinstatement if the student has a record of repeated or willful status violations — a single accidental violation is treated very differently from a pattern
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review the student's SEVIS record history with the DSO for any prior violations, previous reinstatement applications, or prior unauthorized enrollment drops

decision_factors.factor: Whether the internship is integral and required by the degree program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CPT eligibility requires the work to be an integral, required part of the curriculum; elective or optional internships may not qualify and could constitute unauthorized employment
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with DSO that a course exists that awards credit for the specific internship and that the program requires or formally integrates the experience

decision_factors.factor: Employer E-Verify enrollment status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT extension is only available when employed by an E-Verify employer; a non-E-Verify employer disqualifies the student from STEM extension regardless of degree
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask the HR or legal team directly and verify on the E-Verify employer search portal before accepting an offer

decision_factors.factor: Whether the violation involved unauthorized employment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Unauthorized employment is the most severe individual violation; it typically ends F-1 status with no reinstatement option and creates a permanent SEVIS record
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review any compensated activity, including consulting, freelance, stipends, or informal arrangements, against the student's authorized work status at the time

decision_factors.factor: Employer sponsorship execution capacity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: An employer's stated willingness to sponsor is not the same as their operational capacity to execute — failure at the execution level has the same legal consequence as no sponsorship at all
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask for historical sponsorship data, the name of their immigration counsel, and their process for tracking employee status deadlines; evaluate the sophistication of their answers

decision_factors.factor: Current EB-2 vs. EB-3 priority date movement for your country
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For India-born applicants, relative movement between EB-2 and EB-3 in the Visa Bulletin can determine whether a downgrade strategy is worth pursuing — this changes every month
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor the monthly Visa Bulletin Final Action Dates and Dates for Filing charts for both EB-2 and EB-3 for your country of birth; track trajectory over 3-6 months

decision_factors.factor: Total number of H-1B registrations projected for the filing year
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If total registrations drop significantly (50%+ as projected under wage-based deterrence), selection rates for higher-wage entries could reach 50–90%
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor USCIS and immigration news in February–March for early registration volume signals and policy updates

decision_factors.factor: Nationality and TN or E-3 eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian and Mexican nationals may use TN; Australian nationals may use E-3 — both pathways have no lottery, no annual cap pressure, and are indefinitely renewable, making H-1B dependency optional.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm citizenship, then check whether the target occupation appears on the TN or E-3 eligible profession lists.

decision_factors.factor: Salary level relative to H-1B wage tiers
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Under the FY2027+ wage-based H-1B selection system, higher-wage registrations have statistically better lottery odds; a lower salary at a prestigious firm may yield worse lottery outcomes than a higher salary at a smaller firm
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research the prevailing wage levels for your occupation and location using the DOL Foreign Labor Certification Data Center; assess where a given offer falls within wage levels I–IV

decision_factors.factor: Degree type and STEM classification
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM-classified degrees unlock an additional 17-month OPT extension (total 29 months), dramatically increasing the window to transition to employer-sponsored status in the US.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether the degree CIP code appears on DHS's STEM designated degree program list. Confirm with DSO before assuming STEM eligibility.

decision_factors.factor: Whether the student worked more than authorized hours during studies
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Exceeding authorized work hours as a student disqualifies you from the 'work while you wait' provision — meaning you cannot legally work during PGWP processing even after submitting
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review pay stubs, T4s, or employer records against the 20-hour weekly limit that applied while school was in session; flag any weeks of overwork

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience accumulated
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CRS points for Canadian experience jump at 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5-year thresholds — timing your application around these milestones changes your competitiveness
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate your current hours worked; project when you'll cross the next experience-year threshold; weigh waiting vs. applying now

decision_factors.factor: Occupation alignment with category-based draw criteria
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Category-based draws for healthcare, STEM, trades, and French speakers have lower cut-offs — candidates in these sectors have a structural advantage unavailable to others
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Cross-reference your NOC code against IRCC's published category-based draw criteria; monitor draw history to see if your sector has received targeted draws

decision_factors.factor: Language test score readiness (CLB 7 for university, CLB 5 for college)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: As of November 2024, language proof is mandatory for PGWP — a student who graduates but cannot meet the benchmark cannot receive PGWP regardless of program or institution eligibility
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take a diagnostic test early in the final year of study; if scores are borderline, schedule the official test with buffer time for a retest before graduation

decision_factors.factor: NOC TEER classification of available employment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC requires 1 year of Canadian work experience in TEER 0–3; time spent in TEER 4–5 roles does not count, and students who discover this late must work additional time in eligible roles before applying
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the NOC code of any job offer using the ESDC Job Bank NOC search tool and confirm its TEER level before accepting

decision_factors.factor: PGWP Remaining Validity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The entire PNP + PR process can take 12–24 months; if your PGWP expires before PR is granted, you need a bridge work permit — planning must account for this timeline gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate expiry date of PGWP; if less than 18 months remain, either accelerate PNP application or plan for a bridging open work permit application once PR is in process

decision_factors.factor: Type of academic program and its specific full-time credit hour threshold
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The full-time minimum varies by program level — an incorrect assumption about the required credit load can lead to unknowing violations
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with DSO: Undergrad = 12+ credits; Master's = 9–12 (school-dependent); Doctoral = 7+ credits; English Language Program = 18+ hours/week

decision_factors.factor: Visa type: F-1 vs. J-1
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Authorization rules differ significantly — J-1 students need written RO authorization for on-campus work while F-1 students do not; conflating the two leads to unauthorized employment
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the student visa stamp and DS-2019 (J-1) or I-20 (F-1) to confirm status type before assuming any work authorization rule applies

decision_factors.factor: Timing of planned international travel relative to I-20 travel signature validity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: An expired or missing travel signature on the I-20 results in CBP re-entry denial even with a valid visa and EAD
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the travel endorsement date on your I-20; it must be within the past 6 months at the time of re-entry

decision_factors.factor: Time elapsed since violation occurred
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 5-month reinstatement window is a hard cutoff; students who discover violations late lose their reinstatement eligibility before they can act
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Pinpoint the exact date the violation began (e.g., first day of reduced course load, date of move without reporting) and calculate how many days have elapsed

decision_factors.factor: Program type and its downstream immigration eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The program you enroll in determines which post-graduation work authorizations and pathways are available — this must be assessed before enrollment, not after
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Before enrolling, confirm whether the program qualifies for STEM OPT extension (US), PGWP eligibility (Canada), and what visa categories it unlocks or forecloses

decision_factors.factor: Evidence strength for NIW or Exceptional Ability category
decision_factors.why_it_matters: NIW approval rate is 54% and declining (down 13 percentage points with increased scrutiny) — weak evidence packages have meaningful denial risk that delays the entire pathway
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Audit against the 6 NIW evidence types: citations/publications, patents, professional association membership, critical role at distinguished organizations, high relative salary, media coverage — a strong case typically has 3+ types with substantial documentation

decision_factors.factor: Availability of alternative visa pathways if lottery fails
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Students with L-1, O-1, TN, E-3, or J-1 eligibility have fallback options that preserve US work authorization without re-entering the lottery
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess nationality (TN for Canada/Mexico, E-3 for Australia), extraordinary ability evidence (O-1), and multinational employer structure (L-1)

decision_factors.factor: Employer's prior H-1B sponsorship history and approval rate
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Employers with no H-1B history may lack institutional knowledge, established immigration counsel relationships, or internal approval processes — significantly increasing risk of procedural failure.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR directly for number of H-1B petitions filed and approved in the last 3 years; USCIS disclosure data is publicly available and can cross-verify claims.

decision_factors.factor: Pre-completion vs. post-completion OPT usage
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Pre-completion OPT hours count against the 12-month total; using pre-completion OPT reduces the post-graduation work buffer and can compress the timeline for finding H-1B-sponsoring employment
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate total pre-completion OPT hours used and subtract from the 12-month total to understand remaining post-completion eligibility

decision_factors.factor: Time elapsed since falling out of status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 5-month threshold determines whether reinstatement is even possible. Beyond 5 months without exceptional circumstances, the student cannot reinstate and must typically depart.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate from the date the status violation began (e.g., date of unauthorized drop or missed registration). If under 5 months, reinstatement is possible; over 5 months requires proving exceptional circumstances.

decision_factors.factor: Spouse's occupational TEER level and PGWP remaining validity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: As of January 21, 2025, spousal open work permits are only available if the PGWP holder works in TEER 0/1 or select TEER 2/3 roles AND has at least 16 months remaining on the permit
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the NOC code of the PGWP holder's job and confirm TEER classification; calculate months remaining on PGWP at the time the spouse would apply

decision_factors.factor: Province of residence and occupation demand by province
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PNP streams vary dramatically by province and occupation; your province determines which PNP pathways are realistically available to you
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review active PNP streams for your province and occupation on official provincial immigration websites; note expression of interest score thresholds

decision_factors.factor: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A provincial nomination is worth 600 CRS points — the single most powerful factor outside of core profile; it can override an otherwise uncompetitive CRS score entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review each province's PNP streams for your occupation, work location, and employer; assess whether a 'job offer required' stream is accessible given current employment

decision_factors.factor: Field of study alignment with IRCC's eligible fields list (post-June 2025 version)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 178 programs were removed in June 2025 — students in removed fields who do not have grandfathering protection have lost PGWP eligibility entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the current IRCC eligible fields list (not blog posts or institution websites) against your program's CIP code; re-verify if you consumed information published before June 25, 2025

decision_factors.factor: Spousal education and language scores
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For married or common-law candidates, a spouse's CLB 5+ language score and education credentials add meaningful CRS points (estimated 10–40 depending on levels) — potentially closing the gap to an ITA
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have your spouse take IELTS/TEF and run the CRS calculator with and without spousal factors to quantify the actual point difference

decision_factors.factor: Language Proficiency Score (CLB Level)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Most PNP streams require CLB 7+; falling below this threshold disqualifies candidates from streams that would otherwise fit them perfectly
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take IELTS or CELPIP and calculate CLB equivalent; if below CLB 7, prioritize retesting before applying to streams where this is a hard cutoff

decision_factors.factor: Whether the student is in their final semester
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Final semester students are the only group that can carry fewer credits as a matter of course completion need — this exception is commonly underused by students who don't realize it applies to them
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with DSO that the reduced credits are literally all that remains to complete degree requirements — not just a preference for lighter load in the last term

decision_factors.factor: Pre-completion OPT usage and remaining post-OPT months
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Pre-OPT reduces the post-completion OPT pool at a 2:1 ratio, potentially leaving insufficient time to bridge to an H-1B cap-gap if the student entered the lottery
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate remaining post-OPT months = 12 minus (months of pre-OPT used / 2); assess whether this window covers the next H-1B lottery cycle

decision_factors.factor: School transfer timing relative to OPT end date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Setting a transfer release date in SEVIS before OPT ends immediately terminates work authorization with no recovery path
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm current OPT EAD expiration date; only initiate transfer release after that date has passed

decision_factors.factor: Institutional SEVIS certification risk
decision_factors.why_it_matters: DHS can revoke a university's F-1 certification and State Department can revoke J-1 program designation, affecting all international students at that institution — not just the individual violator
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Consider whether the student's violation involves systemic DSO failures (e.g., improper I-20 issuance, inadequate financial verification) that expose the institution

decision_factors.factor: Family and relational factors
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Spouse immigration status, aging parents, and local family assets change the true cost-benefit of staying abroad in ways that are not visible in career-only analysis
