decision_factors.how_to_assess: Explicitly map out each family member's situation: can a spouse work legally, what are the implications for aging parent care, does the family hold illiquid assets (property) in one country

decision_factors.factor: Time horizon commitment to US residence
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For Indian and Chinese nationals, pursuing a green card is a decade-scale commitment under current rules — this changes whether it makes sense to start the process at all vs. pursuing alternative countries
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Honestly assess whether you are willing and able to remain in the US on H-1B or other non-immigrant status for 10+ years with no guarantee of permanent residence under current policy

decision_factors.factor: Employer's awareness of the new wage-based registration rules
decision_factors.why_it_matters: An employer who registers at the wrong OEWS level or misses the window due to process unfamiliarity can cost the employee their entire year's lottery chance
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask directly whether their immigration counsel has briefed them on the FY2027 wage-level entry system and OEWS vs. LCA distinction

decision_factors.factor: Access to multinational employer with foreign subsidiary
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Working abroad for 1 year at a qualifying multinational company unlocks the L-1 intracompany transfer pathway, bypassing the H-1B lottery entirely; L-1A also leads to EB-1C green card.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify whether any target employer has offices in a country where the student has work authorization; confirm L-1 qualifying relationship between entities.

decision_factors.factor: Strength and clarity of Form I-983 Training Plan
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Vague or poorly written I-983s attract DHS audit scrutiny; a strong I-983 clearly connects job duties to the STEM degree and reduces audit risk
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review whether each job duty in the I-983 can be explicitly mapped to a specific course, skill, or competency from the STEM degree curriculum

decision_factors.factor: Whether employer is enrolled in E-Verify
decision_factors.why_it_matters: F-1 STEM OPT extension requires the employer to be enrolled in E-Verify. Without this, STEM OPT cannot be approved regardless of the student's degree or qualifications.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask the hiring manager or HR department directly. Check the E-Verify employer search tool before accepting an offer if STEM OPT is the intended pathway.

decision_factors.factor: Medical exam history and intended occupation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Certain occupations (healthcare, childcare, agricultural work) and prior residence in designated countries trigger mandatory medical exam requirements that add cost and time to the application
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check IRCC's designated country list and intended job duties against the public-health-contact criteria; budget extra time and $85+ in biometric fees if applicable

decision_factors.factor: Intent to live outside Quebec
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC does not apply to Quebec; students planning to stay in Quebec must use Quebec's separate immigration system (QSWP/PEQ)
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm current and intended province of work and residence before selecting the CEC pathway

decision_factors.factor: Canadian study credential length and level
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian study adds 15–30 bonus CRS points; a 2+ year program adds more than a 1-year certificate — this is a fixed historical factor that is already earned
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm program length on transcript; a degree/diploma of 2+ years earns 30 points, 1-year program earns 15 points

decision_factors.factor: Whether a job offer adds CRS points (post-March 2025 rule)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Job offer no longer adds CRS points as of March 2025 — this changes the strategic weight of securing employer sponsorship during the PGWP period vs. focusing purely on CRS score improvement
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Treat job offer primarily as a source of NOC-eligible work experience for CEC, not as a CRS booster; recalibrate Express Entry strategy accordingly

decision_factors.factor: Active PNP streams for your occupation and province of residence
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points, making PNP availability a faster pathway than federal Express Entry for candidates with sub-520 base scores
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor the immigration portal of provinces where you live or work; check NOC-specific draw histories to assess realistic access

decision_factors.factor: Tech vs. Non-Tech Field
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Tech workers have significantly more dedicated PNP pathways (BC Tech Stream, Alberta Accelerated Tech, Saskatchewan Tech Talent) compared to other fields
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether your NOC code appears on the eligible occupation lists for tech-specific PNP streams; if not, you must rely on general skilled worker streams with less targeted access

decision_factors.factor: Graduate program requiring immediate CPT
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The standard 1 full academic year enrollment requirement before CPT is waived for graduate programs where immediate CPT participation is a required curriculum element
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review the graduate program's official curriculum documentation and confirm with DSO whether an immediate CPT exception applies

decision_factors.factor: Degree level already used for OPT
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Students who have already used OPT at a given degree level cannot reuse it at the same level; a new degree at a higher level unlocks fresh eligibility
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review SEVIS history or DSO records to identify which degree levels have had OPT authorized

decision_factors.factor: Criminal record or disciplinary history
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Criminal conviction is an automatic J-1 termination trigger; any criminal record also blocks F-1 reinstatement eligibility
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review whether any arrests, charges, or institutional disciplinary actions are on record; even pending charges can affect immigration status

decision_factors.factor: Emotional honesty about motivation for staying
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Decisions driven by fear of return being perceived as failure lead to suboptimal long-run choices; identifying the true motivation changes the analysis
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask directly: 'Would I make the same decision if returning home were socially celebrated rather than stigmatized?' If the answer changes, the decision is emotionally driven

decision_factors.factor: Premium processing budget availability
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The difference between 15 business days and 8.1 months for I-140 approval can be career-defining — affects job change flexibility, H-1B extension timing, and overall pathway certainty
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Determine whether employer covers the $2,805 premium processing fee or whether you will pay out of pocket; factor this into negotiation with employer

decision_factors.factor: Extraordinary ability documentation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Sufficient documented achievement (publications, citations, awards, speaking invitations, high relative salary) can support an O-1 petition filed year-round with no cap or lottery.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Conduct a structured inventory of all publications, citations, awards, judging/review roles, media mentions, and salary benchmarks against peers; present to an O-1 specialist for assessment.

decision_factors.factor: Cumulative unemployment days across both OPT and STEM OPT periods
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 150-day limit during STEM OPT is cumulative across the entire extension period, not a fresh counter; students who had job gaps early in STEM OPT may hit the limit faster than they realize
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Maintain a running total from the start of the STEM OPT extension period; do not reset the counter when switching employers

decision_factors.factor: Part-time vs. full-time pre-completion OPT usage
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Part-time pre-completion OPT is deducted from post-completion OPT at a half-time rate, reducing the total available post-graduation work authorization.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate: every 2 months of part-time pre-completion OPT = 1 month deducted from the 12-month post-completion OPT cap. Plan accordingly.

decision_factors.factor: TRV or eTA validity for re-entry after travel
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Traveling while PGWP is pending without a valid TRV or eTA means you cannot legally re-enter Canada until PGWP is approved — potentially months of being stranded abroad
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check passport for current TRV or eTA expiry before booking any international travel post-PGWP submission

decision_factors.factor: Sibling's Canadian citizenship or permanent residence status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Having a sibling who is a Canadian citizen or PR adds 15 CRS points — a small but potentially decisive margin near cut-off thresholds
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm sibling's immigration status; if eligible, include in Express Entry profile

decision_factors.factor: Foreign credential recognition (ECA status)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Foreign education credentials must be assessed by a designated ECA organization to receive CRS education points; unassessed credentials receive no points and cannot be appealed retroactively after filing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Initiate ECA assessment through WES, ICAS, or other IRCC-designated bodies as early as possible — processing takes 3–6 months

decision_factors.factor: Long-term immigration pathway security
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A job with better long-term immigration security (clear path to permanent residence) is often worth meaningful near-term trade-offs in salary or title because visa instability has compounding costs
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map out the full immigration pathway from current status to permanent residence for each option; identify which steps depend on lotteries, employer cooperation, or rule changes

decision_factors.factor: Remaining OPT/STEM OPT time at point of lottery failure
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The number of remaining months determines how many additional lottery attempts are possible and how much time exists to pursue alternatives — running out of OPT with no backup is the critical failure mode.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate exact OPT expiration date and subtract the next H-1B lottery start date (March 1) to determine whether another full attempt is possible.

decision_factors.factor: Whether the student is subject to the J-1 two-year home residency requirement
decision_factors.why_it_matters: J-1 students subject to the 212(e) two-year home residency requirement cannot directly change to most nonimmigrant or immigrant statuses without first fulfilling it or obtaining a waiver.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the DS-2019 and visa stamp for the 212(e) notation. Consult with an immigration attorney if uncertain, as this significantly restricts post-program pathways.

decision_factors.factor: Job offer presence post-March 2025
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A Canadian job offer no longer adds CRS points as of March 25, 2025 — its value is now purely for income stability, language practice, and experience accumulation toward CEC
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Evaluate job offers on career development and CEC hour accumulation merits only; remove CRS point calculations from job offer negotiation strategy

decision_factors.factor: Program duration (relative to 2-year threshold for maximum PGWP)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Programs under 8 months generate no PGWP; programs 8 months to under 2 years generate a PGWP equal to program length; programs 2+ years generate the maximum 3-year PGWP — duration directly determines how much time you have to accumulate CEC-eligible work experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate the expected PGWP duration from your program length and model whether it provides enough time (1 year minimum) to qualify for CEC before expiry

decision_factors.factor: Number of online courses in a semester
decision_factors.why_it_matters: F-1 and J-1 students may only count one 3-credit online course toward their full course of study requirement per semester. Exceeding this limit risks status compliance.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count online courses in the registration each semester. If the student needs more than one online course, verify with DSO whether remaining credits can be fulfilled in-person or at a second institution.

decision_factors.factor: Availability of category-based Express Entry draws for your occupation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Category draws have lower CRS cutoffs than all-program draws, making them the primary pathway for applicants from high-competition backgrounds who cannot reach 530+
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check IRCC's historical draw data for your NOC category (tech, healthcare, etc.) and compare cutoff scores to your current CRS
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Program length
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The length of your Canadian study program directly determines the maximum duration of PGWP you can receive (up to 3 years for programs of 2+ years)
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check your official program duration as listed on your study permit and transcripts; confirm with registrar if your program was non-standard length (e.g., 1.5 years)
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

decision_factors.factor: Program duration at graduation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP duration is directly tied to the length of the program completed — switching from a 2-year to a 1-year program can reduce PGWP by 2 years, which cascades into less Canadian work experience and lower Express Entry CRS scores
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the official program duration on the school's website and IRCC's PGWP eligibility rules; confirm whether the target program is 1-year, 2-year, or longer
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

decision_factors.factor: Country of birth for US green card backlog
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Chinese and Indian nationals face decades-long EB-2/EB-3 waits due to per-country caps; nationals of most other countries face waits of a few years or less — this single variable can make US permanent residency practically unattainable within a career
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up current USCIS Visa Bulletin priority dates for your country of birth and employment-based category; calculate realistic wait time against your age and career horizon
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Company size and HR sophistication regarding immigration status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Large employers (e.g. Shopify, RBC, TD) typically have HR teams familiar with PGWP; smaller companies often do not, creating friction that can kill applications before a hiring decision is made
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research target company size; if mid-size or smaller, plan to include a written work authorization statement on resume and prepare a verbal explanation for screening calls
decision_factors._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

decision_factors.factor: Nationality and EB green card priority date backlog
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Indian nationals face decade-plus waits for EB2/EB3; non-Indian nationals may have near-current priority dates, making EB1A or even EB2 far more viable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the USCIS Visa Bulletin for your country of birth under the relevant EB category

decision_factors.factor: Citation quality and reach vs. raw publication count
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A commenter with only 3 first-author papers but high citations questioned whether count matters — suggesting citation impact may be more determinative than volume for NIW
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether citations come from independent researchers, federal reports, or cross-institutional sources, not just within your own lab network
decision_factors._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employer willingness to support alternative visa or relocation pathways
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the employer will sponsor a Canadian office transfer or explore alternative visa categories, the stakes of each individual H-1B lottery round are lower and contingency planning becomes actionable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have a direct conversation with HR before the third lottery cycle; ask specifically whether the company has a Canadian entity and what their policy is on cross-border transfers for visa-challenged employees
decision_factors._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

decision_factors.factor: Long-term US immigration intent
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Someone planning to stay long-term faces compounding risk from Day 1 CPT because it can surface during H-1B adjudication, green card processing, or visa renewals — years after the original work occurred
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask: do I intend to pursue H-1B, EB, or any other long-term US status? If yes, any authorization irregularity in the past becomes a liability
decision_factors._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

decision_factors.factor: Access to a willing cap-exempt employer or professor relationship
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The entire cap-exempt route depends on finding a university, nonprofit research institution, or national lab willing to sponsor — without this relationship, the route is unavailable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether you have existing academic relationships (former advisors, research collaborators); also research DOE national labs and nonprofit research orgs in your field that hire practitioners
decision_factors._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

decision_factors.factor: Cumulative unemployment days remaining
decision_factors.why_it_matters: OPT has a 90-day cumulative unemployment cap; exceeding it jeopardizes OPT status and can trigger RFE or denial during H-1B adjudication
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track total days unemployed since OPT start date, including all gaps across all job changes, not just the most recent one
decision_factors._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency (TEF Canada score)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Bilingual Advantage under Express Entry yields approximately 50 additional CRS points — the single largest discretionary lever available to most applicants
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Estimate time to achieve B1 level; TEF Canada is noted as more lenient than DELF; English speakers can realistically reach B1 in 3–6 months of consistent study

decision_factors.factor: Country of origin and applicable green card backlog
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For Indian nationals, the EB-2 backlog makes a US green card functionally inaccessible within a working career timeline, fundamentally changing the US vs. Canada calculus
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the current USCIS Visa Bulletin for EB-2 India priority dates and compare to your graduation or work start year

decision_factors.factor: PGWP open permit status awareness
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP holders have an open work permit and can change jobs freely — but many holders do not act on this freedom because of anxiety-driven misunderstanding of their actual constraints
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm your permit type: an open work permit has no employer restriction; an employer-specific permit does. PGWP is open.

decision_factors.factor: Program length and type (including co-op/integrated components)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP duration is tied to the length of the program completed; whether co-op months count toward the total affects how long the permit will be
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with your institution whether the co-op or work-integrated learning component is formally part of the degree program, and whether the completion letter reflects the full program duration

decision_factors.factor: Service center assignment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Processing times vary significantly between Texas and Nebraska service centers; the assigned center affects how long an applicant waits
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check USCIS website for current per-center estimates; assignment is based on school address, not applicant mailing address

decision_factors.factor: PGWP length resulting from completed program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP length directly determines how many years of Canadian work experience a graduate can accumulate, which feeds into Express Entry CRS score and PR eligibility timelines
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify the length of the program you will actually complete; check IRCC's PGWP rules for single vs. combined programs; model out the PR timeline difference between PGWP lengths

decision_factors.factor: Work authorization framing clarity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Employers who misread PGWP as requiring sponsorship will silently filter out candidates before a single conversation occurs
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether your resume and outreach explicitly state 'open work permit,' 'no sponsorship required,' and 'pursuing PR independently'

decision_factors.factor: Company size and funding stage
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Large tech companies sponsor routinely with dedicated immigration teams; small startups typically won't unless the candidate is uniquely irreplaceable; mid-size funded startups vary and require explicit inquiry
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check company size, funding stage, and job postings for explicit 'no sponsorship' language; ask the recruiter directly before investing in multi-round interviews

decision_factors.factor: Remaining OPT time vs. H-1B cap season alignment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If remaining OPT expires before the next H-1B approval date, the person falls out of status regardless of job quality or employer willingness
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate OPT expiration date, then check whether it overlaps with the next H-1B cap season start (October 1). If less than ~18 months remain, urgency is critical.

decision_factors.factor: I-20 program end date vs. OPT EAD expiration date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 60-day grace period starts from the I-20 program end date, not from when the OPT EAD card expires — these two dates can differ, creating confusion about when the grace period actually begins
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare the program end date on your I-20 with the end date on your EAD card; whichever triggers the grace period clock is the I-20 program end date

decision_factors.factor: Total full-time CPT months accumulated
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Crossing 12 months of full-time CPT (20+ hrs/week) permanently eliminates OPT eligibility — the threshold is binary and irreversible
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Sum all CPT authorization periods where hours were 20 or more per week; part-time CPT does not count toward the limit

decision_factors.factor: Whether the student's CPT authorization comes from a SEVP-audited or SEVP-terminated school
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the school loses SEVP certification, students may lose status retroactively and be unable to complete their program or transfer cleanly
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check SEVP's Student and Exchange Visitor Information System (SEVIS) records and search for enforcement actions against the specific school

decision_factors.factor: Employer size and H-1B sponsorship history
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Smaller employers may lack experience filing H-1B petitions correctly, increasing RFE or denial risk, and may be less willing to absorb legal and filing costs
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR directly whether they have filed H-1B petitions before; check USCIS public H-1B employer data; ask for the name of the law firm they use

decision_factors.factor: Primary purpose of the employer entity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether an employer qualifies as cap-exempt; an entity that primarily provides services (e.g., medical care) rather than conducting research does not qualify even if it has a research component or university affiliation
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask whether the organization's primary institutional mission is research; university-affiliated hospitals may fail this test at the organizational level but some departments may qualify depending on their specific function

decision_factors.factor: Emotional acceptability of Plan B
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the backup option feels like failure, it amplifies desperation during H-1B uncertainty and degrades decision quality — people who genuinely accept their Plan B report lower anxiety and clearer thinking
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask: 'Would I be okay if this were my actual path for the next 3 years?' If the answer requires convincing yourself, the plan needs more exploration

decision_factors.factor: Country of birth / GC backlog queue position
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines realistic wait time for US permanent residency; India and China nationals face 50+ year waits in some EB categories, making US GC practically unattainable within a career
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the USCIS Visa Bulletin for your category and country of chargeability; compare current priority date against year of entry into queue

decision_factors.factor: Whether the employer is enrolled in E-Verify
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT is invalid if the employer is not E-Verify enrolled — this is a hard eligibility requirement, not a soft preference
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR directly and verify at e-verify.uscis.gov employer search before filing; confirm enrollment status at I-765 submission time and at approval time

decision_factors.factor: Original enrollment date relative to PGWP policy cutoff
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether a student is grandfathered under old rules, regardless of any subsequent program changes
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare the date on the original enrollment letter against the policy cutoff (2024-11-01 for the 2024 PGWP reform)

decision_factors.factor: Cumulative OPT unemployment days remaining
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The 90-day unemployment cap is cumulative across the entire OPT period; once exhausted, status is violated regardless of future employment
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track every gap between jobs carefully; subtract all previous unemployment periods from the 90-day total to know your remaining buffer
decision_factors._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

decision_factors.factor: Cap-exempt employer availability in your field
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Working for a cap-exempt employer eliminates lottery dependency entirely and allows H-1B filing at any time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether universities, affiliated research institutions, or nonprofit research bodies in your field have relevant roles; explore concurrent employment structures with a lawyer
decision_factors._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

decision_factors.factor: Age
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Age contributes up to 110 CRS points and decreases each year after 35; it is the only major factor that cannot be improved and only worsens over time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check current age against CRS age table: 18–35 earns maximum points, declining incrementally each year, with 45+ earning only a few points
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: CRS分数区间与当前抽签分数线的差距
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 决定走联邦Express Entry还是转向省提名（PNP）路径，直接影响PR时间线
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 建立EE Profile后查看当前CRS评分，对比近期联邦综合类抽签分数（评论显示490-530波动），判断差距是否超过50分
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Current immigration status and projected green card / PR timeline
decision_factors.why_it_matters: You cannot sponsor parents for US permanent residence until you hold a green card yourself; if that is 10+ years away, parents may be in their 70s before the process can even begin
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Estimate your realistic green card ETA based on country of birth, visa category, and current USCIS priority date movement; compare that to parents' current age
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: H-1B lottery cumulative failure probability
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A ~25–27% chance of never winning across 3 STEM OPT attempts means roughly 1 in 4 people on the US path are forced out — this is a non-trivial base rate that changes the risk calculus
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate: with ~35% single-draw probability, probability of losing all 3 draws ≈ (0.65)^3 ≈ 27.5%. Treat this as the probability of forced departure before green card process even begins
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: Country of birth (not citizenship)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Green card quotas are allocated by country of birth, not citizenship. India-born and China-born applicants face backlogs of decades; all other nationalities face near-current priority dates for the same EB categories.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify your country of birth and look up the current Visa Bulletin priority date for your EB category + birth country combination
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

decision_factors.factor: Spouse's English language ability
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A spouse who achieves CLB 7 can add up to ~20 CRS points to a combined profile, a meaningful gain requiring no external party cooperation
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have spouse take a practice IELTS to estimate their current level; assess feasibility of reaching CLB 7 within a reasonable study timeline
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Prior biometrics enrollment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Having previously submitted biometrics removes a processing step that can add weeks to PGWP timelines
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check your IRCC account or prior permit documents to confirm whether biometrics are on file and still valid
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

decision_factors.factor: Alternative PR pathways available
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the student plans to pursue PR through employer sponsorship or a Provincial Nominee Program rather than Express Entry based on Canadian work experience, the PGWP duration loss matters less
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess whether the target industry/employer offers LMIA-based sponsorship or whether a relevant PNP stream is accessible regardless of work experience duration
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

decision_factors.factor: Salary gap between US and Canada (especially senior-level)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A 30-50% salary gap at senior levels represents a significant lifetime earnings difference; this gap was acknowledged even by the pro-Canada poster
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare total compensation (base + equity + benefits) for equivalent roles in target cities (e.g., Bay Area vs. Toronto/Vancouver); note that commenters questioned whether the gap has narrowed recently
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Whether the role requires 'Canadian experience'
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some Canadian employers implicitly or explicitly prefer candidates with prior Canadian work history, disadvantaging new graduates without co-op or internship experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check job postings for phrases like 'Canadian experience preferred'; also infer from interview feedback if you are screening well but not advancing
decision_factors._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employer's willingness and ability to support alternative visa paths (L1, EB1B, EB2)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some employers offer L1 as a genuine bypass; others use it as a retention tool without intent to file green card; the difference determines whether this path resolves your status or extends dependency
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask explicitly: will the company file I-140 for EB1B or EB2 during the L1 period? Get a timeline commitment in writing if possible

decision_factors.factor: Nationality and EB-2 priority date backlog
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Indian and Chinese nationals face vastly different wait times even after I-140 approval due to per-country caps; NIW approval does not mean green card is imminent
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the monthly Visa Bulletin for your country's EB-2 priority date; for Chinese nationals, the backlog can be many years
decision_factors._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

decision_factors.factor: Genuine acceptability of Plan B options
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If alternative paths (Canada, home country) are only theoretically considered but never seriously evaluated, they cannot reduce anxiety or inform real decisions; the psychological benefit of having a real Plan B depends on it being genuinely researched
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research Canadian immigration pathways (PGWP eligibility, Express Entry CRS score estimate) or home-country career options in enough concrete detail to form an actual preference, not just awareness
decision_factors._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

decision_factors.factor: Quality and SEVP standing of the CPT-issuing school
decision_factors.why_it_matters: USCIS scrutinizes whether the school's curriculum genuinely requires the internship as a program component; schools with weak academic programs or SEVP warnings are more likely to have their authorizations challenged
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check if the school has received SEVP warnings or has been subject to enforcement actions; look for public lists or attorney advisories
decision_factors._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

decision_factors.factor: Primary employer's willingness to continue employment under concurrent H-1B arrangement
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The concurrent employment model requires the primary employer to tolerate the arrangement; some employers may have legal or HR concerns about an employee holding H-1B sponsored by a third party
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with primary employer's HR/legal whether concurrent employment is acceptable and whether they will support or tolerate the cap-exempt sponsor arrangement
decision_factors._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

decision_factors.factor: New employer's E-Verify status (STEM OPT only)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT authorization requires the employer to be enrolled in E-Verify; working for a non-enrolled employer invalidates STEM OPT
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask the employer directly and verify through the E-Verify employer search tool before accepting the offer
decision_factors._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

decision_factors.factor: Qualifying job offer (TEER 0/1 vs. TEER 2/3)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Job offer value varies (50 vs. potentially 200 points depending on NOC category and draw type); this is a point of active confusion in the community
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm NOC TEER level of the role and whether offer qualifies under federal Express Entry rules; community members in comments flagged uncertainty about 50 vs. 200 threshold

decision_factors.factor: CRS score relative to recent draw cutoffs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Express Entry is competitive and cutoff scores shift between draws — a score that qualifies you today may not qualify you next month or vice versa
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track the last 6-12 months of draw cutoffs on the IRCC website and assess whether your profile score consistently clears or is borderline

decision_factors.factor: Realistic quality of Plan B (return home scenario)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The perceived catastrophic nature of returning home drives desperation, which in turn causes poor career and immigration decisions — overcorrecting to the risk side
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research concrete job opportunities, salary ranges, and lifestyle in your home country; compare against Canada scenario with honest numbers

decision_factors.factor: Study permit expiry date relative to PGWP application date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the study permit expires before the PGWP is approved, the applicant enters 'maintained/implied status' — they can remain in Canada but cannot work during the gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare study permit expiry date with expected PGWP processing time; if gap is likely, plan finances and employment accordingly

decision_factors.factor: Form completeness and accuracy
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Errors or omissions on the I-765 trigger RFEs (Requests for Evidence), which can add weeks to processing time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have DSO review the application before submission; cross-check all fields against official instructions

decision_factors.factor: Whether programs are consecutive with no gap
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether multiple programs can be combined for a longer PGWP; a gap between programs may reset or disqualify the combination
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the end date of the first program and the start date of the next; consult IRCC guidance or a regulated consultant on what gap length, if any, is permissible

decision_factors.factor: Company size and application volume
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Large companies receive hundreds to thousands of applications per role; mid-size companies have thinner applicant pools and more flexibility to engage PGWP holders
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Filter job searches to companies with 50–200 employees; avoid roles showing 500+ LinkedIn applicants

decision_factors.factor: Whether 'no sponsorship' means no H-1B or no visa processes at all
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some companies that refuse H-1B will still complete STEM OPT I-983 forms, meaning the candidate can still work there for up to 3 years post-degree without H-1B
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask explicitly: 'Does your no-sponsorship policy include STEM OPT extension paperwork, or only H-1B petitions?'

decision_factors.factor: Whether immigration commitments are documented in writing
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Written commitments create at minimum a paper trail and at most a legally enforceable basis; verbal commitments have no enforceability and can be retracted without consequence
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review offer letter and any written communications (email, Slack, contract) for explicit sponsorship language; absence of written commitment is a red flag regardless of verbal assurances

decision_factors.factor: Nature of work during OPT gap — authorized employment vs. freelance/consulting
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Whether freelance or consulting work counts as 'employed' for unemployment day tracking is unresolved in community understanding, and getting it wrong could invalidate OPT status
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Seek licensed immigration attorney clarification; do not rely on community consensus — this question (123 upvotes) was left unanswered in the thread

decision_factors.factor: Employer E-Verify enrollment status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT extension is legally unavailable unless the employer is E-Verify enrolled; employer intent or company size does not override this requirement
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask the employer HR directly and verify via the E-Verify employer search tool before accepting the offer

decision_factors.factor: Whether the Day 1 CPT authorization is backed by transferred credits or prior enrollment documentation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Community members raised that students with documented prior enrollment may present a different — potentially lower — risk profile to USCIS
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Evaluate whether the school has a documented academic rationale for Day 1 CPT (e.g., credit transfer policy) versus pure enrollment-day authorization with no prior history

decision_factors.factor: Availability of cap-exempt employer bridge
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Cap-exempt employers (universities, qualifying nonprofits) can sponsor H-1B outside the lottery at any time, providing a path forward even after multiple lottery losses
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research whether your employer has a nonprofit arm, affiliated university, or research entity with primary research purpose; ask HR about internal transfer options to affiliated entities

decision_factors.factor: Employer willingness to share an employee nominally with a cap-exempt entity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Even if a cap-exempt path is legally viable, the primary for-profit employer must agree to the dual-employment structure; some employers refuse
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Raise the arrangement early with the employer's HR and legal team; gauge whether they have existing university partnerships or precedent for such arrangements

decision_factors.factor: Number of consecutive H-1B lottery losses
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each loss year compounds psychological strain and depletes OPT/STEM OPT runway — by year 2 multiple commenters report genuine depression and identity questioning, not just logistical stress
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track how many registration cycles remain on current OPT/STEM OPT status and model the realistic timeline to exhaustion

decision_factors.factor: Salary differential tolerance
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian tech salaries are 30-50% lower than comparable US roles; accepting this cut is the core financial trade-off for immigration certainty
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate total compensation delta over 5-10 years vs. probability-weighted value of US GC; factor in housing, healthcare, and tax differences

decision_factors.factor: Whether the employer has had a STEM OPT employee before
decision_factors.why_it_matters: First-time STEM OPT employers often fill out I-983 incorrectly — wrong NAICS codes, missing STEM connection narrative — triggering RFEs that delay or jeopardize the extension
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask HR if they have processed I-983 before; if not, treat I-983 review by DSO or immigration attorney as mandatory rather than optional

decision_factors.factor: Whether the changed-to program appears on IRCC's eligible program list
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For students NOT protected by grandfathering, the new program must be on the eligible list to qualify for PGWP at all; for grandfathered students it is less critical but still affects PGWP duration cap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check IRCC's current eligible program list; note that the list changes (178 removed, 119 added in June 2025 alone)

decision_factors.factor: Whether your degree appears on the official STEM OPT eligible list
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT extension is only available for designated STEM programs; an unlisted degree disqualifies you regardless of job function
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the official DHS STEM Designated Degree Program List before assuming eligibility
decision_factors._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

decision_factors.factor: Extraordinary ability evidence (for O-1 or EB-1A)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: O-1 and EB-1A bypass the H-1B lottery but require documented evidence of outstanding achievement; the threshold is substantially higher than H-1B
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Audit your profile: peer-reviewed publications and citation counts, awards, media coverage, peer review service, salary relative to field peers
decision_factors._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canadian vs. foreign education credential
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian credentials score higher than equivalent foreign credentials: a Canadian master's earns 135 points vs. a foreign master's which earns 120 points
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare degree level and country of institution against the CRS education table; a Canadian PhD yields the maximum 150 points
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: 工作职位的NOC TEER分类
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC要求工作经验必须属于TEER 0/1/2/3类，低于此分类的工作经验不计入资格；TEER 0/1类的Job Offer还可额外加50-200分
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 在IRCC官网查询当前职位对应的NOC代码和TEER等级，入职前确认
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Parents' current age and health trajectory
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A 10-year delay in reunion eligibility is qualitatively different depending on whether parents are currently 50 or 65; health and mobility decline changes what 'reunion' means in practice
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map the earliest plausible reunion date against parents' projected age and health; assess whether that timeline is acceptable
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: Salary differential between US and Canada
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Bay Area tech roles pay 40–60% more than Toronto equivalents — but this gap narrows when adjusted for cost of living, making the raw number misleading for lifestyle planning
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare after-tax, after-cost-of-living income in your specific city and role, not gross salary figures
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: EB category eligibility (EB-1 vs EB-2 vs EB-3)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For China and India born applicants, the difference between EB-1 and EB-2 priority dates can be 10+ years. EB-1 categories (especially EB-1A self-petition) have materially shorter backlogs.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Audit publications, citations, awards, media coverage, high salary relative to peers — these are EB-1 qualifying evidence. If none exist, EB-2 is likely the only option.
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

decision_factors.factor: French language learning feasibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: French bilingualism bonus adds up to 50 CRS points and is entirely self-controlled — no employer or government approval needed
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess your language learning capacity and available time; CLB 7 in French (approximately B1) is achievable in 3–6 months for motivated learners with some prior exposure
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Completeness and quality of graduation documentation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: IRCC requires a graduation confirmation letter from the registrar specifying degree name and completion date — missing or vague documentation causes delays or refusals
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Request the letter directly from the registrar's office and confirm it explicitly states degree name and official completion date; do not substitute with an unofficial transcript or degree certificate alone
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

decision_factors.factor: Current vs. target program duration comparison
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The impact is asymmetric — switching from 2-year to 1-year is catastrophic for PGWP; switching between two 2-year+ programs has no PGWP impact
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare the official durations of both the current and target programs; calculate the PGWP each would yield
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

decision_factors.factor: Psychological cost of immigration uncertainty
decision_factors.why_it_matters: H-1B lottery failures and green card wait anxiety impose real psychological burden that affects career decisions, job mobility, and quality of life — mentioned by multiple commenters as underweighted in typical analyses
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask honestly: how did you feel during H-1B lottery season? How would you feel being unable to leave a bad employer due to transfer risk?
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Job market density by city and field
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The volume of available positions varies significantly by geography (Toronto vs. other cities) and field (e.g. data science roles noted as fewer than in the US), affecting realistic application conversion rates
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Cross-reference job board volume by city and role type before committing to a job search strategy; community members specifically asked about Toronto vs. other cities as a differentiating variable
decision_factors._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

decision_factors.factor: Tolerance for status dependency on a single employer
decision_factors.why_it_matters: All employer-sponsored paths (H1B, L1, PERM) tie you to one employer; loss of job means loss of status with limited grace periods
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess your financial runway, risk tolerance, and whether you would accept a worse job to preserve status

decision_factors.factor: Availability of diverse evidence types beyond publications
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Peer review invitations, conference invitations, and media citations each address different aspects of recognition and can substitute for gaps in citation count
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Audit what evidence you have across categories: citations, peer review invitations, conference invitations, media mentions, and government/policy references
decision_factors._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

decision_factors.factor: Number of H-1B lottery attempts already used
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each failed lottery year narrows the OPT/STEM OPT runway; by the third failure the window to execute alternatives is tight, making earlier contingency planning critical
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count current lottery attempts against remaining authorized stay; model out the earliest date status would expire if the next lottery also fails
decision_factors._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

decision_factors.factor: H-1B RFE risk exposure from prior CPT history
decision_factors.why_it_matters: H-1B RFEs specifically questioning CPT authorization legitimacy are a documented occurrence; even if ultimately approved, they create delay, legal cost, and uncertainty
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Evaluate whether prior CPT was granted by a school with a credible academic rationale; consult attorney before H-1B filing
decision_factors._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

decision_factors.factor: Remaining OPT/STEM OPT validity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The timeline for finding a cap-exempt sponsor and completing the petition is constrained by how much authorized work authorization remains; running out before approval causes a gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate exact EAD expiration date and estimate processing time (premium processing ~15 business days); begin outreach well before STEM OPT expires
decision_factors._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

decision_factors.factor: Professional field alignment between new job and degree
decision_factors.why_it_matters: OPT requires employment to be directly related to the field of study; a mismatch can invalidate OPT authorization
decision_factors.how_to_assess: DSO must assess and update SEVIS with the new employer; consult DSO if the field alignment is ambiguous
decision_factors._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

decision_factors.factor: Spouse or partner language score
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A qualifying spouse IELTS score adds up to ~20 CRS points that many applicants overlook entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether spouse has taken IELTS or equivalent; CLB 7 is the meaningful threshold for significant point contribution

decision_factors.factor: Salary delta between Canada and US tech markets
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian salaries in tech are genuinely 25-35% lower than comparable Bay Area roles according to the poster — this is a real financial cost that must be weighed against immigration certainty
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare total compensation (including equity) for your specific role and level across both markets using current data, not historical norms

decision_factors.factor: Physical presence in Canada at time of PGWP application
decision_factors.why_it_matters: At least one commenter raised uncertainty about whether remote/abroad applicants qualify — this may affect eligibility or processing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with IRCC guidance or an authorized representative whether completing final semester abroad affects PGWP eligibility

decision_factors.factor: Application timing within the eligibility window
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Applying earlier within the 90-day window places the applicant earlier in the processing queue
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count back 90 days from program end date and target that date for submission

decision_factors.factor: Whether the academic issue can be resolved within the existing program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the problem (research fit, supervisor, coursework) can be fixed without a formal program switch, the student avoids PGWP loss entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Meet with graduate advisor and DSO to explore internal options: changing thesis topic, supervisor, or specialization track without triggering a program change in IRCC records

decision_factors.factor: Canadian network depth
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Referral response rate is approximately 15x cold application rate in Canada; a local network is a multiplier on job search effectiveness
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count how many Canadian-based contacts can make internal referrals; if near zero, networking must precede applications

decision_factors.factor: Industry type (consulting/staffing vs. product company)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Consulting and staffing firms often cannot sponsor due to client site restrictions regardless of their own willingness; product companies have more direct control
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify whether the role places you at client sites; if yes, ask whether the no-sponsorship policy is client-driven

decision_factors.factor: Availability of alternative visa pathways (L-1, O-1, marriage-based, F-1 bridge)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each alternative has strict eligibility requirements and different career/time costs; over-relying on an inaccessible alternative wastes critical runway
decision_factors.how_to_assess: L-1 requires 1+ year abroad with multinational employer; O-1 requires demonstrable extraordinary ability; F-1 bridge requires genuine degree enrollment; marriage-based requires applicable personal situation — each must be individually verified

decision_factors.factor: Voluntary resignation vs. employer-initiated termination during OPT
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Voluntary resignation ends authorized employment but does not automatically end OPT status itself — the distinction affects how unemployment days are counted and what options remain
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with DSO or immigration attorney whether the departure was voluntary and what the current OPT authorization status is post-resignation

decision_factors.factor: OPT EAD expiration date relative to H-1B filing window
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Cap-gap protection only applies if the H-1B petition is filed while OPT status is still valid; students whose EAD expires before April 1 have no cap-gap bridge
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check exact EAD expiration date on the card and compare to the H-1B filing window (April 1 start); if EAD expires before April, cap-gap protection is unavailable

decision_factors.factor: Long-term immigration intent of the student
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For students seeking only short-term work and planning to return home, the risk calculus is different than for those pursuing permanent US residency or H-1B sponsorship
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask explicitly: is H-1B, green card, or long-term US status a goal? If yes, Day 1 CPT risk is categorically higher

decision_factors.factor: US master's degree eligibility for the master's cap pool
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Eligible candidates get two chances in the lottery — the 20k master's cap pool and the general 65k cap — improving selection probability
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm whether your degree is from a US-accredited institution granting a qualifying master's degree; this is binary — you either qualify or you don't

decision_factors.factor: STEM OPT remaining duration
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The amount of time left on STEM OPT determines urgency; if OPT is nearly exhausted, there may not be enough time to structure and file a cap-exempt petition before status lapses
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate OPT expiration date and compare to typical H-1B cap-exempt processing timelines; consult attorney immediately if under 6 months remain

decision_factors.factor: Employer-specific authorization dependency
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Visa status tied to a specific employer removes normal career optionality — job changes that are trivial for citizens carry immigration risk for F-1/H-1B holders, creating compounding stress
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess whether current employer is the sole sponsor and what portability options exist if the employment relationship changes

decision_factors.factor: Field of work / industry niche
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's smaller job market may not support niche specializations; the US offers broader industry diversity and stronger network effects in finance and tech
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Search active job postings in your specific role and level in Toronto/Vancouver vs. NYC/SF/Seattle; assess recruiter activity

decision_factors.factor: Whether the actual work site employer differs from the legal employer (staffing/consulting arrangements)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: In staffing agency or consulting firm placements, it is unclear whether the agency or the client fills out the I-983 — getting this wrong can invalidate the training plan
decision_factors.how_to_assess: This was raised as an unresolved question with 134 upvotes in the thread — consult a DSO or immigration attorney before filing in this scenario

decision_factors.factor: Duration of study and program category
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP duration is not simply equal to study duration; post-2024 policy caps non-eligible-list programs at 2 years maximum regardless of study length, reducing expected PGWP duration for 3-year program graduates
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map study duration against IRCC's tiered formula: <8 months = up to 1 year; 8 months–2 years = equal to study time; 2+ years = up to 3 years (subject to eligible-list cap of 2 years under new rules)

decision_factors.factor: Employer E-Verify enrollment status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT requires the employer to be enrolled in E-Verify; a non-enrolled employer blocks the extension even if all other conditions are met
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask the employer HR or legal team directly whether they are registered on E-Verify before accepting an offer if STEM OPT is your plan
decision_factors._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

decision_factors.factor: Citizenship (Canadian or Mexican)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: TN visa is available at the port of entry with no quota, making it a fast and reliable alternative for eligible professions
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm citizenship status and whether your occupation is on the USMCA/TN eligible profession list
decision_factors._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency (TEF Canada)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Achieving CLB 7 in French adds approximately +50 CRS points — described by the community as often the decisive margin at current cutoff levels
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess current French level; if at zero, estimate 3–6 months of study to reach CLB 7 (roughly B1 level); total cost RMB 3,000–8,000 plus ~200 CAD exam fee
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: 是否有配偶/伴侣且其英语水平
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 配偶英语达标可为主申请人CRS加分，改变联邦抽签竞争力
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 配偶参加语言考试，评估加分幅度是否能弥补CRS缺口
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canada's Super Visa availability vs. US parent visitor visa constraints
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's Super Visa allows up to 2 years per entry legally; US B-2 visitor visas do not offer a comparable long-stay mechanism, making Canada structurally superior for near-term parental co-residence
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Determine whether Super Visa eligibility applies; check current processing times (stated as 3–8 months in the source)
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: Family formation plans (marriage, children)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's child benefits, parental leave, and early childhood education subsidies materially alter the total economic comparison — the financial gap between the two countries shrinks or reverses for families
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Model expected childcare costs, parental leave income replacement, and child benefit payments in both countries against your timeline for family formation
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employer stability and PERM dependency
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each job change may require restarting the PERM labor certification process, resetting portions of the green card timeline. Company bankruptcy or acquisition can interrupt the entire petition.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess employer financial health, M&A likelihood, and whether the role is likely to exist in 5-10 years; also evaluate whether EB-1A or NIW (employer-independent) paths are viable
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience accumulated
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each additional year of Canadian work experience increases CRS points, with the second year yielding a larger increment than the first
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate your current Canadian work experience in months and project when you will cross 1-year and 2-year thresholds
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Industry and role availability in Canada
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some industries (e.g., top-tier tech, certain finance roles) have significantly fewer opportunities in Canada, making a Canada-first strategy a real career trade-off for some fields
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research specific employer and role density in your field in Toronto/Vancouver vs. your US target market
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canada Express Entry CRS score eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A person with a strong tech background and Canadian work experience may be able to obtain Canadian PR in 1-2 years via CEC, which eliminates employer dependency entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the IRCC CRS calculator with your current profile; compare expected ITA timeline to expected US green card timeline

decision_factors.factor: Budget for legal and filing fees
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Cap-exempt H-1B petitions still require attorney fees and filing fees; premium processing adds cost but reduces uncertainty during OPT expiration pressure
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Estimate $4,000–$6,000 total (attorney + USCIS fees including premium processing); clarify whether university or applicant bears these costs
decision_factors._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

decision_factors.factor: Buffer days between resignation and new start date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The gap between leaving one job and starting the next counts toward the 90-day cumulative unemployment limit
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate the difference between last day at old employer and first day at new employer; minimize this window
decision_factors._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Second year of Canadian work experience yields more incremental CRS points than the first year; Canadian experience weighted higher than foreign experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track anniversary of Canadian work start date; plan application timing around second-year milestone if close to cutoff

decision_factors.factor: Employer-tied healthcare stress in the US
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Multiple high-upvote comments (234 upvotes) identify this as an underrated psychological and financial factor — losing a job in the US means losing health coverage, which compounds job-change anxiety
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess whether you have dependents, chronic conditions, or high healthcare utilization — the higher the dependency, the more valuable universal coverage becomes

decision_factors.factor: Whether the 180-day application window has been observed
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Missing the 180-day deadline results in PGWP denial with no recourse — it is a hard cutoff
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify the exact date final marks/transcript were issued and count forward 180 days; apply before that date

decision_factors.factor: Institution's DLI status for both programs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Both programs must be at Designated Learning Institutions for the combined PGWP calculation to apply
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify each institution's DLI status on IRCC's official DLI list before enrolling

decision_factors.factor: Geographic targeting (Canadian office presence)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Remote-only job searches that exclude Canadian offices limit the pool of employers who can legally hire on a Canadian work permit
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify whether target employers have Canadian legal entities; remote roles based entirely outside Canada may not be compatible with PGWP

decision_factors.factor: Timing of disclosure within the interview process
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Disclosing before the company is invested in you adds friction early; disclosing after an offer is made may feel like a bait-and-switch; disclosing before final rounds is the community-validated sweet spot
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Raise it after a technical round but before final/offer stage — the company has shown interest but no offer is contingent yet

decision_factors.factor: Existence of written or digital communications about sponsorship promise
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Slack messages, emails, or other documented communications may support a promissory estoppel claim in some jurisdictions, creating leverage even when no formal contract exists
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Search email, Slack, and offer-related documents for any written mention of H-1B sponsorship by employer representatives

decision_factors.factor: SOC code precision and specialty occupation documentation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Vague or incorrect SOC codes invite USCIS RFEs or denials on specialty occupation grounds; precise codes with job duty evidence reduce scrutiny
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have an immigration attorney map actual job duties to the most specific applicable SOC code, not the most commonly used one for the field

decision_factors.factor: Number of consecutive H-1B lottery losses
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each failed lottery cycle consumes a year of OPT and narrows the remaining window for alternatives; two losses is identified as the threshold where cap-exempt strategies become worth the complexity
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count lottery attempts vs. OPT years remaining; after two losses, alternative strategies should be pursued in parallel with any future lottery entry

decision_factors.factor: CRS score and Express Entry pool competitiveness
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is points-based but not guaranteed; CRS cutoffs fluctuate and some streams have long waits if score is low
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the IRCC CRS calculator; check recent draw cutoffs; evaluate PNP streams (e.g. Alberta, Saskatchewan tech pathways) if federal score is borderline

decision_factors.factor: Quality and specificity of the STEM connection narrative in the I-983
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A vague or generic description of how the role relates to the degree is a primary trigger for USCIS RFEs on STEM OPT applications
decision_factors.how_to_assess: The I-983 should name specific projects, tools, and methods that link the job duties to the STEM degree field — generic job descriptions are insufficient

decision_factors.factor: Timing of PGWP application relative to policy announcement dates
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Applications submitted before a policy change took effect may be processed under prior rules, as illustrated by the hospitality management case in this thread
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track IRCC policy announcement dates; if close to a policy transition, early application submission provides a meaningful buffer

decision_factors.factor: H-1B lottery probability across multiple attempts
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Community-cited probability is approximately 50-60% per attempt over three tries; planning only for year one success without contingencies is risky
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate how many lottery attempts your current OPT and STEM OPT timeline allows, and identify fallback paths before the first attempt
decision_factors._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

decision_factors.factor: STEM OPT remaining duration
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each year of remaining STEM OPT equals one more lottery attempt; knowing how many attempts remain shapes the urgency of backup planning
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate graduation date and STEM OPT extension expiry; map to remaining April lottery windows
decision_factors._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each additional year of Canadian work experience adds meaningful CRS points (1 year = 40 pts, 2 years = 53 pts, 3+ years = 64 pts) and accumulates automatically over time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count verified months of full-time Canadian work experience in an eligible NOC category; plan the immigration timeline around when the next threshold is crossed
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: 是否已有加拿大工作Offer（尤其TEER 0/1类）
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 合格Job Offer可直接加50-200分CRS，大幅提升ITA可能性；同时也可能开启省提名直接路径
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 确认offer是否为永久性全职职位，雇主是否具备提名资质
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canada parent PR lottery win rate (~20–30% per source)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Even after becoming a Canadian PR or citizen, permanent parent sponsorship is subject to annual caps and a lottery, making outcome uncertain
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Treat PR sponsorship as a probabilistic multi-year process, not a guaranteed right; plan Super Visa as the primary near-term tool
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: EB-1/NIW eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Candidates who qualify for EB-1A, EB-1B, or NIW can bypass the backlog entirely or significantly reduce it — this changes the US path from a 5–10 year uncertainty to a potentially fast-tracked one
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Evaluate publication record, patents, awards, salary level relative to peers, and advisory/judging roles against USCIS criteria for EB-1/NIW
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: Personal time horizon and life stage
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A 20-50 year wait affects life decisions differently depending on age, family plans, and career flexibility. Someone at 25 and someone at 40 face categorically different decision trees.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map current age + projected priority date advancement against major life milestones (children's education, retirement, aging parents) to assess true personal cost
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employment type: direct hire vs. agency contract
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Community uncertainty exists about whether agency/contract work qualifies as Canadian work experience for CRS purposes, which affects score calculations and PR eligibility
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify with an authorized immigration consultant whether your specific contract arrangement meets IRCC's definition of 'skilled work experience'
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Housing cost in major Canadian cities
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Toronto and Vancouver housing prices offset some of Canada's lower cost-of-living advantage relative to US cities; smaller Canadian cities are more affordable but have fewer job opportunities
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare housing cost-to-income ratio in your target Canadian city vs. US city; consider whether remote work enables smaller-city living
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Country of origin relative to current ITA cutoff
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Applicants from high-volume countries (India, China) face effectively higher de facto cutoffs due to draw composition; standard strategies may not be sufficient
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track historical ITA cutoffs by draw type and compare to current CRS; if from India or China, assume you need levers beyond baseline profile optimization

decision_factors.factor: Career trajectory ceiling for senior IC roles
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The poster acknowledges the US still has a higher career ceiling for senior individual contributor roles, though notes the gap is closing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research compensation and title progression for your target senior roles at Canadian vs. US companies in your specific function

decision_factors.factor: STEM OPT eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: STEM OPT extends work authorization by 24 months, enabling participation in 2 additional H-1B lottery cycles before status expires
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm your degree field appears on the DHS STEM designated degree program list; confirm your employer meets E-Verify requirements

decision_factors.factor: Job flexibility vs. employer-tethered status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: H-1B ties workers to a specific employer; job changes require new petitions and create gap-of-status risk; Canadian PGWP is open work permit allowing free job switching
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess how stable your current employer relationship is and how likely you are to want to change jobs in the next 2-3 years

decision_factors.factor: Availability of cap-exempt employer options
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Universities and qualified nonprofit research institutions are exempt from the H-1B cap, offering a path to H-1B status without lottery exposure
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research whether target employers or adjacent institutions qualify as cap-exempt; evaluate whether the role type fits
decision_factors._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

decision_factors.factor: Tolerance for Canada as an alternative destination
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is quota-free and points-based, with PGWP providing open work permit flexibility; many H-1B lottery failures have reached PR within 2-3 years
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate CRS score, assess provincial nominee program eligibility, compare lifestyle and career market factors
decision_factors._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

decision_factors.factor: Provincial Nomination (PNP)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points, effectively guaranteeing an ITA — making it the single largest possible score change
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research whether your NOC code, province of work, and employer situation qualify for any PNP stream; consult a provincial immigration advisor
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employer willingness to sponsor continuously
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The US path requires multi-year employer commitment (H-1B + PERM + I-140 + adjustment) — losing sponsorship mid-process resets the clock and may invalidate years of waiting
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess employer stability, track record of sponsoring employees to green card completion, and HR/legal department capacity to manage long-term cases
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: Salary differential tolerance for Canada alternative
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is faster (one commenter: 14 months) but typically involves a salary reduction. The trade-off is speed and status certainty vs. earning power.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate the salary gap between comparable US and Canada roles in your field, then weigh against the psychological and practical cost of indefinite H-1B dependency
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

decision_factors.factor: Draw type (all-program vs. category-based vs. PNP)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Tech-specific or sector-specific draws may clear at lower CRS scores; OP received ITA in a tech-specific draw at 489, not an all-program draw
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor IRCC draw announcements for category-based draws relevant to your NOC; do not only track all-program draw cutoffs
