diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Identify the specific NOC codes targeted in recent category draws; ensure the Express Entry profile NOC aligns with targeted categories; set up IRCC draw alerts
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Focus strategy on improving core CRS factors (language, Canadian experience, French) or pursuing PNP streams to compensate for lack of category draw access

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you know the CIP code of your specific program, and have you verified it against IRCC's current eligible program list (not a third-party site)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student's eligibility assessment is based on authoritative data or potentially outdated/inaccurate secondary sources
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If verified via IRCC directly: confirm the verification was done against the post-June 2025 list, not a prior version
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not verified: halt any PGWP planning and go directly to the IRCC website or consult an RCIC before proceeding

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score, and have you run the official federal calculator in the last 3 months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Reveals whether the candidate has situational awareness of their immigration competitiveness and which specific gaps exist (language, age, education, spousal factors)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If score known: compare it against current draw cut-offs for general, CEC-specific, French-language, and PNP-enhanced draws — identify which category is realistic
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If score unknown: run the calculator immediately; no strategic immigration decisions should be made without a current score

diagnostic_questions.question: When does your PGWP expire, and have you calculated the full timeline from PNP application to PR grant?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has a timeline problem that requires bridging work permit planning or accelerated application strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If PGWP expires in 18+ months: standard PNP + PR timeline is likely manageable with early action
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If PGWP expires in under 18 months: immediately model bridge work permit eligibility and file PNP application at the earliest possible date

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your situation fit one of the 4 federally defined RCL exceptions: academic difficulty, medical condition, final semester, or improper course level placement?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether DSO authorization for reduced course load is even legally available — if the answer is no, the student cannot legally drop below full-time regardless of the hardship
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Do you have the required documentation ready (e.g., licensed physician letter for medical RCL) to present to the DSO before submitting the course drop?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The student must maintain full-time enrollment or explore other options (medical leave of absence, program withdrawal, status change) — RCL is not available

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the start date printed on your EAD card, and have you begun working yet?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether unemployment days are accumulating and how much of the 90-day buffer remains before a status violation occurs
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If working: confirm the employer's position qualifies as related to the field of study for OPT compliance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not working: calculate days elapsed since EAD start date and determine urgency of securing employment

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your current or prospective employer enrolled in E-Verify?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is eligible for STEM OPT extension, which determines if they have 12 or 36 total months of OPT work authorization
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm STEM degree CIP code eligibility and begin STEM extension application before current OPT expires
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess whether employer can enroll in E-Verify; if not, evaluate whether a different employer or freelance arrangement is possible before OPT expires

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you on J-1 status, and is your health insurance currently active and continuously maintained?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is at risk of the single most unrecoverable J-1 violation; health insurance is federally mandated for J-1, and willful lapses permanently terminate status
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the coverage meets J-1 federal requirements (minimum coverage limits, repatriation, medical evacuation); not all health insurance qualifies
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess whether the lapse is willful; if so, the student must consult an immigration attorney immediately as reinstatement is not available for willful J-1 health insurance failures

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you staying in this country because you genuinely believe it compounds better for you over 10 years, or because returning home would feel like failure?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the stay-vs-return decision is analytically grounded or emotionally driven by social framing of return as defeat
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Can you articulate specifically which career, financial, or personal factors compound better here — and have you run the purchasing power comparison?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The decision warrants a structured reanalysis using a 10-year compounding framework before committing to another multi-year immigration cycle

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your employer willing to commit in writing to sponsoring your PERM labor certification?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether the employer-sponsored EB-2 track is a real option or theoretical — many employers verbally indicate willingness but refuse when the process begins
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed to assess PERM timeline, I-140 processing strategy, and priority date implications for your country
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: NIW self-petition becomes the primary pathway — assess eligibility rigorously; also evaluate Canada as an alternative if NIW is not viable

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you hold a master's degree or higher from a US institution?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether you qualify for the additional 20,000 advanced degree exemption pool on top of the regular 65,000 cap
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ensure your employer's counsel registers you under the master's cap exemption and confirm degree conferral date is before the filing deadline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You compete only in the regular 65,000 cap; salary level becomes even more critical to maximize entries

diagnostic_questions.question: What OEWS wage level does your employer intend to classify your position at for the H-1B petition?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Under the post-2025 wage-weighted lottery, this directly determines the student's selection probability — Levels III and IV have materially higher odds than Levels I and II.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you verified this against the Department of Labor prevailing wage data for your occupation and location, and have you negotiated accordingly?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The employer may not yet know or may not have immigration counsel; this is a signal to escalate or consult an independent attorney.

diagnostic_questions.question: Is the employer the student is considering for STEM OPT enrolled in E-Verify?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the job offer is legally compatible with STEM OPT or will force an immediate status crisis upon onboarding
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with I-983 Training Plan preparation and DSO notification
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do not accept or start the role; assess whether employer will enroll in E-Verify before the student's start date, and identify alternative offers

diagnostic_questions.question: How long has this F-1 student been out of status?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether reinstatement through USCIS is still a viable option or whether the student must depart the US.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If under 5 months: initiate reinstatement process immediately through the DSO and USCIS. If over 5 months: assess whether exceptional circumstances apply; if not, the student likely needs to depart and re-enter.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm the student is still in valid status and identify what triggered the concern — then verify SEVIS record is active and enrollment is compliant.

diagnostic_questions.question: Did you at any point work more than 20 hours per week during a semester when school was in session?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student qualifies for 'work while you wait' authorization during PGWP processing — unauthorized work during pending status creates an inadmissibility record
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Do not begin working after submitting the PGWP application until you receive the actual permit; consult an RCIC about whether to disclose the prior work violation in the application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You likely qualify for maintained-status work authorization during processing — confirm the other two criteria (valid study permit at submission, full-time program over 8 months) are also met

diagnostic_questions.question: Does any of your Canadian work experience overlap with periods when you were enrolled as a full-time student?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether co-op or part-time student work is being incorrectly counted in the student's CEC eligibility calculation
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes: exclude those hours and recalculate actual CEC eligibility date — the student may be less eligible than they believe
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: the hours count can proceed — confirm they were worked under PGWP or other non-student authorization

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you tested in French (TEF Canada or TCF Canada), and do you have any prior French language exposure or education?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is leaving up to 50 CRS points and French-stream draw access on the table due to not pursuing French testing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Review current CLB level; if below CLB 7, assess investment required to reach it vs. CRS gap to cut-off; enroll in preparation if ROI is positive
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Determine if there is any French background worth exploring; if none, deprioritize French as a strategy and focus on other improvable factors

diagnostic_questions.question: How long is your program — less than 8 months, 8 months to 2 years, or 2 years or more?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The maximum PGWP duration the student can obtain, which directly determines how much time they have to accumulate CEC-qualifying work experience
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: student qualifies for 3-year PGWP — maximum runway for CEC; plan Express Entry timeline accordingly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If under 2 years: calculate whether PGWP duration is sufficient for 1 year of NOC-eligible work experience before expiry; if not, begin planning employer-specific work permit or program extension options

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your French language proficiency level, and have you taken an official TEF Canada or TCF Canada test?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines access to French-language Express Entry draws (cut-offs 90–150 points lower than general draws) and whether French investment is a viable CRS lever
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If CLB 7+ French: ensure it is documented in your Express Entry profile and model CRS score with French-language draw eligibility factored in
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If below CLB 7 or untested: estimate realistic time to CLB 7 and calculate the CRS point gain; compare to other available improvement strategies

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you verified that your job's NOC code and TEER level appear on the eligible occupation list for the PNP stream you are considering?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has done the foundational eligibility check or is assuming eligibility based on job title or general 'tech worker' identity
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm that job duties in the offer letter match the NOC lead statement to protect against misclassification
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do this immediately before investing time in any specific PNP application; a wrong NOC is a hard block on eligibility

diagnostic_questions.question: How many of your currently enrolled courses are online or distance-learning, and are you counting all of them toward your full-time minimum?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has an undiscovered enrollment compliance problem — students who assume all credits count equally may be unknowingly out of status
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Only one online course (up to 3 credits) counts toward the F-1 full-time minimum — recalculate in-person credits and assess whether the minimum is actually met
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm the single online course being counted does not exceed 3 credits and that remaining credits are from in-person sections

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you on F-1 or J-1 status?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Which set of work authorization rules applies — particularly for on-campus employment, where J-1 requires written RO authorization and F-1 does not
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If J-1: have you obtained written RO authorization for any on-campus work before starting?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If F-1: have you confirmed the on-campus employer is an authorized campus employer within the DSO's definition?

diagnostic_questions.question: When was the travel signature on your I-20 last endorsed by your DSO?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student can legally re-enter the US after international travel without risk of CBP denial
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If within 6 months, travel is permissible; remind student to also carry EAD and employment letter
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If more than 6 months ago or unsigned, student must visit DSO for endorsement before any international departure

diagnostic_questions.question: If you are on J-1, have you confirmed whether you are subject to the two-year home residency requirement?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student's planned H-1B or immigrant visa pathway is legally viable without first obtaining a waiver or completing home country residency
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Begin exploring waiver options (interested government agency, no-objection statement, hardship) well before the H-1B filing deadline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Review DS-2019 funding source and J-1 category with DSO; government-funded or skills-list J-1 holders are typically subject to the requirement

diagnostic_questions.question: Before you chose your program and your employer, did you assess how those choices would affect your immigration options 3–5 years from now?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has already locked themselves into a constrained pathway through uninformed early decisions, or has navigational flexibility remaining
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What specific visa categories did you confirm were accessible given your program type and employer profile?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Map the downstream immigration implications of decisions already made immediately — earlier errors may still be correctable but the window closes quickly

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you know your current EB-2 priority date and whether it is current in the Visa Bulletin?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Reveals whether the student has basic situational awareness of their own green card status — ignorance here suggests they are not managing their own case
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: For India/China: also ask whether they are tracking EB-3 dates comparatively for downgrade strategy assessment
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Immediate action item: look up the current Visa Bulletin before making any career or immigration decisions

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your employer confirmed they have immigration counsel briefed on the FY2027 wage-based OEWS registration rules?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the employer is likely to make a critical procedural error (wrong wage level, duplicate submission, missed window) that invalidates your registration
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ask for the name of the firm and verify they have filed H-1B petitions in 2026 or later
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Escalate urgency with HR; offer to share official USCIS/DHS guidance; consider consulting your own immigration attorney independently

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you a Canadian, Mexican, or Australian national?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether TN (Canada/Mexico) or E-3 (Australia) visa eligibility applies — both are lottery-free, cap-free, and indefinitely renewable, potentially making H-1B dependency unnecessary.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is your intended occupation on the TN or E-3 eligible profession list, and have you evaluated this pathway against H-1B as a primary rather than backup option?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do you have any other nationality-based visa options, or are you fully dependent on H-1B or OPT?

diagnostic_questions.question: How many unemployment days has the student accumulated since their OPT or STEM OPT period began?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is approaching or has exceeded the 90-day (OPT) or cumulative 150-day (STEM OPT) limit and whether F-1 status is currently at risk
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If approaching or over the limit, student must consult DSO immediately and assess whether a school transfer, reinstatement, or departure is necessary
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Establish a tracking system immediately if none exists; do not rely on memory or estimates

diagnostic_questions.question: Is this student's degree classified as STEM under the DHS designated degree program list?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is eligible for an additional 17-month OPT extension, providing up to 29 months of total post-graduation work authorization.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the employer is enrolled in E-Verify. If yes, advise the student to apply for STEM OPT extension before standard OPT expires. If no, the employer must enroll before STEM OPT can be granted.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The student is limited to 12 months of OPT. Advise that they must secure employer H-1B sponsorship or alternative status within this window.

diagnostic_questions.question: When was your language proficiency test taken, and will those results be less than 2 years old on your planned PGWP submission date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student needs to retake a language test before filing — submitting with expired scores guarantees refusal
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the test type is one of the three approved by IRCC (CELPIP General, IELTS General, PTE Core) and that the score meets CLB 5
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Book a retake immediately; factor in test registration lead time and results turnaround time against the 180-day PGWP application deadline

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you intend to remain outside Quebec for work and residence?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether CEC is even the correct pathway; Quebec-based students need the QSWP or PEQ, not Express Entry CEC
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If outside Quebec: CEC pathway is appropriate — proceed with eligibility assessment
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If in Quebec or planning to move there: redirect entirely to Quebec immigration streams; CEC will not apply

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you researched and applied to any Provincial Nominee Program streams aligned with your occupation and province of residence?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is pursuing the most powerful single CRS lever available — a provincial nomination worth 600 points — or ignoring it entirely
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm which streams are being pursued; check if current employer can support a job-offer-required PNP stream; track application status
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Immediately research PNP streams for the candidate's province and occupation; determine if employer is willing to provide a letter of support; treat PNP pursuit as parallel to Express Entry, not sequential

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you completed an approved language test (IELTS, CELPIP, or TEF) and do you have a valid result meeting the required CLB level?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student can actually submit a PGWP application — language test is now a mandatory document, not optional
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If valid test result in hand: confirm the score meets CLB 7 (university) or CLB 5 (college) in all four abilities before filing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no test booked: determine how many months until graduation and schedule immediately — test booking, sitting, and results can take 4-8 weeks minimum

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the NOC TEER level of the job you are currently in or planning to accept on your PGWP?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether current work experience will count toward the 1-year CEC requirement; TEER 4–5 roles do not qualify and may result in the candidate building time with no CEC eligibility progress
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If TEER 0–3: confirm the NOC code is accurate and start tracking your 1-year eligibility clock — ensure you maintain consistent hours and document employment
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If TEER 4–5: urgently evaluate whether a parallel or alternative TEER 0–3 opportunity is available; every month in an ineligible role delays PR

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you genuinely willing and able to settle in the province you are considering for PNP nomination?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is pursuing a specific PNP stream for legitimate settlement reasons or purely for immigration arbitrage — the latter carries integrity risk
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with PNP application and document genuine ties (employer, housing, community) to strengthen the intention to settle
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Counsel the student on the risks of misrepresenting settlement intention; redirect to streams aligned with where they genuinely plan to live

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you had any prior F-1 status violations, SEVIS terminations, or reinstatement applications in your history?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a new reinstatement application faces a higher bar — USCIS denies reinstatement for students with a record of repeated or willful violations
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Consult an immigration attorney before filing — the prior history must be addressed and the current violation must be clearly distinguishable as non-willful
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Proceed with standard reinstatement process if within the 5-month window; document that the current violation was inadvertent

diagnostic_questions.question: Is the internship or co-op you are considering required by your degree program and does it award course credit?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether CPT is the appropriate authorization mechanism or whether the student is attempting to use CPT for an experience that does not meet eligibility requirements
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes: confirm DSO authorization and I-20 endorsement are in place before the first day of work
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: CPT may not be available; explore OPT pre-completion authorization or whether the program can be restructured to qualify

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you planning to transfer to a new school while your OPT is still active?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is about to unknowingly terminate their work authorization by setting a SEVIS transfer release date
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Advise student to delay setting the transfer release date until after OPT end date; connect them with DSO to coordinate timing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm student understands the rule for future planning; if they are considering future enrollment, document the OPT end date for reference

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you ever been out of status — even briefly — at any point during your studies, and did you take formal steps to resolve it at the time?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether unresolved historical violations are sitting in the SEVIS record that will surface during future immigration benefit applications
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Document the exact violation, duration, and resolution steps; if no formal resolution occurred, consult an attorney before filing any new immigration benefit
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Walk through each potential violation category systematically (enrollment gaps, address moves, work authorization) rather than relying on the student's general recollection

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you had a paid consultation with a qualified immigration attorney (not a peer, not a forum) in the last 12 months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has access to calibrated, situation-specific advice or is navigating a complex legal system using unreliable informal inputs
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What specific questions did you ask, and did they review your actual documents and timeline?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Prioritize a consultation before any next major decision; identify whether your situation has unusual elements that make peer advice especially unreliable

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you willing to remain in the US on non-immigrant status for 10+ years with no guaranteed permanent residence outcome?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Tests whether the student has honestly internalized the realistic timeline for India/China nationals — if not, alternative pathways (Canada, return home) should be seriously evaluated
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ensure they have AC21 portability plan, H-1B extension strategy beyond 6-year cap, and contingency plans for layoffs or company closures
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Canada Express Entry, Provincial Nominee Programs, or return-home planning should be explored as higher-probability paths to permanent residence

diagnostic_questions.question: If you are not selected in the H-1B lottery, do you have a viable alternative visa category (O-1, L-1, TN, E-3, J-1)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a lottery failure means leaving the US or whether you have a legitimate path to continued legal work authorization
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Begin preparing the alternative petition in parallel before lottery results so you can file immediately after a 'Not Selected' outcome
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Evaluate Canada immigration pathways (Express Entry, PGWP) or return-home career options as genuine contingency plans now, not after denial

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have peer-reviewed publications, formal awards, above-market salary documentation, or other evidence of recognized achievement in your field?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether an O-1 extraordinary ability petition may be viable — a year-round, cap-exempt alternative to H-1B that is more accessible than most students assume.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you had an immigration attorney conduct a structured O-1 criteria assessment against your documentation?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Are you building toward any of these credentials during your OPT period as a deliberate strategy to open the O-1 pathway before STEM OPT expires?

diagnostic_questions.question: Has the student used any pre-completion OPT hours, and if so how many?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: How much of the 12-month OPT total remains for post-completion use, directly affecting the runway available to secure H-1B sponsorship
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Calculate remaining post-completion OPT months and build the H-1B application timeline (especially lottery registration in March) around that expiration date
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Student has the full 12 months post-completion, providing maximum flexibility for job searching and employer evaluation

diagnostic_questions.question: Has this J-1 student previously used Academic Training at another institution or program level?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The student's remaining AT eligibility, since AT is counted cumulatively across all programs and institutions.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Subtract prior AT usage from the applicable maximum (18 months for undergrad/grad; 36 months for post-doctoral). Authorize only the remaining balance.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The student has the full maximum available, subject to program duration caps. Proceed with standard AT authorization process.

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you planning to travel outside Canada at any point between now and when your PGWP is approved?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is at risk of being stranded outside Canada due to an expired TRV or eTA during the PGWP processing period
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Check TRV and eTA validity in your passport right now — if either expires before your expected PGWP approval date, you must either cancel travel plans or accept the risk of extended time outside Canada
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No additional action needed on this dimension; focus on ensuring other eligibility criteria are met

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you taken your language test and confirmed CLB scores for all four skills individually — not just your overall score?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student actually meets the CLB minimum threshold for their TEER level in every single skill band
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If all four skills meet or exceed threshold: confirm which CLB level was reached and how it affects CRS points
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If any skill is below threshold or untested: the student is not yet Express Entry eligible for CEC — retesting or remediation is the priority

diagnostic_questions.question: How old are you, and how many months until you turn 30 (or how many years past 29 are you)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether age-based CRS point decay is creating urgency in the timing of profile submission or strategy selection
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If approaching 30, calculate net CRS impact of delaying vs. improving another factor; submit profile now if current score is near competitive even if not ideal
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If well under 29, the candidate is at maximum age points and timing is less critical; focus on building experience and language scores

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you enrolled in a transport, aviation, marine, agriculture, or environmental studies program?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is in a high-risk category where PGWP eligibility was removed or severely curtailed in June 2025
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes: immediately verify study permit submission date; if post-November 2024, consult an RCIC about alternative pathways including employer-specific work permits or program transfer
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: proceed with standard field-of-study and CIP code verification process

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your province of residence or work have an active PNP stream aligned to your NOC code?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Identifies whether the 600-point provincial nomination pathway is accessible, which would effectively guarantee an ITA regardless of base CRS score
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If active PNP stream exists: determine the minimum provincial score requirement and apply as soon as eligible — do not wait for a federal draw
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no active stream: monitor draw history of neighboring provinces and assess whether relocating is strategically worthwhile given the 600-point benefit

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have an active Express Entry profile in the pool, and is it linked to your PNP application for an enhanced nomination?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student understands the difference between enhanced and base nomination and whether they are positioned to receive the +600 CRS advantage
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ensure the profile is current, accurate, and the NOC code is consistent across Express Entry profile and PNP application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Create and submit Express Entry profile immediately; a PNP nomination without an active Express Entry profile forces a slower base nomination process

diagnostic_questions.question: Is this your final semester, and are the credits you are enrolled in literally all that remains to complete your degree?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student qualifies for the final semester RCL exception — this is an underused pathway that allows below-full-time enrollment without the other exception criteria
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm this with your DSO and have it authorized in SEVIS — you do not need medical documentation; the degree audit confirming remaining requirements is sufficient
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The final semester exception does not apply; assess which of the other 3 RCL exceptions, if any, fits the situation

diagnostic_questions.question: How many months of pre-completion OPT have you used, and how many months of post-completion OPT will remain?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student will have sufficient post-graduation OPT time to cover the period between graduation and the next H-1B cap selection and start date
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If sufficient time remains: confirm STEM extension eligibility to further extend the buffer if H-1B is not selected in first lottery
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If insufficient time: advise on CPT vs. deferring pre-OPT use and recalculate whether H-1B cap-gap can be maintained

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you used OPT at this degree level before?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has already exhausted OPT eligibility at their current degree level and whether a new degree enrollment could unlock additional eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Explore whether enrollment in a higher degree level is academically and strategically viable to access a fresh 12-month OPT
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Student retains full OPT eligibility at current degree level; ensure application timing is optimized

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you maintained full-time enrollment every semester, including any semester you dropped below full-time without a formal DSO-approved reduced course load authorization?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether an enrollment-based status violation exists; failure to maintain full-time enrollment is one of the primary F-1 and J-1 status termination triggers
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Determine whether the reduced enrollment was formally authorized; check DSO records for a reduced course load approval on file
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Identify the semester(s) in question, calculate the unlawful presence that may have accumulated, and assess reinstatement eligibility if within the 5-month window

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your spouse or partner have independent immigration status, or is their status derivative of yours?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a family dependency risk exists — if the student's status fails, the entire household's legal standing may fail simultaneously
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Does their independent status create additional pathway options (e.g., points contribution in Canada, alternative sponsorship route) that you have factored into your plan?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The student's immigration planning must account for household-level risk; a single failure point affects multiple people and raises the stakes of every decision

diagnostic_questions.question: Has an immigration attorney reviewed your NIW evidence package before filing?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: NIW approval rate is 54% and declining — attorney review is the most reliable predictor of whether the evidence package meets current USCIS scrutiny standards
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the attorney assessed against all three Dhanasar prongs, not just evidence quantity
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Strong recommendation not to file without review — denial costs 4+ months and filing fees, and a rebuttal or appeal is far more expensive

diagnostic_questions.question: Are multiple employers or staffing agencies submitting H-1B registrations on your behalf?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Risk of duplicate submission — under the beneficiary-centric rule, multiple registrations for the same person from the same employer are invalidated
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Clarify whether the submissions are from the same or different legal employers; same-employer duplicates result in denial, cross-employer duplicates are now prohibited under the one-entry-per-beneficiary rule
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No duplicate risk; focus on salary level optimization and registration timing

diagnostic_questions.question: Does any multinational company you are considering have offices in a country where you have existing work authorization or citizenship?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether an L-1 intracompany transfer strategy is viable — working abroad for 1 year at a qualifying multinational enables a US L-1 transfer without the H-1B lottery.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is the employer willing to structure an intentional abroad-first arrangement, and does the role qualify as managerial, executive, or specialized knowledge?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The L-1 pathway is not available; focus assessment on remaining alternatives.

diagnostic_questions.question: Did the student change employers during STEM OPT, and if so, was a new Form I-983 submitted and the DSO notified within 10 days?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is currently in SEVIS compliance or has an unreported violation that could trigger status termination
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm I-983 is fully signed by both student and supervisor and that new employer's E-Verify enrollment has been verified
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If more than 10 days have passed since the employer change without reporting, student should contact DSO immediately to assess remediation options

diagnostic_questions.question: Did this student physically report to the International Student Office and provide their I-94 within 15 days of arriving in the US?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student's SEVIS record has been properly activated and whether the student is in compliance with initial reporting requirements.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the SEVIS record has been activated. File all initial documents and ensure the student's address and status are correctly recorded.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Treat as urgent. Contact the student immediately, verify their I-94 status, and activate the SEVIS record before the 30-day program start deadline to prevent auto-termination.

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your spouse intend to apply for an open work permit based on your PGWP status?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the post-January 2025 spousal OWP restrictions apply and whether the family's dual-income plan is achievable
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What is your NOC code and TEER classification, and how many months remain on your PGWP? — both must meet new thresholds (TEER 0/1 or qualifying TEER 2/3, plus 16+ months remaining)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No action needed on spousal OWP; focus on primary PGWP application

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you intend to live in Quebec, or are you currently employed in Quebec?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is inadvertently ineligible for CEC (which requires intent to reside outside Quebec) and needs to navigate Quebec's separate immigration stream instead
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Redirect entirely to Quebec's skilled worker programs (QSWP); Express Entry CEC is not available; assess Arrima pool eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm the candidate understands CEC's Quebec exclusion and plans to live/work in another province; proceed with CEC strategy

diagnostic_questions.question: If you have a spouse or common-law partner, have they taken an IELTS/TEF and had their education credentials ECA-assessed?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Identifies uncaptured CRS points from spousal factors — a commonly overlooked source of 10–40 additional CRS points that requires only documentation effort, not changing any other circumstance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If tested and assessed: include spousal factors in CRS profile and verify score is calculated correctly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not tested: initiate IELTS/TEF registration and ECA process immediately — both take months and cannot be rushed when you are ready to file

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you reported your current address to your DSO within the past 15 days of any move?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is at risk of a DSO-triggered SEVIS termination due to administrative non-compliance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm records are current; no further action needed on this dimension
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Student must update address with DSO immediately; failure to do so is a mandatory SEVIS termination trigger

diagnostic_questions.question: If your primary employer withdrew their sponsorship tomorrow, what would you do within the next 60 days to maintain legal status?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has a real contingency plan or whether all their immigration security is concentrated in a single employer dependency
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you verified that the contingency pathway is actually accessible to you — not just theoretically possible?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: This is a critical single point of failure; develop and verify at least one alternative pathway before it becomes urgent

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your employer's track record with H-1B sponsorship — how many petitions have they filed and approved in the last 3 years?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the employer has institutional readiness — established immigration counsel, internal approval workflows, and experience navigating USCIS requirements — or whether the student is their first or rare case.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ask for the name of their immigration law firm and whether the same firm will handle your case.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The student should consider retaining independent immigration counsel to monitor the process and flag errors the employer's team may miss.

diagnostic_questions.question: Is this student planning to transfer to a new school while currently on OPT, and if so, when is the proposed transfer release date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student risks losing remaining OPT work authorization by setting the transfer release date before OPT ends.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the transfer release date is set after the OPT end date. If not, work with the student to delay the transfer release date or reconsider the transfer timing.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No immediate OPT risk. Proceed with standard transfer planning.

diagnostic_questions.question: If you completed your highest degree outside Canada, has it been assessed by an IRCC-designated ECA organization?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Unassessed foreign credentials receive no CRS education points; this is a correctable gap but requires 3–6 months to process and cannot be backdated after filing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If ECA complete: confirm it aligns with the Canadian credential level you are claiming in your Express Entry profile
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If ECA not started: begin immediately through WES, ICAS, or another designated body — do not wait until you are otherwise ready to file

diagnostic_questions.question: If you accept a J-1 position, does your specific J-1 program category carry the 2-year home country residency requirement?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether accepting the J-1 will block future H-1B or green card applications for 2 years unless a waiver is obtained — a potentially deal-breaking constraint for students targeting long-term US residency.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Are you prepared to pursue a J-1 waiver, and do you understand the grounds and processing time involved?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm this in writing with an immigration attorney before accepting the offer, as program sponsor categorization is not always transparently communicated.

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current age, and how old will you be at your projected Express Entry application date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: CRS age points peak at 20–29 and decline after 30, accelerating after 35 — a 2-year delay in application timing can cost 15–30+ irrecoverable CRS points, changing whether an ITA is achievable
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If under 29 at application: age is not yet a constraint — focus on other levers; model whether rushing the timeline sacrifices other score components
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If 30 or older, or approaching 30: treat application timing as urgent; each additional year of delay has a compounding negative effect on CRS score

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you or your spouse taken a French language test (TEF Canada or TCF Canada)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the applicant has explored the single highest-ROI CRS lever available to most applicants without employer or government involvement
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What score did you achieve — did you reach CLB 7 in all four components?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: What is your estimated current French level, and have you researched what CLB 7 requires on TEF Canada?
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: How long was your Canadian study program — specifically, was it under 1 year, exactly 1 year, between 1 and 2 years, or 2 years or more?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: This determines the maximum PGWP duration you are eligible for, which affects your entire post-graduation immigration strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: you qualify for a 3-year PGWP — plan your Express Entry or PNP timeline accordingly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If under 2 years: PGWP duration is capped at program length or 1 year — assess whether this is sufficient for your immigration pathway or whether bridging options are needed
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the official duration of your current program vs. the program you want to switch to?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the switch would reduce PGWP duration — the single most important variable in assessing the immigration cost of a program change
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If both are 2+ years: PGWP impact is likely zero — explore whether the switch makes sense on other grounds
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If switching from 2-year to 1-year: quantify the PGWP loss and model the PR timeline impact before proceeding
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your country of birth and which EB category would your US employer sponsor you for?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a US green card is realistically attainable within your career, or whether you face a multi-decade wait
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If India or China EB-2/EB-3: look up current priority date and calculate realistic wait; if it exceeds 10-15 years, the Canada path warrants serious consideration
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not India or China: US green card wait is likely manageable; weigh salary and career factors more heavily
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your resume currently include a visible work authorization line clarifying that no sponsorship is required?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is proactively removing the most common HR-level filter that kills PGWP applications before they reach a hiring manager
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is it placed near the top where a recruiter will see it in the first scan?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Add 'Authorized to work in Canada (Open Work Permit – no sponsorship required)' near the top before the next batch of applications
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your country of birth, and have you checked the current EB priority date for your category?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether PERM-based EB2/EB3 is viable on a reasonable timeline or effectively closed, which changes which alternative paths are worth pursuing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If non-Indian and EB2 is current or near-current, EB1A self-petition or employer-sponsored EB2 with PERM is viable — compare to L1 timeline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If Indian national, PERM-based paths are likely decade-long waits; focus evaluation on EB1A (no PERM required) or Canada PR as primary alternatives

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your research appear in citations by federal agencies or institutions across multiple U.S. states?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether you can satisfy Dhanasar prong 2 (national in scope) with direct evidence rather than inference
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Document each instance with screenshots and source URLs; identify the federal or state entity involved
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Explore indirect evidence: policy documents that cite your field, media coverage, or testimony/conference appearances before national bodies
diagnostic_questions._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you researched your Canadian immigration options (PGWP, Express Entry) in enough detail to know your approximate CRS score or eligibility window?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has a real Plan B or only a theoretical one; also reveals how much runway they have under Canadian pathways
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Does your current employer have a Canadian office, and have you discussed transfer options with HR?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: How many H-1B lottery attempts do you have remaining before your authorized status expires? This determines how urgently you need to build that plan.
diagnostic_questions._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Does the school's curriculum documentation explicitly require the internship/work as an integral component of the degree program — or is the CPT authorization effectively available to any enrollee from day one regardless of coursework?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the CPT authorization has a defensible academic basis that would withstand USCIS scrutiny
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Has an independent attorney reviewed the specific program documentation and confirmed it meets CPT regulatory requirements?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The CPT authorization is likely vulnerable to USCIS challenge; consider whether the downstream risk is acceptable given your long-term immigration goals
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have any existing relationships with university professors or nonprofit research institutions who could sponsor a genuine part-time research role?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the cap-exempt route is practically accessible or requires cold outreach (which is harder and slower)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is the professor willing and does their institution have an immigration office that handles H-1B sponsorships? What is the realistic timeline to formalize the arrangement?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Are there DOE national labs or nonprofit research orgs in your field where you could establish a legitimate research collaboration? Have you considered O-1 as a parallel track?
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: How many total unemployment days have you accumulated since your OPT start date, across all job gaps combined?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person is at risk of exceeding the 90-day cumulative cap, and how much buffer they actually have
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If close to 90 days, ask: do you have a confirmed offer with a start date already secured?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If far from 90 days, standard job-change process applies; focus on DSO notification timing
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your spouse or common-law partner taken a recognized language test (IELTS, CELPIP) for Express Entry purposes?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether up to 20 CRS points are being left on the table from the spouse language score component
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What CLB level did they achieve? CLB 7+ is the key threshold for meaningful contribution.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Prioritize spouse language testing before other optimization tactics — it is likely the lowest-effort high-return action available.

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the current EB-2 priority date for your country of origin, and how does that compare to when you entered the US workforce?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a US green card is realistically obtainable within the person's working career — for Indian nationals, the answer often makes the US path untenable
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If the date is within 5-7 years: assess whether H-1B stability is sufficient to wait it out, factoring in job change risk and political changes to the program
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If the date is 20+ years out: treat the green card as functionally unavailable and reframe the decision as permanent residency in Canada vs. indefinite temporary status in the US

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your work permit open (PGWP) or employer-specific?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether your perceived constraint on changing jobs is real or anxiety-driven; PGWP holders have full freedom to change employers
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If open permit: what is the actual immigration risk of taking the new opportunity? It is likely near zero.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If employer-specific: consult an RCIC about bridge work permits or open permit pathways before declining opportunities

diagnostic_questions.question: What date were your final marks or official transcript issued?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: This is the start of the 180-day PGWP application window — the most critical deadline in the entire process
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Calculate day 180 from that date and confirm the application will be submitted before then
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If transcript has not yet been issued, determine the expected issue date and plan application submission accordingly

diagnostic_questions.question: Which service center was assigned to your I-765, and are you checking that center's specific processing time estimate?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the applicant is benchmarking against the correct center's data; reveals if confusion between school address and mailing address is distorting their expectations
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: How does your actual pending time compare to that center's current published estimate?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Identify your school's state to determine correct center assignment, then check that center's published times on USCIS.gov

diagnostic_questions.question: What will the length of the program you actually complete be — not the program you started, but the one you will finish?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines PGWP eligibility length, which drives the entire post-graduation immigration timeline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If it's 2+ years, confirm it's at a DLI and you will complete it without switching
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If shorter than expected (due to switching), calculate the PGWP loss and model the PR timeline impact before committing

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your resume and outreach explicitly state 'open work permit — no sponsorship required' in a visible location?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is pre-empting the most common filter Canadian employers apply to permit holders
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Does it also clarify you are pursuing PR independently, so employers don't worry about continuity?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Rewrite immediately — this is likely the cause of silent rejections even before your qualifications are evaluated

diagnostic_questions.question: When a company says 'no sponsorship', have you asked whether that excludes STEM OPT extension paperwork?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has actually been disqualified or has simply accepted an ambiguous recruiter statement at face value
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What specifically did they say was excluded — H-1B only, or all immigration paperwork including I-983?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Call back or email the recruiter to clarify; you may still be eligible to work there on STEM OPT for up to 3 years

diagnostic_questions.question: Is the H-1B sponsorship commitment documented anywhere in writing — offer letter, email, or messaging platforms like Slack?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has any legal or practical leverage against the employer, and whether a promissory estoppel claim is worth exploring
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What is the specificity of the language? Does it name a timeline or cap season? Collect and preserve all documentation immediately.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Verbal promises are not enforceable; shift focus to new employer search and calculate remaining OPT runway against next cap season.

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the program end date on your I-20, and what is the expiration date on your OPT EAD card — are they the same?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is correctly identifying when the 60-day grace period clock starts; many students assume it's the EAD expiration date
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm that the grace period begins on that shared date and no work is authorized after that point
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Determine which date is earlier and explain that the grace period begins from the I-20 program end date, not the EAD card expiration

diagnostic_questions.question: How many total weeks of full-time CPT (20+ hours/week) have you completed across all employers and semesters?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is at risk of crossing or has already crossed the 12-month OPT-elimination threshold
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If total is close to or over 52 weeks, immediately consult DSO and an immigration attorney before any further CPT authorization
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If well under 12 months, calculate remaining headroom and establish a hard personal cutoff date for future CPT

diagnostic_questions.question: Did the school require any prior academic enrollment or credit transfer as a condition for Day 1 CPT, or was authorization purely based on same-day enrollment?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether there is a documented academic basis for the CPT authorization that might withstand USCIS scrutiny versus a purely enrollment-triggered authorization with no prior history
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Obtain and preserve all documentation of the credit transfer or prior enrollment; consult an immigration attorney about how to represent this in future petitions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The risk profile is higher; consult an immigration attorney immediately about disclosure strategy and downstream H-1B or green card implications

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your employer have an affiliated nonprofit, research entity, or university partnership that qualifies as cap-exempt?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a cap-exempt H-1B bridge is available, allowing work authorization outside the lottery
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Has HR or legal confirmed that the affiliated entity meets the USCIS primary purpose test for cap-exemption?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Are there other cap-exempt employers in your field you could transfer to while maintaining employment in a related role?

diagnostic_questions.question: Is the entity filing the H-1B petition primarily a research organization, or does it primarily provide services (clinical care, commercial products, etc.) with research as a secondary activity?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the sponsoring entity actually meets the cap-exempt 'primary purpose' test under USCIS criteria
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the entity is also nonprofit or government; then assess whether the applicant's role involves genuine research contribution
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The entity likely does not qualify as cap-exempt regardless of university affiliation; explore other qualifying partners or alternative visa strategies

diagnostic_questions.question: If you did not get selected in the H-1B lottery this year, what would you actually do — and how do you feel about that option?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has a genuinely acceptable backup plan or is operating on a single-path dependency that will cause disproportionate distress on a loss
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you actively researched and emotionally engaged with that option, or is it theoretical?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: What is preventing you from building a real backup plan now, before results come out?

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your country of birth, and have you checked the current USCIS Visa Bulletin priority date for your EB category?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether US GC is a realistic multi-year goal or a multi-decade wait that makes Canada Express Entry categorically more rational
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: How many years is the current wait for your category and birth country — and does that fit within your life planning horizon?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Check immediately; this single variable may change your entire country strategy

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your employer enrolled in E-Verify, and can you confirm their enrollment before you file?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the foundational eligibility requirement for STEM OPT is met — without E-Verify enrollment, the extension cannot be approved regardless of other factors
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm when they enrolled — enrollment must be active at time of filing and approval
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The employer must enroll in E-Verify before the STEM OPT application can proceed; assess whether they are willing and able to do so in time

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your original enrollment date at your Canadian institution?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is protected by grandfathering under the 2024-11-01 PGWP policy cutoff, regardless of any subsequent program changes
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If enrolled before November 1, 2024: confirm full-time status has been maintained continuously; then advise to document and apply early
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If enrolled after November 1, 2024: assess whether the specific program is on IRCC's current eligible program list before advising further

diagnostic_questions.question: How many cumulative days have you been unemployed since your OPT start date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person is approaching or has exceeded the 90-day cumulative unemployment cap
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If over 60 days: How quickly is your next job starting? Do you have documentation of active job search?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If under 30 days: You have meaningful buffer remaining — but track every future gap carefully
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: How many H-1B lottery attempts do you have remaining on STEM OPT?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines urgency and which paths are still viable within the current status window
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 1-2 remain: assess whether parallel-tracking Canada PR or NIW filing now is feasible alongside remaining attempts
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If zero remain: pivot immediately to cap-exempt employer, O-1, NIW, or Canada; no more time to defer
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score, and what has been the cutoff in recent draws for your NOC category?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is realistically competitive for a near-term federal draw or needs an alternative strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If within 50 points: which single factor (French, spousal language, additional work experience) can close the gap fastest?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If more than 50 points below: have you investigated PNP streams for your NOC code and province of residence?
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: 你的项目是几年制的？
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: 直接决定PGWP时长，进而决定你有多少时间积累工作经验、等待ITA、并完成PR申请
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: 若2年及以上：3年PGWP，时间充裕，可以按标准路径规划
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: 若1年：PGWP仅1年，需立即评估是否应转向PNP或其他加速路径
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your realistic estimated green card receipt date, and how old will your parents be at that point?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the US path even permits parental sponsorship within a meaningful timeframe for the person's family situation
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If green card is 10+ years away, are you comfortable with parents being 70+ before sponsorship begins? Have you modeled Canada as an alternative?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If green card is within 3–5 years, calculate IR-5 processing time (~1 year post-citizenship) and assess whether that total timeline is acceptable
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your primary optimization target: maximizing career earnings ceiling or achieving immigration status certainty as early as possible?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: These two goals currently map to different countries — clarifying priority eliminates the false premise that one path dominates the other on all dimensions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If career ceiling: Are you in a field (finance, top-tier tech) where the US salary premium is large enough to justify 5–10 years of status uncertainty?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If status certainty: Do you have factors that boost your CRS score (French, provincial nomination, Canadian work experience) to make the Canada timeline predictable?
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your country of birth (not citizenship)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines which per-country quota applies and which Visa Bulletin cutoff dates govern your wait time — the single most important variable in green card timeline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If India or China: immediately model EB-1 vs EB-2 priority date gap and assess EB-1A/EB-1B/NIW eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If neither India nor China: standard EB-2/EB-3 timelines apply and the backlog is dramatically shorter — different strategic calculus
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your occupation's NOC code, and has IRCC run a category-based draw for that category in the past 12 months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the applicant is eligible for category draws with lower CRS cutoffs, which may be their most realistic pathway to an ITA
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What was the CRS cutoff in those draws, and how far is your current score from that threshold?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You may be waiting for an all-program draw where cutoffs for your background may be 530+; consider whether province-specific streams are available
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already submitted biometrics to IRCC during a prior permit or visa application?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether a biometric appointment will add processing time to your PGWP application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm biometrics are still valid (10-year validity); expect faster processing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Budget additional time and cost for biometric enrollment; locate the nearest collection site in advance
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What PR pathway are you planning to use after graduation?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether PGWP duration is actually critical — students using employer sponsorship or PNP streams may be less affected by a shorter PGWP than Express Entry candidates
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If Express Entry via Canadian work experience: PGWP duration is high-stakes; do not reduce it without fully understanding the CRS score impact
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If employer sponsorship or PNP: assess whether those pathways are realistically accessible before discounting PGWP duration as a concern
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: After getting Canadian PR, do you plan to work in the US? Have you checked the PR residency obligation?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person understands that Canadian PR has a physical presence requirement (730 days in 5 years) that can be violated by long-term US work
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If planning extended US work: consult an immigration lawyer about maintaining PR status; do not assume PR is a passive 'backup'
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If staying in Canada: residency obligation is straightforward to meet
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have any Canadian work experience (co-op, internship, part-time) to list on your resume?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate will face the 'Canadian experience' filter that multiple commenters identified as a real and recurring barrier
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ensure it is prominently framed in Canadian context with Canadian company names and location visible
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Explore whether volunteer work, school projects with Canadian organizations, or part-time employment can be positioned as Canadian experience proxies
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your employer explicitly committed to filing I-140 during your L1 period, and by what date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Distinguishes employers using L1 as a genuine green card bridge from those using it as a retention mechanism with no green card intent
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Validate that the I-140 category and timeline fits within your L1-B 5-year window with margin
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Treat the L1 as a visa-only solution, not a green card path; evaluate independent routes (EB1A self-petition, Canada PR) in parallel

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your country of birth's current EB-2 priority date on the Visa Bulletin?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether I-140 approval translates into a realistic near-term green card or simply preserves a priority date for a multi-year wait
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If current, assess whether to pursue adjustment of status or consular processing timeline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If severely backlogged (China, India), evaluate whether NIW is worth pursuing now vs. later, and whether concurrent H-1B or other status management is needed
diagnostic_questions._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you making career or life decisions differently because of visa uncertainty — avoiding visibility, relationships, or long-term commitments?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person is in the anticipatory withdrawal pattern; if yes, they are likely compounding the harm of visa uncertainty beyond what is necessary
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is the behavior actually protecting you, or is it reducing the quality of your current life and your chances of a good outcome in any scenario?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: How are you managing the psychological pressure of the March-May lottery window? What coping mechanisms are in place?
diagnostic_questions._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What are your long-term US immigration goals — do you plan to apply for H-1B, green card, or other status that requires USCIS review of your full immigration history?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: How much compounding risk a questionable CPT authorization creates; the longer the intended stay, the more exposure points exist
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Any CPT irregularity will be reviewable at each future application stage; the risk does not expire after the CPT period ends
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Risk is lower but still present if you ever re-enter the US or apply for any visa; consult an attorney about your specific situation
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: How much time remains on your STEM OPT, and has your primary employer explicitly committed to or withdrawn from continued H-1B sponsorship?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The urgency of the situation and whether the primary employer is still a viable path or whether alternative routes must be pursued immediately
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If employer is still willing: is there time to attempt one more lottery cycle while simultaneously building a cap-exempt backup?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If employer has withdrawn: how many months remain on STEM OPT, and can a cap-exempt petition be filed and approved via premium processing within that window?
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your new employer enrolled in E-Verify?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether STEM OPT employment is valid at all; a non-enrolled employer disqualifies STEM OPT authorization
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Has the I-983 Training Plan been signed and submitted before your start date?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do not begin work; require E-Verify enrollment before your start date or reconsider the offer
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current English language score, and are you above CLB 10?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether further English investment has diminishing returns and French bilingual route should be prioritized instead
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Stop investing in English retests; redirect time and resources to French (TEF Canada B1) for ~50 additional points.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Bring English to CLB 10 first, then evaluate French as the next lever.

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your role qualify as TEER 1 (or equivalent) for Express Entry purposes?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether you are eligible for the Federal Skilled Worker or Canadian Experience Class streams — eligibility gatekeeping is a non-obvious prerequisite that many candidates overlook
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed to CRS score calculation and draw cutoff comparison
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess PNP pathways that may have different NOC/TEER eligibility criteria, or identify whether a job change could qualify your role

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you concretely researched what your Plan B (returning home) would actually look like?
