diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether your Plan B fear is based on reality or an unchallenged catastrophic narrative; the answer predicts how much desperation is distorting your current decisions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If researched: is it actually catastrophic, or merely different? Use the realistic picture to recalibrate your risk tolerance.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not researched: the fear is likely inflated — spend time concretizing the scenario before making any major immigration-driven sacrifices

diagnostic_questions.question: Was your program a co-op or work-integrated learning program, and does your completion letter reflect the full program duration including co-op months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the PGWP will reflect the full integrated program length or only the academic component — directly affecting permit duration
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the registrar letter explicitly names the full program duration including co-op
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Request clarification from the registrar and potentially from IRCC on how co-op months are counted

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your DSO submitted a formal inquiry or congressional inquiry on your behalf, or have you only contacted USCIS directly?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the applicant has exhausted the most effective escalation channels; DSO and congressional inquiry routes are distinct from and more effective than direct USCIS contact center inquiries
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: How long ago was the DSO inquiry submitted, and has there been any case movement since?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Prompt immediate DSO engagement, especially if case is severely delayed

diagnostic_questions.question: Can the academic problem you're trying to solve be fixed within your current program without a formal program change?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether a program switch is actually necessary or whether internal program changes (supervisor, focus, track) can achieve the same outcome
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Pursue the internal change; consult graduate advisor and DSO to ensure it doesn't trigger a program change in IRCC records
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If a switch is unavoidable, calculate full PGWP impact and explore whether a program of equivalent or greater length is available as an alternative

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you attended any Canadian in-person tech meetups or local events in the past 4 weeks?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is building the local network that drives the 15x referral response rate advantage
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you explicitly asked contacts to refer you internally? Passive networking is not the same as referral generation.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Cold application strategy alone is unlikely to succeed — local event attendance is the highest-leverage activity per hour for Canadian tech job search

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you on OPT right now, or have you not yet started OPT?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate currently has independent work authorization or is approaching a gap period that changes urgency and framing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: You can answer work authorization questions with 'yes' confidently; sponsorship is only relevant for H-1B conversation
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Timeline to OPT start date and any CPT usage become critical factors that affect how you frame authorization with recruiters

diagnostic_questions.question: How many months of OPT remain, and does that runway overlap with the next H-1B cap registration window (typically early March)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has time to find a new sponsor and file in time, or whether they need to consider bridge strategies like F-1 re-enrollment or cap-exempt employers
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Prioritize job search immediately; target offers by November-December to allow time for filing preparation.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Evaluate cap-exempt employer bridge, F-1 bridge, or other visa categories as interim solutions.

diagnostic_questions.question: How many total days have you been unemployed across your entire OPT period — not just in this current gap?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is at risk of exceeding the 90-day aggregate unemployment limit without knowing it
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If approaching or past 90 days aggregate, advise immediate consultation with a licensed immigration attorney
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Remind them that the counter is cumulative — all future gaps will add to this total

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the exact expiration date on your OPT EAD card, and does it fall before April 1?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether cap-gap protection is available to the student if they enter the H-1B lottery
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If EAD expires before April 1, cap-gap is not available — student must explore alternatives (change of status, departure, cap-exempt employers)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If EAD is valid through April 1 or later, cap-gap protection is available as long as the petition is timely filed

diagnostic_questions.question: Has the student's Day 1 CPT school received any SEVP audit notices or enforcement actions?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether there is an active institutional risk that could strand the student mid-program or flag their SEVIS record
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Begin immediate contingency planning: identify transfer schools, understand grace period rules, consult an attorney
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: SEVP status can change rapidly; continue monitoring and do not assume current good standing is permanent

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your employer successfully filed and received H-1B approvals for other employees before?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the employer has institutional competence and willingness to manage the H-1B process correctly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Which law firm did they use, and do they have an immigration specialist on retainer?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: What is the employer's contingency plan if the petition is denied or the employee loses the lottery?

diagnostic_questions.question: Has the applicant consulted an immigration attorney specifically about the dual-employment cap-exempt structure, or are they planning to DIY or rely on HR?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the arrangement will be properly structured to withstand USCIS scrutiny
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with attorney guidance; ensure the attorney has specific experience with cap-exempt dual-employer petitions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Flag this as a high-risk gap immediately; the post explicitly identifies this as not a DIY situation

diagnostic_questions.question: How are you managing the March–May waiting period emotionally?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has identified the waiting period as a distinct high-risk anxiety window and prepared for it, or is treating it as a neutral administrative process
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What specific practices or support structures are in place during that window?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Consider that multiple experienced commenters identify the waiting period as more psychologically damaging than the outcome itself — what support can you put in place proactively?

diagnostic_questions.question: How many H-1B lottery cycles have you already lost?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: How much STEM OPT time has been consumed and what window remains before forced departure
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ losses: how many years of STEM OPT remain, and do you have an active Canada or other country contingency in motion?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: First loss: still time to build a parallel strategy; don't wait for a third loss to start planning

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your employer processed an I-983 for a STEM OPT employee before?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The likelihood that HR will fill out the form correctly without additional guidance — first-time employers are a high-risk scenario
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Still have your DSO review the completed I-983 before submission
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Treat DSO or immigration attorney review of the I-983 as mandatory, not optional

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your program (the one you will graduate from) currently appear on IRCC's eligible program list?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student faces a PGWP duration cap of 2 years, or no PGWP eligibility at all, under the post-2024 framework
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the list version being checked is current (list changed significantly in June 2025); check for your specific institution's offering
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess grandfathering protection; if not grandfathered, evaluate alternative pathways (PNP, in-Canada work experience accumulation, returning to eligible program)

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your degree program on the official DHS STEM Designated Degree Program List?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person is actually eligible for STEM OPT or is assuming eligibility based on the field name alone
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed to confirm employer E-Verify status
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: STEM OPT extension is not available; plan H-1B strategy around standard 12-month OPT only
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have documented evidence of extraordinary ability (citations, awards, media, peer review, high relative salary)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines O-1 and EB-1A eligibility, which are the most powerful lottery-independent US paths
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Consult an immigration attorney about O-1 or EB-1A; these can move quickly if evidence is strong
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do not count on these paths in the short term; prioritize cap-exempt employer, NIW, or Canada instead
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you taken a French language test, or assessed your current French proficiency level?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has explored the highest-ROI controllable score lever available to most applicants
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes and CLB 7+ achieved: the +50 points is already captured; move to next factor
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: estimate time and cost to reach CLB 7; for most Mandarin-speaking candidates this is 3–6 months at manageable cost
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: 你当前或目标职位的NOC TEER等级是多少？
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: 决定工作经验是否计入CEC资格，以及是否有资格获得Job Offer加分
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: 若TEER 0/1：可享受Job Offer加分（50-200分），CEC资格最强
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: 若TEER 4/5：工作经验完全不计入CEC，必须转换职位类别
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you investigated Canada's Super Visa as a near-term parental co-residence option?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person is aware of a relatively fast (3–8 month processing), flexible mechanism that allows parents to legally reside in Canada for up to 2 years per entry
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you begun the application? Does the parent meet health insurance and financial requirements?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: This should be evaluated immediately if parental co-residence in the next 1–3 years is a priority
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have a realistic path to EB-1A, EB-1B, or NIW?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: If yes, the US green card path compresses from 5–10 years to potentially 1–3 years, which fundamentally changes the uncertainty calculus
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you had an attorney evaluate your record specifically against EB-1/NIW criteria, or are you estimating based on general impressions?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Are you prepared to operate under EB-2 backlog uncertainty for the duration — and does your employer have a track record of sponsoring employees through full green card completion?
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have peer-reviewed publications, citations, awards, or evidence of being in the top percentage of your field?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether EB-1B (Outstanding Researcher) or EB-1A (Extraordinary Ability) pathways are viable, which have materially shorter backlogs for China and India born applicants
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Consult an immigration attorney specifically about EB-1A self-petition and EB-1B — do not default to EB-2 without exploring these first
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: EB-2 employer-sponsored or EB-2 NIW (if specialty qualifies) may be the primary path — model realistic wait time against personal life timeline
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your work experience through a direct employer or an agency/staffing firm?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the work experience claimed for CRS purposes meets IRCC's eligibility criteria — agency arrangements may have different qualification rules
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you verified with an immigration consultant that this arrangement qualifies as 'skilled work experience' under Express Entry definitions?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Direct employment is generally straightforward to document; focus on ensuring your T4s and employment records are in order
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your registrar's office issued a formal graduation confirmation letter that explicitly states your degree name and completion date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether your documentation is IRCC-compliant; unofficial transcripts or degree certificates alone are insufficient
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with application; verify the letter uses the exact degree name as on your study permit
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Request this letter immediately — processing time at registrar's office can consume weeks of your 180-day window
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you explored whether you can change research direction or specialization within your current program without formally switching programs?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has exhausted internal flexibility before accepting the immigration cost of a formal program switch
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes and it's not possible: document this and then model the PGWP impact of switching
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not explored: this should be the first step — the post author resolved their situation this way
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already started H-1B transfer processing at the new employer before leaving the old one?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Under portability rules, a worker may be able to start at the new employer once transfer is filed (not approved) — but many workers don't know this and wait unnecessarily or miss the 60-day window
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If transfer is filed: confirm with an immigration attorney whether you can start work under portability
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not filed: ensure you file before Day 60 of the grace period; do not wait for approval to start
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the size of the companies you are primarily targeting?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Predicts how much HR education about PGWP will be required and how likely implicit 'PR/citizen preferred' filtering may occur
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If large: less friction expected on status; focus on technical and cultural fit
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If small/mid: prepare a clear verbal PGWP explanation and consider whether their job postings contain citizenship/PR language that signals they may not hire on work permits
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have publications, open-source contributions, speaking invitations, or documented peer recognition in your field?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether EB1A self-petition is worth a serious evaluation — three of the ten USCIS criteria met is sufficient
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Consult an EB1A-specialized attorney for a credential audit; the PERM bypass alone could save 2-4 years
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Focus on building qualifying criteria proactively (peer review, open source, media citations) if EB1A is a medium-term goal

diagnostic_questions.question: Of your planned recommenders, how many have no co-authorship or direct collaboration with you?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The strength of your external recognition evidence — independent recommenders carry more weight than collaborators
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If majority independent, proceed; coach each recommender to speak to national impact, not just personal qualities
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If mostly collaborators, actively seek independent experts who have cited your work or reviewed it, even without personal relationship
diagnostic_questions._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already resigned, or do you still have your current job?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether unemployment days are already accumulating; determines urgency of finding next role
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Calculate days elapsed since resignation; if approaching 90 cumulative, contact DSO immediately
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Good position — secure offer first before resigning to minimize gap
diagnostic_questions._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you explored category-based or sector-specific Express Entry draws relevant to your NOC code?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether applicant is only tracking all-program draw cutoffs and missing lower-cutoff draw opportunities in their field
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Are you monitoring IRCC announcements specifically for your category? Tech-specific draws may clear below 490.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Begin tracking category-specific draws; all-program cutoffs may not be the relevant benchmark for your profile.

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you planning to maintain Canadian PR status while spending significant time working in the US?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person understands Canadian PR residency obligation requirements — PR holders must spend 730 days in Canada within each 5-year period or risk losing status
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Assess whether the planned US work period would breach the residency obligation and whether Canadian citizenship should be prioritized first before taking US roles
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No immediate concern, but flag this as a future planning issue if US opportunities arise post-PR

diagnostic_questions.question: Were you physically present in Canada for your final semester, or did you complete it remotely from abroad?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: May affect PGWP eligibility — IRCC has specific rules about remote-learning portions and physical presence requirements
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Standard PGWP application process likely applies
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Consult IRCC guidelines or a regulated immigration consultant before applying, as remote-completion rules have evolved

diagnostic_questions.question: How many days before your needed work start date is your EAD still pending?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Urgency level and whether escalation is warranted; 30+ days before needed start is the threshold for active escalation per community consensus
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Initiate dual escalation: USCIS contact center + DSO inquiry immediately
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Continue monitoring but prepare DSO inquiry materials in advance

diagnostic_questions.question: If you are stacking two programs, are they consecutive with no gap between completion of one and start of the next?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether IRCC will combine the program lengths for PGWP calculation or treat only the final program as the basis
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Verify with IRCC guidance what gap length, if any, is permissible — a standard April-to-September academic gap may or may not qualify
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assume the combined calculation may not apply and plan PGWP length based on the final program alone

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you targeting companies with 50–200 employees, or focusing primarily on large brand-name employers?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate's company targeting strategy accounts for applicant volume dynamics in Canadian tech
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Are those mid-size companies in Toronto/Waterloo corridor or distributed — Canadian office presence matters for work permit validity
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Large Canadian tech companies are flooded; mid-size companies offer meaningfully higher response rates for first Canadian role

diagnostic_questions.question: Has the recruiter asked 'will you now or in the future require sponsorship?' or only 'are you authorized to work?'
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the recruiter is asking about current authorization (where OPT = yes) or future H-1B need (where honesty about future sponsorship is required)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Be honest about future H-1B need while clearly distinguishing it from current OPT authorization
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You are not obligated to volunteer future sponsorship needs until the question is asked or you reach final/offer stage

diagnostic_questions.question: Does the person qualify for any alternative visa category — L-1, O-1, or marriage-based?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether there are realistic fallback pathways beyond H-1B cap or F-1 bridge
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Consult an immigration attorney immediately to assess eligibility timeline and filing requirements.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Options narrow to new H-1B sponsor, cap-exempt bridge employer, or F-1 re-enrollment; do not waste time planning around inaccessible pathways.

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you on standard post-completion OPT or on a STEM OPT extension?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Which unemployment day limit applies — 90 days for standard OPT, 150 days for STEM OPT
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If STEM OPT, confirm they have up to 150 aggregate days but that the E-Verify employer requirement still applies
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If standard OPT, apply the 90-day aggregate limit strictly

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your current or prospective employer enrolled in E-Verify?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether STEM OPT extension is legally accessible at that employer
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm enrollment is active and employer is willing to complete the STEM OPT I-983 training plan
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: STEM OPT extension is not possible at this employer regardless of any other factor — evaluate whether to negotiate E-Verify enrollment or seek a different employer

diagnostic_questions.question: Is long-term US immigration — H-1B, green card, or permanent residency — part of the student's plan?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the downstream risk of retroactive unlawful presence or RFEs is material to their actual goals
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Day 1 CPT risk-reward is categorically unfavorable; explore alternatives like traditional CPT-eligible programs or OPT timing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Risk calculus changes, but student should still understand the consular interview flagging risk if they ever apply for a US visa in the future

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you hold a US master's degree, and was your H-1B registration submitted under the master's cap pool?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has been receiving both available lottery chances or only one
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the registration explicitly selected master's cap exemption, not just the general cap
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess whether a degree from a US-accredited institution qualifies under USCIS master's cap criteria

diagnostic_questions.question: How many H-1B lottery attempts has the applicant already made, and how much STEM OPT time remains?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The urgency level and whether there is sufficient time to structure and file a cap-exempt petition before status lapses
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If two or more losses with under 12 months of OPT remaining, treat cap-exempt exploration as urgent and begin attorney consultation immediately
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If only one loss with ample OPT remaining, cap-exempt strategy can be explored methodically alongside continued lottery participation

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the length of your Canadian graduate program?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Directly determines PGWP duration and therefore the runway available to gain Canadian work experience and apply for PR
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: you qualify for a 3-year PGWP — use that time aggressively to build CRS score
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If 1 year: 1-year PGWP is a very tight window; evaluate whether a PNP pathway or bridging program extension is available

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your legal employer the same entity as the company where you physically work and are supervised?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a staffing agency or consulting firm structure creates ambiguity about who must sign and submit the I-983
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Standard I-983 process applies
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Consult DSO or immigration attorney immediately — the agency vs. client I-983 responsibility question is unresolved in community guidance and requires expert input

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already graduated, and if so, when did you graduate relative to the most recent PGWP policy announcement?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether early application could capture eligibility under prior rules before the new policy fully takes effect
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If recently graduated and policy change is recent: advise immediate PGWP application to maximize chance of processing under prior rules
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If still enrolled: plan program completion timeline with awareness of upcoming policy review cycles

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your employer registered on E-Verify?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether STEM OPT is actually accessible at this employer, regardless of other eligibility factors
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with I-983 preparation and STEM OPT application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: STEM OPT cannot be used at this employer; evaluate whether employer will register or whether a different employer is needed
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you a Canadian or Mexican citizen?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: TN visa eligibility, which is quota-free and obtainable at the port of entry for eligible professions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Verify your occupation is on the TN eligible list and whether your employer qualifies; TN may be faster than any other path
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: TN is not available; assess other paths
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your accompanying spouse have language test results on file?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has captured the spousal language bonus (~20 CRS points) — a low-effort gain that is frequently overlooked
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the score meets CLB 7 and is submitted correctly in the EE profile
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess spouse's English or French level; if near CLB 7, this is a quick win worth prioritizing

diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: 判断联邦EE路径是否可行，还是应该直接规划PNP或法语加分策略
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: 若差距在50分以内：考虑法语、Job Offer或配偶语言加分填补缺口
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: 若差距超过80分：优先考虑Alberta/Saskatchewan PNP等分数要求更低的省提名项目

diagnostic_questions.question: Is parental reunion a 'nice to have' or a genuine life priority that should influence your country choice?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Clarifies whether the person should weight the parental reunion factor heavily in US vs. Canada path selection, or treat it as secondary
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Then Canada's structural advantages (Super Visa, faster PR path for some) should be explicitly modeled against US economic advantages
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Then optimize for career and immigration stability; parental reunion logistics can be handled reactively
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your 5–10 year family formation plan, and have you modeled childcare/parental leave costs in both countries?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The salary differential between the US and Canada looks very different before vs. after accounting for Canadian family benefits — the economic comparison changes materially for people planning to have children
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you run actual numbers on Canadian child benefit (CCB), Quebec/Ontario childcare subsidies, and parental leave replacement rates vs. US costs?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If family formation is not in your 5–10 year plan, raw salary differential is a more valid comparison metric
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: How stable is your current employer, and what happens to your green card petition if the company is acquired or closes?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Employer-sponsored green cards (EB-2, EB-3 via PERM) are tied to the employer; instability creates petition interruption risk that resets years of progress
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If employer is stable: proceed but build contingency awareness; if EB-1A or NIW viable, pursue in parallel as insurance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If employer is at risk: prioritize employer-independent pathways (EB-1A, NIW) or evaluate Canada PR as a decoupled alternative

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Redirect time investment to French language study rather than English re-testing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Improving English to CLB 10 is still a valid priority before pivoting to French

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your new role clearly align with your degree field of study?

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with standard DSO notification process
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Consult DSO before accepting the offer; do not assume the role qualifies

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you from a high-volume Express Entry country (India, China) and currently sitting between 460 and 480 CRS?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether standard profile optimization is sufficient or whether the applicant needs to pursue structural levers (French, job offer, PNP)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: French bilingual route and a qualifying job offer are likely the only meaningful paths to a competitive federal CRS; also evaluate PNP options.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Profile optimization may be sufficient; assess gap to current draw cutoffs before pursuing major structural changes.

diagnostic_questions.question: When does your current study permit expire relative to your expected PGWP approval date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a gap period exists in which the applicant will be on maintained status — and therefore unable to work
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Plan for a work-free period; do not accept employment during the gap without confirmed PGWP approval
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If study permit remains valid through expected approval, no gap risk exists

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your program changed its name or structure due to institutional restructuring — and does IRCC's records reflect the program you are actually completing?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: A mid-degree program restructuring could create ambiguity in IRCC's assessment of program length and eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Obtain documentation from the institution confirming the program's length and DLI status; consider proactively contacting IRCC or a regulated consultant
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No action needed on this dimension

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you considered a contract or agency role as an entry point to get Canadian work experience on your resume?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is aware of the 'backdoor' pathway that sidesteps the chicken-and-egg problem of needing Canadian experience to get Canadian experience
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is the contract role at a company or in a domain that leads to the type of permanent role you want?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Contract/agency roles are a known alternative entry path surfaced by the community — worth evaluating if direct search stalls past 10–12 weeks

diagnostic_questions.question: Is the work you're doing during a gap period freelance, consulting, or self-employment — and have you confirmed with an attorney whether that counts as 'employed' for OPT purposes?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is unknowingly accumulating unemployment days while believing freelance work keeps them compliant
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Flag this as an unresolved area requiring licensed immigration attorney guidance before assuming compliance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm that only authorized employment as defined by OPT terms counts against unemployment day calculations

diagnostic_questions.question: What SOC code is your employer planning to use for the H-1B filing, and does it match your actual job duties?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the filing is at risk of an RFE or denial due to vague or mismatched specialty occupation classification
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is there documented evidence — job description, salary benchmarks, degree requirements — supporting specialty occupation under that specific SOC code?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Have your attorney map your actual duties to the most precise applicable SOC code before filing

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you planning to ever live or work outside the US after obtaining a green card?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the US worldwide income tax obligation on GC holders is a material long-term financial risk for this person
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Factor in the tax implications of GC status vs. Canadian PR (Canada does not tax worldwide income in the same way) into your long-term financial model
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Tax issue is less relevant; focus on the PR timeline and salary trade-offs

diagnostic_questions.question: Does the I-983 contain specific project descriptions linking your degree field to your actual job duties?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the training plan is substantive enough to withstand USCIS scrutiny or is likely to trigger an RFE
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Review whether the NAICS code used matches the employer's actual industry classification
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Revise before submission — generic job descriptions without degree linkage are a primary RFE trigger

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you had an explicit, documented conversation with your employer about H-1B sponsorship?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has a real commitment or is operating on an assumption that could fail at the worst moment
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Has the employer confirmed they will cover premium processing costs?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Raise H-1B sponsorship immediately; do not wait — internal approvals take time

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your employer or a potential employer affiliated with a university, nonprofit research institution, or government research agency?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Cap-exempt H-1B eligibility, which removes the lottery entirely
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Work with a lawyer to structure the employment relationship correctly to qualify for cap-exempt filing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Cap-exempt path is not directly available; explore concurrent appointment structures or transition to a cap-exempt employer

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your NOC TEER classification, and have you checked which PNP streams specifically list your occupation?

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no matching streams: refocus on federal CRS improvement; consider whether a different province's labor market offers PNP-eligible roles in your field

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current age, and when do you need permanent status to make major life decisions (housing, children's schooling, caring for parents)?

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If life milestones precede projected green card: forces honest reassessment of US-only strategy vs. Canada PR or other alternatives
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If timeline is comfortable: can optimize within the US system without urgency pressure
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you currently in STEM OPT or still on initial OPT, and when does your work authorization expire?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: How many more lottery cycles you can survive before losing status, and whether STEM OPT extension is still available as a runway
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Calculate how many April lottery registrations fall within your remaining STEM OPT window
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Apply for STEM OPT extension immediately if eligible — do not wait until initial OPT is near expiration

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score, and have you checked provincial nominee program (PNP) streams in your field?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a federal Express Entry pathway is competitive or whether a PNP route offers a faster, lower-threshold alternative
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If score is borderline: Alberta and Saskatchewan tech PNPs have historically had lower cutoffs — are you eligible and willing to commit to that province?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Research PNP streams before concluding that Canadian PR is out of reach based on federal CRS alone

industry_realities.common_belief: Any staff member at a university can answer immigration-related questions for international students.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Only a CUNY Designated School Official (DSO) or Responsible Officer (RO) is federally authorized to provide immigration advisement to F-1 and J-1 students. Non-authorized staff who provide immigration guidance create personal and institutional liability.
industry_realities.implication: International students should always be redirected to the official DSO or RO office for any immigration question, no matter how minor it seems. Other staff should never attempt to interpret immigration regulations.

industry_realities.common_belief: You need to wait for your diploma or attend convocation before you can apply for the PGWP
industry_realities.actual_reality: The PGWP application can and should be submitted as soon as you receive your letter of completion — the diploma and convocation are irrelevant to the application timeline
industry_realities.implication: Students who wait for their diploma may unnecessarily consume weeks or months of their 180-day window, increasing risk of missing the deadline if any complications arise

industry_realities.common_belief: Any work experience in Canada while on a study permit or PGWP counts toward permanent residency through CEC.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Only post-graduation work (not done while enrolled full-time) in TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3 occupations counts; co-op terms and TEER 4/5 jobs are explicitly excluded regardless of how long or how many hours were worked.
industry_realities.implication: Students must audit their work history carefully and not assume time in Canada equals CEC-eligible time; the eligibility calculation is more restrictive than most expect.

industry_realities.common_belief: Getting a Canadian job offer is the most important thing for Express Entry because it boosts your CRS score significantly
industry_realities.actual_reality: As of March 25, 2025, job offers add zero CRS points — the 50–200 point arranged employment bonus was eliminated. A job offer's immigration value is now limited to enabling experience accumulation for CEC eligibility and supporting PNP applications in streams that still require employment.
industry_realities.implication: Students and advisors using pre-2025 strategies need to recalibrate entirely; the job offer is now an input to experience accumulation, not a direct CRS lever

industry_realities.common_belief: Any Canadian college or university gives international graduates access to a PGWP
industry_realities.actual_reality: Since November 1, 2024, non-degree program graduates must study in an IRCC-approved field of study — institution eligibility alone is no longer sufficient for college diploma and certificate graduates
industry_realities.implication: Students must verify program-level eligibility, not just institution-level eligibility, before enrolling in any non-degree Canadian program with PR intentions

industry_realities.common_belief: If you study in Canada, you will automatically get a PGWP after graduation
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP eligibility depends on program type, program field, program duration, study permit application date, and now language test results — multiple students in ineligible programs discover post-graduation that no PGWP will be issued
industry_realities.implication: PGWP eligibility must be verified before enrollment, not assumed; the burden is on the student, not the institution, to confirm they qualify

industry_realities.common_belief: Express Entry is the main immigration pathway for skilled workers in Canada and PNP is a backup for people who don't qualify
industry_realities.actual_reality: For most international graduates with CRS scores below the ITA threshold, PNP is not a backup — it is the primary pathway, and the +600 points from a nomination effectively guarantees an ITA that would otherwise be unachievable
industry_realities.implication: Students should research and pursue PNP streams starting at the 1-year work experience mark, not after failing to receive an ITA through federal draws

industry_realities.common_belief: The school (DSO) is responsible for monitoring and maintaining your F-1 status — they will catch problems and alert you
industry_realities.actual_reality: F-1 status maintenance is entirely the student's legal responsibility; DSOs report changes to SEVIS but are not obligated to proactively prevent individual students from falling out of compliance
industry_realities.implication: Students must self-monitor their enrollment status every semester and proactively contact the DSO before any change that could affect full-time status — waiting to be told is a losing strategy

industry_realities.common_belief: CPT is unlimited — you can use as much as you want without any long-term consequences
industry_realities.actual_reality: CPT itself has no duration cap, but full-time CPT exceeding 12 cumulative months permanently and irrevocably eliminates OPT eligibility — a separate, devastating consequence that CPT rules themselves do not mention
industry_realities.implication: Students must treat the 12-month full-time CPT threshold as a hard ceiling on career planning, not a guideline, and track cumulative months actively rather than trusting employers or program advisors to warn them

industry_realities.common_belief: The 60-day grace period after graduation is a safety buffer that gives you time to figure out your next steps
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 60-day grace period is a hard-deadline transition window; it begins at academic completion (not ceremony), and every day of inaction in it consumes irreplaceable time — there is no extension
industry_realities.implication: Students must initiate OPT applications or status change decisions before graduation, not after; treating the grace period as buffer almost guarantees procedural failure

industry_realities.common_belief: If I graduate without incident, any past compliance issues are behind me and won't affect my career immigration process
industry_realities.actual_reality: SEVIS records are permanent and time-stamped; USCIS routinely cross-references SEVIS history against H-1B, OPT, and green card applications — violations from early in a student's program surface years later with full documentation
industry_realities.implication: Students must audit their SEVIS compliance history before filing any post-graduation immigration benefit, not after receiving an RFE

industry_realities.common_belief: If an employer says 'yes we sponsor,' your immigration pathway is secure
industry_realities.actual_reality: Sponsorship willingness and sponsorship execution capacity are entirely different things; employers who have never navigated the process frequently fail at the operational level despite genuine intent
industry_realities.implication: Treat verbal and written sponsorship commitments as the beginning of due diligence, not the end; verify historical execution before making the commitment bilateral

industry_realities.common_belief: If you are highly qualified, the EB-2 green card process is primarily about documentation and waiting a reasonable amount of time
industry_realities.actual_reality: For India-born applicants, qualifications are essentially irrelevant to wait time — a brilliant researcher from India waits longer than a minimally qualified applicant from an undersubscribed country purely due to the 7% per-country cap
industry_realities.implication: Indian and Chinese nationals should evaluate the US green card pathway based on per-country quota realities first, and qualifications second — the strategic question is not 'can I qualify?' but 'will the visa be available in my lifetime?'

industry_realities.common_belief: The H-1B lottery is purely random, so salary and job level don't affect your chances
industry_realities.actual_reality: As of FY2027, the lottery is wage-weighted — a Level IV salary earns 4 entries vs. 1 for Level I, potentially increasing odds by 4x before accounting for reduced total registrations
industry_realities.implication: International students should treat salary negotiation as immigration strategy, not just financial planning — a $10K salary bump that crosses a wage level threshold could be worth more than years of career advancement

industry_realities.common_belief: The H-1B lottery is a standard process that employers manage routinely, and most international students who apply get selected eventually.
industry_realities.actual_reality: In peak years, H-1B selection rates were as low as 15–20% even for advanced degree applicants; the new wage-weighted system means odds vary dramatically by wage level, employer type, and position classification — and many employers lack the institutional knowledge to optimize the filing.
industry_realities.implication: Students must treat H-1B as a probabilistic event with a real failure scenario, not a standard process step, and must independently build and maintain backup pathways.

industry_realities.common_belief: OPT starts automatically when you graduate and you can apply whenever you are ready after finishing your degree
industry_realities.actual_reality: OPT requires a proactive application with a strict timeline: the earliest you can apply is 90 days before graduation and the latest is 60 days after — missing these windows results in denial, not a delayed start
industry_realities.implication: Students must build OPT application planning into their graduation timeline, treating it as equally important as thesis submission or final exams

industry_realities.common_belief: If a student has a valid visa stamp, they are maintaining valid immigration status in the US.
industry_realities.actual_reality: A visa stamp and immigration status are completely separate legal concepts. A visa stamp allows entry at the border; status is determined by SEVIS record, I-20/DS-2019 validity, and compliance with enrollment and employment rules. A student can have an expired visa but valid status, or a valid visa but be out of status.
industry_realities.implication: Students and advisors must track status compliance (enrollment, employment, address reporting) independently of visa stamp expiration. Never assume a valid visa means valid status.

industry_realities.common_belief: Any Canadian college program from a designated learning institution qualifies you for a PGWP
industry_realities.actual_reality: Since November 1, 2024, students who applied for their study permit on or after that date must also have graduated from a program in one of five approved occupational fields (agriculture, healthcare, STEM, trades, transport); other programs do not qualify
industry_realities.implication: Students in business, arts, humanities, and social science programs who applied for study permits after November 2024 may have no path to PGWP regardless of graduation status — they need to investigate this before completing their program, not after

industry_realities.common_belief: Getting a job offer from a Canadian employer will significantly boost your Express Entry CRS score and improve your PR chances.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Since March 2025, job offer points were removed from the Express Entry CRS calculation; a job offer no longer adds direct CRS points.
industry_realities.implication: Students should not make career decisions or negotiate employment primarily for immigration CRS benefit from job offers; effort is better spent on language score improvement and PNP if CRS is low.

industry_realities.common_belief: Express Entry is a single waiting list where everyone competes equally and higher scores eventually get selected
industry_realities.actual_reality: Express Entry runs multiple draw types — general, program-specific, and category-based — with different cut-offs. Category-based draws have consistently lower cut-offs and target specific occupations; a candidate who would never receive a general draw ITA may easily receive a category-based ITA
industry_realities.implication: Candidates must actively monitor all draw types and align their NOC code and profile to match category draw criteria rather than passively waiting in a general pool

industry_realities.common_belief: If my program was on the eligible list when I enrolled, I am safe
industry_realities.actual_reality: IRCC overhauled the eligible fields list in June 2025, removing 178 programs — students whose study permits postdate November 2024 and who are in removed fields have lost PGWP eligibility with no recourse
industry_realities.implication: Eligibility must be verified against the current list, not the list that existed at enrollment; continuous monitoring or professional consultation is necessary for non-degree students

industry_realities.common_belief: Any Canadian work experience on a PGWP counts toward permanent residency through Express Entry
industry_realities.actual_reality: Only Canadian work experience in NOC TEER 0, 1, 2, or 3 counts toward CEC eligibility; TEER 4–5 work (which includes most entry-level retail, food service, and manual labor roles) generates zero CEC eligibility regardless of duration
industry_realities.implication: Job selection during the PGWP period is an immigration decision, not just a career one — prioritizing TEER 0–3 roles over higher-paying TEER 4–5 roles is often the correct strategic choice

industry_realities.common_belief: You need a job offer to get a provincial nomination
industry_realities.actual_reality: Ontario's Human Capital Priorities stream is a major exception — it draws directly from the Express Entry pool with no job offer requirement, and Ontario is Canada's largest tech employment market
industry_realities.implication: Tech workers without a job offer should not assume they are ineligible for PNP; they should maintain an active Express Entry profile in a tech NOC to be eligible for Ontario NOIs

industry_realities.common_belief: Dropping one or two courses is a minor administrative matter that can be undone or explained away
industry_realities.actual_reality: F-1 status operates as a binary — you are either in status or out of status; dropping one course that takes you below the full-time minimum without DSO approval is a federal immigration violation with no automatic remedy
industry_realities.implication: Students must treat any enrollment change that could affect full-time status as an immigration event requiring DSO consultation, not just an academic scheduling decision

industry_realities.common_belief: Receiving the OPT approval notice from USCIS means you can start working
industry_realities.actual_reality: Work cannot legally begin until the physical EAD card is in the student's possession; the approval notice and I-797C receipt are not work authorization documents
industry_realities.implication: Students must factor EAD card delivery time into job start date negotiations and must refuse or defer job start dates that precede EAD card arrival, even if it creates friction with employers

industry_realities.common_belief: OPT belongs to the student and travels with them when they transfer to a new school
industry_realities.actual_reality: OPT is school-specific and is legally tied to the authorizing institution's SEVIS record; it terminates automatically and immediately when a SEVIS transfer release date is set — no exceptions
industry_realities.implication: Any student on OPT who is considering school transfer must delay the SEVIS transfer release until after OPT ends or they will lose work authorization with no recovery option

industry_realities.common_belief: Status violations only matter if someone catches them at the time they happen
industry_realities.actual_reality: Unlawful presence accumulates from the date of violation, not the date of discovery; a student can cross the 180-day bar and trigger a multi-year re-entry ban without anyone notifying them — the first notice often comes at a port of entry or during a visa application abroad
industry_realities.implication: Students must act on potential violations immediately upon discovery, not wait to see if they become a problem

industry_realities.common_belief: The best source of immigration advice is peers who have been through the same process
industry_realities.actual_reality: Immigration outcomes are highly dependent on individual circumstances — nationality, program type, employer, timing, application specifics — and people share successes far more than failures; peer advice has severe survivorship bias and calibration error
industry_realities.implication: Use forum research to generate the right questions, then pay for a qualified attorney to answer those questions for your specific situation

industry_realities.common_belief: NIW is an easy path for STEM PhD holders — having a doctorate from a good university is enough
industry_realities.actual_reality: NIW approval rate has dropped to 54% with increased USCIS scrutiny — a PhD alone is insufficient; the Dhanasar test requires demonstrating national importance of the specific proposed endeavor, not just academic credentials
industry_realities.implication: STEM PhDs pursuing NIW must build a case around the national importance of their specific research area with concrete evidence (citations, patents, peer review, critical organizational role) — not just submit a diploma and CV

industry_realities.common_belief: Having multiple companies register you in the H-1B lottery increases your chances of selection
industry_realities.actual_reality: Under the beneficiary-centric rule effective FY2027, each unique individual gets only one entry regardless of how many employers submit — and duplicate submissions from the same employer result in denial
industry_realities.implication: Students should focus entirely on salary optimization at a single high-quality employer rather than seeking multiple registrations

industry_realities.common_belief: O-1 visa is only for globally famous scientists, artists, or executives and is not a realistic option for most international students.
industry_realities.actual_reality: O-1 eligibility is assessed against a structured set of criteria (publications, awards, media coverage, salary benchmarks, judging roles, original contributions) and many students with strong academic records satisfy more criteria than they realize; immigration attorneys who specialize in O-1 regularly approve cases for candidates at the mid-career level.
industry_realities.implication: Students should always conduct a documented O-1 criteria assessment with a specialist before ruling it out, particularly those with publications, institutional affiliations, or above-market compensation.

industry_realities.common_belief: As long as you are actively job searching, unemployment days do not count against your OPT limit
industry_realities.actual_reality: Unemployment days are counted purely by calendar days without valid employment — job searching activity has zero legal bearing on the count; only being employed in a qualifying position stops the clock
industry_realities.implication: Students must prioritize securing employment quickly after OPT start and treat any gap between jobs as a countdown, not a neutral period

industry_realities.common_belief: Being placed on academic probation or being told by a department not to register for courses is a valid reason to take fewer than full-time credits.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Academic probation is not a recognized reduced course load exception under 8 CFR 214.2(f)(6)(iii). A student who follows their department's instruction to reduce enrollment without DSO authorization falls out of status, regardless of the departmental guidance.
industry_realities.implication: Students on academic probation must consult the DSO before reducing enrollment. If no valid RCL exception applies, they may need to transfer temporarily to another school to maintain full-time enrollment.

industry_realities.common_belief: Once you submit your PGWP application, you can automatically work full-time while you wait
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 'work while you wait' provision has three specific qualifying conditions — all must be met simultaneously; failing even one (e.g., having worked overtime during school) means you are not authorized to work and doing so creates an inadmissibility issue
industry_realities.implication: Students should audit their own work history before assuming work authorization, rather than starting a full-time job the day after submission

industry_realities.common_belief: If your CRS score is too low today, you just need to wait longer in the Express Entry pool and eventually your turn will come.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Draw cutoffs are not guaranteed to decline to any given score; students with low CRS scores can expire out of the pool or run out of PGWP validity before receiving an ITA.
industry_realities.implication: Low CRS score students must treat PNP as a parallel immediate strategy, not a fallback after waiting fails — the cost of waiting is PGWP time, which is finite.

industry_realities.common_belief: International students who worked during their degree already have the Canadian work experience needed for CEC
industry_realities.actual_reality: CEC explicitly excludes co-op work terms and self-employment. Only post-graduation paid skilled work (TEER 0–3) counts. Many students who 'worked in Canada' during school have zero CEC-eligible hours.
industry_realities.implication: Students should not include any co-op, internship, or campus employment hours in CEC eligibility calculations; the clock for CEC-eligible experience effectively starts at graduation with a PGWP

industry_realities.common_belief: A Canadian job offer significantly boosts Express Entry CRS score and effectively guarantees a PR invitation
industry_realities.actual_reality: As of March 2025, job offers no longer add CRS points in Express Entry — the longstanding 50-200 point boost has been eliminated
industry_realities.implication: PGWP holders must focus on maximizing language scores, educational credential assessments, and accumulated Canadian work experience as their primary CRS levers — employer sponsorship alone is no longer a competitive advantage in the points system

industry_realities.common_belief: Getting a Canadian job offer boosts your Express Entry score significantly
industry_realities.actual_reality: Job offer points were removed from the CRS scoring system on March 25, 2025 — a job offer provides no CRS point boost and does not meaningfully accelerate Express Entry under current rules
industry_realities.implication: Students should stop optimizing their job search for employer-based CRS gains and redirect that strategic energy toward language scores, French proficiency, and PNP streams

industry_realities.common_belief: The settlement intention requirement in PNP nominations is a formality that IRCC doesn't enforce
industry_realities.actual_reality: While not legally enforceable post-PR, provinces actively monitor and have tightened criteria in response to abuse; repeated misrepresentation of settlement intent can constitute misrepresentation in an immigration application, which carries serious consequences
industry_realities.implication: Students should apply to PNP streams in provinces where they have genuine ties or real employment, not purely as a CRS arbitrage mechanism

industry_realities.common_belief: A compassionate DSO can approve a Reduced Course Load retroactively for a student who had a genuine emergency
industry_realities.actual_reality: DSOs have zero legal authority to authorize RCL retroactively — federal regulations require authorization before the course drop; DSOs who attempt retroactive authorization are themselves violating SEVIS reporting obligations
industry_realities.implication: Students in emerging crises (medical, family, mental health) must contact the DSO while the crisis is active — even a brief call or email before dropping courses protects status in a way no retroactive action can

industry_realities.common_belief: All large or tech-sector employers are automatically enrolled in E-Verify and will qualify for STEM OPT extension
industry_realities.actual_reality: E-Verify enrollment is not automatic for any employer regardless of size or sector; enrollment must be verified on a case-by-case basis before accepting an offer intended to enable STEM OPT extension
industry_realities.implication: STEM degree holders should verify E-Verify enrollment status as part of the offer evaluation process — before signing — and should treat non-enrollment as a disqualifying factor unless the employer commits to enrolling before the STEM extension filing deadline

industry_realities.common_belief: 90 unemployment days is a generous window to find a new job during OPT
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 90 days is a cumulative lifetime limit across the entire OPT period, not a per-job-search reset; USCIS processing delays, gaps between jobs, and onboarding delays all count against it simultaneously
industry_realities.implication: Students must treat each day without an active employer on record as a resource expenditure, maintain a running count, and treat approaching 75 days as an emergency threshold

industry_realities.common_belief: An EAD approval notice means I am authorized to work
industry_realities.actual_reality: Work authorization for F-1 OPT requires the physical EAD card; the approval notice does not constitute authorization — one day of work before the card arrives is a permanent SEVIS unauthorized employment record
industry_realities.implication: Students must structure their employment start date around EAD card receipt, not approval notice date; employers should be educated on this distinction

industry_realities.common_belief: The stay-vs-return decision is primarily about quality of life and career opportunity in the destination country vs. home country right now
industry_realities.actual_reality: The decision is primarily about which trajectory compounds more over 10 years across career, financial, relational, and personal dimensions — and the immigration stability tax on the stay path is consistently underweighted in informal analysis
industry_realities.implication: Force a structured long-run comparison that explicitly quantifies immigration costs (attorney fees, opportunity cost, mental energy, foregone entrepreneurship) before concluding the stay path is superior

industry_realities.common_belief: EB-2 is always a better category than EB-3 because it has a higher preference
industry_realities.actual_reality: For India-born applicants, EB-3 priority dates can advance faster than EB-2 in certain periods due to how retrogression affects each category differently — making a strategic downgrade from EB-2 to EB-3 legally advisable
industry_realities.implication: India-born EB-2 holders must monitor both EB-2 and EB-3 Visa Bulletin dates monthly and consult an attorney when EB-3 dates move ahead of EB-2 dates for their country

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-exempt employers (universities, nonprofits) are less desirable because they pay less
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt employers provide H-1B access year-round with no lottery risk, no annual uncertainty, and the ability to transfer to a lottery-subject employer later while maintaining H-1B status
industry_realities.implication: For students in research, data, policy, or technical fields, cap-exempt employers are strategically superior immigration vehicles — even at slightly lower salaries — especially during high-uncertainty lottery years

industry_realities.common_belief: Once an employer agrees to sponsor H-1B, the student's immigration path is secure.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Employer agreement to sponsor is only the start; the petition must be correctly classified at the right wage level, filed in the correct window, and selected in the lottery — each step has failure modes the employer may not be equipped to prevent without experienced immigration counsel.
industry_realities.implication: Students should independently verify employer immigration readiness, understand the wage level classification, and maintain their own attorney relationship rather than fully delegating to the employer.

industry_realities.common_belief: The STEM OPT 150-day unemployment limit is a fresh allowance on top of the regular OPT 90-day limit
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 150-day limit is cumulative across the entire STEM OPT extension period — it is not a per-job-gap allowance nor a reset from regular OPT; gaps early in the STEM OPT period permanently reduce the remaining buffer
industry_realities.implication: Students must track unemployment days from the very first day of STEM OPT and not assume they have three separate allowances across their OPT periods

industry_realities.common_belief: Health insurance is optional for F-1 students, so it is a reasonable budget item to skip.
industry_realities.actual_reality: While CUNY does not mandate health insurance for F-1 students, a single medical emergency can generate bills exceeding tens of thousands of dollars. Additionally, becoming sick without insurance can make students uninsurable for pre-existing conditions, affecting their ability to obtain coverage later.
industry_realities.implication: F-1 students should be strongly advised to purchase health insurance that includes medical evacuation and repatriation coverage, even if not mandated, as uninsured illness can derail enrollment entirely.

industry_realities.common_belief: A PGWP holder's spouse is automatically eligible for an open work permit
industry_realities.actual_reality: As of January 21, 2025, spousal OWPs require the PGWP holder to work in TEER 0 or 1, or select TEER 2/3 shortage occupations, AND to have at least 16 months remaining on their PGWP at the time of the spouse's application — most entry-level PGWP holders will not meet these thresholds
industry_realities.implication: Families planning finances around dual income should verify the PGWP holder's TEER classification before the spouse declines other options; many couples who qualified under old rules no longer do

industry_realities.common_belief: CEC requires significant financial proof of settlement funds, similar to other PR pathways.
industry_realities.actual_reality: CEC has no settlement fund requirement — this is a unique and significant advantage over FSW, which does require proof of funds.
industry_realities.implication: Students choosing between PR pathways should not let concern about settlement funds deter them from CEC; it is one of the most accessible PR pathways financially for graduates already employed in Canada.

industry_realities.common_belief: French language skills only matter if you want to live in Quebec or are a native French speaker
industry_realities.actual_reality: French proficiency at CLB 7+ adds up to 50 CRS points to any candidate's score and opens access to French-stream draws with lower cut-offs — regardless of province of intended residence or native language. This is one of the highest-yield improvable CRS factors available.
industry_realities.implication: Any candidate within 50 points of recent draw cut-offs who has any French language background should immediately invest in TEF/TCF preparation as a priority strategy

industry_realities.common_belief: The program name on my acceptance letter tells me whether I qualify for PGWP
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP eligibility is determined by the program's CIP code, a technical classification code that may not match the descriptive program name — and submitting the wrong CIP code is treated as misrepresentation with a 5-year ban consequence
industry_realities.implication: Students must obtain the official CIP code from their institution and cross-reference it with the IRCC list — never rely on program name matching alone

industry_realities.common_belief: French is only relevant if you want to live in Quebec or work in a French environment
industry_realities.actual_reality: French language proficiency at CLB 7+ adds significant CRS points and unlocks access to French-language Express Entry draws with cut-offs 90–150 points lower than general draws — it functions as a pure immigration point mechanism independent of where you live or work
industry_realities.implication: Non-Francophone students should evaluate French investment as an immigration optimization tool, not a lifestyle choice; the ROI in CRS points is often higher than any other available lever

industry_realities.common_belief: All tech jobs qualify for tech-specific PNP streams
industry_realities.actual_reality: Eligibility is tied to specific NOC codes and TEER levels — a 'tech job' by industry convention may not be on a province's eligible occupation list, and TEER 4 or misclassified roles will result in rejection
industry_realities.implication: Students must verify their exact NOC code against provincial occupation lists before applying, not assume that working in tech is sufficient

industry_realities.common_belief: If you've been out of status for a while, it's better to quietly re-enroll full-time and hope no one notices before applying for OPT or other benefits
industry_realities.actual_reality: SEVIS contains a complete enrollment history; any gap in full-time enrollment without authorized RCL will surface during OPT application review, H-1B sponsorship background checks, or visa renewal interviews — and misrepresentation of the gap is a separate, more serious violation
industry_realities.implication: Students who discover a past status violation must address it proactively through reinstatement or legal counsel rather than hoping it goes undetected — the cost of discovery later vastly exceeds the cost of remediation now

industry_realities.common_belief: J-1 students can work on campus the same way F-1 students can — no special paperwork needed
industry_realities.actual_reality: J-1 students must obtain written authorization from their Responsible Officer before beginning any on-campus work; this is a mandatory requirement with no exception, unlike F-1 where on-campus work is incident to status
industry_realities.implication: J-1 students should never begin on-campus employment based on peer observation of F-1 norms; they must proactively contact their RO and obtain written authorization first

industry_realities.common_belief: As long as your visa and EAD are valid, you can re-enter the US after international travel
industry_realities.actual_reality: CBP requires a valid passport, unexpired F-1 visa stamp, I-20 with a travel signature dated within the past 6 months, EAD card, and an employment letter — any missing document can result in re-entry denial
industry_realities.implication: International travel during OPT must be planned with a DSO appointment as a mandatory pre-departure step; last-minute travel without updated I-20 endorsement is high risk

industry_realities.common_belief: J-1 and F-1 are essentially similar student visas with the same rules
industry_realities.actual_reality: J-1 violations are categorically more severe: J-1 termination requires immediate departure with no grace period; certain J-1 violations (health insurance) have no reinstatement path; and J-1 holders may face a two-year home residency requirement that F-1 holders do not
industry_realities.implication: Students choosing between F-1 and J-1 — or advising others — must treat them as fundamentally different compliance regimes, not equivalent alternatives

industry_realities.common_belief: Immigration planning is something you do when an immigration deadline approaches
industry_realities.actual_reality: The decisions that most constrain your immigration options — program choice, employer selection, track record building — must be made years before the relevant visa deadline; by the time urgency arrives, the window for the best options has often closed
industry_realities.implication: Immigration planning must be integrated into every major career and education decision from the beginning, not treated as a separate later-stage problem

industry_realities.common_belief: Once an employer starts your green card process, you're on a clear track and just need to wait
industry_realities.actual_reality: PERM takes ~1 year, I-140 takes 8+ months without premium processing, and the priority date may not become current for decades — each stage has its own failure risks, and employer commitment can evaporate through layoffs, restructuring, or policy changes
industry_realities.implication: Employees must actively manage their own case, know every date and status, and maintain contingency plans rather than passively trusting that the employer's process will deliver permanent residence

industry_realities.common_belief: An 'entry-level' job title means you will be registered at OEWS Level I
industry_realities.actual_reality: OEWS wage level is determined by the offered salary relative to prevailing wage benchmarks for your occupation and location — not by job title. A high-paying 'junior' role can qualify for Level III or IV registration
industry_realities.implication: Students and employers should actively check OEWS thresholds and register at the highest level the salary supports, regardless of title — this is legal and explicitly permitted under the DHS rules

industry_realities.common_belief: Negotiating salary risks the employer withdrawing H-1B sponsorship, so students should accept the first offer.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Under the post-2025 wage-weighted lottery, higher wage level directly improves H-1B selection probability; negotiating to reach Wage Level III or IV simultaneously increases compensation and immigration odds — the two goals are aligned, not in tension.
industry_realities.implication: Students should research OEWS wage levels for their occupation and negotiate deliberately, framing it as aligned with the employer's interest in successful H-1B selection.

industry_realities.common_belief: Any tech or engineering job qualifies for STEM OPT as long as the degree is in a STEM field
industry_realities.actual_reality: STEM OPT requires that the specific job duties be directly related to the specific degree — a computer science graduate in a general sales or management role without technical duties may not satisfy the requirement, even at a tech company
industry_realities.implication: Students must evaluate each job offer for degree-relatedness, not just employer industry, and ensure the I-983 can truthfully document that connection

industry_realities.common_belief: If all the classes a student needs are full, they can just take fewer credits that semester and fix it next term.
