industry_realities.actual_reality: Course unavailability is explicitly not a recognized exception to the full-time enrollment requirement. The student must register for other available courses to reach the full-time threshold or explore concurrent enrollment at another school.
industry_realities.implication: Students must maintain full-time enrollment regardless of course availability. DSOs should proactively inform students about concurrent enrollment options at other CUNY schools or external institutions as a compliance strategy.

industry_realities.common_belief: If your PGWP application is denied, you have some time to appeal or arrange your affairs before needing to leave Canada
industry_realities.actual_reality: A PGWP denial immediately ends your legal status in Canada — you must stop working immediately and are required to leave; there is no automatic appeal period, though you may be able to apply for visitor status restoration
industry_realities.implication: Students should treat a PGWP application with the same seriousness as a final job interview — getting it wrong has immediate, severe, and largely irreversible consequences in the short term

industry_realities.common_belief: The typical international student-to-PR journey takes 5+ years and is highly uncertain.
industry_realities.actual_reality: For graduates who follow the PGWP → TEER-qualified job → CEC → Express Entry pathway optimally, PR approval within 2–3 years of graduation is a realistic and common outcome.
industry_realities.implication: Students should not self-select out of the PR process out of perceived complexity or length; the pathway is structured and well-defined for those who plan proactively.

industry_realities.common_belief: A provincial nomination is hard to get and only for candidates already deeply integrated into a province
industry_realities.actual_reality: Many PNP streams target specific occupations or employers and are accessible to PGWP holders with as little as a qualifying job offer or short period of provincial employment. A successful provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points — virtually guaranteeing an ITA regardless of base score.
industry_realities.implication: PNP pursuit should be treated as an active parallel strategy from the moment a PGWP holder starts working, not as a last resort after failing to get a general draw ITA

industry_realities.common_belief: Language ability developed through years of studying in English in Canada satisfies the PGWP language requirement
industry_realities.actual_reality: IRCC requires a formal language test result (IELTS Academic/General, CELPIP General, or TEF Canada/TCF Canada) — demonstrated English proficiency through academic study does not substitute for a scored test meeting CLB benchmarks
industry_realities.implication: All non-degree and degree graduates must schedule and complete an approved language test before applying for PGWP; academic transcripts and letters from professors are not acceptable substitutes

industry_realities.common_belief: The Canada immigration system is straightforward because it's points-based and not a lottery
industry_realities.actual_reality: While there is no lottery, CRS cut-off scores have risen significantly (general draws now 520+, CEC-specific 531–534), making competition intense; the system rewards those who optimize across multiple dimensions simultaneously, not just those who show up with Canadian experience
industry_realities.implication: Students who plan only one variable (e.g., 'just get Canadian work experience') and ignore others (French, spousal factors, age timing, PNP) will find themselves below cut-off despite meeting basic eligibility requirements

industry_realities.common_belief: Getting a 3-year PGWP means you have plenty of time to figure out immigration
industry_realities.actual_reality: A 3-year PGWP sounds long, but the PNP + PR process can take 12–24 months, and the work experience accumulation clock doesn't start until after graduation — students who delay job searching or PNP research by even 6 months can end up in a precarious timeline
industry_realities.implication: PGWP holders should begin immigration planning and job searching in tech-PNP-eligible provinces immediately after graduation, treating month 12 of employment as the critical action trigger

industry_realities.common_belief: Summer break means you can freely skip enrollment without any immigration consequence
industry_realities.actual_reality: Summer non-enrollment is permitted under the annual vacation rule only — it does not extend to any other term, and students must plan to enroll full-time in the fall following summer break
industry_realities.implication: Students must confirm with their DSO that their planned summer non-enrollment qualifies under the annual vacation rule (not all students in all program stages qualify automatically) and must not carry this assumption into fall or spring planning

industry_realities.common_belief: Unauthorized employment is a minor administrative issue that can be corrected after the fact
industry_realities.actual_reality: Even a single day of unauthorized employment terminates F-1 status, bars reinstatement, and permanently closes pathways to H-1B and green card — there is no cure once it occurs
industry_realities.implication: Students and employers must treat work authorization compliance as a zero-tolerance issue; the asymmetry between the ease of the violation and the severity of the consequence requires extreme caution

industry_realities.common_belief: Graduation ceremony date and program completion date are the same thing
industry_realities.actual_reality: Graduation ceremonies are often held months after the actual academic completion date; the immigration clock starts at academic completion, which is often in December for spring ceremony graduates
industry_realities.implication: Students must obtain their official academic completion date from the registrar and use that — not the ceremony date — as the basis for all immigration timeline calculations

industry_realities.common_belief: Address reporting is an administrative formality with no real enforcement consequence
industry_realities.actual_reality: Address change failures are among the most commonly triggered SEVIS violations precisely because students underestimate them; they consistently appear in H-1B RFEs and cause 3–4 month processing delays and attorney fees
industry_realities.implication: DSOs should proactively remind students of the 15-day (F-1) and 10-day (J-1) rules at every housing transition point; students should treat it like a legal filing deadline

industry_realities.common_belief: A high salary offer is the most important variable when evaluating a job offer as a visa holder
industry_realities.actual_reality: The immigration pathway security embedded in a job offer — employer stability, sponsorship capacity, clear path to permanent residence — often has greater long-run economic value than marginal salary differences, because visa instability has large compounding costs
industry_realities.implication: Build an explicit immigration pathway analysis into every job offer evaluation and weight it alongside compensation, not subordinate to it

industry_realities.common_belief: The EB-2 process costs are primarily the employer's problem
industry_realities.actual_reality: While employers pay PERM and I-140 costs, the employee bears the enormous indirect costs: career mobility constraints, geographic constraints, delayed entrepreneurship, psychological burden of status uncertainty, and the optionality cost of staying when better opportunities exist elsewhere
industry_realities.implication: Students should factor the full cost of employment-based sponsorship — including constrained career mobility — when evaluating whether US green card pursuit makes sense vs. alternative countries where permanent residence is faster

industry_realities.common_belief: The H-1B lottery is your only or primary path to long-term US work authorization as an international student
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple cap-exempt and non-cap pathways exist (O-1, L-1, TN, E-3, J-1, cap-exempt H-1B employers), and under the new wage system, better-positioned candidates may see selection rates approaching 50–90%
industry_realities.implication: Students should build a multi-track strategy: optimize for lottery while simultaneously preparing alternative petitions, rather than treating the lottery as the single make-or-break event

industry_realities.common_belief: STEM OPT automatically extends anyone in a technical or scientific field.
industry_realities.actual_reality: STEM OPT eligibility depends on the exact degree program appearing on the official DHS STEM Designated Degree Program List, not on the field of work or the department name — many degrees in data, information systems, and applied technical fields do not qualify.
industry_realities.implication: STEM OPT eligibility must be verified against the official list before graduation; planning 36 months of OPT runway without this verification is a significant planning risk.

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-gap requires a separate filing or extension application to continue working after OPT expires
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-gap extension of both F-1 status and work authorization is automatic when an H-1B petition is timely filed — no separate USCIS filing is needed
industry_realities.implication: Students should focus energy on ensuring the employer files the H-1B petition on time rather than on preparing redundant paperwork; however they must confirm the petition has actually been filed

industry_realities.common_belief: Transferring to a new school handles all immigration paperwork automatically.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Academic acceptance at a new school does not transfer the student's SEVIS record. The student must formally notify the current DSO, submit a transfer recommendation form, and have the DSO release the SEVIS record to the new school. Only then can the new school issue an I-20.
industry_realities.implication: Students planning to transfer must initiate the SEVIS transfer process explicitly with their current DSO, separate from the academic admission process. Failure to do so can result in a status gap.

industry_realities.common_belief: Having a job offer before applying for PGWP is an advantage you should declare in the application
industry_realities.actual_reality: Declaring a job offer in the IRCC questionnaire routes your application toward an employer-specific closed work permit, not the open PGWP; the correct answer is 'No' to the job offer question to obtain an open permit
industry_realities.implication: Students should follow the step-by-step questionnaire guide exactly as written rather than trying to 'honestly' reflect their current situation — the system's logic is counterintuitive and correct answers are not always the most transparent-seeming ones

industry_realities.common_belief: A PhD or master's degree from a foreign university is the strongest educational credential for CRS purposes
industry_realities.actual_reality: While a PhD earns 150 CRS points (maximum for education), it must be accompanied by a valid ECA from a designated organization for foreign credentials. Additionally, a Canadian degree adds the 15–30 Canadian study bonus on top — making Canadian-educated candidates structurally stronger than equally qualified foreign-educated candidates at the same degree level.
industry_realities.implication: Candidates with foreign credentials must complete ECA before submitting their profile; those with Canadian credentials should ensure both the degree level and Canadian study bonus are correctly included in their CRS calculation

industry_realities.common_belief: Graduate certificates and graduate diplomas at Canadian institutions are equivalent to a university master's degree for PGWP purposes
industry_realities.actual_reality: Only university bachelor's, master's, and PhD degrees are exempt from the field-of-study requirement — graduate certificates and graduate diplomas, even those offered by universities, are classified as non-degree programs and must meet field-of-study eligibility
industry_realities.implication: Students choosing between a university master's degree and a graduate certificate for cost savings should understand the graduate certificate pathway carries materially higher PGWP eligibility risk

industry_realities.common_belief: You can start planning your Canadian permanent residency strategy after you graduate
industry_realities.actual_reality: The most impactful CRS variables — program type, program field, age at application, study permit application date — are determined by decisions made before or at enrollment, often 2–4 years before graduation
industry_realities.implication: Immigration strategy must begin at program selection, not at graduation; the PGWP period is too late to recover from enrollment decisions that created structural CRS disadvantages

industry_realities.common_belief: You can enroll in a new degree program at the same level to get a fresh OPT authorization
industry_realities.actual_reality: OPT eligibility resets only when the new degree is at a different level (e.g., completing a bachelor's and then completing a master's); same-level degrees do not grant a new OPT period
industry_realities.implication: Students who plan to use additional degrees as an immigration bridge must ensure the new degree is at a higher academic level than the one that already generated OPT

industry_realities.common_belief: If I disclose a past violation on an immigration form, it will automatically disqualify my application
industry_realities.actual_reality: Concealing a known violation and having it discovered through SEVIS cross-referencing constitutes misrepresentation — a separate and more severe violation that can result in permanent inadmissibility, far worse than the original violation
industry_realities.implication: Students with known compliance gaps should disclose proactively with attorney-prepared explanatory documentation rather than attempt concealment

industry_realities.common_belief: Immigration decisions are primarily legal and logistical challenges
industry_realities.actual_reality: Immigration decisions are among the most emotionally loaded decisions people make — intersecting with identity, belonging, family, and perceived success — and the emotional loading systematically distorts analysis toward status quo bias and fear of return
industry_realities.implication: Explicitly separate the emotional and analytical dimensions of major immigration decisions; consult people who can challenge your reasoning, not just validate your preferences

industry_realities.common_belief: The H-1B registration fee ($215) is refundable if not selected
industry_realities.actual_reality: The $215 registration fee is explicitly non-refundable regardless of selection outcome
industry_realities.implication: Employers bear a real per-head cost for each registration, which may influence whether smaller or less committed employers bother to register lower-priority candidates — students should confirm employer commitment early

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-exempt employers (universities, nonprofits) are less desirable immigration options because they pay less and limit career growth.
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt employers eliminate lottery risk entirely, allow indefinite H-1B filing year-round, and provide stable status that can support credential-building toward O-1 or EB-1 green card pathways — which are both cap-exempt and faster than EB-2/EB-3 for many nationalities.
industry_realities.implication: Students should evaluate cap-exempt positions not only on immediate compensation but on the long-term immigration stability and credential-building they enable, particularly for those with research or academic backgrounds.

industry_realities.common_belief: Prestigious employers with strong brand names are the best choice during OPT because they improve H-1B prospects
industry_realities.actual_reality: Under the FY2027+ wage-based H-1B lottery system, salary level is a direct mechanical determinant of lottery selection odds — a higher salary at a less recognized employer may statistically outperform a lower salary at a brand-name firm
industry_realities.implication: Students should evaluate job offers through a dual lens of career development and compensation level relative to H-1B wage tiers, explicitly modeling lottery probability as part of the decision

industry_realities.common_belief: J-2 dependent income can be used to help support another international student's enrollment costs.
industry_realities.actual_reality: J-2 authorized employment income is explicitly prohibited from being used as financial support for issuing a Certificate of Eligibility to any other student, including the J-1 principal holder.
industry_realities.implication: Students who plan to use a spouse's J-2 employment income to cover costs for another international student's enrollment documentation will find the application rejected or the I-20/DS-2019 invalidated.

industry_realities.common_belief: Only your own qualifications matter for Express Entry
industry_realities.actual_reality: For married or common-law candidates, a spouse's language test results and education assessment can add 10–40 CRS points under the spousal factor component — making spouse documentation a direct immigration priority
industry_realities.implication: Married students should treat their spouse's IELTS/TEF exam and ECA as part of their own immigration preparation, not as optional paperwork for the spouse

industry_realities.common_belief: If you don't get H-1B, you must either leave the US or go back to school.
industry_realities.actual_reality: There are at least 10 documented alternative pathways including O-1, TN, E-3, L-1, cap-exempt H-1B, EB-1 direct green card, and working abroad temporarily — each with specific eligibility criteria that many students never assess.
industry_realities.implication: Students should map their eligibility across all 10 pathways before each lottery result, so that a rejection triggers immediate execution of a pre-evaluated backup rather than a reactive scramble.

industry_realities.common_belief: The employer or HR department is responsible for ensuring immigration compliance during OPT and STEM OPT
industry_realities.actual_reality: The legal obligation for SEVIS reporting, unemployment day tracking, and status compliance rests with the student — employer failures are the student's legal problem, not the employer's
industry_realities.implication: Students must treat themselves as their own immigration compliance officer, independently tracking all deadlines and reporting obligations regardless of what HR does or says

industry_realities.common_belief: If I start working on campus right when I arrive in the US, I am fine as long as I have a valid I-20.
industry_realities.actual_reality: F-1 students may not begin on-campus employment more than 30 days before the actual start of classes, even if they arrived 30 days before the program start date and have a valid I-20.
industry_realities.implication: Students who arrive early and begin work immediately may be engaging in unauthorized employment. Employment start must be coordinated with the class start calendar, not the arrival date.

industry_realities.common_belief: If your English is already excellent (CLB 10+), retaking IELTS will improve your CRS score
industry_realities.actual_reality: CRS points for language are capped — once you reach the maximum CLB band, no additional English test score will increase your CRS
industry_realities.implication: High-English applicants should pivot immediately to French study rather than investing time in English re-testing
industry_realities._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Once your study permit expires, you must leave Canada or your status is illegal
industry_realities.actual_reality: If you have submitted a PGWP application before your study permit expired, you are in 'implied status' and may legally remain in Canada while the application is processed
industry_realities.implication: Graduates do not need to leave Canada between graduation and PGWP approval, but they must have submitted the application before the study permit expires to benefit from implied status
industry_realities._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Graduate Certificate programs are graduate-level, so they should give the same or similar PGWP as a master's degree
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP duration is determined by the length of the completed program, not its academic level — a 1-year Graduate Certificate yields only 1 year of PGWP regardless of how rigorous the coursework is
industry_realities.implication: Students must evaluate programs by duration for immigration purposes, not by academic level or prestige; a shorter but more prestigious program can be worse for PR timelines
industry_realities._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: If you work hard and stay in the US, you'll eventually get your green card
industry_realities.actual_reality: For Indian and Chinese nationals in EB-2/EB-3, the backlog is so severe that many will not receive a green card before retirement age; this is a structural feature of the per-country cap system, not a temporary delay
industry_realities.implication: Indian and Chinese nationals should treat US permanent residency as unlikely within a normal career horizon and plan accordingly — either pursuing Canada or accepting indefinite H-1B status
industry_realities._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: A work permit in Canada means the employer needs to sponsor you or file paperwork
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP is an open work permit issued by the Canadian government directly to the graduate; employers do not need to submit any application, file an LMIA, or provide sponsorship — the holder can work for any employer without any employer action
industry_realities.implication: PGWP holders should explicitly communicate this distinction in resumes and interviews, rather than assuming employers know it; failure to do so causes unnecessary rejections
industry_realities._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: EB1A (extraordinary ability) is only for Nobel Prize winners or top academics — it is irrelevant for software engineers
industry_realities.actual_reality: EB1A requires meeting 3 of 10 USCIS criteria; tech professionals with peer-reviewed publications, significant open-source contributions, or documented industry influence have successfully self-petitioned; the bar is high but not academic-elite-only
industry_realities.implication: SWEs who dismiss EB1A without a credential audit may be forgoing the fastest available green card path (no PERM, no employer dependency, minimal wait for non-Indian nationals)

industry_realities.common_belief: Using a lawyer for EB-2 NIW gives you a higher approval rate than self-filing
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple community members questioned this assumption; the petitioner self-filed successfully in 14 months. The quality of the Personal Statement and evidence package matters more than whether an attorney prepared it — attorneys vary widely in NIW experience
industry_realities.implication: Self-filing is viable for researchers who invest time in understanding the Dhanasar framework and can write compelling English narratives; the attorney premium is not automatic
industry_realities._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Being too visible or indispensable at work will discourage your employer from helping you find visa alternatives, because they'll just rely on you and not want to lose you to another country
industry_realities.actual_reality: Employers invest in visa solutions for employees they value highly; low visibility reduces the business case for absorbing H-1B costs or arranging cross-border transfers. Indispensability is more likely to motivate employer support, not suppress it.
industry_realities.implication: International workers should focus on demonstrating clear value rather than staying invisible; the 'be invisible to stay safe' strategy is likely counterproductive for securing employer-sponsored solutions
industry_realities._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: If USCIS hasn't explicitly declared Day 1 CPT illegal, it must be safe to use
industry_realities.actual_reality: USCIS enforces CPT legitimacy through case-by-case adjudication — they can invalidate a specific CPT authorization retroactively without ever issuing a blanket rule against Day 1 CPT, and that retroactive invalidation carries severe consequences
industry_realities.implication: Absence of an explicit USCIS prohibition is not legal safety; the risk is adjudicatory, not regulatory, and surfaces unpredictably during future benefit applications
industry_realities._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: If your employer won't sponsor H-1B after lottery failures, your only options are to find a new employer willing to sponsor or leave the US
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt employers (universities, nonprofit research institutions, DOE national labs) can file H-1B petitions outside the annual cap at any time; concurrent employment allows maintaining the primary job while the cap-exempt employer holds the H-1B sponsorship
industry_realities.implication: Students exhausting OPT should actively explore cap-exempt routes — including part-time research roles at universities — as a parallel strategy, not a last resort
industry_realities._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: The 90-day unemployment limit on OPT resets or applies separately to each job gap
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 90-day limit is cumulative across the entire OPT period; all unemployment gaps since OPT start date are added together
industry_realities.implication: Students must track total unemployment days from OPT start, not just the current gap; even someone who never had a long single gap can exceed the limit through multiple short ones
industry_realities._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Improving your English IELTS score is the best way to boost CRS points once you have a strong profile
industry_realities.actual_reality: Past CLB 10, English score improvements yield only ~6 marginal CRS points; French bilingual (TEF Canada B1) yields approximately 50 points and is achievable in 3–6 months for English speakers
industry_realities.implication: Applicants at CLB 10 should redirect language study effort entirely to French rather than retaking IELTS

industry_realities.common_belief: The US immigration system is hard but if you get an H-1B and have a good employer, you'll eventually get your green card
industry_realities.actual_reality: For Indian nationals in EB-2, the current priority date is around 2012-2013 — meaning someone entering the queue today faces a 40+ year wait. This is not a processing delay; it is a structural quota system that makes green cards functionally inaccessible for most Indian tech workers within a normal career
industry_realities.implication: Indian nationals should not plan their career around eventual US green card receipt — it must be treated as unlikely rather than delayed, and Canada or other pathways evaluated as primary alternatives

industry_realities.common_belief: As a PGWP holder you need to stay at your current employer to protect your PR pathway
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP is an open work permit — holders can change employers freely without any impact on their PR eligibility or Express Entry profile
industry_realities.implication: PGWP holders who understand this can make career decisions based on growth and fit rather than false immigration constraints, avoiding unnecessary career stagnation

industry_realities.common_belief: The PGWP length matches your program length exactly — a 4-year bachelor's gets a 4-year PGWP
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP is capped at 3 years maximum regardless of program length; a 4-year bachelor's does not yield a 4-year PGWP (raised as genuine uncertainty in comment rank=8, upvotes=234 — this confusion is widespread)
industry_realities.implication: Graduates from longer programs should not plan their PR timeline assuming more than 3 years of PGWP work authorization

industry_realities.common_belief: The processing time listed on the USCIS website tells you how long your EAD will take
industry_realities.actual_reality: The published time is a statistical average; actual individual outcomes ranged from 6-8 weeks to 6+ months for the same application type in the same period, and processing is batch-based, not strictly first-in-first-out
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should treat the published estimate as a rough midpoint and build significant buffer into job start date planning, not use it as a reliable deadline

industry_realities.common_belief: Switching programs within the same institution is a minor academic administrative change with no immigration consequences
industry_realities.actual_reality: A formal program switch resets PGWP eligibility to the length of the newly completed program, regardless of how much time was spent in the original program — the OP would have lost 2 years of PGWP by switching from a 2-year Master's to a 1-year Graduate Certificate
industry_realities.implication: Students must treat any program change as an immigration decision, not just an academic one, and consult their DSO or international student advisor before proceeding

industry_realities.common_belief: Large Canadian tech companies (Shopify, Amazon Canada, Google Canada) are the best targets for PGWP holders because they are established and well-resourced
industry_realities.actual_reality: These companies receive the highest application volumes and are the hardest to break into without a referral; mid-size companies (50–200 people) are significantly more receptive and have lower competition
industry_realities.implication: PGWP job seekers should invert their targeting instinct and lead with mid-size companies, treating large tech as a secondary or referral-dependent track

industry_realities.common_belief: If a company says 'we don't sponsor visas', international candidates on OPT cannot work there
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple corroborated comments confirm that 'no sponsorship' often means no H-1B petitions specifically; many such companies will still complete STEM OPT I-983 forms and be E-Verify enrolled, allowing OPT and STEM OPT extension work authorization
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should always clarify the scope of a 'no sponsorship' policy before self-disqualifying; this distinction can mean 1–3 additional years of employment eligibility

industry_realities.common_belief: If a reputable company verbally promises H-1B sponsorship during hiring, you can count on it
industry_realities.actual_reality: Verbal promises carry no legal weight for immigration commitments; company policy can change at any time and employees have no enforceable claim without written documentation, making promissory estoppel claims difficult and costly to pursue
industry_realities.implication: OPT holders must treat written offer-letter language as the only reliable form of sponsorship commitment, regardless of company reputation or recruiter assurances

industry_realities.common_belief: The 60-day grace period gives you time to find and start a new job after H-1B rejection or job loss
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 60-day grace period allows you to remain in the US to transition status, transfer schools, or prepare to depart — you cannot work during this period without new authorization
industry_realities.implication: Students who receive H-1B rejections must have a status change fully in place before they can begin any new employment — the grace period does not serve as a buffer for continued job-searching-while-employed

industry_realities.common_belief: Part-time CPT counts toward the 12-month limit that eliminates OPT eligibility
industry_realities.actual_reality: Only full-time CPT (20+ hours/week) counts toward the 12-month limit; part-time CPT (under 20 hours/week) does not count regardless of duration or number of semesters
industry_realities.implication: Students who have done extensive part-time CPT need not worry about OPT eligibility loss from that work; students should precisely categorize each authorization as full-time or part-time

industry_realities.common_belief: USCIS has never banned Day 1 CPT, so using it is legally safe
industry_realities.actual_reality: The absence of an explicit ban does not confer safety. SEVP has actively shut down Day 1 CPT schools; consular officers treat these schools as red flags; and USCIS has issued RFEs on H-1B petitions questioning prior Day 1 CPT work authorization — with some denials resulting
industry_realities.implication: Students should evaluate the actual enforcement record, not just the absence of a formal rule; the legal gray zone is actively being used against applicants in downstream petitions

industry_realities.common_belief: The H-1B lottery is purely random and there is nothing you can do to improve your chances beyond registering
industry_realities.actual_reality: While selection is random, post-selection approval probability is significantly affected by SOC code precision, quality of specialty occupation documentation, and the competence of the filing attorney — all controllable factors
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should invest in petition quality even if they cannot control lottery odds; a selected petition that gets denied wastes the lottery slot

industry_realities.common_belief: Any employer affiliated with a university is cap-exempt and can sponsor an H-1B without going through the lottery
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt status depends on the entity's primary purpose — not its affiliation. For-profit university spinouts, foundations that only grant money without conducting research, and hospitals whose primary function is patient care do not qualify. The 'primary purpose' test may even be applied at the department level within large institutions.
industry_realities.implication: Applicants must verify the specific filing entity's eligibility — not just its connection to a university — before investing time and legal fees in a cap-exempt strategy

industry_realities.common_belief: The emotional and psychological impact of H-1B uncertainty is a personal weakness or something to push through privately
industry_realities.actual_reality: An immigration lawyer with direct client experience explicitly confirms the chronic stress is real and underacknowledged; the uncertainty structure — lottery randomness, employer dependency, government rule changes — constitutes a genuine form of chronic stress that warrants professional support
industry_realities.implication: Seeking therapy or mental health support is a rational response to a structurally stressful situation, not a sign of inability to cope — treating it as such improves decision quality and resilience

industry_realities.common_belief: If you keep applying to H-1B, you'll eventually get it — it's just a matter of time
industry_realities.actual_reality: Each lottery is independent at roughly 25-30% odds; there is no cumulative advantage. Meanwhile, STEM OPT is capped at 3 years total regardless of lottery outcomes, creating a hard deadline
industry_realities.implication: Students should treat each lottery loss as a trigger to activate contingency planning, not simply reapply and wait

industry_realities.common_belief: If your degree is on the STEM designated degree program list, your job automatically qualifies for STEM OPT
industry_realities.actual_reality: The degree being on the STEM list is necessary but not sufficient — USCIS also evaluates whether the specific job duties have a direct relationship to the STEM degree, which must be documented in the I-983 with specificity
industry_realities.implication: Even students with clearly STEM degrees (e.g., Information Systems) need to write a detailed STEM connection narrative — the credential alone does not carry the application

industry_realities.common_belief: PGWP duration simply equals how long you studied — 2 years of study means 2 years of PGWP, 3 years means 3 years
industry_realities.actual_reality: Post-2024 PGWP policy introduced a program-eligibility-list dimension: programs not on IRCC's eligible list are capped at a 2-year PGWP maximum, regardless of study duration; the duration formula is tiered and program-category-dependent
industry_realities.implication: Students in 3-year programs outside the eligible list should not plan their immigration timeline (e.g., Express Entry CRS accumulation) assuming a 3-year work permit

industry_realities.common_belief: STEM OPT is tied to the employer, so changing jobs means restarting the STEM OPT application
industry_realities.actual_reality: STEM OPT authorization is tied to the individual, not the employer. When changing jobs, the new employer simply needs to submit a new I-983 Training Plan; the authorization period itself does not restart
industry_realities.implication: Students should not avoid job changes out of fear of losing STEM OPT; they just need to ensure the new employer is E-Verify enrolled and file the new I-983 promptly
industry_realities._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: If you miss H-1B three times, you have no real option but to leave the US
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple legitimate US paths exist: cap-exempt employer H-1B (no lottery), O-1 (no lottery), EB-1A (direct green card, no lottery), EB-2 NIW (self-petition green card, no lottery); Canada PR is also a viable parallel track with transparent point-based selection
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should map their profile against these alternatives early rather than treating departure as the default outcome of lottery failure
industry_realities._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: You need to be fluent or near-native in French to gain the French language CRS bonus
industry_realities.actual_reality: CLB 7 in French — the threshold for the ~50-point bonus — corresponds to approximately B1 level, which does not require conversational fluency; it can be achieved through structured study in 3–6 months by most candidates
industry_realities.implication: Candidates who dismiss French as unrealistic may be leaving 50 CRS points on the table based on a false assumption about the required proficiency level
industry_realities._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: 加拿大移民路径需要抽签，运气成分大，不可预测
industry_realities.actual_reality: Express Entry基于CRS积分制，规则透明，分数可以通过法语、Job Offer、配偶英语等方式主动提升；相比美国H-1B的随机抽签，可预期性更强
industry_realities.implication: 应将移民规划视为可量化的积分管理问题，主动寻找加分杠杆，而非被动等待
industry_realities._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Getting a US green card means you can bring your parents to the US without much additional wait
industry_realities.actual_reality: Green card holders sponsoring parents fall into a numerically-limited preference category with potential 5–10+ year backlogs; only US citizens can sponsor parents under the uncapped IR-5 category with ~1 year processing
industry_realities.implication: The reunion clock for parents doesn't start when you get a green card — it starts when you naturalize, which adds another 5+ years minimum to the timeline for most people
industry_realities._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Canadian employers don't need to sponsor PGWP holders, so hiring them is friction-free and they'll be treated the same as citizens/PRs in hiring
industry_realities.actual_reality: Many Canadian HR departments don't distinguish between PGWP (open, employer-unrestricted) and employer-specific work permits (which require LMIA sponsorship). Seeing 'work permit' on a profile, they assume sponsorship cost and complexity, and self-eliminate
industry_realities.implication: PGWP holders must proactively educate recruiters and HR at the first contact point — the legal advantage of PGWP is not automatically communicated by the credential itself
industry_realities._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: H-1B is a temporary visa and green card is the straightforward next step that follows after a few years of work
industry_realities.actual_reality: For India-born and China-born applicants, the green card backlog is so severe that H-1B effectively becomes a permanent status for decades — new EB-2 India entrants in 2024 face estimated waits into the 2070s
industry_realities.implication: The decision to stay in the US on H-1B is not a short-term bridge to green card — it is a commitment to indefinite temporary status, which changes the entire risk calculus of career and life planning
industry_realities._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: All-program Express Entry draws are the main path to ITA for most applicants
industry_realities.actual_reality: For applicants from high-competition countries (India, China), all-program draw cutoffs frequently exceed 530, making category-based draws (tech, healthcare, trades, French-language proficiency) the realistic pathway for scores in the 470–510 range
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should actively track category draw eligibility by NOC code rather than passively waiting for all-program draws
industry_realities._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: PGWP duration is simply '3 years for any graduate student'
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP duration is tied to the length of your study program, capped at 3 years. A 1-year program yields approximately 1 year of PGWP; a 1.5-year program does not automatically round up; a 2+ year program yields 3 years
industry_realities.implication: Students in accelerated or non-standard programs must calculate their expected PGWP duration carefully before making post-graduation immigration plans
industry_realities._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Academic advisors will flag immigration consequences when you discuss program changes with them
industry_realities.actual_reality: Academic advisors optimize for academic outcomes; immigration implications of program switches are outside their scope and they typically will not raise them
industry_realities.implication: Students must proactively consult their international student immigration advisor as a separate step for any program change — it will not happen automatically
industry_realities._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: The salary gap between US and Canada has narrowed significantly in recent years
industry_realities.actual_reality: This was raised as a question by a commenter (rank=8) but not confirmed; the original poster cited 30-50% gaps for senior engineers, and no informed commenter provided data contradicting this for senior-level roles
industry_realities.implication: Do not assume the gap has closed without independently verifying current compensation data for your specific role and seniority level
industry_realities._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Job postings requiring PR or Canadian citizenship are legal standard practice in Canada
industry_realities.actual_reality: Requiring PR or citizenship status as a job condition may violate the Canadian Human Rights Act in many contexts, though the original poster notes enforcement is practically difficult due to high legal costs
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should be aware this may be discriminatory but should make a practical decision about whether to pursue that employer; the community consensus is to move on rather than litigate
industry_realities._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-exempt H1B jobs at universities or nonprofits are a niche workaround not worth pursuing due to lower pay
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple community members confirmed this path results in real H1B status without lottery risk, and the green card timeline can be shorter than waiting for H1B lottery luck plus PERM; some returned to industry afterward with green card in hand
industry_realities.implication: The salary discount should be modeled as a time-bounded cost, not a permanent penalty; for people who have failed lottery 3+ times, the expected value calculation often favors the cap-exempt path

industry_realities.common_belief: EB-2 NIW has a minimum publication count requirement
industry_realities.actual_reality: There is no published minimum — USCIS evaluates the totality of evidence. A petitioner with 3 high-citation first-author papers may have a stronger case than one with 10 low-impact papers
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should not self-disqualify based on paper count; citation quality, evidence breadth, and narrative strength matter more
industry_realities._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Researching or planning for Canada or returning home means giving up on the US path
industry_realities.actual_reality: Seriously researching Plan B options reduces anxiety and improves decision quality for all scenarios, including the US path; it does not commit you to leaving and does not signal defeat to employers
industry_realities.implication: Reframe Plan B research as risk management, not concession; people who do this report lower chronic anxiety and better outcomes regardless of lottery result
industry_realities._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: If my H-1B gets approved, my prior CPT history is no longer a problem
industry_realities.actual_reality: H-1B approval does not immunize prior immigration history; unlawful presence or unlawful employment findings can be raised at later stages including green card adjudication, consular interviews, or re-entry after travel
industry_realities.implication: Getting through H-1B does not close the risk window; people who used Day 1 CPT carry exposure at every subsequent immigration touchpoint
industry_realities._source: us_visa_Day_1_CPT的真实风险_一个差点中招的故事.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-exempt H-1B is a different visa category that creates complications when switching jobs later
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt H-1B is still an H-1B; once approved, the holder can transfer (port) to a cap-subject employer via H-1B transfer without re-entering the lottery, as the cap exemption applies to the petition, not the visa holder permanently
industry_realities.implication: Workers who obtain H-1B via cap-exempt route are not permanently locked into nonprofit/academic employment — they can move to private sector employers through standard H-1B transfer
industry_realities._source: us_visa_H_1B三连败后成功上岸cap_exempt经历分享.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Changing jobs on OPT requires re-applying for a new EAD or restarting the OPT process
industry_realities.actual_reality: The OPT EAD is not employer-specific; it does not need to be reissued when changing jobs. Only SEVIS needs to be updated by the DSO
industry_realities.implication: Students should not delay job changes out of fear of losing their EAD; the administrative burden is DSO notification, not USCIS re-filing
industry_realities._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: TEF Canada is too hard or French is too time-consuming to be a realistic short-term strategy
industry_realities.actual_reality: TEF Canada is noted by experienced commenters (445 upvotes) as more lenient than DELF; B1 is described as achievable in 3–6 months of consistent study for English speakers
industry_realities.implication: The French bilingual route is being systematically underutilized because applicants overestimate the difficulty barrier

industry_realities.common_belief: Express Entry is purely meritocratic — the highest scorers always get invited
industry_realities.actual_reality: While CRS is points-based and avoids country-of-origin quotas for PR, draw cutoffs fluctuate significantly between rounds. A score that clears one draw may not clear the next, and timing matters alongside raw score
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should actively monitor draw patterns and consider CRS score improvement strategies (language scores, additional credentials, PNP nomination) rather than waiting passively once they have a profile submitted

industry_realities.common_belief: Implied/maintained status while waiting for PGWP approval means you can continue doing what your study permit allowed, including working
industry_realities.actual_reality: Maintained status lets you remain in Canada legally, but you cannot work until the PGWP is actually approved and issued — the work authorization does not carry over
industry_realities.implication: Graduates who accept jobs or start work during the gap period may be working without authorization, creating serious immigration compliance issues

industry_realities.common_belief: Premium processing is available for the I-765 OPT application and can speed up the process
industry_realities.actual_reality: Premium processing is explicitly NOT available for OPT I-765 applications; there is no fee-based expedite option
industry_realities.implication: Applicants who budget or plan around a premium processing option will find it unavailable; the only acceleration levers are early submission, clean forms, and DSO-mediated escalation for delayed cases

industry_realities.common_belief: Completing more programs always results in a longer PGWP through stacking
industry_realities.actual_reality: IRCC only combines program lengths if programs are consecutive at DLIs with no gap; a gap between programs may result in PGWP being based only on the final program, making additional earlier credentials irrelevant to PGWP length
industry_realities.implication: Students who plan credential stacking strategies must verify the consecutiveness requirement and any permissible gap window before relying on a combined PGWP calculation

industry_realities.common_belief: Canadian employers understand what a PGWP is and know it doesn't require sponsorship
industry_realities.actual_reality: Many Canadian employers — including recruiters at established companies — see 'work permit' and assume it means employer sponsorship is required; this confusion causes silent rejection
industry_realities.implication: Candidates must proactively and explicitly educate every employer contact about what PGWP means in plain terms, rather than assuming the label is self-explanatory

industry_realities.common_belief: Asking whether you'll need sponsorship in the future is illegal national origin discrimination
industry_realities.actual_reality: The question 'will you now or in the future require sponsorship?' is legally permissible because it asks about work authorization status, not national origin or citizenship; employers are prohibited from discriminating based on citizenship status but can screen for authorization
industry_realities.implication: Candidates who believe this is illegal may prepare incorrect responses or feel wrongly targeted; understanding the legal boundary helps candidates answer accurately and confidently

industry_realities.common_belief: L-1 and O-1 visas are accessible fallback options when H-1B sponsorship falls through
industry_realities.actual_reality: L-1 requires at least 1 year of employment abroad with a qualifying multinational employer; O-1 requires demonstrable extraordinary ability — most early-to-mid career professionals do not qualify for either
industry_realities.implication: These should not be treated as realistic contingency plans for most OPT holders; planning should center on finding a new H-1B cap-subject sponsor or a cap-exempt bridge employer

industry_realities.common_belief: Losing your job terminates your OPT authorization immediately
industry_realities.actual_reality: Termination of employment does not end OPT status by itself — you can be between jobs for up to 90 aggregate days on post-completion OPT before the status becomes invalid
industry_realities.implication: Students who think losing a job immediately ends their OPT may take unnecessary panic actions (departing the US, abandoning status) when they actually have legal buffer time

industry_realities.common_belief: Losing OPT eligibility due to CPT overage means you cannot legally work in the US after graduation
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt H-1B employment (universities, nonprofits, government research organizations) remains available and is not subject to the annual lottery; at least one commenter recovered by working as a research assistant at their university under a cap-exempt H-1B
industry_realities.implication: Students who lose OPT eligibility should research cap-exempt employer pathways before concluding US employment is impossible

industry_realities.common_belief: Once you graduate and start OPT or get H-1B approved, your Day 1 CPT history is no longer reviewable
industry_realities.actual_reality: USCIS can and does revisit prior work authorization history during H-1B petitions and green card applications; approval of OPT does not constitute USCIS sign-off on the legitimacy of prior CPT
industry_realities.implication: Students who have already used Day 1 CPT must proactively disclose to their immigration attorneys and prepare documentation, rather than assuming the issue is closed

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-exempt H-1B sponsorship is only available after you already hold H-1B status
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt employers can sponsor H-1B for F-1 OPT holders who have never held H-1B status — the cap-exempt path is available regardless of prior H-1B history
industry_realities.implication: F-1 OPT holders who have lost the lottery should actively seek cap-exempt employers rather than assuming they are locked out

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-exempt H-1B arrangements are a loophole or a gray area that USCIS frowns upon
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt petitions are a fully legitimate statutory pathway when properly structured; the risk arises only when the cap-exempt employment is not genuine. The strategy is legitimate when done correctly, per both the post author and corroborating commenters.
industry_realities.implication: Eligible candidates should not avoid this path out of unfounded concern about legitimacy; they should pursue it properly with legal counsel rather than avoiding it or improvising

industry_realities.common_belief: Official immigration guidance covers what you need to know to navigate the process
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple commenters, including an immigration lawyer, note that the emotional and psychological reality of the immigrant experience is almost completely absent from official guidance — the community fills this gap
industry_realities.implication: Students and visa holders need to actively seek peer community support alongside official/legal guidance, as the two sources address different but equally real needs

industry_realities.common_belief: The green card process takes a few years after getting H-1B, so getting H-1B is basically the finish line
industry_realities.actual_reality: For Indian and Chinese nationals, EB category backlogs mean GC wait times are 50+ years in some cases — H-1B is not a path to GC for these populations within a reasonable timeframe
industry_realities.implication: Country of birth must be part of the visa strategy calculation from day one, not discovered after years on H-1B

industry_realities.common_belief: You must stop working once your current EAD expires if the new one hasn't arrived
industry_realities.actual_reality: If the I-765 receipt notice was obtained before the current EAD's expiration date, the timely-filed rule allows continued employment — the receipt notice plus the expired EAD together serve as I-9 documentation
industry_realities.implication: Students should proactively educate their employer's HR about this rule before the expiry date to prevent unnecessary work stoppages driven by employer misunderstanding

industry_realities.common_belief: Once a PGWP policy is announced, the eligible program list is stable
industry_realities.actual_reality: The eligible program list has undergone significant changes within months of initial publication — approximately 178 programs were removed and 119 added in June 2025 alone, less than a year after the November 2024 announcement
industry_realities.implication: Students should not select programs based solely on a single snapshot of the eligible list; they should verify currency of the list and build redundancy into their immigration strategy

industry_realities.common_belief: H-1B lottery and STEM OPT are mutually exclusive or interfere with each other
industry_realities.actual_reality: H-1B registration and STEM OPT run concurrently without conflict. You can participate in the H-1B lottery while on STEM OPT, and if selected, transition to H-1B status on October 1st
industry_realities.implication: Students should register for H-1B every eligible year regardless of STEM OPT status; missing a lottery year wastes an attempt
industry_realities._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: EB-2 NIW requires an exceptionally high bar that most STEM students cannot meet
industry_realities.actual_reality: Under the Matter of Dhanasar three-prong test, many STEM graduates can qualify — the threshold is described by informed commenters as lower than commonly assumed for candidates with research backgrounds
industry_realities.implication: STEM candidates should not self-screen out of NIW without actually consulting an attorney or reviewing Dhanasar criteria; many may qualify without realizing it
industry_realities._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Provincial Nomination (PNP) is a backup plan for people who failed to get selected in the federal Express Entry draw
industry_realities.actual_reality: PNP is an active parallel strategy and for candidates below 450 CRS it is often the primary viable pathway; a nomination adds 600 points, effectively guaranteeing an ITA regardless of federal cutoffs
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should research PNP eligibility proactively and in parallel with building their EE profile, not as an afterthought after missing multiple federal draws
industry_realities._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: PGWP是普通工作签证，需要雇主担保才能工作
industry_realities.actual_reality: PGWP是开放工作许可（open work permit），持有人可在任何加拿大雇主工作，无需雇主担保
industry_realities.implication: 毕业生可以自由选择雇主、换工作，无需担心'绑定'特定雇主，这与美国OPT/H-1B的强雇主绑定逻辑完全不同
industry_realities._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Canada's parent reunion path is straightforward once you become a PR or citizen
industry_realities.actual_reality: The Parent and Grandparent Program (PGP) has annual intake caps distributed by lottery or first-come-first-served, with ~20–30% selection rates and 24–30 month processing after selection
industry_realities.implication: Plan for Super Visa as the primary co-residence tool; treat PGP as a probabilistic long-term bonus, not a reliable near-term solution
industry_realities._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: China nationals face the same green card backlog as Indian nationals in the US, making the wait essentially career-length
industry_realities.actual_reality: EB-2 China priority dates are approximately 5–10 years behind (circa 2019–2020 as of the post), while EB-2 India is 50+ years. Chinese nationals face a meaningful but potentially career-survivable wait — materially better than the Indian situation
industry_realities.implication: Chinese nationals should not dismiss the US green card path as hopelessly long — the wait is significant but plannable, especially combined with EB-1/NIW strategies
industry_realities._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: EB-1 is only for Nobel laureates or equivalent — not realistic for most people
industry_realities.actual_reality: EB-1A (Extraordinary Ability) and EB-1B (Outstanding Researcher) have achievable standards for many STEM professionals with publication records, citations, peer review participation, and high compensation relative to peers — and EB-1A requires no employer sponsorship or PERM
industry_realities.implication: Many China-born and India-born applicants who could qualify for EB-1 never pursue it because they self-screen out based on a misconception, missing a path with dramatically shorter backlogs
industry_realities._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: French language learning requires near-fluency and years of study to benefit your CRS
industry_realities.actual_reality: CLB 7 in French (approximately B1 level) is sufficient to claim the bilingualism bonus of up to 50 CRS points, and is achievable in approximately 3 months of focused study for motivated learners
industry_realities.implication: French study is a time-bounded, self-controlled strategy accessible to most applicants — not just heritage speakers or those with prior French exposure
industry_realities._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Express Entry is essentially a lottery like H-1B
industry_realities.actual_reality: Express Entry is a points-based system (CRS score) with transparent cutoffs; while cutoff scores fluctuate, the system rewards controllable factors (language scores, education, work experience, provincial nominations) unlike H-1B which is purely random
industry_realities.implication: Candidates can actively improve their CRS score and time their application strategically, unlike H-1B where the only lever is applying and hoping
industry_realities._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Canada's job market for tech and data science roles is comparable to the US
industry_realities.actual_reality: Community commenters noted that fields like data science have noticeably fewer open positions in Canada compared to the US, meaning conversion rates and total opportunity volume differ materially
industry_realities.implication: International graduates targeting data science or specialized tech roles in Canada should expect a longer search and may need to cast a wider geographic or role-type net
industry_realities._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: The L1-B company transfer to Canada is a trap or unusual workaround
industry_realities.actual_reality: Large tech companies routinely use L1-B as a structured H1B lottery bypass; the path is well-established — 1 year in Canada office → L1-B petition → return to US; the risk is the 5-year hard cap, not the path itself
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should evaluate the L1-B offer on its merits (green card filing commitment, 5-year planning) rather than rejecting it out of unfamiliarity

industry_realities.common_belief: EB-2 NIW and EB-2 PERM share the same priority date queue, so nationality backlog affects both equally
industry_realities.actual_reality: Both NIW and PERM fall under the EB-2 preference category and are subject to the same per-country annual limits and Visa Bulletin priority dates; they do share the same queue
industry_realities.implication: Chinese and Indian nationals pursuing NIW must plan for the same multi-year backlog as PERM — NIW does not provide a faster path to the actual green card for high-backlog nationalities
industry_realities._source: immigration_EB_2_NIW自己申请全攻略_I_140批准_分享经验.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: OPT unemployment violations only matter during the OPT period itself
industry_realities.actual_reality: OPT compliance history can be scrutinized during H-1B adjudication; a 4-month gap on OPT triggered an H-1B RFE for at least one commenter (134 likes)
industry_realities.implication: OPT violations have long-tail immigration consequences; students should treat OPT compliance as part of their overall immigration record, not a temporary status with no downstream effects
industry_realities._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: A job offer always adds 200 points to CRS
industry_realities.actual_reality: Job offer point value depends on NOC TEER category and draw type; in OP's case (TEER 0/1, federal draw) it added 50 points; the 200-point figure is widely cited but appears to apply in specific circumstances that many commenters could not clearly articulate
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should verify the exact point value of their specific job offer scenario against current IRCC rules rather than relying on community-circulated figures

industry_realities.common_belief: Once you have Canadian PR, you are free to work anywhere — including back in the US — without affecting your status
industry_realities.actual_reality: Canadian PR comes with a residency obligation (730 days in Canada per 5-year period) — spending 1-2 years working in the US can put PR status at risk if the obligation is not met
industry_realities.implication: Anyone considering using Canadian PR as a safety net while continuing to work primarily in the US needs to carefully plan around residency requirements or prioritize obtaining citizenship first

industry_realities.common_belief: You need to wait for the physical degree parchment before applying for PGWP
industry_realities.actual_reality: IRCC accepts the official registrar convocation or completion letter — the physical parchment is not required and waiting for it risks missing the 180-day deadline
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should apply as soon as they have the registrar letter, which is typically available shortly after convocation

industry_realities.common_belief: Submitting a case inquiry through the USCIS website will produce meaningful movement or a real response
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple applicants report receiving only boilerplate responses from USCIS contact center inquiries; DSO-submitted inquiries and congressional inquiries are reported to be significantly more effective for stalled cases
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should not treat the USCIS online inquiry tool as an effective escalation mechanism and should route serious delays through their DSO

industry_realities.common_belief: Applying to many jobs (80+) is a numbers game that will eventually yield results
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cold application response rate is approximately 2%; referral response rate is approximately 30% — networking is not supplementary to job search, it IS the job search for PGWP holders in Canada
industry_realities.implication: Time allocation should heavily favor networking and referral generation over cold application volume, especially after week 4 with poor results

industry_realities.common_belief: Recruiters understand the difference between OPT, STEM OPT, and H-1B
industry_realities.actual_reality: A high-upvote comment (387 upvotes) with multiple corroborating replies confirms that many recruiters conflate all immigration-related processes; candidates often need to educate recruiters on the spot that OPT is already their own work authorization
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should prepare a clear, jargon-free explanation of their current status and proactively manage recruiter misunderstanding rather than assuming the recruiter knows the difference

industry_realities.common_belief: University international offices provide complete and reliable guidance on OPT compliance rules
industry_realities.actual_reality: Multiple community members report that international offices glossed over or omitted the 90-day unemployment rule entirely
industry_realities.implication: Students should independently verify OPT compliance rules — particularly unemployment limits — with a licensed immigration attorney rather than relying solely on DSO guidance

industry_realities.common_belief: Cap-gap protection automatically applies to any F-1 student whose H-1B was filed for the upcoming fiscal year
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-gap protection requires that the H-1B petition was filed before the OPT EAD expired; students whose EAD expires before the April 1 filing window open have no cap-gap coverage
industry_realities.implication: Students must proactively check EAD expiration date against the H-1B filing window and plan status accordingly — automatic assumption of cap-gap coverage is a documented source of out-of-status incidents

industry_realities.common_belief: University hospital systems and large nonprofits automatically qualify as cap-exempt H-1B sponsors
industry_realities.actual_reality: Cap-exempt status depends on whether the organization's primary purpose is nonprofit research or higher education — hospital systems sometimes qualify and sometimes do not, requiring case-by-case legal analysis
industry_realities.implication: Do not assume a nonprofit employer is cap-exempt without confirming with an immigration attorney who has reviewed the organization's USCIS qualification

industry_realities.common_belief: Canada PR takes years and is nearly as uncertain as the US GC process
industry_realities.actual_reality: Express Entry can deliver PR invitations in 6-12 months for candidates with competitive CRS scores; PNP streams can offer faster routes for lower-scoring candidates in specific provinces
industry_realities.implication: Canada is not a fallback — it is a structurally faster and more predictable pathway for many international students, especially those from backlogged countries

industry_realities.common_belief: The H-1B master's cap gives a significant advantage, effectively guaranteeing selection
industry_realities.actual_reality: The 20,000 master's cap slots are drawn first, but unselected master's-cap applicants are then entered into the regular 65,000 pool — so master's degree holders get two chances per registration, not a guaranteed outcome
industry_realities.implication: Master's holders should still plan for lottery failure; the advantage is meaningful but not determinative given overall selection rates
industry_realities._source: zhihu_F_1留学生从OPT到H_1B_中间有哪些需要注意的关键节点.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Canada is a fallback or consolation option compared to staying in the US
industry_realities.actual_reality: Canada's Express Entry PR process is quota-free and points-based (transparent and predictable), PGWP provides open work authorization with no employer-tying anxiety, and many candidates reach PR within 2 years — outcomes that are structurally better than H-1B lottery dependence for some profiles
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should evaluate Canada as a primary strategic option, not only as a last resort after US paths are exhausted
industry_realities._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Overseas (non-Canadian) work experience does not meaningfully contribute to CRS
industry_realities.actual_reality: Foreign work experience in an equivalent NOC role contributes +13 points (1–2 years) or +25 points (3+ years) to CRS; this factor is frequently overlooked by candidates who assume only Canadian experience counts
industry_realities.implication: Candidates with substantial pre-immigration work experience should document and claim it correctly in their EE profile
industry_realities._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: 读完书就可以直接申请移民，工作经验要求不重要
industry_realities.actual_reality: CEC要求过去3年内有至少1年符合TEER 0/1/2/3类的全职加拿大工作经验，学历本身不直接授予移民资格
industry_realities.implication: 必须将读书阶段的实习/Co-op、毕业后的职位NOC分类纳入规划，不能等到毕业才考虑
industry_realities._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: The US and Canada are roughly equivalent for bringing parents to live with you
industry_realities.actual_reality: Canada's Super Visa gives parents a legal, renewable multi-year stay option (up to 2 years per entry) with 3–8 month processing — no equivalent mechanism exists in the US B-2 system; this is a structural advantage Canada holds for near-term parental co-residence
industry_realities.implication: For people who prioritize living with aging parents in the next 5–10 years, Canada's Super Visa represents a meaningfully better near-term path than the US
industry_realities._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Changing jobs during green card processing means starting over completely
industry_realities.actual_reality: An approved I-140 priority date can often be preserved even after changing employers under AC21 portability rules, though PERM may need to be redone — the situation is nuanced, not binary
industry_realities.implication: Fear of losing green card progress should not automatically prevent job changes — the actual impact depends on which stage the petition is at, but many people stay in bad jobs unnecessarily based on this misconception
industry_realities._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: For STEM OPT, I-983 can be submitted after starting work as long as the employer is E-Verify enrolled
industry_realities.actual_reality: I-983 and E-Verify enrollment must be in place before the first day of work, not during onboarding
industry_realities.implication: STEM OPT holders must proactively push employers to complete documentation before start date; beginning work early creates an unauthorized employment risk
industry_realities._source: us_visa_OPT期间换工作完整攻略_我踩过的所有坑.txt

industry_realities.common_belief: Federal all-program Express Entry draws are the only pathway worth tracking
industry_realities.actual_reality: Category-based draws (e.g., tech-specific) and PNP streams often clear at lower effective CRS scores; OP received ITA in a tech-specific draw at 489, and PNPs are highlighted as having lower requirements
industry_realities.implication: Applicants should diversify their draw monitoring strategy and evaluate PNP eligibility in parallel with federal Express Entry

industry_realities.common_belief: STEM OPT extension is the same burden on employers as H-1B sponsorship
industry_realities.actual_reality: STEM OPT extension requires the employer to fill out I-983 training plan forms and be E-Verify enrolled — a minor administrative ask — whereas H-1B requires employer petition, attorney fees of $3,000–$8,000, and USCIS processing; these are categorically different asks
industry_realities.implication: Candidates should not conflate STEM OPT extension with H-1B sponsorship when discussing employment flexibility with employers; framing STEM OPT as 'minimal paperwork' is accurate and reduces employer resistance

industry_realities.common_belief: Years of US work experience will transfer fully to Canadian Express Entry CRS scoring
industry_realities.actual_reality: Canadian work experience is valued more heavily in CRS calculations than foreign work experience; US years may contribute but not equivalently to Canadian work history
industry_realities.implication: Delaying a Canada pivot costs PR-relevant points; earlier entry into the Canadian workforce is strategically advantageous

junior_vs_senior_signals.dimension: Understanding the visa vs. status distinction
junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Assumes that having a valid F-1 or J-1 visa stamp in their passport means they are in valid immigration status and can work or travel freely.
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Tracks status compliance separately from visa stamp validity. Knows that status depends on SEVIS record activity, full-time enrollment, and authorized employment — and that an expired visa stamp does not affect status while inside the US.
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: Confusing visa and status leads students to make decisions (travel, drop courses, accept work) based on the wrong legal framework, resulting in status violations.

junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Waits for convocation or diploma before initiating PGWP application; treats graduation ceremony as the official start of the post-study clock
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Begins preparing PGWP application documents as soon as final grades are submitted; submits the moment the letter of completion arrives by email
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: The 180-day window is fixed and non-extendable; every day of unnecessary delay is a day of lost buffer against processing complications, document issues, or IRCC delays

junior_vs_senior_signals.dimension: How they evaluate a job offer on PGWP
junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Accepts first available professional job without checking NOC code or TEER level; assumes any office or technical job qualifies for CEC
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Looks up NOC code and TEER level before accepting; confirms the role is TEER 0/1/2/3 and that duties match the NOC description, not just the job title
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: A year in the wrong TEER category is a year of PGWP burned with zero PR progress — an irreversible cost in a time-constrained system

junior_vs_senior_signals.dimension: Understanding of job offer value post-2025
junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Focuses heavily on getting a Canadian job offer as the key immigration strategy, believing it will add 50–200 CRS points and accelerate Express Entry selection
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Knows that job offer CRS points were eliminated in March 2025; values a job offer only for income, CEC hour accumulation, and potential PNP stream eligibility — not as a direct CRS lever
junior_vs_senior_signals.why_it_matters: A student still operating on pre-2025 logic may accept a lower-paying offer expecting an immigration advantage that no longer exists, or fail to pursue higher-yield strategies like French language improvement or PNP

junior_vs_senior_signals.naive_pattern: Checks whether the school is on the IRCC designated learning institution list and stops there — assumes institution eligibility equals program eligibility
junior_vs_senior_signals.informed_pattern: Checks the specific program's CIP code against IRCC's current eligible fields list, confirms it was not removed in the June 2025 overhaul, and verifies that the credential type (degree vs. non-degree) determines whether field-of-study review is even required
