heuristics.rule: PGWP duration equals the length of the completed program, capped at 3 years
heuristics.condition: When evaluating which program to enroll in or complete in Canada
heuristics.action: Choose programs of 2+ years if maximizing PGWP duration is a priority, since any program over 2 years triggers the 3-year maximum
heuristics.because: Directly stated in the post: 2-year master = 3-year PGWP; 1-year Graduate Certificate = 1-year PGWP. This is the core mechanical rule the post is built around
heuristics._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

heuristics.rule: Canadian Express Entry is rule-based, not lottery-based — plan accordingly
heuristics.condition: When comparing US H-1B lottery uncertainty to Canadian immigration pathways
heuristics.action: Treat CRS score optimization as a controllable variable unlike H-1B lottery; build a plan around hitting a target CRS score
heuristics.because: Poster explicitly contrasted H-1B lottery (random) with Express Entry ITA (score-based threshold), and this framing received high engagement
heuristics._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

heuristics.rule: Prioritize referral and direct application channels over mass-apply platforms
heuristics.condition: When conducting a first job search in Canada as an international graduate
heuristics.action: Rank effort investment as: internal referrals first, company direct website second, LinkedIn outreach to hiring managers third, career fairs fourth, and Easy Apply platforms last
heuristics.because: Original poster explicitly ranked these by effectiveness from personal experience, and community engagement did not contradict this ordering
heuristics._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

heuristics.rule: Have an explicit conversation with your employer about contingency plans before the third lottery cycle
heuristics.condition: When you have already missed the H-1B lottery twice and are approaching a third attempt...
heuristics.action: Proactively discuss with HR whether the company can support a transfer to a Canadian office or alternative visa route if the third lottery also fails
heuristics.because: The poster reports doing exactly this and achieving a concrete agreement; waiting until after a third failure leaves no time to execute alternatives before status expires
heuristics._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

heuristics.rule: Optimize spouse language score as a secondary lever before other tactics
heuristics.condition: When you have a spouse or common-law partner and they have not taken a language test
heuristics.action: Have spouse take IELTS and aim for at least CLB 7
heuristics.because: CLB 7 from a spouse added ~20 points in OP's case; corroborated by comment [3] (267 upvotes) calling it a 'sleeper' that commenters had not accounted for

heuristics.rule: Use PNPs as a fallback if your federal CRS score is not clearing Express Entry draws
heuristics.condition: When a candidate's CRS score is not high enough to receive an ITA through federal Express Entry draws
heuristics.action: Actively research and pursue Provincial Nominee Programs — particularly Alberta and Saskatchewan, which have been described as aggressive in nominations
heuristics.because: The original poster identifies PNPs as a 'genuine backdoor' for candidates whose federal score isn't clearing, corroborated by the upvoted comment asking about Saskatchewan and Alberta PNP requirements

heuristics.rule: Research and concretize your Plan B rather than leaving it as a vague worst-case scenario
heuristics.condition: When the possibility of returning home is functioning as a catastrophic narrative driving desperation in your immigration decisions
heuristics.action: Actively research what returning home would realistically look like — job market, salary, quality of life — and write it down concretely
heuristics.because: OP and a top commenter (upvotes: 176) both reported that converting a vague catastrophe into a concrete realistic alternative significantly reduced desperation and improved decision-making clarity

heuristics.rule: Use the official registrar convocation or completion letter — not a program completion email from a professor
heuristics.condition: When gathering documents to prove program completion for PGWP...
heuristics.action: Request the formal letter from the registrar's office before applying
heuristics.because: IRCC explicitly requires the official registrar document; OP almost made this mistake themselves and flagged it as a near-failure point (rank=6)

heuristics.rule: Explore within-program changes before switching programs
heuristics.condition: When the academic issue (e.g., research focus, supervisor) can potentially be resolved without a formal program change
heuristics.action: Work with graduate advisors to change focus, supervisor, or track within the existing program rather than officially switching programs
heuristics.because: The OP achieved the same academic outcome (changed research focus) without losing PGWP eligibility by staying in the same program

heuristics.rule: Shift from cold applications to active networking after 4 weeks of poor response
heuristics.condition: When cold application response rate is below 5% after 4+ weeks
heuristics.action: Pivot to local meetups, tech events, and direct relationship-building to generate referrals
heuristics.because: Referral response rate (~30%) is roughly 15x cold application rate (~2%), as corroborated by multiple commenters; Canadian hiring networks are locally weighted

heuristics.rule: Choose Canada program length strategically to maximize PGWP duration
heuristics.condition: When selecting a Canadian graduate program as an international student...
heuristics.action: Choose a program of at least 2 years to qualify for a 3-year PGWP (the maximum), rather than a 1-year diploma that yields only a 1-year PGWP
heuristics.because: PGWP length equals program length, capped at 3 years; a 1-year program gives only 1 year of open work authorization, severely compressing the timeline to PR

heuristics.rule: Apply for PGWP as early as eligible — do not delay post-graduation
heuristics.condition: When a student has already met PGWP eligibility requirements and is approaching or past graduation
heuristics.action: Submit the PGWP application as soon as possible after graduation, without waiting
heuristics.because: A real case in this thread shows a student whose PGWP was approved under old rules because she applied before new policy took effect; waiting increases risk of being caught by subsequent policy changes

heuristics.rule: If your CRS is below 450 federally, pivot strategy to Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) rather than waiting for a federal draw
heuristics.condition: When a candidate's CRS score is below 450 and short-term score improvement is limited
heuristics.action: Prioritize learning French for +50 points, research target-province PNP streams, consult a provincial immigration advisor, and continue accumulating Canadian work experience

heuristics.rule: 法语CLB 7是CRS加分ROI最高的单一动作（约+50分）

heuristics.rule: PGWP holders must proactively explain their open work permit status to Canadian employers
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder is job-searching in Canada and an employer's HR sees 'work permit' on paperwork...

heuristics.rule: For India-born applicants, EB-2 India wait time now exceeds most reasonable career planning horizons — treat Canada PR as a parallel or alternative path, not a fallback

heuristics.rule: Monitor category-based draws (tech, healthcare, etc.) separately from all-program draws if you are from a high-competition background
heuristics.condition: When your CRS is below the all-programs cutoff (often 530+ for Indian/Chinese applicants) but above category draw cutoffs
heuristics.action: Track IRCC's category-specific invitation rounds rather than waiting for an all-program draw
heuristics.because: The poster explicitly noted that category draws (e.g., tech worker) have significantly lower CRS cutoffs than all-program draws, and this is what ultimately got them an ITA at ~490

heuristics.rule: If you have previously submitted biometrics, expect a faster PGWP processing timeline due to biometric exemption
heuristics.condition: When you have already provided biometrics during a prior Canadian visa/permit process...
heuristics.action: Note your biometric exemption status in your application; do not plan extra time for biometric appointment delays

heuristics.rule: Explore intra-program flexibility before switching programs
heuristics.condition: When a student wants to change research direction or specialization but is enrolled in a multi-year program
heuristics.action: Negotiate with supervisor and departmental advisor to change direction within the existing program rather than formally switching to a new program

heuristics.condition: When enrolled in a Canadian program that offers co-op opportunities
heuristics.action: Prioritize completing at least one co-op placement before graduating

heuristics.rule: Target a qualifying job offer as a major CRS lever if below cutoff
heuristics.condition: When stuck below 470 and no current job offer exists
heuristics.action: Actively seek a job offer from an employer willing to submit LMIA or qualifying offer, particularly in TEER 0/1 NOC categories
heuristics.because: A qualifying job offer added 50 points in OP's case (TEER 0/1 federal draw level); OP moved from 460 to 491 using this in combination with French

heuristics.rule: Treat Canadian PR as a timing-sensitive milestone, not just a score milestone
heuristics.condition: When planning your Express Entry application timeline
heuristics.action: Monitor draw cutoffs over time rather than assuming a static CRS score is sufficient — a 480 score may clear one draw and not another
heuristics.because: The original poster explicitly notes that draw cutoffs fluctuate, meaning score alone does not guarantee invitation — timing of draws matters independently

heuristics.rule: Learn the immigration system mechanics deeply enough to identify which factors you can actually control
heuristics.condition: When CRS score fluctuations and draw uncertainty are causing generalized anxiety
heuristics.action: Study National Draw Profiles and identify the specific CRS components within your control (language scores, additional education, etc.) and act on those
heuristics.because: OP reported that understanding controllable factors restored a sense of agency, directly reducing anxiety

heuristics.rule: Check biometrics validity before applying — valid biometrics from a study permit do not need to be redone
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP applicant already has unexpired biometrics on file from a prior study permit application...
heuristics.action: Confirm biometrics are still valid and note this in application; do not assume a new biometrics appointment is needed
heuristics.because: Corroborated by OP's timeline (no new biometrics needed) and comment rank=11 (upvotes=134) confirming the same experience

heuristics.rule: Consecutive programs at DLIs can be combined for PGWP length, but only with no meaningful gap
heuristics.condition: When a student has completed or will complete multiple programs sequentially
heuristics.action: Ensure programs are consecutive (typical academic gaps like April graduation to September start may be acceptable, but verify with IRCC guidance), both at DLIs, and combined length is accurate
heuristics.because: IRCC allows combining consecutive program lengths for PGWP calculation, but a gap between programs risks disqualifying the combination

heuristics.rule: Target mid-size companies (50–200 employees) in Canadian tech corridors over large brand-name employers
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder is searching for first Canadian tech job
heuristics.action: Focus applications on mid-size companies in Toronto/Waterloo rather than Amazon, Google, or Shopify
heuristics.because: Large Canadian tech companies (Shopify, etc.) are flooded with applications; mid-size companies have lower applicant volumes and are more likely to engage — confirmed by multiple commenters

heuristics.rule: If you've lost the H-1B lottery 2+ times, model a Canada pivot seriously
heuristics.condition: When an F-1/STEM OPT holder has lost H-1B multiple consecutive times...
heuristics.action: Evaluate a move to Canada via PGWP + Express Entry rather than continuing to lottery-loop
heuristics.because: Multiple community members corroborate switching after repeated H-1B losses and obtaining Canadian PR in under a year; the compounding probability of failure grows with each additional lottery cycle

heuristics.rule: Preserve all enrollment and program-change documentation
heuristics.condition: When a student has changed programs during their studies and plans to apply for PGWP
heuristics.action: Retain original enrollment letter (showing initial enrollment date), program-change approval documents, and full-time status records
heuristics.because: Commenters with direct experience advise this as a practical safeguard for demonstrating grandfathering eligibility to IRCC

heuristics.rule: Have your spouse take a language test (CLB 7) to gain ~20 additional CRS points for the principal applicant
heuristics.condition: When the principal applicant has a spouse and the couple's combined CRS is borderline
heuristics.action: Have the accompanying spouse prepare for and pass a recognized language test to CLB 7
heuristics.because: Multiple comments confirm spousal language score contributes approximately +20 points to the principal applicant's CRS, a low-effort incremental gain
heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

heuristics.rule: CRS分数不足时，优先考虑Alberta或Saskatchewan PNP而非继续等待联邦抽签
heuristics.condition: 当你的CRS分数比联邦综合类门槛低50-80分，且愿意在特定省份工作时
heuristics.action: 在AAIP或Saskatchewan PNP目标省份找工作，通过省提名路径申请PR

heuristics._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

heuristics.rule: Accumulating a second year of Canadian work experience adds more CRS points than the first year
heuristics.condition: When you are on PGWP and approaching or completing your first year of Canadian work experience
heuristics.action: Factor time as an asset — continuing to work toward year 2 yields additional CRS points incrementally
heuristics.because: The poster explicitly identified this as a scoring lever ('the second year gives more points than the first'), confirmed by CRS table structure
heuristics._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

heuristics.rule: For applicants from high-cutoff countries (India, China), treat French bilingual and Canadian job offer as the two primary levers
heuristics.condition: When you are from India or China and CRS is stuck in the 460–480 range
heuristics.action: Prioritize French language study and securing a qualifying job offer above all other score optimization tactics
heuristics.because: OP explicitly states these are the two biggest real levers for high-ITA-score country nationals; corroborated by community agreement in top comments

heuristics.rule: Use the precise framing: 'I can work for any Canadian employer without employer-specific authorization. You do not need to sponsor me or file any applications. I am pursuing PR independently.'
heuristics.condition: When communicating work authorization status to Canadian employers unfamiliar with PGWP
heuristics.action: Deploy this specific language in cover letters, recruiter calls, and interview intros
heuristics.because: Generic references to 'work permit' trigger sponsorship assumptions; spelling out exactly what the employer does NOT need to do removes the barrier

heuristics.rule: Plan CRS improvement on a multi-year timeline rather than panicking in year one of PGWP
heuristics.condition: When a PGWP holder's CRS is insufficient in their first year of Canadian work experience
heuristics.action: Map out the timeline: one more year of Canadian work experience plus a French test can close a significant gap; do not make rash decisions under pressure
heuristics.because: Comment with 423 upvotes explicitly describes candidates who were short in year one but sufficient by year two due to +work experience and French score, concluding 'don't panic, plan on the timeline'
heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

heuristics.rule: Consider PNP streams if federal Express Entry score is not clearing
heuristics.condition: When CRS is insufficient for federal all-program draws
heuristics.action: Investigate provincial nominee programs with lower effective CRS requirements, especially those tied to provincial job offers or sector-specific streams
heuristics.because: Comment [7] (145 upvotes) notes PNPs often have lower CRS requirements than federal draws and are worth pursuing if federal score is not competitive

heuristics.rule: Research your NOC code's PNP stream eligibility before you need the nomination, not after
heuristics.condition: When a candidate is building their immigration strategy and has a specific job/NOC code
heuristics.action: Look up which provincial PNP streams (e.g., BC Tech Pilot, Ontario Human Capital Priority) target your specific NOC code early in the process
heuristics.because: Comment with 356 upvotes warns against waiting until nomination stage to check stream requirements, as different provinces have different occupational category requirements
heuristics._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: Study permit application date relative to November 1, 2024
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Students who applied for their study permit on or after November 1, 2024 face an additional field-of-study eligibility requirement that earlier cohorts do not; this can render a completed degree worthless for PGWP purposes
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the original study permit application submission date (not approval date) against November 1, 2024; then verify the program's CIP code against IRCC's current eligible fields list

decision_factors.factor: TEER classification of your Canadian job
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether your work experience counts toward CEC at all; also sets the language benchmark you must meet
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Search the NOC database at canada.ca using your job title and duties; confirm TEER level matches 0, 1, 2, or 3

decision_factors.factor: CEC eligibility vs. FSWP eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC candidates compete in a smaller, often lower-cut-off pool and earn additional CRS points for Canadian experience; FSWP candidates compete globally without that advantage
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm whether candidate has 1,560 hours of TEER 0–3 skilled work in Canada within the past 36 months, excluding co-op and self-employment

decision_factors.factor: Degree level (university degree vs. non-degree college/diploma)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: University degree holders are exempt from the field-of-study requirement entirely; college/diploma graduates must meet it — this creates a binary eligibility difference, not a spectrum
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether the credential is a bachelor's, master's, or PhD awarded by a university — graduate certificates and graduate diplomas do NOT qualify as university degrees for PGWP purposes even if offered at a university

decision_factors.factor: Program type: university degree vs. diploma/certificate
decision_factors.why_it_matters: University degree students (bachelor's and above) retain PGWP eligibility regardless of field; diploma/certificate students are subject to the post-June 2025 eligible fields list, making program selection a direct immigration variable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm the credential type with the institution (degree vs. diploma/certificate) and check the current IRCC eligible fields list for diploma programs

decision_factors.factor: Current CRS Score
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines how urgently a PNP nomination is needed and which streams are accessible — lower scores make PNP the primary (not backup) strategy
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate your current CRS score using the IRCC tool; compare against historical ITA cutoffs for CEC and Canadian Experience Class draws; if 50+ points below recent cutoffs, treat PNP as essential

decision_factors.factor: Career compounding trajectory by market
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The ceiling, meritocracy, and compensation structure differ significantly between home country and destination country for specific roles — choosing the wrong market means slower career velocity for years
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research senior-level roles in your field in both markets: compensation, equity availability, decision-making autonomy, and whether your nationality is an asset or a disadvantage at the top

decision_factors.factor: Study permit validity at time of PGWP application
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A lapsed study permit at the moment of submission is a hard disqualifier — there is no grace period
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare study permit expiry date against your intended application submission date; flag any gap greater than zero days as a critical risk requiring legal advice

decision_factors.factor: Current CRS cutoff scores in Express Entry CEC draws
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether you will receive an ITA passively or need to pursue PNP — the single biggest strategic fork in the road
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor IRCC draw results on the official site; compare your projected CRS score against the last 6–12 CEC-specific draw cutoffs

decision_factors.factor: Current CRS score relative to recent draw cut-offs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A CRS score below recent cut-offs means indefinite waiting unless the candidate improves a scoreable factor or targets category-based draws
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use IRCC's CRS tool; compare current score against the last 6 months of general and category-based draw cut-offs from the IRCC website

decision_factors.factor: Program's CIP code vs. program name
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP eligibility is determined by CIP code, not the descriptive name of a program — two programs with similar names can have different CIP codes and thus different eligibility statuses
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Request the CIP code directly from the institution's registrar and cross-reference with IRCC's published eligible programs list; do not rely on the institution's marketing materials

decision_factors.factor: Study permit application date relative to June 25, 2025
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines which PGWP eligibility rules apply — students who applied before this date are protected under previous rules; those who applied after must meet the new program field requirements
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the date on your study permit application confirmation from IRCC; this is a binary threshold, not a spectrum

decision_factors.factor: Existence and Location of Canadian Job Offer
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Most PNP streams (BC, Alberta, Saskatchewan) require a provincial job offer; Ontario HCP does not — this single factor determines which streams you can access
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm whether your job offer is full-time, indeterminate or 1-year+, and from an employer in a province with an active tech PNP stream; verify the NOC code maps to eligible occupations

decision_factors.factor: Purchasing power vs. absolute salary
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A high nominal salary in a destination country can generate less actual savings and wealth than a lower nominal salary in a lower cost-of-living home market
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate post-tax take-home in each market, subtract realistic cost of living, and compare savings rate and investment opportunity — not gross salary

decision_factors.factor: Full-time enrollment status throughout the program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A single semester (other than the final term) where you were enrolled part-time can disqualify you from PGWP entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review official enrollment history with the registrar for every semester; flag any semester listed as part-time and consult an RCIC before applying

decision_factors.factor: PGWP duration remaining at point of CEC eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: You must maintain valid status throughout the PR process; if PGWP expires before PR is approved, you face a status gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate PGWP expiry date; subtract expected PR processing time (~6 months post-ITA); confirm you have sufficient buffer

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency level
decision_factors.why_it_matters: French skills at CLB 7+ add up to 50 CRS points and access to French-stream draws with consistently lower cut-offs — one of the highest-yield improvable factors
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check current French test scores (TEF Canada / TCF Canada); estimate cost and time investment to reach CLB 7 vs. CRS score gap to recent cut-off

decision_factors.factor: Study permit submission date relative to November 1, 2024 and June 25, 2025 cutoffs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Grandfathering rules mean students who submitted before each cutoff are assessed under the rules in effect at that time — the same program can be PGWP-eligible or ineligible depending solely on when the permit was submitted
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Pull the IRCC application submission confirmation (not approval date, not start date — submission date) and map it against both cutoffs

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency (current and achievable level)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: French at CLB 7+ unlocks both additional CRS points and access to French-language draws with cut-offs 90–150 points lower than general draws — this single factor can be the difference between qualifying and not qualifying
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take a French placement test and estimate realistic time to CLB 7 given current level; compare the CRS gain against other available levers

decision_factors.factor: Province of Employment or Intended Settlement
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Provincial nominations carry an expectation of settlement in that province — applying to a province you have no intention of staying in creates compliance risk and ethical exposure
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess where your employer, social network, and housing situation are anchored; avoid nominating provinces purely for immigration convenience if you plan to immediately relocate

decision_factors.factor: Immigration stability tax
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The mental energy, financial cost, and opportunity cost of maintaining and transitioning visa status is a real drag on career performance and life quality that is systematically underweighted in stay-vs-return decisions
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Estimate hours per year spent on immigration administration, attorney fees, and enumerate specific opportunities foregone due to visa restrictions (entrepreneurship, job switching, travel)

decision_factors.factor: Language test score and recency
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Minimum CLB 5 is required for college graduates; scores older than 2 years are rejected regardless of score level
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm test score meets CLB 5 equivalency for the specific test taken (CELPIP, IELTS General, or PTE Core) and that the test date is within 2 years of the intended PGWP submission date

decision_factors.factor: Language test scores (CLB level achieved)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Sets your CEC eligibility floor and directly adds CRS points — higher scores above the minimum significantly boost your CRS
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take IELTS General or CELPIP and convert to CLB; determine whether you meet the minimum for your TEER level and whether retaking could raise CRS materially

decision_factors.factor: Age trajectory
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CRS awards maximum age points (110 without spouse) between ages 20–29; points decline after 29 — waiting to improve other factors may cost age points that cannot be recovered
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate current age points and projected age points at the earliest realistic ITA date; if declining, factor the net CRS impact into strategy timing

decision_factors.factor: Program duration relative to the 2-year threshold
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Programs of 2+ years yield the maximum 3-year PGWP; programs under 2 years yield a shorter permit, directly reducing the window for accumulating CEC-eligible work experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate total program length in months; if choosing between programs, assess whether extending by a semester crosses the 2-year threshold

decision_factors.factor: Age at time of Express Entry application
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CRS age points peak at 20–29 and decrease progressively after 30, accelerating after 35 — a 2-year delay in filing can cost 15–30+ CRS points that cannot be recovered
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the CRS calculator to model your score at current age vs. age at graduation vs. 1 year post-graduation; identify whether urgency is warranted

decision_factors.factor: NOC TEER Category of Current Job
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Only TEER 0/1/2/3 experience counts toward CEC eligibility and most PNP tech streams; TEER 4/5 work does not qualify
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up your job title on the IRCC NOC database and verify the TEER level; confirm with your employer's HR that the job duties match the NOC description

decision_factors.factor: Employer sponsorship execution capacity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: An employer's stated willingness to sponsor is not the same as their operational capacity to execute — failure at the execution level has the same legal consequence as no sponsorship at all
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask for historical sponsorship data, the name of their immigration counsel, and their process for tracking employee status deadlines; evaluate the sophistication of their answers

decision_factors.factor: Whether the student worked more than authorized hours during studies
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Exceeding authorized work hours as a student disqualifies you from the 'work while you wait' provision — meaning you cannot legally work during PGWP processing even after submitting
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review pay stubs, T4s, or employer records against the 20-hour weekly limit that applied while school was in session; flag any weeks of overwork

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience accumulated
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CRS points for Canadian experience jump at 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5-year thresholds — timing your application around these milestones changes your competitiveness
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate your current hours worked; project when you'll cross the next experience-year threshold; weigh waiting vs. applying now

decision_factors.factor: Occupation alignment with category-based draw criteria
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Category-based draws for healthcare, STEM, trades, and French speakers have lower cut-offs — candidates in these sectors have a structural advantage unavailable to others
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Cross-reference your NOC code against IRCC's published category-based draw criteria; monitor draw history to see if your sector has received targeted draws

decision_factors.factor: Language test score readiness (CLB 7 for university, CLB 5 for college)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: As of November 2024, language proof is mandatory for PGWP — a student who graduates but cannot meet the benchmark cannot receive PGWP regardless of program or institution eligibility
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take a diagnostic test early in the final year of study; if scores are borderline, schedule the official test with buffer time for a retest before graduation

decision_factors.factor: NOC TEER classification of available employment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC requires 1 year of Canadian work experience in TEER 0–3; time spent in TEER 4–5 roles does not count, and students who discover this late must work additional time in eligible roles before applying
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the NOC code of any job offer using the ESDC Job Bank NOC search tool and confirm its TEER level before accepting

decision_factors.factor: PGWP Remaining Validity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The entire PNP + PR process can take 12–24 months; if your PGWP expires before PR is granted, you need a bridge work permit — planning must account for this timeline gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate expiry date of PGWP; if less than 18 months remain, either accelerate PNP application or plan for a bridging open work permit application once PR is in process

decision_factors.factor: Program type and its downstream immigration eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The program you enroll in determines which post-graduation work authorizations and pathways are available — this must be assessed before enrollment, not after
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Before enrolling, confirm whether the program qualifies for STEM OPT extension (US), PGWP eligibility (Canada), and what visa categories it unlocks or forecloses

decision_factors.factor: Spouse's occupational TEER level and PGWP remaining validity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: As of January 21, 2025, spousal open work permits are only available if the PGWP holder works in TEER 0/1 or select TEER 2/3 roles AND has at least 16 months remaining on the permit
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the NOC code of the PGWP holder's job and confirm TEER classification; calculate months remaining on PGWP at the time the spouse would apply

decision_factors.factor: Province of residence and occupation demand by province
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PNP streams vary dramatically by province and occupation; your province determines which PNP pathways are realistically available to you
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review active PNP streams for your province and occupation on official provincial immigration websites; note expression of interest score thresholds

decision_factors.factor: Provincial Nominee Program (PNP) eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A provincial nomination is worth 600 CRS points — the single most powerful factor outside of core profile; it can override an otherwise uncompetitive CRS score entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review each province's PNP streams for your occupation, work location, and employer; assess whether a 'job offer required' stream is accessible given current employment

decision_factors.factor: Field of study alignment with IRCC's eligible fields list (post-June 2025 version)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 178 programs were removed in June 2025 — students in removed fields who do not have grandfathering protection have lost PGWP eligibility entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the current IRCC eligible fields list (not blog posts or institution websites) against your program's CIP code; re-verify if you consumed information published before June 25, 2025

decision_factors.factor: Spousal education and language scores
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For married or common-law candidates, a spouse's CLB 5+ language score and education credentials add meaningful CRS points (estimated 10–40 depending on levels) — potentially closing the gap to an ITA
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have your spouse take IELTS/TEF and run the CRS calculator with and without spousal factors to quantify the actual point difference

decision_factors.factor: Language Proficiency Score (CLB Level)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Most PNP streams require CLB 7+; falling below this threshold disqualifies candidates from streams that would otherwise fit them perfectly
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Take IELTS or CELPIP and calculate CLB equivalent; if below CLB 7, prioritize retesting before applying to streams where this is a hard cutoff

decision_factors.factor: Family and relational factors
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Spouse immigration status, aging parents, and local family assets change the true cost-benefit of staying abroad in ways that are not visible in career-only analysis
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Explicitly map out each family member's situation: can a spouse work legally, what are the implications for aging parent care, does the family hold illiquid assets (property) in one country

decision_factors.factor: Medical exam history and intended occupation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Certain occupations (healthcare, childcare, agricultural work) and prior residence in designated countries trigger mandatory medical exam requirements that add cost and time to the application
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check IRCC's designated country list and intended job duties against the public-health-contact criteria; budget extra time and $85+ in biometric fees if applicable

decision_factors.factor: Intent to live outside Quebec
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC does not apply to Quebec; students planning to stay in Quebec must use Quebec's separate immigration system (QSWP/PEQ)
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm current and intended province of work and residence before selecting the CEC pathway

decision_factors.factor: Canadian study credential length and level
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian study adds 15–30 bonus CRS points; a 2+ year program adds more than a 1-year certificate — this is a fixed historical factor that is already earned
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm program length on transcript; a degree/diploma of 2+ years earns 30 points, 1-year program earns 15 points

decision_factors.factor: Whether a job offer adds CRS points (post-March 2025 rule)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Job offer no longer adds CRS points as of March 2025 — this changes the strategic weight of securing employer sponsorship during the PGWP period vs. focusing purely on CRS score improvement
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Treat job offer primarily as a source of NOC-eligible work experience for CEC, not as a CRS booster; recalibrate Express Entry strategy accordingly

decision_factors.factor: Active PNP streams for your occupation and province of residence
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points, making PNP availability a faster pathway than federal Express Entry for candidates with sub-520 base scores
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor the immigration portal of provinces where you live or work; check NOC-specific draw histories to assess realistic access

decision_factors.factor: Tech vs. Non-Tech Field
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Tech workers have significantly more dedicated PNP pathways (BC Tech Stream, Alberta Accelerated Tech, Saskatchewan Tech Talent) compared to other fields
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether your NOC code appears on the eligible occupation lists for tech-specific PNP streams; if not, you must rely on general skilled worker streams with less targeted access

decision_factors.factor: Emotional honesty about motivation for staying
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Decisions driven by fear of return being perceived as failure lead to suboptimal long-run choices; identifying the true motivation changes the analysis
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask directly: 'Would I make the same decision if returning home were socially celebrated rather than stigmatized?' If the answer changes, the decision is emotionally driven

decision_factors.factor: TRV or eTA validity for re-entry after travel
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Traveling while PGWP is pending without a valid TRV or eTA means you cannot legally re-enter Canada until PGWP is approved — potentially months of being stranded abroad
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check passport for current TRV or eTA expiry before booking any international travel post-PGWP submission

decision_factors.factor: Sibling's Canadian citizenship or permanent residence status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Having a sibling who is a Canadian citizen or PR adds 15 CRS points — a small but potentially decisive margin near cut-off thresholds
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm sibling's immigration status; if eligible, include in Express Entry profile

decision_factors.factor: Foreign credential recognition (ECA status)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Foreign education credentials must be assessed by a designated ECA organization to receive CRS education points; unassessed credentials receive no points and cannot be appealed retroactively after filing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Initiate ECA assessment through WES, ICAS, or other IRCC-designated bodies as early as possible — processing takes 3–6 months

decision_factors.factor: Long-term immigration pathway security
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A job with better long-term immigration security (clear path to permanent residence) is often worth meaningful near-term trade-offs in salary or title because visa instability has compounding costs
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map out the full immigration pathway from current status to permanent residence for each option; identify which steps depend on lotteries, employer cooperation, or rule changes

decision_factors.factor: Job offer presence post-March 2025
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A Canadian job offer no longer adds CRS points as of March 25, 2025 — its value is now purely for income stability, language practice, and experience accumulation toward CEC
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Evaluate job offers on career development and CEC hour accumulation merits only; remove CRS point calculations from job offer negotiation strategy

decision_factors.factor: Program duration (relative to 2-year threshold for maximum PGWP)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Programs under 8 months generate no PGWP; programs 8 months to under 2 years generate a PGWP equal to program length; programs 2+ years generate the maximum 3-year PGWP — duration directly determines how much time you have to accumulate CEC-eligible work experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate the expected PGWP duration from your program length and model whether it provides enough time (1 year minimum) to qualify for CEC before expiry

decision_factors.factor: Availability of category-based Express Entry draws for your occupation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Category draws have lower CRS cutoffs than all-program draws, making them the primary pathway for applicants from high-competition backgrounds who cannot reach 530+
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check IRCC's historical draw data for your NOC category (tech, healthcare, etc.) and compare cutoff scores to your current CRS
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Program length
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The length of your Canadian study program directly determines the maximum duration of PGWP you can receive (up to 3 years for programs of 2+ years)
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check your official program duration as listed on your study permit and transcripts; confirm with registrar if your program was non-standard length (e.g., 1.5 years)
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

decision_factors.factor: Program duration at graduation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP duration is directly tied to the length of the program completed — switching from a 2-year to a 1-year program can reduce PGWP by 2 years, which cascades into less Canadian work experience and lower Express Entry CRS scores
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Look up the official program duration on the school's website and IRCC's PGWP eligibility rules; confirm whether the target program is 1-year, 2-year, or longer
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

decision_factors.factor: Company size and HR sophistication regarding immigration status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Large employers (e.g. Shopify, RBC, TD) typically have HR teams familiar with PGWP; smaller companies often do not, creating friction that can kill applications before a hiring decision is made
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research target company size; if mid-size or smaller, plan to include a written work authorization statement on resume and prepare a verbal explanation for screening calls
decision_factors._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employer willingness to support alternative visa or relocation pathways
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the employer will sponsor a Canadian office transfer or explore alternative visa categories, the stakes of each individual H-1B lottery round are lower and contingency planning becomes actionable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have a direct conversation with HR before the third lottery cycle; ask specifically whether the company has a Canadian entity and what their policy is on cross-border transfers for visa-challenged employees
decision_factors._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency (TEF Canada score)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Bilingual Advantage under Express Entry yields approximately 50 additional CRS points — the single largest discretionary lever available to most applicants
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Estimate time to achieve B1 level; TEF Canada is noted as more lenient than DELF; English speakers can realistically reach B1 in 3–6 months of consistent study

decision_factors.factor: Country of origin and applicable green card backlog
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For Indian nationals, the EB-2 backlog makes a US green card functionally inaccessible within a working career timeline, fundamentally changing the US vs. Canada calculus
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the current USCIS Visa Bulletin for EB-2 India priority dates and compare to your graduation or work start year

decision_factors.factor: PGWP open permit status awareness
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP holders have an open work permit and can change jobs freely — but many holders do not act on this freedom because of anxiety-driven misunderstanding of their actual constraints
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm your permit type: an open work permit has no employer restriction; an employer-specific permit does. PGWP is open.

decision_factors.factor: Program length and type (including co-op/integrated components)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP duration is tied to the length of the program completed; whether co-op months count toward the total affects how long the permit will be
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with your institution whether the co-op or work-integrated learning component is formally part of the degree program, and whether the completion letter reflects the full program duration

decision_factors.factor: PGWP length resulting from completed program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP length directly determines how many years of Canadian work experience a graduate can accumulate, which feeds into Express Entry CRS score and PR eligibility timelines
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify the length of the program you will actually complete; check IRCC's PGWP rules for single vs. combined programs; model out the PR timeline difference between PGWP lengths

decision_factors.factor: Work authorization framing clarity
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Employers who misread PGWP as requiring sponsorship will silently filter out candidates before a single conversation occurs
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether your resume and outreach explicitly state 'open work permit,' 'no sponsorship required,' and 'pursuing PR independently'

decision_factors.factor: Original enrollment date relative to PGWP policy cutoff
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether a student is grandfathered under old rules, regardless of any subsequent program changes
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare the date on the original enrollment letter against the policy cutoff (2024-11-01 for the 2024 PGWP reform)

decision_factors.factor: Age
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Age contributes up to 110 CRS points and decreases each year after 35; it is the only major factor that cannot be improved and only worsens over time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check current age against CRS age table: 18–35 earns maximum points, declining incrementally each year, with 45+ earning only a few points
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: CRS分数区间与当前抽签分数线的差距
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 决定走联邦Express Entry还是转向省提名（PNP）路径，直接影响PR时间线
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 建立EE Profile后查看当前CRS评分，对比近期联邦综合类抽签分数（评论显示490-530波动），判断差距是否超过50分
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Spouse's English language ability
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A spouse who achieves CLB 7 can add up to ~20 CRS points to a combined profile, a meaningful gain requiring no external party cooperation
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Have spouse take a practice IELTS to estimate their current level; assess feasibility of reaching CLB 7 within a reasonable study timeline
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Prior biometrics enrollment
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Having previously submitted biometrics removes a processing step that can add weeks to PGWP timelines
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check your IRCC account or prior permit documents to confirm whether biometrics are on file and still valid
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

decision_factors.factor: Alternative PR pathways available
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the student plans to pursue PR through employer sponsorship or a Provincial Nominee Program rather than Express Entry based on Canadian work experience, the PGWP duration loss matters less
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess whether the target industry/employer offers LMIA-based sponsorship or whether a relevant PNP stream is accessible regardless of work experience duration
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

decision_factors.factor: Salary gap between US and Canada (especially senior-level)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A 30-50% salary gap at senior levels represents a significant lifetime earnings difference; this gap was acknowledged even by the pro-Canada poster
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare total compensation (base + equity + benefits) for equivalent roles in target cities (e.g., Bay Area vs. Toronto/Vancouver); note that commenters questioned whether the gap has narrowed recently
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Whether the role requires 'Canadian experience'
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some Canadian employers implicitly or explicitly prefer candidates with prior Canadian work history, disadvantaging new graduates without co-op or internship experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check job postings for phrases like 'Canadian experience preferred'; also infer from interview feedback if you are screening well but not advancing
decision_factors._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

decision_factors.factor: Genuine acceptability of Plan B options
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If alternative paths (Canada, home country) are only theoretically considered but never seriously evaluated, they cannot reduce anxiety or inform real decisions; the psychological benefit of having a real Plan B depends on it being genuinely researched
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research Canadian immigration pathways (PGWP eligibility, Express Entry CRS score estimate) or home-country career options in enough concrete detail to form an actual preference, not just awareness
decision_factors._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

decision_factors.factor: Qualifying job offer (TEER 0/1 vs. TEER 2/3)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Job offer value varies (50 vs. potentially 200 points depending on NOC category and draw type); this is a point of active confusion in the community
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm NOC TEER level of the role and whether offer qualifies under federal Express Entry rules; community members in comments flagged uncertainty about 50 vs. 200 threshold

decision_factors.factor: CRS score relative to recent draw cutoffs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Express Entry is competitive and cutoff scores shift between draws — a score that qualifies you today may not qualify you next month or vice versa
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track the last 6-12 months of draw cutoffs on the IRCC website and assess whether your profile score consistently clears or is borderline

decision_factors.factor: Realistic quality of Plan B (return home scenario)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The perceived catastrophic nature of returning home drives desperation, which in turn causes poor career and immigration decisions — overcorrecting to the risk side
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research concrete job opportunities, salary ranges, and lifestyle in your home country; compare against Canada scenario with honest numbers

decision_factors.factor: Study permit expiry date relative to PGWP application date
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the study permit expires before the PGWP is approved, the applicant enters 'maintained/implied status' — they can remain in Canada but cannot work during the gap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare study permit expiry date with expected PGWP processing time; if gap is likely, plan finances and employment accordingly

decision_factors.factor: Whether programs are consecutive with no gap
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Determines whether multiple programs can be combined for a longer PGWP; a gap between programs may reset or disqualify the combination
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check the end date of the first program and the start date of the next; consult IRCC guidance or a regulated consultant on what gap length, if any, is permissible

decision_factors.factor: Company size and application volume
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Large companies receive hundreds to thousands of applications per role; mid-size companies have thinner applicant pools and more flexibility to engage PGWP holders
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Filter job searches to companies with 50–200 employees; avoid roles showing 500+ LinkedIn applicants

decision_factors.factor: Salary differential tolerance
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian tech salaries are 30-50% lower than comparable US roles; accepting this cut is the core financial trade-off for immigration certainty
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate total compensation delta over 5-10 years vs. probability-weighted value of US GC; factor in housing, healthcare, and tax differences

decision_factors.factor: Whether the changed-to program appears on IRCC's eligible program list
decision_factors.why_it_matters: For students NOT protected by grandfathering, the new program must be on the eligible list to qualify for PGWP at all; for grandfathered students it is less critical but still affects PGWP duration cap
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check IRCC's current eligible program list; note that the list changes (178 removed, 119 added in June 2025 alone)

decision_factors.factor: Canadian vs. foreign education credential
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian credentials score higher than equivalent foreign credentials: a Canadian master's earns 135 points vs. a foreign master's which earns 120 points
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare degree level and country of institution against the CRS education table; a Canadian PhD yields the maximum 150 points
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: 工作职位的NOC TEER分类
decision_factors.why_it_matters: CEC要求工作经验必须属于TEER 0/1/2/3类，低于此分类的工作经验不计入资格；TEER 0/1类的Job Offer还可额外加50-200分
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 在IRCC官网查询当前职位对应的NOC代码和TEER等级，入职前确认
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Parents' current age and health trajectory
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A 10-year delay in reunion eligibility is qualitatively different depending on whether parents are currently 50 or 65; health and mobility decline changes what 'reunion' means in practice
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map the earliest plausible reunion date against parents' projected age and health; assess whether that timeline is acceptable
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: Salary differential between US and Canada
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Bay Area tech roles pay 40–60% more than Toronto equivalents — but this gap narrows when adjusted for cost of living, making the raw number misleading for lifestyle planning
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare after-tax, after-cost-of-living income in your specific city and role, not gross salary figures
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: French language learning feasibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: French bilingualism bonus adds up to 50 CRS points and is entirely self-controlled — no employer or government approval needed
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess your language learning capacity and available time; CLB 7 in French (approximately B1) is achievable in 3–6 months for motivated learners with some prior exposure
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Completeness and quality of graduation documentation
decision_factors.why_it_matters: IRCC requires a graduation confirmation letter from the registrar specifying degree name and completion date — missing or vague documentation causes delays or refusals
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Request the letter directly from the registrar's office and confirm it explicitly states degree name and official completion date; do not substitute with an unofficial transcript or degree certificate alone
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

decision_factors.factor: Current vs. target program duration comparison
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The impact is asymmetric — switching from 2-year to 1-year is catastrophic for PGWP; switching between two 2-year+ programs has no PGWP impact
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare the official durations of both the current and target programs; calculate the PGWP each would yield
decision_factors._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

decision_factors.factor: Psychological cost of immigration uncertainty
decision_factors.why_it_matters: H-1B lottery failures and green card wait anxiety impose real psychological burden that affects career decisions, job mobility, and quality of life — mentioned by multiple commenters as underweighted in typical analyses
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Ask honestly: how did you feel during H-1B lottery season? How would you feel being unable to leave a bad employer due to transfer risk?
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Job market density by city and field
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The volume of available positions varies significantly by geography (Toronto vs. other cities) and field (e.g. data science roles noted as fewer than in the US), affecting realistic application conversion rates
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Cross-reference job board volume by city and role type before committing to a job search strategy; community members specifically asked about Toronto vs. other cities as a differentiating variable
decision_factors._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

decision_factors.factor: Spouse or partner language score
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A qualifying spouse IELTS score adds up to ~20 CRS points that many applicants overlook entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Check whether spouse has taken IELTS or equivalent; CLB 7 is the meaningful threshold for significant point contribution

decision_factors.factor: Salary delta between Canada and US tech markets
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canadian salaries in tech are genuinely 25-35% lower than comparable Bay Area roles according to the poster — this is a real financial cost that must be weighed against immigration certainty
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare total compensation (including equity) for your specific role and level across both markets using current data, not historical norms

decision_factors.factor: Physical presence in Canada at time of PGWP application
decision_factors.why_it_matters: At least one commenter raised uncertainty about whether remote/abroad applicants qualify — this may affect eligibility or processing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Confirm with IRCC guidance or an authorized representative whether completing final semester abroad affects PGWP eligibility

decision_factors.factor: Whether the academic issue can be resolved within the existing program
decision_factors.why_it_matters: If the problem (research fit, supervisor, coursework) can be fixed without a formal program switch, the student avoids PGWP loss entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Meet with graduate advisor and DSO to explore internal options: changing thesis topic, supervisor, or specialization track without triggering a program change in IRCC records

decision_factors.factor: Canadian network depth
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Referral response rate is approximately 15x cold application rate in Canada; a local network is a multiplier on job search effectiveness
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count how many Canadian-based contacts can make internal referrals; if near zero, networking must precede applications

decision_factors.factor: Field of work / industry niche
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's smaller job market may not support niche specializations; the US offers broader industry diversity and stronger network effects in finance and tech
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Search active job postings in your specific role and level in Toronto/Vancouver vs. NYC/SF/Seattle; assess recruiter activity

decision_factors.factor: Duration of study and program category
decision_factors.why_it_matters: PGWP duration is not simply equal to study duration; post-2024 policy caps non-eligible-list programs at 2 years maximum regardless of study length, reducing expected PGWP duration for 3-year program graduates
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Map study duration against IRCC's tiered formula: <8 months = up to 1 year; 8 months–2 years = equal to study time; 2+ years = up to 3 years (subject to eligible-list cap of 2 years under new rules)

decision_factors.factor: French language proficiency (TEF Canada)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Achieving CLB 7 in French adds approximately +50 CRS points — described by the community as often the decisive margin at current cutoff levels
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess current French level; if at zero, estimate 3–6 months of study to reach CLB 7 (roughly B1 level); total cost RMB 3,000–8,000 plus ~200 CAD exam fee
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: 是否有配偶/伴侣且其英语水平
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 配偶英语达标可为主申请人CRS加分，改变联邦抽签竞争力
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 配偶参加语言考试，评估加分幅度是否能弥补CRS缺口
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canada's Super Visa availability vs. US parent visitor visa constraints
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's Super Visa allows up to 2 years per entry legally; US B-2 visitor visas do not offer a comparable long-stay mechanism, making Canada structurally superior for near-term parental co-residence
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Determine whether Super Visa eligibility applies; check current processing times (stated as 3–8 months in the source)
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: Family formation plans (marriage, children)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's child benefits, parental leave, and early childhood education subsidies materially alter the total economic comparison — the financial gap between the two countries shrinks or reverses for families
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Model expected childcare costs, parental leave income replacement, and child benefit payments in both countries against your timeline for family formation
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience accumulated
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each additional year of Canadian work experience increases CRS points, with the second year yielding a larger increment than the first
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate your current Canadian work experience in months and project when you will cross 1-year and 2-year thresholds
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Industry and role availability in Canada
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some industries (e.g., top-tier tech, certain finance roles) have significantly fewer opportunities in Canada, making a Canada-first strategy a real career trade-off for some fields
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research specific employer and role density in your field in Toronto/Vancouver vs. your US target market
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canada Express Entry CRS score eligibility
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A person with a strong tech background and Canadian work experience may be able to obtain Canadian PR in 1-2 years via CEC, which eliminates employer dependency entirely
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the IRCC CRS calculator with your current profile; compare expected ITA timeline to expected US green card timeline

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Second year of Canadian work experience yields more incremental CRS points than the first year; Canadian experience weighted higher than foreign experience
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track anniversary of Canadian work start date; plan application timing around second-year milestone if close to cutoff

decision_factors.factor: Employer-tied healthcare stress in the US
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Multiple high-upvote comments (234 upvotes) identify this as an underrated psychological and financial factor — losing a job in the US means losing health coverage, which compounds job-change anxiety
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess whether you have dependents, chronic conditions, or high healthcare utilization — the higher the dependency, the more valuable universal coverage becomes

decision_factors.factor: Whether the 180-day application window has been observed
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Missing the 180-day deadline results in PGWP denial with no recourse — it is a hard cutoff
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Identify the exact date final marks/transcript were issued and count forward 180 days; apply before that date

decision_factors.factor: Institution's DLI status for both programs
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Both programs must be at Designated Learning Institutions for the combined PGWP calculation to apply
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify each institution's DLI status on IRCC's official DLI list before enrolling

decision_factors.factor: Geographic targeting (Canadian office presence)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Remote-only job searches that exclude Canadian offices limit the pool of employers who can legally hire on a Canadian work permit
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify whether target employers have Canadian legal entities; remote roles based entirely outside Canada may not be compatible with PGWP

decision_factors.factor: CRS score and Express Entry pool competitiveness
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is points-based but not guaranteed; CRS cutoffs fluctuate and some streams have long waits if score is low
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Use the IRCC CRS calculator; check recent draw cutoffs; evaluate PNP streams (e.g. Alberta, Saskatchewan tech pathways) if federal score is borderline

decision_factors.factor: Timing of PGWP application relative to policy announcement dates
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Applications submitted before a policy change took effect may be processed under prior rules, as illustrated by the hospitality management case in this thread
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track IRCC policy announcement dates; if close to a policy transition, early application submission provides a meaningful buffer

decision_factors.factor: Years of Canadian work experience
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Each additional year of Canadian work experience adds meaningful CRS points (1 year = 40 pts, 2 years = 53 pts, 3+ years = 64 pts) and accumulates automatically over time
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Count verified months of full-time Canadian work experience in an eligible NOC category; plan the immigration timeline around when the next threshold is crossed
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: 是否已有加拿大工作Offer（尤其TEER 0/1类）
decision_factors.why_it_matters: 合格Job Offer可直接加50-200分CRS，大幅提升ITA可能性；同时也可能开启省提名直接路径
decision_factors.how_to_assess: 确认offer是否为永久性全职职位，雇主是否具备提名资质
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在加拿大读完研究生_毕业后移民有多现实_从PGWP到PR需要几年.txt

decision_factors.factor: Canada parent PR lottery win rate (~20–30% per source)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Even after becoming a Canadian PR or citizen, permanent parent sponsorship is subject to annual caps and a lottery, making outcome uncertain
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Treat PR sponsorship as a probabilistic multi-year process, not a guaranteed right; plan Super Visa as the primary near-term tool
decision_factors._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

decision_factors.factor: Employment type: direct hire vs. agency contract
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Community uncertainty exists about whether agency/contract work qualifies as Canadian work experience for CRS purposes, which affects score calculations and PR eligibility
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify with an authorized immigration consultant whether your specific contract arrangement meets IRCC's definition of 'skilled work experience'
decision_factors._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

decision_factors.factor: Housing cost in major Canadian cities
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Toronto and Vancouver housing prices offset some of Canada's lower cost-of-living advantage relative to US cities; smaller Canadian cities are more affordable but have fewer job opportunities
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Compare housing cost-to-income ratio in your target Canadian city vs. US city; consider whether remote work enables smaller-city living
decision_factors._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

decision_factors.factor: Country of origin relative to current ITA cutoff
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Applicants from high-volume countries (India, China) face effectively higher de facto cutoffs due to draw composition; standard strategies may not be sufficient
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Track historical ITA cutoffs by draw type and compare to current CRS; if from India or China, assume you need levers beyond baseline profile optimization

decision_factors.factor: Career trajectory ceiling for senior IC roles
decision_factors.why_it_matters: The poster acknowledges the US still has a higher career ceiling for senior individual contributor roles, though notes the gap is closing
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research compensation and title progression for your target senior roles at Canadian vs. US companies in your specific function

decision_factors.factor: Job flexibility vs. employer-tethered status
decision_factors.why_it_matters: H-1B ties workers to a specific employer; job changes require new petitions and create gap-of-status risk; Canadian PGWP is open work permit allowing free job switching
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Assess how stable your current employer relationship is and how likely you are to want to change jobs in the next 2-3 years

decision_factors.factor: Tolerance for Canada as an alternative destination
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is quota-free and points-based, with PGWP providing open work permit flexibility; many H-1B lottery failures have reached PR within 2-3 years
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate CRS score, assess provincial nominee program eligibility, compare lifestyle and career market factors
decision_factors._source: zhihu_H_1B三次抽签都没中的话_留在美国还有哪些合法途径.txt

decision_factors.factor: Provincial Nomination (PNP)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A provincial nomination adds 600 CRS points, effectively guaranteeing an ITA — making it the single largest possible score change
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Research whether your NOC code, province of work, and employer situation qualify for any PNP stream; consult a provincial immigration advisor
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: Salary differential tolerance for Canada alternative
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada PR via Express Entry is faster (one commenter: 14 months) but typically involves a salary reduction. The trade-off is speed and status certainty vs. earning power.
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate the salary gap between comparable US and Canada roles in your field, then weigh against the psychological and practical cost of indefinite H-1B dependency
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国绿卡排期对印度_中国大陆出生的申请人有多严重.txt

decision_factors.factor: Draw type (all-program vs. category-based vs. PNP)
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Tech-specific or sector-specific draws may clear at lower CRS scores; OP received ITA in a tech-specific draw at 489, not an all-program draw
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Monitor IRCC draw announcements for category-based draws relevant to your NOC; do not only track all-program draw cutoffs

decision_factors.factor: Canadian experience bias in local job market
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Some Canadian employers specifically prefer candidates with prior Canadian work experience, creating a structural disadvantage for new PGWP holders entering the market
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Review job postings in your target role and city for language about Canadian experience requirements; ask people in your network about hiring preferences at target employers

decision_factors.factor: Job offer from Canadian employer
decision_factors.why_it_matters: A qualifying job offer (NOC TEER 0/1) adds +50 CRS points; certain special conditions can add up to +200 points
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Verify the NOC TEER classification of the current or prospective role; confirm employer willingness to provide a valid LMIA-exempt or LMIA-backed offer for EE purposes
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

decision_factors.factor: CRS score competitiveness and boost factors
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Canada's Express Entry is points-based with no lottery — having provincial nomination, French language skills, or Canadian work experience can push scores above the cut-off, making PR timing predictable
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Calculate current CRS score, identify available boost factors (provincial streams, language testing, job offer), and compare against recent cut-off scores
decision_factors._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

decision_factors.factor: Overseas (non-Canadian) work experience
decision_factors.why_it_matters: Foreign work experience in an equivalent NOC role also contributes CRS points (1–2 years = +13 pts, 3+ years = +25 pts), a factor many candidates overlook
decision_factors.how_to_assess: Document prior non-Canadian work experience in the same NOC category; verify it meets IRCC's foreign work experience definition
decision_factors._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Waiting until convocation or diploma receipt to start PGWP application
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: Significant portion of the 180-day application window is silently consumed, increasing risk of missing the deadline if any document delays occur; also delays work authorization start date
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Students who assume the diploma is required before applying — a very common misunderstanding for first-generation international students

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Accepting a TEER 4 or 5 job on PGWP because it is available and pays decently
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: Every hour worked in that role generates zero CEC-eligible experience; the clock on PGWP runs down while PR eligibility does not advance
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Recent graduates in fields with competitive job markets who take survival jobs while searching for professional roles

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Taking any available job in Canada to accumulate Canadian work experience quickly
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: If the job is below TEER 0–3 (i.e., TEER 4 or 5), those hours do not count toward CEC eligibility — the student accumulates time in Canada without building the qualifying experience needed for Express Entry
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Recent graduates who accept service industry, retail, or other low-skilled jobs out of financial necessity while on PGWP

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Enrolling in a 1-year graduate certificate at a Canadian college to save time and money
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: You receive only a 1-year PGWP, which may be insufficient to accumulate the 1 year of skilled work experience required for Canadian Experience Class Express Entry — creating a cliff where you must either find employer-specific work permit sponsorship or leave Canada
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Cost-conscious international students who choose shorter programs to minimize tuition and get into the workforce faster

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Enrolling in a shorter, cheaper college diploma or certificate program to save money
hidden_tradeoffs.unstated_consequence: If the program field is not on the post-June 2025 eligible list and the study permit was filed after June 25, 2025, there is no PGWP — the cost savings on tuition are offset by the loss of legal work authorization and the entire immigration pathway
hidden_tradeoffs.who_it_affects: Cost-conscious international students choosing college over university, especially those who applied for study permits after June 2025

hidden_tradeoffs.choice: Accepting a provincial nomination from a province primarily for immigration advantage
