failure_patterns.pattern: Making academic program changes without calculating downstream immigration impact
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Academic and immigration advisors operate in separate systems; students naturally consult academic advisors for academic decisions and don't think to loop in immigration implications
failure_patterns.consequence: Irreversible structural decisions (program switches) that permanently reduce PGWP duration before the student realizes the impact
warning_signs: Student is mid-program and feeling dissatisfied with research direction
Student frames the decision purely as academic ('this program fits me better')
No mention of consulting international student office before deciding
failure_patterns._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

failure_patterns.pattern: Underweighting psychological cost of immigration uncertainty in career planning
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Salary and career opportunity are quantifiable; psychological stress is not, so it gets systematically discounted in rational-seeming analyses
failure_patterns.consequence: Years of high anxiety around H-1B renewal, lottery, job changes, and layoffs; decision paralysis during critical career moments
warning_signs: Framing the US vs. Canada decision purely in terms of salary and career opportunity
Dismissing 'stress' as a soft factor not worth quantifying
failure_patterns._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

failure_patterns.pattern: Over-investing in Easy Apply and mass-submission platforms at the expense of referral and direct outreach
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Mass applying feels like action and is low-friction, creating an illusion of effort while delivering poor results; referral-building requires social capital and feels uncertain
failure_patterns.consequence: Low callback rates, prolonged search timelines, and growing discouragement despite high application volume
warning_signs: Sending 50+ applications with fewer than 5% callback rate
Spending more time on job boards than on LinkedIn networking or alumni outreach
failure_patterns._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

failure_patterns.pattern: Refusing to seriously research or plan for alternative immigration paths until forced to by a failed lottery
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Equating Plan B research with admitting defeat — a cognitive reframe where considering alternatives feels like abandoning the primary goal
failure_patterns.consequence: When the H-1B fails, the person is in emotional crisis AND lacks any prepared alternative path, leading to rushed decisions under maximum stress
warning_signs: Saying 'I'll figure out Canada/going home if it comes to that'
Never having looked up PGWP eligibility or Express Entry CRS score despite multiple lottery failures
Experiencing the lottery result as binary (success = stay, failure = catastrophe) with no middle ground
failure_patterns._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

failure_patterns.pattern: Not accounting for spouse language score in CRS profile planning
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: CRS score calculators are often run for the primary applicant alone; spouse contribution is a less prominent feature and easily overlooked
failure_patterns.consequence: Applicant reaches a plateau in CRS optimization without realizing a 20-point gain is available through a spouse IELTS test
warning_signs: Applicant has a spouse but no mention of spouse language scores in profile
Applicant is close to cutoff and exhausting other levers

failure_patterns.pattern: Treating a CRS score as a static qualification threshold rather than a dynamic cutoff that shifts each draw
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: The points-based system feels like a test with a fixed passing score — candidates assume that once they hit a certain number they will eventually be invited
failure_patterns.consequence: Candidates stop improving their profile after reaching a score they believe is sufficient, then miss draws when cutoffs rise or fail to account for score expiry or profile changes
warning_signs: Asking 'is my score high enough?' without specifying which draw or timeframe
Not monitoring draw cutoff history or IRCC draw announcements after submitting an Express Entry profile

failure_patterns.pattern: Compulsive IRCC portal checking — refreshing multiple times per day during wait periods
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Uncertainty and lack of control drives compulsive information-seeking behavior; each check provides a momentary sense of agency even though the portal rarely updates
failure_patterns.consequence: Wait periods feel subjectively endless; anxiety is amplified rather than resolved; cognitive bandwidth consumed by a behavior that yields no new information
warning_signs: Checking the portal more than once per day
Feeling worse after checking because nothing has changed

failure_patterns.pattern: Using an informal completion email from a professor or department instead of the official registrar letter
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Applicants receive completion confirmation via email first and assume any official-looking document suffices
failure_patterns.consequence: IRCC rejects or flags the application for insufficient proof of program completion
warning_signs: Applicant's proof-of-completion document is not on registrar letterhead
Document is an email rather than a formal letter with an official signature

failure_patterns.pattern: Assuming program stacking always results in a longer PGWP regardless of gaps
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: The combining rule feels logical — more education should mean more PGWP — so students assume any sequence of programs adds up, without knowing the consecutive requirement
failure_patterns.consequence: Student plans their immigration timeline assuming a 3-year PGWP from combined programs but receives a shorter one due to a gap between programs
warning_signs: Student has a gap between credential completion and next program start
Student has not verified with IRCC guidance or a regulated consultant whether the specific gap is permissible

failure_patterns.pattern: Over-relying on cold LinkedIn Easy Apply for roles with 500+ applicants
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Low-effort application channel feels productive; candidates mistake volume of submissions for effectiveness
failure_patterns.consequence: Weeks of zero response while depleting motivation and time that could be spent networking
warning_signs: Applying to 80+ jobs with fewer than 3 phone screens in first 4 weeks
All applications submitted via Easy Apply with no direct outreach

failure_patterns.pattern: Learning about a PGWP policy change only after it takes effect and retrospectively catastrophizing about past decisions
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: IRCC policy announcements are not proactively communicated to enrolled students; students often hear through community forums with delay, then apply the new rules retroactively to their own situations incorrectly
failure_patterns.consequence: Unnecessary anxiety and sometimes premature abandonment of a valid PGWP pathway due to misreading grandfathering protections
warning_signs: Student asks 'am I completely ineligible now?' without referencing their enrollment date
Student conflates the program-change date with the enrollment date when assessing grandfathering

failure_patterns.pattern: Candidates research PNP streams only after deciding they need a nomination, missing eligibility windows tied to specific NOC codes or employer situations
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Just-in-time research bias: candidates assume PNP eligibility can be assessed and acted on quickly, not realizing streams open and close, and that employer relationships or NOC classification must be in place in advance
failure_patterns.consequence: Missing active PNP streams that matched their profile, forcing another cycle or a lower-value pathway
warning_signs: Candidate asks 'which province should I target for PNP?' only after their EE profile has been sitting for 12+ months
Candidate unsure of their own NOC TEER classification
failure_patterns._source: zhihu_加拿大Express_Entry的CRS分数怎么计算_有哪些可以主动提升的因素.txt

failure_patterns.pattern: Assuming Canadian employer will understand PGWP = no sponsorship required, without explaining it
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: PGWP holders know their own status intimately; they project that knowledge onto HR departments who encounter international work authorization rarely
failure_patterns.consequence: Qualified candidates are filtered out before interview because HR assumed sponsorship cost/complexity, when none was actually required
warning_signs: Applying to Canadian jobs without a cover letter or resume note clarifying work authorization
Not raising PGWP status proactively in early recruiter calls
failure_patterns._source: zhihu_美国H_1B和加拿大PR_对于中国背景的留学生_哪条路更现实.txt

failure_patterns.pattern: Confusing job offer point values (50 vs. 200) without verifying current rules
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: The 200-point figure circulates in older community posts and remains in community memory even as rules change; applicants do not verify against current IRCC guidelines
failure_patterns.consequence: Applicants may overestimate the CRS impact of a job offer and make career decisions based on inflated expectations
warning_signs: Applicant citing '200 points for job offer' without specifying draw type or NOC category
Confusion in comments about when 50 vs. 200 applies

failure_patterns.pattern: Assuming maintained status allows work during the PGWP processing gap
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: The concept of 'implied/maintained status' sounds like continued authorization to do what the prior permit allowed
failure_patterns.consequence: Graduate works without authorization during the gap period, creating unauthorized work history that could affect future immigration applications
warning_signs: Applicant says 'my study permit is technically expired but I'm on implied status so I can still work'
Applicant does not distinguish between the right to remain and the right to work

failure_patterns.pattern: Delaying networking until cold applications fail, rather than running both in parallel from day one
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Networking feels uncertain and uncomfortable; candidates default to the familiar structured process of submitting applications
failure_patterns.consequence: 4–6 weeks of low-yield activity before pivoting, compressing the effective job search timeline on a finite PGWP
warning_signs: No meetup or event attendance in first month of job search
LinkedIn connections are primarily non-Canadian or former classmates with no hiring leverage

failure_patterns.pattern: Ignoring PNP streams when federal Express Entry CRS score is borderline
failure_patterns.why_it_happens: Provincial Nominee Programs are less visible and feel more complex; students focus on the headline Express Entry pool and don't research province-specific tech pathways
failure_patterns.consequence: Sits in Express Entry pool without invitation while Alberta or Saskatchewan PNP streams with lower thresholds go unused
warning_signs: Person only discusses Express Entry CRS score without mentioning PNP
Located in or willing to relocate to a province with active tech PNP stream

diagnostic_questions.question: What date did you submit your original study permit application — before or after November 1, 2024?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the field-of-study CIP code eligibility requirement applies to this student, which is a hard additional gate for PGWP eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What is your program's CIP code, and is it on IRCC's current list of eligible fields linked to agriculture, healthcare, STEM, trades, or transport?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You are not subject to the field-of-study restriction; proceed to verify the other eligibility criteria (full-time status, study permit validity, language test)

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the NOC/TEER classification of the job you are currently working or plan to work in Canada?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the work experience will count toward CEC at all and which CLB minimum applies
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If TEER 0/1/2/3 confirmed: what is your current CLB score and does it meet the threshold for that TEER level?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If TEER 4/5 or unknown: the student must either reclassify the role correctly or understand that zero CEC hours are accumulating — immediate career strategy pivot may be needed

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you accumulated 1,560 hours of skilled work in Canada (TEER 0–3) within the past 36 months, excluding co-op and self-employment?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate qualifies for CEC — the most strategically advantageous Express Entry stream for international students
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the NOC codes of the roles qualify as TEER 0–3 and that employment was paid and outside Quebec; proceed with CEC-based Express Entry profile
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess how many months remain on PGWP; determine if 1,560 hours can be accumulated before PGWP expiry; if not, evaluate FSWP eligibility or PGWP extension options

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the credential type you will receive — a university bachelor's, master's, or PhD, or a college diploma, certificate, or graduate certificate?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is subject to the field-of-study requirement at all — university degree holders are entirely exempt, while non-degree holders must meet it
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If university degree: confirm only that language requirement (CLB 7) is met — no further field eligibility check needed
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If non-degree: proceed to verify the program's CIP code against the current IRCC eligible fields list before any other planning

diagnostic_questions.question: When did you submit your Canadian study permit application — before or after June 25, 2025?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines which set of PGWP eligibility rules applies; students before this date are protected under previous rules, those after must meet new field-of-study requirements
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If before June 25, 2025: your program field does not affect PGWP eligibility — focus on duration and language requirements instead
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If after June 25, 2025: immediately verify your specific program field against the current IRCC eligible fields list — do not assume eligibility

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score and how does it compare to the last 6 months of Express Entry draw cutoffs?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student can realistically receive an ITA through federal draws alone or must treat PNP as their primary pathway
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If within 30 points of cutoff: focus on CRS improvement levers (language retest, job offer points) while maintaining PNP as backup
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If 50+ points below cutoff: redirect immediately to PNP streams and identify which provinces match their job situation and geography

diagnostic_questions.question: Can you name your exact current visa status expiration date and the deadline for filing your next status transition?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is actively managing their immigration timeline or operating reactively
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What is your Plan B if your primary transition pathway fails — lottery loss, employer withdrawal, or denial?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: This is the most urgent priority before any other career or immigration planning; find out immediately and set calendar reminders 6 and 12 months in advance

diagnostic_questions.question: Were you enrolled as a full-time student in every semester of your program except the final term?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether there is a hidden PGWP disqualification due to a part-time semester that the student may not even remember or understand as problematic
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm with the registrar's office that each semester's enrollment record officially shows full-time status before submitting
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Immediately consult an RCIC — a part-time semester (other than the final term) disqualifies you from PGWP, and you need to explore alternative pathways before applying

diagnostic_questions.question: When does your PGWP expire, and how many CEC-eligible hours have you accumulated so far?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has enough PGWP runway to complete 1,560 hours, submit Express Entry, and receive PR before status expires
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If sufficient runway: calculate whether waiting for a higher CRS experience threshold (e.g., 2 years) is worth it before applying
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If runway is tight: immediately evaluate PNP options and bridging open work permit eligibility to avoid status gap

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score calculated using IRCC's official tool (not a third-party calculator), and does it include any job offer points?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has an accurate, post-March 2025 CRS score; job offer points are no longer valid and inflate older estimates
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Recalculate without job offer points; identify the true gap between current score and recent draw cut-offs
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm the score is current and accurate; compare against general and category-based draw cut-offs to determine competitiveness

diagnostic_questions.question: On what date was your Canadian study permit application submitted to IRCC?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Which version of PGWP rules applies to the student — pre-November 2024 (no field or language requirements), post-November 2024 but pre-June 2025, or post-June 2025 (full current rules with June 2025 overhaul)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If before November 1, 2024: student is under old rules — only institution eligibility matters, no field or language requirement
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If after November 1, 2024: determine if before or after June 25, 2025 to establish which version of the eligible fields list applies

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your Canadian program a university degree (bachelor's or above) or a college diploma/certificate?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: University degree students have blanket PGWP eligibility regardless of field; diploma/certificate students face field-specific eligibility restrictions and higher compliance risk
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If university degree: PGWP field risk is resolved — shift focus to program duration, language test requirements, and CRS planning
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If diploma/certificate: immediately check program field against IRCC's eligible list and confirm study permit application date relative to June 25, 2025

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have a current, full-time job offer in Canada, and if so, in which province?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Immediately narrows which PNP streams are accessible — most require a provincial job offer, and the province determines which stream applies
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Verify NOC code, TEER level, and whether the province has an active tech PNP stream for that occupation
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Direct to Ontario HCP (no job offer required) and flag that job searching in BC or Alberta would unlock additional streams

diagnostic_questions.question: When you say 'my employer will sponsor me,' what specifically have they told you about their immigration attorney, filing timeline, and internal process?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the sponsorship commitment is operationally real or a well-intentioned but unexecuted assurance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have they successfully sponsored someone at your visa category before — and can you speak to that person?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Treat the sponsorship as unconfirmed; ask the specific operational questions before relying on this employer as your primary immigration pathway

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your study permit currently valid, and will it still be valid on the date you plan to submit the PGWP application?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student faces a hard eligibility barrier that cannot be overcome by simply filing the application quickly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with application preparation; confirm the exact expiry date and build in a buffer for any document-gathering delays
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Contact an RCIC immediately to assess whether status restoration is possible; do not submit a PGWP application with an expired study permit without legal advice

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your projected CRS score, and how does it compare to the last 12 months of CEC draw cutoffs?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is competitive for a direct ITA or structurally needs PNP to obtain PR
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If above recent cutoffs: prioritize submitting a strong Express Entry profile promptly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If below recent cutoffs: immediately research PNP streams in your province and occupation — do not wait for the pool to reach your score

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your occupation fall into a sector that has received category-based Express Entry draws — such as healthcare, STEM, skilled trades, or French language?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has access to lower-cut-off draws that could result in an ITA at a score that would fail in a general draw
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Identify the specific NOC codes targeted in recent category draws; ensure the Express Entry profile NOC aligns with targeted categories; set up IRCC draw alerts
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Focus strategy on improving core CRS factors (language, Canadian experience, French) or pursuing PNP streams to compensate for lack of category draw access

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you know the CIP code of your specific program, and have you verified it against IRCC's current eligible program list (not a third-party site)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student's eligibility assessment is based on authoritative data or potentially outdated/inaccurate secondary sources
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If verified via IRCC directly: confirm the verification was done against the post-June 2025 list, not a prior version
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not verified: halt any PGWP planning and go directly to the IRCC website or consult an RCIC before proceeding

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score, and have you run the official federal calculator in the last 3 months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Reveals whether the candidate has situational awareness of their immigration competitiveness and which specific gaps exist (language, age, education, spousal factors)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If score known: compare it against current draw cut-offs for general, CEC-specific, French-language, and PNP-enhanced draws — identify which category is realistic
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If score unknown: run the calculator immediately; no strategic immigration decisions should be made without a current score

diagnostic_questions.question: When does your PGWP expire, and have you calculated the full timeline from PNP application to PR grant?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has a timeline problem that requires bridging work permit planning or accelerated application strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If PGWP expires in 18+ months: standard PNP + PR timeline is likely manageable with early action
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If PGWP expires in under 18 months: immediately model bridge work permit eligibility and file PNP application at the earliest possible date

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you staying in this country because you genuinely believe it compounds better for you over 10 years, or because returning home would feel like failure?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the stay-vs-return decision is analytically grounded or emotionally driven by social framing of return as defeat
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Can you articulate specifically which career, financial, or personal factors compound better here — and have you run the purchasing power comparison?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The decision warrants a structured reanalysis using a 10-year compounding framework before committing to another multi-year immigration cycle

diagnostic_questions.question: Did you at any point work more than 20 hours per week during a semester when school was in session?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student qualifies for 'work while you wait' authorization during PGWP processing — unauthorized work during pending status creates an inadmissibility record
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Do not begin working after submitting the PGWP application until you receive the actual permit; consult an RCIC about whether to disclose the prior work violation in the application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You likely qualify for maintained-status work authorization during processing — confirm the other two criteria (valid study permit at submission, full-time program over 8 months) are also met

diagnostic_questions.question: Does any of your Canadian work experience overlap with periods when you were enrolled as a full-time student?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether co-op or part-time student work is being incorrectly counted in the student's CEC eligibility calculation
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes: exclude those hours and recalculate actual CEC eligibility date — the student may be less eligible than they believe
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: the hours count can proceed — confirm they were worked under PGWP or other non-student authorization

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you tested in French (TEF Canada or TCF Canada), and do you have any prior French language exposure or education?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is leaving up to 50 CRS points and French-stream draw access on the table due to not pursuing French testing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Review current CLB level; if below CLB 7, assess investment required to reach it vs. CRS gap to cut-off; enroll in preparation if ROI is positive
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Determine if there is any French background worth exploring; if none, deprioritize French as a strategy and focus on other improvable factors

diagnostic_questions.question: How long is your program — less than 8 months, 8 months to 2 years, or 2 years or more?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The maximum PGWP duration the student can obtain, which directly determines how much time they have to accumulate CEC-qualifying work experience
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: student qualifies for 3-year PGWP — maximum runway for CEC; plan Express Entry timeline accordingly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If under 2 years: calculate whether PGWP duration is sufficient for 1 year of NOC-eligible work experience before expiry; if not, begin planning employer-specific work permit or program extension options

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your French language proficiency level, and have you taken an official TEF Canada or TCF Canada test?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines access to French-language Express Entry draws (cut-offs 90–150 points lower than general draws) and whether French investment is a viable CRS lever
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If CLB 7+ French: ensure it is documented in your Express Entry profile and model CRS score with French-language draw eligibility factored in
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If below CLB 7 or untested: estimate realistic time to CLB 7 and calculate the CRS point gain; compare to other available improvement strategies

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you verified that your job's NOC code and TEER level appear on the eligible occupation list for the PNP stream you are considering?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has done the foundational eligibility check or is assuming eligibility based on job title or general 'tech worker' identity
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm that job duties in the offer letter match the NOC lead statement to protect against misclassification
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Do this immediately before investing time in any specific PNP application; a wrong NOC is a hard block on eligibility

diagnostic_questions.question: Before you chose your program and your employer, did you assess how those choices would affect your immigration options 3–5 years from now?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has already locked themselves into a constrained pathway through uninformed early decisions, or has navigational flexibility remaining
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What specific visa categories did you confirm were accessible given your program type and employer profile?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Map the downstream immigration implications of decisions already made immediately — earlier errors may still be correctable but the window closes quickly

diagnostic_questions.question: When was your language proficiency test taken, and will those results be less than 2 years old on your planned PGWP submission date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student needs to retake a language test before filing — submitting with expired scores guarantees refusal
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the test type is one of the three approved by IRCC (CELPIP General, IELTS General, PTE Core) and that the score meets CLB 5
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Book a retake immediately; factor in test registration lead time and results turnaround time against the 180-day PGWP application deadline

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you intend to remain outside Quebec for work and residence?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether CEC is even the correct pathway; Quebec-based students need the QSWP or PEQ, not Express Entry CEC
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If outside Quebec: CEC pathway is appropriate — proceed with eligibility assessment
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If in Quebec or planning to move there: redirect entirely to Quebec immigration streams; CEC will not apply

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you researched and applied to any Provincial Nominee Program streams aligned with your occupation and province of residence?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is pursuing the most powerful single CRS lever available — a provincial nomination worth 600 points — or ignoring it entirely
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm which streams are being pursued; check if current employer can support a job-offer-required PNP stream; track application status
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Immediately research PNP streams for the candidate's province and occupation; determine if employer is willing to provide a letter of support; treat PNP pursuit as parallel to Express Entry, not sequential

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you completed an approved language test (IELTS, CELPIP, or TEF) and do you have a valid result meeting the required CLB level?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student can actually submit a PGWP application — language test is now a mandatory document, not optional
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If valid test result in hand: confirm the score meets CLB 7 (university) or CLB 5 (college) in all four abilities before filing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no test booked: determine how many months until graduation and schedule immediately — test booking, sitting, and results can take 4-8 weeks minimum

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the NOC TEER level of the job you are currently in or planning to accept on your PGWP?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether current work experience will count toward the 1-year CEC requirement; TEER 4–5 roles do not qualify and may result in the candidate building time with no CEC eligibility progress
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If TEER 0–3: confirm the NOC code is accurate and start tracking your 1-year eligibility clock — ensure you maintain consistent hours and document employment
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If TEER 4–5: urgently evaluate whether a parallel or alternative TEER 0–3 opportunity is available; every month in an ineligible role delays PR

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you genuinely willing and able to settle in the province you are considering for PNP nomination?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is pursuing a specific PNP stream for legitimate settlement reasons or purely for immigration arbitrage — the latter carries integrity risk
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed with PNP application and document genuine ties (employer, housing, community) to strengthen the intention to settle
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Counsel the student on the risks of misrepresenting settlement intention; redirect to streams aligned with where they genuinely plan to live

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you had a paid consultation with a qualified immigration attorney (not a peer, not a forum) in the last 12 months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has access to calibrated, situation-specific advice or is navigating a complex legal system using unreliable informal inputs
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What specific questions did you ask, and did they review your actual documents and timeline?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Prioritize a consultation before any next major decision; identify whether your situation has unusual elements that make peer advice especially unreliable

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you planning to travel outside Canada at any point between now and when your PGWP is approved?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is at risk of being stranded outside Canada due to an expired TRV or eTA during the PGWP processing period
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Check TRV and eTA validity in your passport right now — if either expires before your expected PGWP approval date, you must either cancel travel plans or accept the risk of extended time outside Canada
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No additional action needed on this dimension; focus on ensuring other eligibility criteria are met

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you taken your language test and confirmed CLB scores for all four skills individually — not just your overall score?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student actually meets the CLB minimum threshold for their TEER level in every single skill band
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If all four skills meet or exceed threshold: confirm which CLB level was reached and how it affects CRS points
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If any skill is below threshold or untested: the student is not yet Express Entry eligible for CEC — retesting or remediation is the priority

diagnostic_questions.question: How old are you, and how many months until you turn 30 (or how many years past 29 are you)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether age-based CRS point decay is creating urgency in the timing of profile submission or strategy selection
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If approaching 30, calculate net CRS impact of delaying vs. improving another factor; submit profile now if current score is near competitive even if not ideal
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If well under 29, the candidate is at maximum age points and timing is less critical; focus on building experience and language scores

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you enrolled in a transport, aviation, marine, agriculture, or environmental studies program?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is in a high-risk category where PGWP eligibility was removed or severely curtailed in June 2025
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes: immediately verify study permit submission date; if post-November 2024, consult an RCIC about alternative pathways including employer-specific work permits or program transfer
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: proceed with standard field-of-study and CIP code verification process

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your province of residence or work have an active PNP stream aligned to your NOC code?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Identifies whether the 600-point provincial nomination pathway is accessible, which would effectively guarantee an ITA regardless of base CRS score
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If active PNP stream exists: determine the minimum provincial score requirement and apply as soon as eligible — do not wait for a federal draw
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no active stream: monitor draw history of neighboring provinces and assess whether relocating is strategically worthwhile given the 600-point benefit

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have an active Express Entry profile in the pool, and is it linked to your PNP application for an enhanced nomination?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student understands the difference between enhanced and base nomination and whether they are positioned to receive the +600 CRS advantage
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ensure the profile is current, accurate, and the NOC code is consistent across Express Entry profile and PNP application
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Create and submit Express Entry profile immediately; a PNP nomination without an active Express Entry profile forces a slower base nomination process

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your spouse or partner have independent immigration status, or is their status derivative of yours?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a family dependency risk exists — if the student's status fails, the entire household's legal standing may fail simultaneously
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Does their independent status create additional pathway options (e.g., points contribution in Canada, alternative sponsorship route) that you have factored into your plan?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: The student's immigration planning must account for household-level risk; a single failure point affects multiple people and raises the stakes of every decision

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your spouse intend to apply for an open work permit based on your PGWP status?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the post-January 2025 spousal OWP restrictions apply and whether the family's dual-income plan is achievable
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What is your NOC code and TEER classification, and how many months remain on your PGWP? — both must meet new thresholds (TEER 0/1 or qualifying TEER 2/3, plus 16+ months remaining)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No action needed on spousal OWP; focus on primary PGWP application

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you intend to live in Quebec, or are you currently employed in Quebec?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is inadvertently ineligible for CEC (which requires intent to reside outside Quebec) and needs to navigate Quebec's separate immigration stream instead
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Redirect entirely to Quebec's skilled worker programs (QSWP); Express Entry CEC is not available; assess Arrima pool eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Confirm the candidate understands CEC's Quebec exclusion and plans to live/work in another province; proceed with CEC strategy

diagnostic_questions.question: If you have a spouse or common-law partner, have they taken an IELTS/TEF and had their education credentials ECA-assessed?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Identifies uncaptured CRS points from spousal factors — a commonly overlooked source of 10–40 additional CRS points that requires only documentation effort, not changing any other circumstance
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If tested and assessed: include spousal factors in CRS profile and verify score is calculated correctly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not tested: initiate IELTS/TEF registration and ECA process immediately — both take months and cannot be rushed when you are ready to file

diagnostic_questions.question: If your primary employer withdrew their sponsorship tomorrow, what would you do within the next 60 days to maintain legal status?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student has a real contingency plan or whether all their immigration security is concentrated in a single employer dependency
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you verified that the contingency pathway is actually accessible to you — not just theoretically possible?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: This is a critical single point of failure; develop and verify at least one alternative pathway before it becomes urgent

diagnostic_questions.question: If you completed your highest degree outside Canada, has it been assessed by an IRCC-designated ECA organization?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Unassessed foreign credentials receive no CRS education points; this is a correctable gap but requires 3–6 months to process and cannot be backdated after filing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If ECA complete: confirm it aligns with the Canadian credential level you are claiming in your Express Entry profile
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If ECA not started: begin immediately through WES, ICAS, or another designated body — do not wait until you are otherwise ready to file

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current age, and how old will you be at your projected Express Entry application date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: CRS age points peak at 20–29 and decline after 30, accelerating after 35 — a 2-year delay in application timing can cost 15–30+ irrecoverable CRS points, changing whether an ITA is achievable
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If under 29 at application: age is not yet a constraint — focus on other levers; model whether rushing the timeline sacrifices other score components
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If 30 or older, or approaching 30: treat application timing as urgent; each additional year of delay has a compounding negative effect on CRS score

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you or your spouse taken a French language test (TEF Canada or TCF Canada)?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the applicant has explored the single highest-ROI CRS lever available to most applicants without employer or government involvement
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What score did you achieve — did you reach CLB 7 in all four components?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: What is your estimated current French level, and have you researched what CLB 7 requires on TEF Canada?
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_Express_Entry_CRS从450到490的真实经历_法语是关键.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: How long was your Canadian study program — specifically, was it under 1 year, exactly 1 year, between 1 and 2 years, or 2 years or more?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: This determines the maximum PGWP duration you are eligible for, which affects your entire post-graduation immigration strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: you qualify for a 3-year PGWP — plan your Express Entry or PNP timeline accordingly
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If under 2 years: PGWP duration is capped at program length or 1 year — assess whether this is sufficient for your immigration pathway or whether bridging options are needed
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP申请全流程_从毕业到拿到permit只用了5周.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the official duration of your current program vs. the program you want to switch to?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the switch would reduce PGWP duration — the single most important variable in assessing the immigration cost of a program change
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If both are 2+ years: PGWP impact is likely zero — explore whether the switch makes sense on other grounds
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If switching from 2-year to 1-year: quantify the PGWP loss and model the PR timeline impact before proceeding
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_PGWP项目切换的大坑_差点损失两年work_permit.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your resume currently include a visible work authorization line clarifying that no sponsorship is required?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is proactively removing the most common HR-level filter that kills PGWP applications before they reach a hiring manager
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is it placed near the top where a recruiter will see it in the first scan?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Add 'Authorized to work in Canada (Open Work Permit – no sponsorship required)' near the top before the next batch of applications
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_在加拿大找第一份工作的经验_PGWP身份怎么向雇主解释.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you researched your Canadian immigration options (PGWP, Express Entry) in enough detail to know your approximate CRS score or eligibility window?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the person has a real Plan B or only a theoretical one; also reveals how much runway they have under Canadian pathways
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Does your current employer have a Canadian office, and have you discussed transfer options with HR?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: How many H-1B lottery attempts do you have remaining before your authorized status expires? This determines how urgently you need to build that plan.
diagnostic_questions._source: immigration_关于H_1B抽签后身份焦虑_说几句心里话.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your spouse or common-law partner taken a recognized language test (IELTS, CELPIP) for Express Entry purposes?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether up to 20 CRS points are being left on the table from the spouse language score component
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What CLB level did they achieve? CLB 7+ is the key threshold for meaningful contribution.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Prioritize spouse language testing before other optimization tactics — it is likely the lowest-effort high-return action available.

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the current EB-2 priority date for your country of origin, and how does that compare to when you entered the US workforce?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a US green card is realistically obtainable within the person's working career — for Indian nationals, the answer often makes the US path untenable
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If the date is within 5-7 years: assess whether H-1B stability is sufficient to wait it out, factoring in job change risk and political changes to the program
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If the date is 20+ years out: treat the green card as functionally unavailable and reframe the decision as permanent residency in Canada vs. indefinite temporary status in the US

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your work permit open (PGWP) or employer-specific?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether your perceived constraint on changing jobs is real or anxiety-driven; PGWP holders have full freedom to change employers
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If open permit: what is the actual immigration risk of taking the new opportunity? It is likely near zero.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If employer-specific: consult an RCIC about bridge work permits or open permit pathways before declining opportunities

diagnostic_questions.question: What date were your final marks or official transcript issued?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: This is the start of the 180-day PGWP application window — the most critical deadline in the entire process
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Calculate day 180 from that date and confirm the application will be submitted before then
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If transcript has not yet been issued, determine the expected issue date and plan application submission accordingly

diagnostic_questions.question: What will the length of the program you actually complete be — not the program you started, but the one you will finish?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines PGWP eligibility length, which drives the entire post-graduation immigration timeline
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If it's 2+ years, confirm it's at a DLI and you will complete it without switching
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If shorter than expected (due to switching), calculate the PGWP loss and model the PR timeline impact before committing

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your resume and outreach explicitly state 'open work permit — no sponsorship required' in a visible location?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is pre-empting the most common filter Canadian employers apply to permit holders
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Does it also clarify you are pursuing PR independently, so employers don't worry about continuity?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Rewrite immediately — this is likely the cause of silent rejections even before your qualifications are evaluated

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your original enrollment date at your Canadian institution?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student is protected by grandfathering under the 2024-11-01 PGWP policy cutoff, regardless of any subsequent program changes
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If enrolled before November 1, 2024: confirm full-time status has been maintained continuously; then advise to document and apply early
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If enrolled after November 1, 2024: assess whether the specific program is on IRCC's current eligible program list before advising further

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current CRS score, and what has been the cutoff in recent draws for your NOC category?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is realistically competitive for a near-term federal draw or needs an alternative strategy
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If within 50 points: which single factor (French, spousal language, additional work experience) can close the gap fastest?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If more than 50 points below: have you investigated PNP streams for your NOC code and province of residence?

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your primary optimization target: maximizing career earnings ceiling or achieving immigration status certainty as early as possible?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: These two goals currently map to different countries — clarifying priority eliminates the false premise that one path dominates the other on all dimensions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If career ceiling: Are you in a field (finance, top-tier tech) where the US salary premium is large enough to justify 5–10 years of status uncertainty?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If status certainty: Do you have factors that boost your CRS score (French, provincial nomination, Canadian work experience) to make the Canada timeline predictable?

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your occupation's NOC code, and has IRCC run a category-based draw for that category in the past 12 months?

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: What was the CRS cutoff in those draws, and how far is your current score from that threshold?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: You may be waiting for an all-program draw where cutoffs for your background may be 530+; consider whether province-specific streams are available

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already submitted biometrics to IRCC during a prior permit or visa application?

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Budget additional time and cost for biometric enrollment; locate the nearest collection site in advance

diagnostic_questions.question: What PR pathway are you planning to use after graduation?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether PGWP duration is actually critical — students using employer sponsorship or PNP streams may be less affected by a shorter PGWP than Express Entry candidates
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If Express Entry via Canadian work experience: PGWP duration is high-stakes; do not reduce it without fully understanding the CRS score impact
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If employer sponsorship or PNP: assess whether those pathways are realistically accessible before discounting PGWP duration as a concern

diagnostic_questions.question: After getting Canadian PR, do you plan to work in the US? Have you checked the PR residency obligation?

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If staying in Canada: residency obligation is straightforward to meet
diagnostic_questions._source: canada_加拿大vs美国移民路线的真实对比_从两边都走过的角度.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: Do you have any Canadian work experience (co-op, internship, part-time) to list on your resume?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate will face the 'Canadian experience' filter that multiple commenters identified as a real and recurring barrier
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Ensure it is prominently framed in Canadian context with Canadian company names and location visible
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Explore whether volunteer work, school projects with Canadian organizations, or part-time employment can be positioned as Canadian experience proxies

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your current English language score, and are you above CLB 10?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether further English investment has diminishing returns and French bilingual route should be prioritized instead
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Stop investing in English retests; redirect time and resources to French (TEF Canada B1) for ~50 additional points.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Bring English to CLB 10 first, then evaluate French as the next lever.

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your role qualify as TEER 1 (or equivalent) for Express Entry purposes?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether you are eligible for the Federal Skilled Worker or Canadian Experience Class streams — eligibility gatekeeping is a non-obvious prerequisite that many candidates overlook
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Proceed to CRS score calculation and draw cutoff comparison
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess PNP pathways that may have different NOC/TEER eligibility criteria, or identify whether a job change could qualify your role

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you concretely researched what your Plan B (returning home) would actually look like?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether your Plan B fear is based on reality or an unchallenged catastrophic narrative; the answer predicts how much desperation is distorting your current decisions
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If researched: is it actually catastrophic, or merely different? Use the realistic picture to recalibrate your risk tolerance.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If not researched: the fear is likely inflated — spend time concretizing the scenario before making any major immigration-driven sacrifices

diagnostic_questions.question: Was your program a co-op or work-integrated learning program, and does your completion letter reflect the full program duration including co-op months?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the PGWP will reflect the full integrated program length or only the academic component — directly affecting permit duration
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the registrar letter explicitly names the full program duration including co-op
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Request clarification from the registrar and potentially from IRCC on how co-op months are counted

diagnostic_questions.question: Can the academic problem you're trying to solve be fixed within your current program without a formal program change?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Determines whether a program switch is actually necessary or whether internal program changes (supervisor, focus, track) can achieve the same outcome
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Pursue the internal change; consult graduate advisor and DSO to ensure it doesn't trigger a program change in IRCC records
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If a switch is unavoidable, calculate full PGWP impact and explore whether a program of equivalent or greater length is available as an alternative

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you attended any Canadian in-person tech meetups or local events in the past 4 weeks?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is building the local network that drives the 15x referral response rate advantage
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you explicitly asked contacts to refer you internally? Passive networking is not the same as referral generation.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Cold application strategy alone is unlikely to succeed — local event attendance is the highest-leverage activity per hour for Canadian tech job search

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your program (the one you will graduate from) currently appear on IRCC's eligible program list?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the student faces a PGWP duration cap of 2 years, or no PGWP eligibility at all, under the post-2024 framework
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the list version being checked is current (list changed significantly in June 2025); check for your specific institution's offering
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess grandfathering protection; if not grandfathered, evaluate alternative pathways (PNP, in-Canada work experience accumulation, returning to eligible program)

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you taken a French language test, or assessed your current French proficiency level?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has explored the highest-ROI controllable score lever available to most applicants
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If yes and CLB 7+ achieved: the +50 points is already captured; move to next factor
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no: estimate time and cost to reach CLB 7; for most Mandarin-speaking candidates this is 3–6 months at manageable cost

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: This should be evaluated immediately if parental co-residence in the next 1–3 years is a priority

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Direct employment is generally straightforward to document; focus on ensuring your T4s and employment records are in order

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Are you monitoring IRCC announcements specifically for your category? Tech-specific draws may clear below 490.

diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Large Canadian tech companies are flooded; mid-size companies offer meaningfully higher response rates for first Canadian role

diagnostic_questions.question: What is the length of your Canadian graduate program?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Directly determines PGWP duration and therefore the runway available to gain Canadian work experience and apply for PR
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If 2+ years: you qualify for a 3-year PGWP — use that time aggressively to build CRS score
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If 1 year: 1-year PGWP is a very tight window; evaluate whether a PNP pathway or bridging program extension is available

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you already graduated, and if so, when did you graduate relative to the most recent PGWP policy announcement?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether early application could capture eligibility under prior rules before the new policy fully takes effect
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If recently graduated and policy change is recent: advise immediate PGWP application to maximize chance of processing under prior rules
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If still enrolled: plan program completion timeline with awareness of upcoming policy review cycles

diagnostic_questions.question: Does your accompanying spouse have language test results on file?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has captured the spousal language bonus (~20 CRS points) — a low-effort gain that is frequently overlooked
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Confirm the score meets CLB 7 and is submitted correctly in the EE profile
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Assess spouse's English or French level; if near CLB 7, this is a quick win worth prioritizing

diagnostic_questions.question: 你的预估CRS分数与当前联邦抽签分数线差距是多少？
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: 判断联邦EE路径是否可行，还是应该直接规划PNP或法语加分策略
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: 若差距在50分以内：考虑法语、Job Offer或配偶语言加分填补缺口
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: 若差距超过80分：优先考虑Alberta/Saskatchewan PNP等分数要求更低的省提名项目

diagnostic_questions.question: Is parental reunion a 'nice to have' or a genuine life priority that should influence your country choice?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Clarifies whether the person should weight the parental reunion factor heavily in US vs. Canada path selection, or treat it as secondary
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Then Canada's structural advantages (Super Visa, faster PR path for some) should be explicitly modeled against US economic advantages
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Then optimize for career and immigration stability; parental reunion logistics can be handled reactively
diagnostic_questions._source: zhihu_在美国还是加拿大工作_对国内父母将来的团聚有多大影响.txt

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your 5–10 year family formation plan, and have you modeled childcare/parental leave costs in both countries?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: The salary differential between the US and Canada looks very different before vs. after accounting for Canadian family benefits — the economic comparison changes materially for people planning to have children
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Have you run actual numbers on Canadian child benefit (CCB), Quebec/Ontario childcare subsidies, and parental leave replacement rates vs. US costs?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If family formation is not in your 5–10 year plan, raw salary differential is a more valid comparison metric

diagnostic_questions.question: Is your English score already at CLB 10 or above?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether re-taking English language tests is a useful strategy — at CLB 10+, additional English improvement yields diminishing or zero CRS returns
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Redirect time investment to French language study rather than English re-testing
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Improving English to CLB 10 is still a valid priority before pivoting to French

diagnostic_questions.question: Are you from a high-volume Express Entry country (India, China) and currently sitting between 460 and 480 CRS?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether standard profile optimization is sufficient or whether the applicant needs to pursue structural levers (French, job offer, PNP)
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: French bilingual route and a qualifying job offer are likely the only meaningful paths to a competitive federal CRS; also evaluate PNP options.
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Profile optimization may be sufficient; assess gap to current draw cutoffs before pursuing major structural changes.

diagnostic_questions.question: When does your current study permit expire relative to your expected PGWP approval date?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether a gap period exists in which the applicant will be on maintained status — and therefore unable to work
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Plan for a work-free period; do not accept employment during the gap without confirmed PGWP approval
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If study permit remains valid through expected approval, no gap risk exists

diagnostic_questions.question: Has your program changed its name or structure due to institutional restructuring — and does IRCC's records reflect the program you are actually completing?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: A mid-degree program restructuring could create ambiguity in IRCC's assessment of program length and eligibility
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Obtain documentation from the institution confirming the program's length and DLI status; consider proactively contacting IRCC or a regulated consultant
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: No action needed on this dimension

diagnostic_questions.question: Have you considered a contract or agency role as an entry point to get Canadian work experience on your resume?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate is aware of the 'backdoor' pathway that sidesteps the chicken-and-egg problem of needing Canadian experience to get Canadian experience
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: Is the contract role at a company or in a domain that leads to the type of permanent role you want?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: Contract/agency roles are a known alternative entry path surfaced by the community — worth evaluating if direct search stalls past 10–12 weeks

diagnostic_questions.question: What is your NOC TEER classification, and have you checked which PNP streams specifically list your occupation?
diagnostic_questions.what_it_reveals: Whether the candidate has a viable PNP pathway that could add 600 CRS points and should be pursued in parallel with federal EE
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_yes: If streams exist: are you employed or job-seeking in that province? Does your employer qualify to support a nomination?
diagnostic_questions.follow_up_if_no: If no matching streams: refocus on federal CRS improvement; consider whether a different province's labor market offers PNP-eligible roles in your field
